description:
Pick-Up Pizzas comes with nine mini-pizzas which fit into the round openings on the "jumbo pizza" playing board. The game also comes with two pizza delivery persons. Vinnie with a green cap and Bella with an orange cap. All the mini-pizzas and both delivery persons contain magnets.

Because magnets have polarity, five of the mini-pizzas can ONLY be picked up by Vinnie. Four of the pizzas can ONLY be picked up by Bella. Can you help Vinnie and Bella figure out which pizzas to pick up? Be the first to deliver three pizzas, and win. Hurry! The pizzas are getting cold!

Zero-Sum is an abstract strategy, tile-laying, area control game for 2, played with dominoes and chips.

Players take turns laying down dominoes and manipulating the playing surface to gain points by the end of their 13th turn. The player with the highest score wins. But that would be too simple... too bland. So to change things up, every turn you give your opponent points as you're giving yourself points. Strategy reigns supreme here.

boardgame: HeroClix: The Lord of the Rings
description:
This game is based on the popular Mage Knight system, but utilizes Lord of the Rings characters from the JRR Tolkien Universe and is compatible with the figures from other Heroclix sets.

The game is a miniatures skirmish game played on a grid (ie, no rulers).  Most of the details for each miniature are printed directly on each miniature base.  As the characters take damage, their base can be rotated thereby (typically) weakening their characteristics to reflect wounds or fatigue.

From the publisher's website:

“The Lord of the Rings" HeroClix miniatures game will be sold in two different packages: a campaign style box set and the traditional booster format. The all-in-one campaign style box set will include everything a player needs, including NEW epic level rules and maps. The booster format includes the figures, similar to other previous HeroClix releases. With whichever format chosen, all of the twenty or more figures will be compatible with other HeroClix miniatures games.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A patriotic game. Board looks like a Ludo board game but it is modified to represent the four Allied countries in the First World War. Players are either England, France, Belgium or Russia. The traditional Home space is renamed Berlin so it is a race for each country to get to Berlin first.

Shooting game. 6 targets, made of card. The targets are 'stood up' in wooden supports. A nice cannon is used to shoot at the targets. A player must knock over a ship to count as a 'success' and earn a point. You can decide before you start the winning conditions, first to score 5 successes wins, that sort of thing.
Box - "A battling good game".
I'm guessing the date but it looks to me like a WWII vintage game, when children, particularly boys, were very interested in all things military.

In Hört! Hört! (Hear! Hear!)

A board showing several objects is placed on the table. (The game comes with various different boards.)

Then, the board is covered, and a card is drawn which names all of these objects but one. This card is read out loud, and the players have to name the object that was missing. The first player to give the right answer keeps the card, and the next card is drawn.

After all cards have been given out, the player who collected the most cards wins the game.

boardgame: Wir reisen mit Karl May -Durch die Wüste-
description:
Object of the game:
Be the first to reach field no 110

Simple 'Roll and Move' game 
based on the book/story of 'Karl May',
well known German novelist;
Schmidt aquired rights to republish Game 1950 -
2nd edition is equipped with 6 cute pewter figures.

From the publishers description on the games box: Once a month all witches meet for their witches dance on the magic mountain. The witch with the wildest dance is rewarded with glittering gemstones. For this she is not only helped by her raven, also the magic wand and the die will bring her additional luck.

A roll and move game but with added gemstone collection by the witches and a second pawn, the raven.

Object of the game:
Be the first arriving at field 34

Simple 'Roll and Move' game, learning the cities & places of interest of the Black Forest.
Equipped with large plastic pawns, die and playing board.
traditional Black Forest dress

You finally have a new opponent for your Power Grid games: a robot that acts as an additional player. Thanks to his different actions and special abilities he is a strong opponent. You will manage the robot's "decisions" and can use him to act against the other opponent(s).

With this expansion, Power Grid is an exciting and fun experience for two (and more) players!

Power Grid: The Robots is designed especially for two players, but you can use a robot with up to five human players, too. You can even use more than one robot in a game.

The robot consists of five tiles, one tile with rules for each phase (except phase 1 »Determine Player Order« and phase 5 »bureaucracy«) plus a special ability, which the robot uses during the game.

With a total of 30 tiles - six different tiles for each phase - your Power Grid games will never be the same again!

Power Grid: The Robots FAQ has many Frequently Asked Questions which have appeared multiple times in the forum!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Power Grid: Supply Contract (Funkenschlag: Der Liefervertrag) is a promotional card for Power Grid.

Originally available for a donation at the 2F-Spiele booth during Spiel 2011, Supply Contract was also included with Spielbox 2013/#1, and has been available through the BGG Store since November 2012.

The Supply Contract lets you permanently move one step back in player order, both immediately after the card is purchased and each round after player order is determined; hopefully you have enough cash in hand when this card shows up in the power plant draw pile.

Fünf Tierfreunde (Five Animal Friends) is a memory game.

The game is played with 25 tiles. Each tile shows one of five baby animals: a foal, a piglet, a kitten, a baby dog and a baby goat. At the beginning of the game, these tiles are arranged in a 5x5 grid. On your turn, you roll two dice, which show the five animals and a question mark. You then have to turn over two adjacent tiles that show the animals shown on the dice. If you roll a question mark, you may choose what animal you will turn over. If you turn over a correct pair, you gain a friends token. The tiles are turned facedown again, and are covered with a cloud token. Until another successful pair has been turned over, the two covered tiles cannot be used again.

You win the game when you have collected four, five or seven friends tokens(depending on the number of players).

Publisher Blurb:

The March of Progress
The Jihad is over. A new Republic has been born. In the wake of one of humanity’s greatest conflicts, the Inner Sphere, Periphery and Clans must adapt to new political and industrial realities - a new balance of power. But changes on the map are only the beginning. The Word of Blake’s war has scattered a new wave of technological progress across nearly every realm, giving rise to a new generation of combat units. Only the sheer devastation of the relentless fighting has slowed the arms race that is sure to come... but for how long?

Technical Readout: Prototypes introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, ’Mech, and aerospace units that debuted in the years after the birth of the Republic of the Sphere. Featuring technologies rarely seen outside the testing fields or Solaris arenas, these new war machines provide a peek at the new age to come as mankind faces a new century.

Futbolider tries to simulate a european football team president doing this with a mix of several games. It's a board game for two players or two teams. In Futbolider you have to:
Buy properties and build sport installation and stadiu. 
Sign players in order to build your team. 
Score goals winning football trivia quizzes.
Disqualify your opponent using red cards.

Players try to complete as many telephone calls as possible for the highest score.

Themed around the exchange telephone numbering system once utilized in the United States, players spin for exchanges or draw numbers to add them to calls.  Each exchange can only be called once in the game; once all numbers have been drawn, the game is over.

Smoldering ruins are all that serve as a reminder of our once civilized world. Wary survivors nervously forage for meager food and scrap, constantly haunted by the shadow of death. Ravenous hordes of zombies march a desperate humanity toward extinction. Trust is but a memory, peace is no longer an option, and safety no longer exists…

Yet there is hope. A number of Visionaries are rising within the remaining human havens. These individuals have amassed followings to be reckoned with, engulfing the denizens of the waste in something far more than the daily struggle for survival.

What they have started, you must finish. With your leadership, humanity stands a chance to reclaim the Wasteland from the walking dead and revive civilization. Will your people unite with others against the zombie menace or crush and exploit their rivals

2-4 players clash for the future of humanity in Wasteland, a post apocalyptic zombie survival strategy game. Take control as one of eight factions pitted against one another and hordes of ravenous zombies, struggling to survive and rebuild civilization in their image!

With the help of your people’s Visionaries your fledgling band explores the waste, battling the restless dead and scavenging for resources. Victory brings hope, expanding your territory and raising morale. Survivors flock to your banner, shaping the future of your society with new ideas and issues. Will your faction rise to lead the rest of humanity back from the brink of extinction, or will they die alongside it?

Wasteland is inspired by games like Fantasy Flight’s Twilight Imperium, Flying Frog Productions Last Night on Earth, Avalon Hill’s Axis and Allies, and other favorites of the OHMS Gaming team. We’re confident it will satisfy your cravings of crushing your enemies, slaying zombies around you, and hearing the lamentations of your friends!

Major Features of Wasteland:


     ZOMBIES! ZOMBIES! ZOMBIES! Constant waves coercing players into uneasy alliances!
     Eight Factions with variable powers, each with three unique Visionaries, giving you the ability to customize your faction to your play style and adding replay value!
     A modular, gradually revealed hex map for a new Wasteland every game.
     Scouting encounters and Site cards adding additional risk and reward to conquering areas.
     Resource management, simulating the scarcity of useful material and encouraging strategic thinking.
     Speed based combat system relying on the management of your resources.

This deluxe game of Puerto Rico began as Puerto Rico: Anniversary Edition, a tenth anniversary edition of Andreas Seyfarth's Puerto Rico that celebrates Puerto Rico's  debut at Spiel 2001 with a production-ready copy that met mass acclaim.

Game play is the same as the original Puerto Rico game: The players are plantation owners in the days when ships had sails. By growing up to five different kind of crops – corn, indigo, sugar, tobacco, and coffee – they try to run their business more efficiently than their close competitors: growing crops and storing them efficiently, developing San Juan with useful buildings, deploying their colonists to best effect, selling crops at the right time, and, most importantly, shipping their goods back to Europe for maximum benefit.

The game system lets players choose the order of the phases in each turn by allowing each player to choose a role from those remaining when it is his turn. No role can be selected twice in the same round. The player who ends up with the most victory points – achieved by shipping goods and building – wins the game.

Puerto Rico: Anniversary Edition and its follow-up versions include two expansions: Puerto Rico: Expansion I – New Buildings, that first appeared in 2002 in Spielbox magazine and was later published by alea and Rio Grande Games, and the Puerto Rico: Expansion II – The Nobles expansion from alea's Schatzkiste collection of game expansions.

The game has completely redesigned components and artwork: The cardboard doubloons are now sixty metal coins, while the fifty octagonal resources have been replaced with colored wooden crates. The building tiles are now illustrated, and the cardboard for those tiles and other components are nearly 3 mm thick to give the bits more heft. The individual player boards, the VP tokens, the ships, and other components have also been redesigned.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: San Guo Zhi: Heroes of the Three Kingdoms
description:
San Guo Zhi is a series of board game based on the epic battle of the Three Kingdoms (San Guo), and its  first game in the series published in 2009 is Heroes of the Three Kingdoms, an original design collectible card came (CCG).

Heroes of the Three Kingdoms CCG is designed based on the historic characters and events. Therefore, the game rules that each card in the draw deck must be unique, and the same character can not appear on the battlefield at the same time.

During the game, player send generals to the battlefield based on the type of weaponry prepared. Player can also preset strategy card or play it from the hand. When one player's provision depleted, other player wins the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

boardgame: NUTS!: War Without End – Weird War 2
description:
From the publisher's website:

Weird War 2 Start with the inherent weirdness of World War II, add the public fascination with the new science being unleashed around them and top off with the popular pulps and comic books of the day – and you have set the stage for Weird War II. Welcome to the Weird War, a no-holds barred clash of chaos in which Combat Walkers stride the battlefields, Yankee Rocketmen blaze through the skies and ray guns reduce tanks to glowing scrap metal.

Here's what's included:

Some actual technology history.
Fantastic technologies.
Weird Science unit and vehicle lists.
Special or expanded rules.
General rules on creating your own Weird Science campaigns.
Sample scenarios that may be fought head-to-head with players taking opposing sides, cooperatively against the game or solo as either side.

NUTS! War Without End (WWE) is a supplement for the game NUTS! 2.0 by Two Hour Wargames. A copy of that rule set, as well as gaming implements and miniatures, are required to play.

A variation of the classic cricket action game. Small plastic fielders, a mechanism to allow batting, another one to allow bowling, a ball and a field made up of different run areas. It doesn’t get much simpler. Sister product of Balyna Football. Both games were designed to be a competitor to Subbuteo's cricket and football games.
In many ways the game is quite surreal, with the bowler being represented by an aluminim funnel from which the fielding team bowls the ball. The batsman is a counter weight which is held by a piece of cotton by the player. When the ball is bowled, the batsman releases the counter weight and, depending on the angle can play a variety of shots. Great scoreboard too, but completely useless as the scoreboard is metal and the score numbers cardboard. They fall apart far too quickly.

boardgame: He's Out! The New Test Match Game
description:
The rules say "With this game it is possible to follow exactly the normal course of a test match from the selection of the teams down to the final bowling analysis".
The game uses a spinner mechanism which overlays 3 generic batting cards, allowing the player to simulate different quality batsmen and bowlers.
A simple cricket game from the 20s, and one with some potential to modify if you put the effort in to create unique player discs for each batsman. The mechanism is similar to the one used in the classic Cadaco’s All Star Baseball, which was released 13 years after He’s Out. The game is endorsed by Wally Hammond.

Das letzte Bankett: Comtessa is a limited edition additional role for the party game Das letzte Bankett available from the publisher at Spiel 2011.

In the game, each player takes on the role of a guest at a banquet for the king, with each guest secretly part of one of two factions that want to bring about the king's demise. Will the king be opened with a dagger before the poison can do its work?

As with the other role cards, Comtessa has her skills printed on the back of the card.

In Die Panzerknacker (The Safe Crackers), the players attempt to get loot from safes by breaking the secret codes.

The game is played with 18 loot cards, 18 secret code cards, and four dice. At the beginning of the game, all the cards are placed face-up on the table, then the dice are rolled. Three of the dice (colored blue, red and yellow) show different numbers ranging from 1 to 18. The fourth die shows three "up" arows and three "down" arrows. If the "up" arrow is rolled, the players should consult the code card that matches the highest die rolled; otherwise they should consult the card that matches the lowest die rolled.

The code card shows a puzzle that uses the remaining two dice, specifically a table that involves some calculation with the numbers to find the right loot. After a player has determined the correct loot, he takes the corresponding card from the table. If he has picked the right card, he gets to place the card in front of himself. The first player to collect five cards wins.

The Battle of Loano occurred on 23-24 November 1795 during the War of the First Coalition. The French Army of Italy defeated the combined Austrian and Sardinian forces.
Loano battlefield presented some features which the wargaming world had never previously explored or only  partially: mountain warfare in the age of French Revolutionary Wars. The battle allowed the French to maintain a bridgehead in Italy with more chances of supply than the naval option, which was very dangerous due to cruising British ships, and saw the birth of the republican military star, general André Masséna.

boardgame: TC Tennis: Expansion Kit 1 – World Class Women
description:
World Class Women

From VPG's first and successful sports game, TC Tennis, designer Terry Coleman brings us it's first expansion: World Class Women. This first ever expansion of TC Tennis gives players a chance to pit 24 of the finest female tennis players in history against each other, in the same fun, fast-playing format as in the parent TC Tennis game.

With plenty of new players and strategies involved, make your way to the top and become a World Class Tennis Champion!

Note: This Expansion Kit is not a ‘stand alone’ game. You must own a copy of TC Tennis to use it.

Game Data:

Complexity: 3 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 3 on a 9 scale
Unit scale: Individual tennis players

Game Components:

• One Expansion #1 Rulesheet
• 20 Strategy cards
• 24 Player cards
• 8 single-sided square markers

Objective: Be the first to reach Berlin

equipment: 6 coloured cars, 35 (city) cards, 2 dice, large board with 35 (numbered) cities,
rules inside cover

Simple 'Roll and Move' game:
Each player starts in Berlin, drawing (city) cards to determine his route

Note: 1st edition (before WW II) differs from 2nd edition (after WW II) ==> see images
because of the success of the game it was reedited after WW II
without borders and - of course - expensive tin cars

Windfall: Galaxy is a game of expansion and business on a galaxy wide scale.  Each player represents one of earth's major companies in the governing conglomerate.  They have to work together, using each other as a resource, to build and make better the structures they own to create a better future for everyone. But, when it comes to business, someone has to get ahead, and in the universe of Windfall, being the best means being the one with power.

Windfall: Galaxy is a tableau building game where the goal is to get the most VP's from the central pool and build the best structures.  It's fast paced as player turns are just 1 action, and unlike other games, everything on the table can be used, not just by the player that controls it.

Windfall: Galaxy takes trading to heart as one of the first things a player gets to do during his turn.  There are many reasons to trade: To get a resource you need, to make a card you own better, or to use something of someone elses.

The sector board gives players a chance to use other player's turns to their advantage.  By piggy backing of an action, they can use their structures in the same sector, to perform a small action like drawing cards or taking small amounts of VP's, before it even gets to their turn.

Trapped! is an abstract game of capturing the opponents pawns, played on a 5-per-side hexagonal board (61 hex in total). Players take turns to either move their pawn(s) or add walls to the edge(s) of a playing space. Pawns cannot jump walls or other pawns and become trapped when they are confined to a single cell - when all edges are blocked by a combination of walls, pawns (of either player) and/or board edge.

Players have two 'moves' per turn, so can move one pawn 2 spaces, two pawns 1 space each, move one pawn and place one wall, or place two walls.

A player wins when all opposing pawns become trapped at the same time.

Each player starts with three pawns and can play walls from a shared pool of 120.

A cricket game "First-Class cricket in your home". The publisher goes on to say that it is hard to imagine a more thrilling game on your table.
The board is "gaily coloured" and represents the Kensington Oval, a revered cricket ground in South London.
The fielding team player can place his fielders on different locations on the board, hopefully to stop runs. The bowling and batting seems to be decided by card play (but it could be as simple as draw a card, read the result, i.e. no skill). Unfortunately, I only have a magazine advert to go by as this game is unknown in the British collecting circle.

"I remember back before the great war, we didn't have any fancy machinery guns to add to our two-winged air-scrapers.  So, when two hot-shot fly-boys needed to settle their differences, they grabbed a buddy and had them duke it out mano-a-mano on the wings of aeroplanes. Those crowds would sure hoot and holler as we flew nearly 50 mph to heights of almost 30 feet.  You kids wouldn't remember those days.  So, I made this paper and card game-set box to show you nancies how we used to do it.  Back when flying and racing was pure.

Did I say racing?  Why I can't really remember why we were racin' too.  But, hell, that's exactly how I remember it, so that's how you're gonna play it.  The old-fashioned way.  We called it "Punchin' Planes" and that's how it was."  ~Grandpappy

In "Good 'Ol Punchin' Planes" players relive the days of early Pre-WW1 aviation.  The days when the only way to fight was to have a buddy stand on your wing and engage in fisticuffs (machine-guns were too heavy, or course).  Through the use of dual-purpose action cards, players have to navigate obstacles on the modular race-track while their fighters spar on the wings of their planes.  Will you win over the crowd and cross the finish line or go down in a ball of flames?

Play is fast and actions are revealed simultaneously.  While the game uses a rock-paper-scissors mechanic for fighting (punch, kick, grapple), there is always the option for the losing player to "trade blows," essentially turning the tables.  But, since cards are not always drawn each turn, losing a battle may sometimes be more beneficial than winning.  With obstacles that bring players one step closer to explosion, hand management, and plane positioning complicating each situation, "Punchin' Planes" is a short game for 2 players with a surprising strategic depth.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Shakespeare CARDUTA is a simple card game based on the traditional Japanese card game Hyakunin Isshu      (100 Poems by 100 Poets), which uses one hundred ancient Japanese short poems. "CARDUTA" is a made-up word from "card" and the Japanese word "uta", which means "songs".

The full set includes 48 word cards and 48 word-and-picture cards, with the cards containing quotations (and half-quotations) from Shakespeare's plays. For example, a word card might read "No profit grows where is no pleasure taken.", while the corresponding word-and-picture card reads only "where is no pleasure taken", with the picture giving a clue to the first half of the quote.

To play, lay out all the picture cards face-up on the table. One person draws a word card and reads the first half of it; everyone else looks for the matching picture card, with the first player to pick up the correct card claiming it. The next player then draws a word card, etc. The player who claims the most picture cards wins.

Shakespeare CARDUTA is also available in two half-game versions: a Tragedy Set and a Comedy Set, each with 24 word cards and 24 picture cards.

In Kings Plate, based on the traditional Japanese chess game Shogi, the player's goal is to checkmate the opponent's king.

To do this, you must use the various units in your army, with each unit having different abilities in terms of movement and special actions. Players can create their own armies from the pieces included in the game, thus forcing them (and their opponents) to rethink their strategy each game.

The "Classic" edition of Kings Plate comes with a plastic game board and metal pieces.

boardgame: Kings Plate: Card Game – Series I
description:
In Kings Plate, based on the traditional Japanese chess game Shogi, the player's goal is to checkmate the opponent's king.

To do this, you must use the various units in your army, with each unit having different abilities in terms of movement and special actions. Players can create their own armies from the cards included in the game, thus forcing them (and their opponents) to rethink their strategy each game.

Kings Plate: Card Game - Series I includes a king for each player and a variety of army units. These cards include suits and ranks as in a regular deck of cards, and they can be mixed with the other series in the Kings Plate family.

In EndBreaker! SCG: Build & Break – AmatsuKagura, a deck-building and card-selecting game, you are a leader of a brigade of EndBreakers, those who can gaze into the eyes of others and see their ending in a scene from that person's new future, with critical implications.

This game is set in AmatsuKagura, one of the city states of the world of EndBreakers, a world threatened by the Masquerades: those possessed by a mysterious power, the Thorns. You recruit party-members (Job cards) in the tavern, learn powerful skills (Ability cards), clear missions (Scenario cards) and battle against monstrous evils (Masquerade cards). You earn victory points (Dalc cards) by clearing scenarios or winning battles.

The Masquerade cards are revealed one by one during game play, and each player must fight this monster, earning VP according to how well they do. After three battles are resolved, the game ends and the player with the most VPs wins.

EndBreaker! SCG: Build & Break – AmatsuKagura, which introduces the battle skill "Hyper", is both a standalone game and an expansion for EndBreaker! SCG: Build & Break.

boardgame: Kings Plate: Card Game – Series II
description:
In Kings Plate, based on the traditional Japanese chess game Shogi, the player's goal is to checkmate the opponent's king.

To do this, you must use the various units in your army, with each unit having different abilities in terms of movement and special actions. Players can create their own armies from the cards included in the game, thus forcing them (and their opponents) to rethink their strategy each game.

Kings Plate: Card Game - Series II includes a king for each player and a variety of army units, including powerful monsters like devils, unicorns, dragons and angels. These cards include suits and ranks as in a regular deck of cards, and they can be mixed with the other series in the Kings Plate family.

boardgame: Kings Plate: Card Game – Series III
description:
In Kings Plate, based on the traditional Japanese chess game Shogi, the player's goal is to checkmate the opponent's king.

To do this, you must use the various units in your army, with each unit having different abilities in terms of movement and special actions. Players can create their own armies from the cards included in the game, thus forcing them (and their opponents) to rethink their strategy each game.

Kings Plate: Card Game - Series III includes a king for each player and a variety of army units, including the strongest monsters yet and advanced human units. These cards include suits and ranks as in a regular deck of cards, and they can be mixed with the other series in the Kings Plate family.

Around 1300 A.D., Scotland and England are at war. The Scottish clans no longer tolerate the oppression by the English kings and ally themselves with each other in order to gain their freedom. Only by working together can they overcome the powerful English army in the battles of Stirling, Falkirk and Bannockburn. But there is a heavy rivalry between the clans; each of the clan leaders wants to contribute as much as possible in order to be crowned as king of Scotland in the end, and they are not afraid to resort to treachery if needed.

In order to have the most points at the end of 1314 A.D. and rule over Scotland, you will need to increase your clan's prosperity without neglecting warfare and heroism.

UNRELEASED GAME

    
    
         Note that all details for this game are preliminary. If you have information about this game, you are invited to edit this section or submit corrections.

    



In order to quell the civil unrest in Nordland, King Harald Blauzahn orders his son Sven Gabelbart to build a castle: Trelleborg. However, as soon as the work on the first houses commences, there is already evidence of sabotage. It seems some of Forkbeard's own people are involved, but no saboteur has been caught. This places a double burden on the people: to build the castle and to uncover Trelleborg's secret!In Das Geheimnis von Trelleborg, players gain investigation points by investigating the secret. However, it is just as important to gain construction points by aiding in the construction of the castle and loyalty points by supporting life in the village.

When the Stone Circle has been completed, the player who can contribute most to the construction of the castle wins.

KARMABUSINESS, a game to stimulate the Entrepreneur that lies within us all, is a simple and realistic simulation of the world of business in which the creation of a successful company is not the objective, but merely the means by which we attain a far less prosaic goal: the realization of our Dreams.

In KARMABUSINESS you will:


    Start up Businesses at different levels.
    Develop your Negotiation skills.
    Undertake exciting tests for your Personal Development.
    Use your Resources to generate employment and wealth.
    Detect and take advantage of business opportunities.
    Set aside part of your profit for Social Well-being (Karma).
    Invest in Advertising to increase your market share.
    Secure the capital to drive your projects through Loans.
    Acquire Shares in your opponents' companies.
    Discover your Dreams and make them reality!

Designed by Toco, this free DIY dicegame won first place in a design-contest at GeekDo. (Twilight Imperium Express.)

Planet Masters is a dicegame that contains all the excitement from boardgames about planetary control and war. It captures much of the core essence: clash of fleets, embittering politics and empire building. Planet Masters compiles cunning game mechanics into a very compact dicegame.

A set of only 4 dice per player is necessary to play. Each player starts with one planet dice to build expand his empire. Players roll planet dice for resources to build weapons (dice) and gain power. This power can be used to conquer planets from other players.
Some free planets (dice) are in a common pool called "Galaxy". When a player rolls a "Transporter" ship icon on his weapon dice, then she/he can capture a fresh planet to add to his empire. First player to reach a preset amount of victory points wins.

While playing other boardgames of space conquest can consume hours per game, this express version enables you to play a quick game that only lasts a couple of minutes. Now you can conquer galaxies during lunchtime! Invading planets and building a galactic empire has never been this fast.

Handmade Wars is a strategy war game. It consists of electronic pdf files, which the user before the first play, has to print and cut the necessary pieces out. The game emulates the development of an empire, by building villages, gathering resources and in the end constructing military camps and units to conquer the enemy.

The players in the beggining have only their capital city and some resources. They have to build worker villages next to the field's resource blocks to generate more resources each turn, needed to build more buildings. Specific buildings allow military units to be produced (which also cost resources). The military units can attack the enemy's units or buildings, with a dice rolling battle system.

The winner is the player who manages to destroy the enemy's capital city!

Objective: Be the first finishing the game circuit twice (field 50); 2nd goal: Your team (2 players) should be the first

Each team chooses a car with the same colour white/red/dark blue/yellow. Determine sequence of players - use only one die to move your car.

First round: only red fields 'create' events

Second round: only blue fields 'create' events

Due to mechanics of 'event orders' it is recommendable to help
your 'stranded' partner.

The design of the cars is - of course - suitable, the painting of the board very detailed and cute.

Remark of the publisher: More than 6 players can participate - you have to buy additional cars
Pantheon: Neue Untermieter is a set of six tiles for the 2011 release Pantheon that was available as a promotional item at the Österreichisches Spiele Museum booth at Spiel 2011.

User Summary

This is a relatively simple title in which players advance race cars on a track by spinning a spinner. Each space on the spinner has two colors with associated numbers. When the spinner lands on a spot, both cars with the appropriate color move the number of spaces indicated. (For example: There's a spinner spot that shows a yellow 5 and a blue 3. When the spinner lands on this spot, the Yellow Car moves five spaces, and the Blue Car moves three.

Cars may change lanes if they have an unoccupied spot to move to. (A car that is boxed in my even move backwards to get free.) If two cars "collide" by occupying the same space, then the car that initiated the collusion is penalized two laps, and the car that got hit, one lap.

The number of laps is determined by player agreement at the beginning of the game.

Game includes a board, four plastic cards, and an insert with a spinner and a series of peg holes to track the laps. (Some plastic pegs are also included.)

There is no indication of copyright year on the box, but based on other similar titles by the same publisher, it was probably produced in the 1960s.

From the publisher:
You are a court gardener of the mythical Queen Semiramis. All plants have gotten mixed up and your task is to put them in such an order that neither plants of the same shape nor plants of the same color are standing next to one another. There is only one unoccupied space - and after solving the task, this space should be in the center of the board.

The fronts of the 24 cards show different starting positions. The first tasks can be solved within a relatively short time, but then they become increasingly more difficult.

The game board for Ennea consists of nine wooden squares, each with four holes on them and each having a different pattern of grooves that connect these holes to one another and to the edges of the square. Designer Michail Antonow has created six games that can be played on this game board, each of them lasting 10-15 minutes. Four of these games – Bockspringen ("Leapfrog"), Wie du mir... ("What Goes Around..."), Berühr mich nicht! ("Don't Touch Me!") and Landung ("Stop") – start with an assembled game board, while the other two – Die vier Quellen ("The Four Springs") and Eine Insel entsteht ("An Island Forms") – create the playing area from these squares over the course of the game.


 Game description from the publisher:

Neun Quadrate, vor jedem Spiel neu angeordnet, machen die Faszination dieses Spieles aus, genauso wie die unterschiedlichen Spielmöglichkeiten!

Viel Tüftelei und Überlegung war notwendig, um Ennea entstehen zu lassen. 9 Quadrate mit gänzlich verschiedenen asymmetrischen Linien sollten es sein. Diese Linien mussten dann auch fortlaufend über alle Quadrate hinweg zusammen passen.

Sechs verschiedene Spiele für 2 Personen hat Michail Antonow sich einfallen lassen. Die Spieldauer bewegt sich immer zwischen 10 und 15 Minuten, die Vielfalt ist trotzdem groß - da dank des variablen Spielbretts kein Spiel wie das andere ist.

Abstract patriotic game, made at the start of WWII and trying to appeal mainly to young boys. Each player places his planes (pieces) in one corner, if a 4 player game, or in two corners if a 2 player game. Movement is one square at a time, with the objective to be the first to get all your planes first to the opposite side of the board. The gun placements in the middle of the board are out of bounds for the planes. If an enemy plane is next to one of your planes, you may fly over it, but, note, no planes are ever captured.
The game has been inspired by Chinese Chequers.

User Summary

This title includes 4 "Exciting" race games Grand Pree Road Race, Demolition Derby, Drag Race Championships, & The 500 Mile Race. These games share some of the same components, but each game has a unique board, and two of the games have card decks just for them. It appears that none of these games were ever produced separately. All games may be played solo, or with a number of other players based on the game.

Grand Pree Road Race

Players use cars 1 through 12 and move the their cars around a game board track as instructed by a spinner and by use of a deck of cards that comes with the game. Special rules govern how many cars may occupy a space, and which car(s) get to move in the event of too may cars heading toward the same space. First player to pass the finish line wins.

Demolition Derby

Up to 30 cars may be included, although 10 players seems to me the suggested maximum. (This allows each player to control three cars.) Game is played on the Demolition Derby board which features some Safe spots where cars may not be hit.

Cars move by means of a spinner (numbered 1 to 30) to determine which car is moved and a special deck of cards which determines distance and direction of movement. (Some cards allow for a choice about direction.) Since there is a set turn order, a player may end up moving an opponent's car.

When a car passes onto or over another car, the stationary car takes a Hit. Cars that take three Hits are eliminated from the game and removed from play. A wipe-off scoreboard with a crayon and a felt eraser are included to keep track of Hits. Play continues until only one car remains.

Drag Race Championships

Again all 30 cars are used. The game uses the Drag Race board. Before play begins, the 1-30 spinner is used to determine which cars are in which Heats. Play progresses by using the 1-12 spinner which shows which color car is moved, along with the roll of a single die to determine distance. One car advances from each of the 6 Heats. A final round is played to determine the Champion.

The 500 Mile Race

This game is played on the box insert which shows an oval track with various Sequence Tables printed in the center. Game may be played with up to 30 players, each controlling an individual car. The game starts with the Sequence Marker on Sequence Table "A". Two dice are rolled and the numbers are added together. (The Sequence Table contains results from 2 to 12.) The result of the Sequence Table tells which cars may be moved... these cars are all advanced 3 spaces on the track. In the next turn the Sequence Marker is advanced and Sequence Table "B" is used. The Sequence Marker moves each turn, and goes back to Table A again if the game last long enough. Some Sequence Table results show hazards like Crash, Pit Stop, etc. The 1-30 spinner is used to determine which car is affected.

Four cars abreast stop cars behind them from advancing. However, there are a couple of spots on the board where cars are considered to crash if there are ever four cars on them. (This cuts down on the amount of log jams in a really crowded game.) Play continues until a car crosses the finish line. In the event of a tie, play continues until one car advances ahead of another.

The (many) game elements for this title include: one double-sided rules sheet, 30 die-cut pressboard cars, a sequence marker, a double-sided game board (with the Grand Pree race board on one side, and the Demolition Derby and Drag Race boards on the other), a wipe-off scoreboard (for Demolition Derby), a crayon & felt eraser, a 1-30 spinner, a 1-12 spinner, two 6-sided dice, a deck of 20 cards with an image of a tire on the back (for Grand Pree Road Race), & a deck of 20 cards with checkered flags on the back (for Demolition Derby).

No publication date is included with this title.

Objective: Be the first to finish

'Roll and Move' game with some modifications.
Of course, there are some fields to accelerate or decelerate your car (your bride is waiting for you, you have to make a phone call...), but using your three dice ==> only '1' and '5' are giving you the speed you need. It's up to your choice - if you gain a 1 or 5, to roll the remaining 2 dice again for all or nothing (early 'Can't Stop).

Remarks:
Due to (sometimes) small changes it's very difficult to track versions and define the publishing date of the first edition;
there are (at least) 3 different Gräfe editions and from 1970 the Spika one; if you check the boards you can detect a (confused?) numbering system - see images. 
Some Sellers (e.g. on ebay) declare a publishing date mid 1935, but to my knowlegde those 'Bikini maids', printed on the early edition were not permitted/accepted by National Socialists. But even more confusing - one auction shows hand written notes (to replace (lost) rules) in Sütterlin writing.
 - feedback is highly appreciated -

Relive the glorious moments of the epic race between Hare and Tortoise! Several decades ago, Hare suffered a crushing defeat against a determined Tortoise. This painful affair threw disgrace and dishonour onto the Hare's clan and it would take many generations for the affront to be forgotten. Forgotten, did I say? Never! Hare is determined to restore his family's honor – but Tortoise will not give up without a fight.

Woodland Race is an asymmetrical race game in which audacity and strategy will be your best assets! The players build the track over successive rounds, sometimes helping Hare and sometimes helping Tortoise. Hare is fleet of foot but will often need to stop for a rest, whilst Tortoise is slow but moves with purpose. Many obstacles arise on the way, and who will win is up to you...

From the back of the box

There is a new major rally... and you've hit the highway. Two American Icons, The Monopoly Brand and Harley-Davidson, have joined forces to create the most popular board game on two wheels. Monopoly, the world's most popular board game, proudly introduces a game designed exclusively for the Harley-Davidson enthusiast.

Buy, sell and trade the great motorcycles, engines, rallies and all that makes the Harley-Davidson lifestyle what it is! Hop on your Electra Glide Standard or Heritage Springer, but be sure to ride clear of your opponent's properties--landing on the wrong space could cost you big!

Game includes: Game Board, Property Cards, Money & Banker's Tray, 2 Dice, Chance Cards, Community Chest Cards, Package of 32 Garages, Package of 12 Dealerships, & 8 Pewter Custom Game Tokens.

Buchstabengitter is a word-searching game.

The game comes with 160 different word search puzzles, like the ones that appear in the newspaper. However, players will search for words simultaneously. Each round, a die is rolled, which indicates how many words must be found. Then, that number of cards is drawn; there is a word on each card.

Now, the players have to search for the words in the puzzle and mark them on their sheet. The round ends when a player has found all the words. Then, each player gains one point for each word found. The player that has gained the most points after four rounds wins the game.

The puzzles do not only contain each German word, but also the English translation, which makes it possible to use this game to learn English.

Game description from the publisher:

No one is safe from the schemes the Dark Elves hatch in their City of Winter. With three copies each of 20 unique cards, half of which support the Dark Elves and their evil schemes, City of Winter is an invaluable Battle Pack for players looking to harness the Dark Elves and their fury.

Additionally, all the factions gain exciting new tactics cards that can return to the deck after they are played. The fifth Battle Pack in "The Capital Cycle" for Warhammer: Invasion The Card Game, City of Winter presents dramatic new deck-building options for the Dark Elves and other factions with 60 new cards (three copies each of 20 different cards).
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Warhammer Invasion – Living Card Game

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Schwer-Sturmgrenadiere Command Squad – "Heavy Kommandotrupp"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

As the battles of Dust Tactics intensify, NCO Command Squads inspire their allies to tremendous feats with their new special abilities. Marching to the front lines in their protective battle suits, the Axis Heavy Kommandotrupp withstand enemy small arms fire as they call in artillery strikes, repair walkers and send them back into battle, or boost soldiers with powerful, untested chemicals. These powerful squads provide clear direction for other troops and offer players tremendous army-building flexibility.

Dust Tactics: "Heavy Kommandotrupp" - Schwer-Sturmgrenadiere Command Squad includes a unit card and three distinct miniatures.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Heavy Rangers Command Squad – "Corps Officers"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

As the battles of Dust Tactics intensify, NCO Command Squads inspire their allies to tremendous feats with their new special abilities. Marching to the front lines in their protective battle suits, the Allied Corps Officers withstand enemy small arms fire as they call in artillery strikes, repair walkers and send them back into battle, or boost soldiers with powerful, untested chemicals. These powerful squads provide clear direction for other troops and offer players tremendous army-building flexibility.

Dust Tactics: "Corps Officers" - Heavy Rangers Command Squad includes a unit card and three distinct miniatures.

Notch - A Tumbledum dice game from REMCO from 1955. This is a very simple game where you turn a tumbler over. That tumbler has dice in it which results in your roll for the turn. You can land on various spaces on a board (that board is actually a rolled up piece of plastic that has to be opened and flattened before you can play) There are go back spaces and there are GET NOTCHES spaces for capturing outlaws. When you reach the finish space the one with the most notches wins the game.

Vehicles is a quick, challenging worker placement game.  Unlike other worker placement games which can take two or more hours to play, Vehicles is designed to take about 20 minutes per player.  That means a game usually lasts about an hour and a half or less.  It's also quick to set up and get going.

Each of your vehicles is a worker in this game and each has its own abilities.  Move your Car to a land space to buy a part, or move your boat to the salvage where your car cannot reach.  You can build additional worker vehicles.  Will you build the fast rocket that always goes first but cannot carry cargo, or the sneaky submarine that can share a space with another vehicle?

Vehicles was created for The Game Crafter's first game design contest.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Call Up Put Down is a boardgame from Trading Pursuits Pty Ltd, a trading education company.

The objective of the game is to earn $1,000,000 from trading options. They begin with a starting capital (usually $10,000), and then make decisions whether to buy / sell Call options or Put options based on the charts viewed on the DVD.

The DVD contains 50 sets of charts, and only one chart is viewed at a time (by selecting a card that contains numbers 1 to 50 at random). The players make their decisions, and then moves to the next screen of the trade by using the DVD controls. When it reaches the last day to trade, all players must sell their options, and compute the final balance, which is recorded on the Trading Diary.

The board contains a track showing amounts from $0 to $1,000,000, and player's tokens move forward or back on the track depending on their balance. The first player who reaches $1,000,000 wins.

Components:
- 1 board with a dollar-sign track
- 6 tokens
- trading diary pads to record trades
- calculation pads to record stop-loss and profit targets
- strategy cards depicting signals when to buy/sell call/put options
- 2 DVDs (an Options informative DVD and a Trades DVD)
- game manual
- pencils

A battery operated British game. In the 1930's, Speedway was a tremendously popular sport so companies were quite keen to add a Speedway game to their portfolio.

This is a game of chance, no skill required at all. The board has a wheel with six different coloured speedway bikes on it. Players are invited to put their bets on to a Roulette-like board. You could play for real money or buttons. When all the bets are placed, you flick a switch and the wheel will spin. As each bike goes past a connection, their colour light flashes on, so with a fast spinning wheel, the lights all flash, adding to the excitement. When the wheel stops, there will be one light on and that is the race winner. Winning punters collect their bets and another race can start. The winner must be the one who has the most money when the game ends.

This is a simple roll and move game. If you land on an orange square you must obey the instructions. The best squares allow you to take shortcuts. The first to get to the end of the track, wins.

Dan Dare was a hero from boy's comics, making his debut in the Eagle comic in 1950. He was drawn by Frank Hampson. The character was an immediate hit and there was even a weekly serial transmitted on Radio Luxembourg.

An indoor quoits game. You thow your rings, with the aim of getting them to settle on to a hook and you score points for every success. The first to reach a pre-set number of points, wins. Or, it can be ended after a pre-set time limit and then the player with the most points, wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Dan Dare Shooting game by Chad Valley is a target game with the board featuring several aliens advancing. The gun, is a spring loaded Pop gun with corks. Object of the game is to be the first to knock out the alien targets.

During the early 1950's the Comic Hero Dan Dare spurred several similar target games that can easily be confused. Featured on this link is Chad Valley's version with the simple title "Dan Dare Shooting" game. Chad Valley also produced another Target Game called "Dan Dare Big Game Hunt on Venus".

Listed elsewhere on BGG is a Dan Dare Target game by the company Glevum with a very similar title called "Dan Dare Space Shooting".
A simple roll and move game. The board represents central London, with lots of crossroads, so your route might be diverted if you land on the wrong square. Nice metal playing pieces.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: WW1 Special Packs
description:
The first WW1 products for Wings of Glory are two extra-large models: the WW1 bombers Caproni CA.3 and Gotha G.V. Each Wings of Glory: WW1 Special Pack contains one painted and assembled 1/144 model with the flight stand and the maneuvre cards related to that airplane, the special rules leaflet, the plane consolle, and counters required to play with these models. The WWI bombers are featured in two different color schemes each.

Wings of War players can add these new models and their rules to the game without any problem.

Wings of Glory WWI planes are 100% compatible with Wings of War, using the same scale, base-fittings and pegs.

Reimplements: 

    Wings of War: Flight of the Giants
    Wings of War: Deluxe Set
    Wings of War: Miniatures


See Also:

    Wings of Glory: WW2 Starter Set

An action cricket game.

One player is the batsman, the other the bowler. The two, rather scary, figures have handles so that their arm can be pulled back and then released forward. The bowler's arm will launch a ball at the batsman. He, in reply, will try to swing his bat and connect with the ball.
The player who scores the most runs (i.e. hits) will win.

Sir John Berry "Jack" Hobbs (1882 - 1963) is widely regarded as one of the best opening batsmen of all time

Issue 4 of Heat of Battle's ASL magazine.

The magazine has 44 pages, contains 176 vehicle counters for ASL, 14 new scenarios for ASL, a new Deluxe ASL map (named BF1), a sheet with Deluxe ASL Rubble Overlays, and an APC Player Aid, as well as various articles about ASL.

From the table of contents:

Notes from the Command Post - Editoral
German use of Soviet Tanks - Red Steel
Rules Analyses - Slopes in ASL
Designer's Notes - Onslaught to Orsha
Chow in ASL - The German Gulasch Kanone
Scenario Analysis - RBF-17 Hot Spot
Heat of Battle Errata - HOB Errata and Q&A

The following scenarios for ASL are included:
RBF39: THEY REALLY KNOW HOW TO DIE; 14 September 1939; Brochow-Solchaczew, Poland
RBF40: KING'S GAMBIT; 9 April, 1940; Copenhagen, Denmark
RBF41: VATUTIN'S RIGHT HOOK; 14 July, 1941; Sol'sty, Russia
RBF42: KEYSTONE BEFORE MOSCOW; 26 November, 1941; Istra, Russia
RBF43: BLOOD FACTORY; 24 October, 1942; Stalingrad, Russia
RBF44: STEELINGRAD; 26 October, 1942; Stalingrad, Russia
RBF45: LONE BRIDGE OVER THE VOLGA; 14 October, 1941; Kalinin, Russia
RBF46: TEMPEST AT TOMBE; 1 July, 1943; Tombe Village, New Georgia, Solomon Islands
RBF47: SPLITTING THE SEAM; 30 July, 1944; Sept-Vents, France
RBF48: THE NARROW FRONT; 13 October, 1944; Würselen, Germany
RBF49: FROM BAD TO WÜRSELEN; 15 October, 1944; Würselen, Germany
RBF50: A PERFECT MATCH; 25 December, 1944; Foy-Notre Dame, Belgium
RBF51: FINAL GLORY; 3 May, 1945; Lampertswalde, Germany
RBF52: CHOU EN-LAI'S PROMISE; 6 October, 1945; Along the Tientsin-Peiping Road, China

An Australian cricket board game. It takes into account Test, One Day, Twenty20 and even back yard cricket.
The game is designed to replicate game conditions including real life statistics and score keeping. Lords of Cricket is fun and educational for kids, improving their maths skills while teaching them the fundamentals of cricket. The box says 'Great for players of all ages from 7 to 107' so any of you 108 year old cricket game fans are out of luck.

The Lords of Cricket playing board is divided into four parts, one for each of the different versions of a game of cricket. The board consists of six circles, each one representing the six balls in an over. Each game of Lords of Cricket is played with cricket ball-shaped cards, each designed to imitate different formats of the game of cricket.
Red cards are for Test cricket 
White cards are for One Day International and Back Yard cricket 
Yellow cards are used for Twenty-20 cricket 
There are four dice 
White - Ball Dice - numbered from 1 to 6 representing the number of the ball in the over 
Yellow - Appeal Dice - used to determine the type of appeal 
Red - Out Dice - used to determine how a batsman is out 
Black - Reverse Decision Dice - used to reverse an Appeal decision

In the 22nd century, the white mineral has become one of the most important resources. The material is a catalyst that enables many new production processes. Each player is a pilot of one of his company’s spacecraft, on the way to the planet GH-328 in the Panajay system. This undeveloped planet has large deposits of the white mineral.

The goal of each player in TF22 is to leave the planet with as much white mineral and as many units as possible. At the start of the game, each player lands on the planet with a landing craft and several units: a production unit, transporters, and combat units. The production unit can be used to create new units by consuming white mineral. The transports can be used to gather ore and bring it to the landing craft (though they can also carry some of the other units). The combat units can be used to capture or destroy enemy units. Each turn, players receive a number of action points to perform these actions.

Players should not only watch out for the units of other players. They also need to keep an eye on the tide. This can change every round. Areas that were previously accessible only by naval units may suddenly become accessible to land units instead, and vice versa.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A collection of six scenarios for Advanced Squad Leader, featuring Canadian units during World War II:

MLR1: HANA-SAKU; 19 December 1941; Mt. Butler, Hong Kong Island
MLR2: TROTEVAL FARM; 24 July 1944; Near Verrieres, Normandy, France
MLR3: MOOSHOF MELEE; 26 February 1945; Mooshof, Germany
MLR4: SNEAK ATTACK; 16 April 1945; The Village of Sneek, Holland
MLR5: THIRD TIME LUCKY; 1 August 1944; St. Martin-de-Fontenay, France
MLR6: GRENADE AND BAYONET; 1 March 1945; The Siegfried Line on the western edge of the Hochwald Forest, Germany

Volume 7, No. 3 of Critical Hit's ASL magazine, containing articles, scenarios and a historical map (Pointe du Hoc) for Advanced Squad Leader.

The following articles are included:
- Scenario Analysis: Akrotiri Gone Awry (a scenario from this issue)
- Küstenjäger: German Coastal Commandos in ASL
- Incidents with the 7th: A first-hand account of British combat on the battlefields of Normandy in 1944
- Tigers to the Front: A review of the scenario pack
- Busting the Bocage: A review of the ASL module

The following scenarios are included (BtB scenarios are played on the enclosed historical map):
BtB#1: NO NEED FOR ORDERS; 6 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
BtB#2: SHRAPNEL MOTIVATION; 6 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
BtB#3: A FUTILE HOUR; 6 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
BtB#4: ROUT THE KRAUT; 6 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
BtB#5; WALKING WOUNDED; 6 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
BtB#6: FROM OMAHA WITH LOVE; 8 June 1944; Pointe du Hoc, France
CH#170: BRIDEGROOMS  OF DEATH; 14 August 1936, Badajoz, Spain
CH#171: INTERNACIONALE FOLLIES; 14 December 1936, Boadilla, Spain
CH#172: WINTER BLUES; 19 March 1942; North of Novgorod, Russia
CH#173: 'BITCHES' ON THE BEACH; 11 August 1945; Sakhalin Island
CH#174: SETTLING AN OLD SCORE; 17 August 1945; Sakhalin Island
CH#175: ESCAPE HATCH; 24 June 1941; Grodno, Russia
CH#176: TOUCHDOWN!; 28 May 1940; Abbeville, France
CH#177: AKROTIRI GONE AWRY; 20 May 1941; Akrotiri Peninsula, Crete
CH#178: CROCODILE HUNT; 8 February 1945; Frasselt, Germany
CH#179: ACROSS THE BEREZINA; 9 July 1941; near Osipovitchi, Russia
CH#180: THE RUINS OF SILLEGNY; 19 September 1944; Sillegny, France
CH#181: THUNDER AT SEELOW; 16 April 1945; Seelow, Germany

Asara: Die Gaben des Kalifen (The Caliph's Gifts), an expansion included in the 2011 Wolfgang Kramer Almanach published by Spielbox, can be used with both the basic and advanced Asara game.

For the basic game, five locations on the board (such as the middle section of the window and the +8 space of the bank) are marked with treasure chest markers. One card from a deck of 20 is placed face-up next to each space. Whenever a player goes to one of these spaces with a buyer, he takes the card, and executes the action shown on the card: acquire 4-6 gold, buy one tower tile of the depicted type (top, middle or bottom), earn two prestige points, or choose one buyer from the deck. At the end of each year, each location receives a new card (in addition to the ones still there).

In the advanced game, one of the spaces in the "House of the Lighted Windows" is marked with a treasure chest marker as well, and also receives cards with bonus actions. In addition, four cards are added to the deck which force a player to buy a lighted window at a cost of four gold when he visits the space.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Los Angeles, 10th of August 1953. A ceiling fan rotates awkwardly, slowly pushing stale air around an ill lit office. Near a darkened window a lonely detective sits in a rickety old chair, feet on a desk, reading a police report. A couple of filing cabinets, a coat rack with a fedora, a bottle of bourbon and an ashtray overflowing with cigarette butts complete the scene. "Someone needs to get to the bottom of this case, it sure as hell won't solve itself!"

Vicious City is a narrative deduction game for 2-5 investigators. A crime has been committed and the players are tasked with solving the case, attempting to gain a conviction against one guilty suspect whilst ensuring another suspect, who they know is innocent, is absolved.

When the last evidence token is drawn from the bag the current round is completed and the case is resolved. Players are awarded victory points based on the outcome of the case and the player who scores the highest victory point total wins the game.

Fighting Eagles uses a simple game system to simulate the air battles of 1918 fought between the American and German air forces. Based on Rohrbaugh's Showtime Hanoi design, players will play head-to-head matches pitting several Allied aircraft vs. their German counterparts.

Allied player wins the game if their German target is destroyed and more German aircraft are destroyed than Allied. Any other result is a German win. If Red Baron (Fokker Dr1) is shot down or leaves the map the best the German can do is tie.

In addition the game will include two historical scenarios: Red Baron's Last Flight and Frank Luke's Last Flight and several variants including additional missions of bombing & balloon busting.

Players will need a standard deck of cards and one six-sided die (D6) to play the game.

Each copy of Fighting Eagles is composed of the following:


     One set of 40 die cut counters
     One 8.5 by 11 map with the Turn Record Track
     A set of rules

Players roll a colored die and pick a card of the appropriate color. Then they have to build whatever is shown on the card, as fast as possible. The first to finish gets to pick first one VP chip - but there is some luck involved in getting the better chips.
A very different version of The Game of Life, where money matters very little - in fact, the player with most happiness chips is the winner at the end!

Based on the Detetive (Cluedo) I.P. but with very different mechanics. Players have to discover who is the secret character, based on four characteristics: male/female, child/adult, dark hair/light hair, with hat/without hat. Simple rules, aimed at children.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description: Based on Banco Imobiliário (Monopoly), but with simpler rules and with a shorter playing time. Mainly, properties here are grouped by colors and symbols - and a player can build whenever he has a complete set of colors *or* a complete set of symbols.

Produced by www.GreenBusinessOwner.com, this game makes sustainability come to life in a fun, interactive way. Players are investors looking to make more than just financial returns by helping local entrepreneurs in Hawai'i develop sustainability-oriented businesses like organic farms, geothermal plants, green building companies, solar installers, and bike shops. The game is ideal for those who are interested in sustainability as a better way to do business, and can be played by 2-4 players ages 13+. It's also a great learning tool, and the parent company has produced free downloadable lesson planning materials for teachers! Check out www.GBOHawaii.com for more info on that.

The game consists of a beautiful board with the map of Hawai'i, several decks of playing cards used in the game, a green business property deck of businesses you can invest in, dice, player pieces, and a stack of money.

The game is set in Hawai'i, and players act as impact investors. Their goal is to start businesses that have a triple bottom line effect: make money, create good paying green jobs, and deal with three main environmental challenges of energy, food, and waste. To establish businesses, players must obtain certain resources like human resources (entrepreneurs, green collar workers, and the wild card--green MBA's). Some businesses require permits, mostly for larger developments and energy projects, and it's a humorous twist on permitting processes in local governments--full of possibilities and potential frustrations. Players must also navigate public policy and chance events that may or may not be helpful in achieving the goal of a sustainable economy in Hawai'i.

The player with the best Triple Bottom Line (people, planet, profit) score wins the game!

boardgame: Uncensored, The Party Game of Shouting Obscenities
description:
"I've compiled the most obscene words and embellished each one with politically correct artwork for your own enjoyment. In this game each player is stamped with one of these improper words which would be totally inappropriate in almost any other situation, however I've created a game that generates an environment that turns vulgarity into a socially acceptable fun time. Win by cussing out your friends and family. The game has three intensifying rounds of play so hilarious you'll swear you never had so much fun."

"Everyone draws a card from the obscene word deck.  After everyone has shares their new lable, players take turns flipping their cards in front of them. When the card onto of your pile matches someone elses , shout their lable before they shout yours.  The loser collects both piles of cards. The first one to get of their cards is the winner.  "

Game description on kickstarter page.

boardgame: Legend of the Cipher: The Game of Hip-Hop
description:
Legend of the Cipher: The Game of Hip Hop is a race to the top in professional hip hop. In order to succeed, you’ll make Moves, collaborate with Allies, put on Shows, employ Crew, experiment with Venture strategies, and buy Shine. As your Hype and Cash grow, your Career will evolve with your Opportunities, and you’ll learn to count your Bars as you kick your rhymes in a cycle of Ciphers and Battles that lead to the acquisition of skills, wisdom, and respect. Strategic deckbuilding and card play gives rise to multiple lyrical Battles and Ciphers in a revolutionary game that concisely emulates the reality of hip hop today... ALL of hip hop.

Legend of the Cipher is a deckbuilding game, where players start with a basic deck that produces Bars and Cash, which they will use to acquire Opportunity cards each turn to add to their deck. Each turn, players must also perform at Shows, using their Bars to attempt to out-rap the other players. This involves actual rapping, although one can easily play the included variant without raps. Successful raps will be rewarded with Hype, which is used to level up your rapper, in turn increasing both your hand size and production of Bars. The objective of LOC is to be the first player to level up enough to become a Legend of the Cipher.

From the Kickstarter entry.

The World's 1st Multiplayer Sudoku Game

"Dü-KO is like Sudoku, but only funner! Many efforts have been made to turn Sudoku into a board game, but the integrity of the puzzle always ended up being compromised. It was always a pain to setup and the truth be told not a real fun board game - with no real advantage for you the player.

So here comes Du-KO taking the concept of Sudoku to a whole new level, allowing for multi-players but still respecting the integrity of the solitaire player too. From 1- 9 players of all ages from 8 to 88 can easily and happily play Du-KO for a guaranteed good time.

The object of the game is to finish your section of the Sudoku grid first before your opponent(s). Each player takes their turn placing a peg in an open space within their designated portion of the puzzle. You'll go from clueless to competent your first game, and by your second game you'll have advanced from competent to competitive. Here's where it gets cut-throat and smokin' hot! You've been warned.

The amazing part about Du-KO is that it is self-checking! When you play, the game will tell you instantly that whether you’re wrong or right. If the peg is correct it will set all the way down, if incorrect it will remain slightly elevated and then must be removed and replayed on your next turn."

Objective: To earn the most shillings.

From TV series early 60's 'Nicki' (known as Noddy elsewhere)
it's a simple 'Roll and Move' game
Even some sources name 4 players only - 6 children may participate.
In the English edition there are only four car playing pieces.
artists: Eelco Martinus ten Harmsen van der Beek

description:
Party game that tests guests' sporting knowledge. Each guest is given a name of a sportsman, on a card but the letters spelling that name are all jumbled up. Guests are encouraged to mingle and, whilst chatting, casually ask each other if they can see their card and write down who they think the sportsman is. The one who identifies the most in the quickest time, wins.
Lots of variations of subject matter were also made by this publisher.

The games were mainly listed as being from the Dainty Series.

Party game. Guests are each given a card with a jumbled up proverb on it. Durind the party they can show each other their card, and if they know the correct answer, they fill in their record sheet. The person with the most right in the shortest time, wins.

Part of the publisher's 'Dainty' Series of Evening Party Games

A clever party game. Each guest is given a card and all guests are encouraged to show each other their cards whilst chatting. If you know the answer, you write it on your record sheet. The guest who gets the most correct answers in the shortest time, wins.

It is clever because you only have the images of tracks (footprints and the like) to go on and some are quite subtle.

A Malaysian abstract board game for two players. One takes the role of the Tiger, who is trying to eat the Men (orang-orang) and avoid capture. The Men must corner (or otherwise immobilize) the Tiger in order to win.

The Tiger is just one token on the board, and the Tiger can only jump over (eat) rows with an odd number of Man tokens. Men can move, but before that, the Man player takes their turn placing new Man tokens down on the board. Once the pool of 22 Men (some versions call for 24) has been emptied, the Man player may freely move the Man tokens.

Rimau is an asymmetrical strategy abstract that uses an expanded Alquerque board.

Dinosaur Cowboys is a fast playing (45 to 60 minutes) skirmish game of competing posses composed of up to 5 members and 1 dinosaur. Each character in the posse can be customized to fulfill a different role and purchase weapons and equipment from the many options available. Combat takes place in the futuristic landscape of the year 2285, where a supervolcano erupted and changed the course of mankind forever.

In the general the game is built around D12s and is meant to be light, thematic, and fast playing. Players build a Posse containing a Leader, 2-4 Members, and up to 1 Dinosaur, all of which benefit from some unique mechanics. Each Member has an Allegiance such as Duster or Neotechnoist which helps differentiate them (both thematically and statistically). Otherwise there are fairly standard statistics (Speed, Defense, Hitpoints, etc.) which can be customized by spending Improvement Points to really help define a role for each character. Furthermore Active and Passive Traits are selected to let characters do cool things like Crippling Shot, Speed Reload, Go For the Eyes, etc. Weapons and equipment are then purchased from a list of over 75 items ranging from laser Six-Shooters to plasma Shotguns.

A standard game takes place on a tabletop covered in terrain and uses 28mm miniatures and plastic dinosaur toys. Activation of entities is done on a model-by-model basis instead of the entire Posse at once, so the battles are very fluid with plenty of time to react to the enemy. This is especially true as Initiative is rolled at the start of each Activation "set" instead of staying the same for the whole turn.

Posses battle each other in one-off standalone games or in a linked campaign where further Improvement Points and Neodollars can be used to build and grow the group. Likewise injuries can accrue on characters that were taken out of action during an encounter. Deployment, objectives, and features can be used to add depth and variety to each battle beyond "kill everything".

The full rules are free to download and can be used with any range of miniatures. There is also a free "beginner version" (called Quickdraw) which condenses the core mechanics to under 10 pages, so players can jump into the game very easily.

Objective: first on target

Simple 'Roll and Move' game with cute meeples, based 'GDR rules' for educational purposes.  Nice drawings

"Many millions of years ago, on the planet Cybertron, life existed, but not life as we know it today. Intelligent robots that could think and feel inhabited the cities. They were called Autobots and Decepticons. But the brutal Decepticons were driven by a single goal: total domination. They set out to destroy the peace-loving Autobots, and a war between the forces of good and evil raged across Cybertron, devastating all in its path, draining the planet's once rich sources of energy. The Autobots, on the verge of extinction, battled valiantly to survive." - The Opening Narration from "More Than Meets the Eye, Part I

When an elite team of Autobots left Cybertron in search of energon, they were pursued by Megatron and his Decepticons. They crashed on Earth four million years ago and fell into a state of involuntary hibernation. Then, in 1984 they awakened, making Earth into a battlefield, with energon as the prize. Now the heroic Optimus Prime and his valiant Autobots must stop Megatron and the cruel Decepticons from plundering the Earth and fueling a war of galactic conquest.

Transformers: Heavy Metal War is a card game that allows players to command their favorite Transformers from the first two seasons of the classic 1984 cartoon show. Each player must manage their hand and deploy Transformers with a variety of powers in order to complete missions, each giving the victor a unique reward. You will not only have the likes of Wheeljack, Jazz, Starscream, and Soundwave at your command, but also a host of lesser known Transformers, including Tracks and Reflector, as well as cards for human allies and unforeseen events.

So what are you waiting for? "Transform and roll out!"

Endicott Epidemic: The Board Game - an action-packed game of survival and Zombie combat.

Can your band of survivors escape the Zombie-infested village of Endicott before the town falls victim to an eradicating nuclear purge? Or will you become infected by the Undead and turn on your allies? The possibilities are endless in a game that utilizes randomization and custom structure mechanics that immerse players in a completely fresh scenario with every new round of play.

Based on the ENDICOTT EPIDEMIC multi-media series created by filmmaker and author Douglas Harvey, Endicott Epidemic: The Board Game is the first full board game that accompanies the Endicott Epidemic: Infectious Contagion card game.  While Infectious Contagion was the condensed, "lite" version of the main game, EE:TBG is an epic battle for survival that offers all of the insane mechanics and suspenseful buildup to destruction that the card game couldn't bring to the table (literally).

Utilizing game boards acting as maps based on primary areas of the Endicott Epidemic story (with fully functional expansion "connections" to mix and match your favorite ares as the game expands), players will drop their chosen characters (each with their own unique abilities that can be used strategically) into the heart of the epidemic and begin battling the Undead using classic roll-and-move mechanics as well as a series of unique "Intrique" cubes that modify gameplay with every roll.  A deck of Inquest cards act as side-missions that can be ignored or completed to gain resources used to fight and survive, or to gain special tokens that can be used in many different combinations to unlock new areas of play as the game progresses.

Combat is as simple as choosing your weapon (or brawling unarmed, if you so choose) and rolling the Combat Cubes.  Each face of each cube means a different value, giving you numerous combinations of attack to dish out against your chosen enemy.  But while rolling the cubes is the simple part, bear in mind that your enemies can (and most of the time *will*) fight back or dodge your attack entirely.  Strategize your plan of action before engaging the enemy to give you the best course through the hordes of Undead.  Or, if you think you have the stealth abilities to do so, roll the dice to try to evade the groups of enemies and slip through unnoticed and live to fight another turn.

But beware - hidden with the game's deck of cards lay numerous deadly obstacles ranging from stronger than average 'Boss' Zombies, Black Ops agents, and even a handful of mutant Hazard Zombies, which spread MBI's toxic plume everywhere they go.  Once these villains begin to appear, the spreading plume is something the Survivors must be extremely wary of.  Plume markers begin to build in each area and once they reach their critical mass, deadly chain reactions are caused that could potentially wipe out every living thing on the board.  The biggest and most brutal enemy within the deck, however, is the Countdown - eight cards that constantly remind players of the impending nuclear purge.  Once the eighth and final card is drawn, the game is over - regardless of whether or not the survivors have completed their mission to find the Hermit, his critical intel, and his escape route.
