With all of the chaos going on as each Survivor makes their way through the town to complete their mission, the gameplay could get a little too intense for some people's liking.  Endicott Epidemic: The Board Game utilizes a completely customizable experience so that players can create the best scenario that is tailored to how they want to enjoy their game.  Removing certain aspects of the game like Hazard Zombies or Plume counters can greatly reduce the amount of suspenseful buildup that could turn players off from a game that could potentially be nearly impossible to win.  Inventory management and play deck building are also ways a group of players can ease the difficulty and ensure that the game, if played with all the right moves and strategic plans, can indeed be won without laying at the mercy of the Countdown or the roll of a die.

The board game, at its core, is designed for 2-8 players.  However, like Infectious Contagion, the board game also allows a single player to enjoy the game experience as the lone survivor of Endicott’s shift from living to undead.  Other game modes include a point-based Survivor mode that removes the Countdown and many other aspects of the game to allow players (or factions/teams of players) to battle waves of Undead, tallying up points at the end of each round and crowning a winner after a set amount of battles.  Or, players (or teams) can simply battle each other without the inclusion of Zombies (but with a Countdown) in a vastly customizable VS mode.

A ticking time-bomb's clock, swarms of Undead that will chain themselves together to create deadly hordes, an ever-spreading toxic plume beneath your feet, stealthy Black Ops agents that mark you for execution when you fall within their sights, a limited number of resources that must be used in conjunction with an even more limited inventory system, a constant surge of adrenaline with every roll of the Intrigue cubes, making allies with your fellow Survivors only to find your group surrounded by Zombies with only two bullets and a grenade...all of this and more is what builds your game up from a simple beginning to an all-out epic battle for survival.  Think you can handle it?

Join the Exploration League of Humanity (ELH) for a thrilling exploration of the galaxy.  Discover new planets, moons, and space stations as the most technologically advanced exploration carrier of the ELH, the Arcturus, travels from star system to star system.

Each planet the Arcturus travels to plays out as a round where only one card may be played per player.  Earn exploration credits by contributing the most to exploring the planet or give yourself an edge by upgrading your starship with new weapons and systems.  You can even become the ambassador to foreign space stations, helping you build even better starships to captain.

At the end of the game, the player with the most exploration credits wins the game.

Splice is an evolution of Spline that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set, as it uses a 4x4 holes board and balls in three colours (white, black and red).

Start: The board starts empty.

Play: Players take turns placing either:

1) a ball of their colour on any hole or any platform that contains
at least one red ball, or

2) a red ball on any hole or any platform.

End: A player wins by making a flat line spanning side to side, or
corner to corner, on any level, composed entirely of friendly and
neutral balls (at least one of each).

If the placement of a red ball achieves this for both players, then
the mover wins.

Strategy: White should place at least one red ball on the board level,
otherwise Black can force a win due to parity. White should defend
this red ball so that Black can’t exploit it.

Spoing is a capture game that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set , as it uses a 4x4 holes board and balls in three colours (white, black and red).

Setup:

Place 8 black balls and 8 white balls according to this figure. Red balls are considered ‘neutral’:

● ● ● ●
● ● ● ●
○ ○ ○ ○
○ ○ ○ ○

Rules:

Two players, White and Black, alternate turns moving a ball of their colour or a neutral ball (red) to jump over a single ball of another colour and land on any ball to capture it. Jumps may be diagonal (as per Checkers) or orthogonal. Players must capture a ball each turn.
The ball landed on is removed and the ball jumped over is converted to the colour that is not the jumper or the jumpee, e.g. if the jumper is a white ball and the jumpee is a black ball then the jumpee gets replaced with a neutral ball (red).

The game ends immediately when a player can't move on their turn (which could happen if there are not three balls in a row somewhere, or if all remaining triples are unusable by the player). The winner is the player with the most balls of their colour on the board.

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

Soft cover:

http://www.lulu.com/shop/cameron-browne-and-nestor-romeral-andres/shibumi-rule-book/paperback/product-18940123.html

Downloadable PDF:

http://www.nestorgames.com/rulebooks/SHIBUMI_EN.pdf

Sphex is a connection game that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set, as it uses a 4x4 holes board and balls in two colours (white and black).

Rules: The board starts empty. The opening player places one ball of their colour, then players take turns placing two balls of their colour on legal positions (holes or platforms). The mover can additionally place a neutral ball on their turn, in any order.

Aim: A player wins by connecting any two opposite sides of the board level with a chain of their balls. Balls connect if they touch, i.e. orthogonally adjacent or resting upon each other.

A simple trivia party game.
12 sheets of different questions are provided. During a pause at your party, you can hold an imprompu quiz. The winner is the one who gets the most right answers

Party game. There are 15 sheets, with three pictures on each sheet. Guests are given a sheet each and, during the evening, must study the pictures and make a list of all the things wrong with each picture. For instance, a man might be holding an unopened umbrella but he is holding the umbrelly upside down.

The guest with the most correct observations wins.
Party game. Players are asked trivia questions to score points but if they get an answer wrong, they must do a forfeit. The one who avoids the most forfeits, wins
Party game. Each guest is handed a picture. As they mingle during the evening, they try to match their picture. The first to be successful, wins.

Children's party game. Each child is given a shopping list and an article card. The object is that each child will ask other guests what their article is. If that article is on their shopping list, then they can mark it off. First to complete their shopping list, wins

Party Game. Guests are given a card. During the evening, you will be given clues by other guests as to the telephone number you are trying to find. The first guest to guess their telephone number correctly, wins
(By the way, in the 50's you didn't just have a number, you have a place-name that comes before the number e.g. Maida Vale 1234 would be a possible number).

Foundations is a 2 players abstract strategy game that is played using board and pieces of an Isaac set.

The aim of the game is getting rid of the value of your pieces by carefully placing them on the board or on already played pieces, thus creating a tridimensional structure developed on different levels.

Players take turns placing one of their pieces in ascending order of length. If they can't find space for a piece, they must build on the next level in height so that both ends of the piece rest on top of two pieces of its same colour.

When players are forced to build on new levels, they can begin with a smaller piece, but they must then play again in ascending order of length and they cannot play in lower levels anymore.

The game ends when players cannot place any more pieces on the board. The winner is the player with less points still in hand. In case of a tie, the winner is the player with less points at the highest level on the board.

Children's party game.
Each child is given an answer sheet and must try to find out the ages of the other guests. This encourages social skills as you probe for answers. The first to complete their record sheet, wins.

Two party games in one box.
The first. Each guest gets half a head picture and must find their other half to make a complete head. First to do so, wins
The second. Each guest is given a card with a word on it. They must then find another guest's word that will form a correct longer word when you link the two words together. First to do so, wins.

Hari is a 2 players abstract strategy game that is played using board and pieces of an Isaac set.

Players take turns sliding, discarding and placing their pieces in order to have the most points on the board.

Pieces must be used in ascending order of length and sacrifice short pieces to make room for longer ones is often required to achieve victory.

Action game. The cat board has two holes, one worth 2 points, the other 4 points. Players take turns in trying to throw a ball into the cat's mouth. If you are successful, you score the points depending on which hole the ball comes out from. The one who scores the most points, wins.

boardgame: War Planes A Card Game for Aircraft Spotters
description:
A box of Second World War aircraft identification cards titled 'War Planes - A Card Game for Aircraft Spotters'. The game was a Quartet game but with the unusual difference that the sets were not equal. The thrifty government, at the outbreak of war, ensured that players could also use the cards for regular card games by taking out the extra suit of 13 cards.
The pack comprises of 67 cards organised as follows:
65 illustrated cards representing five different views each of thirteen British and German aircraft, and two 'Key' cards.

It was intended that Spotters, at airfields or on the roofs of London buildings, could practice recognising aircraft to improve their skills.

Contents: 90 square cards, cardboard stands, score pad & pencil

Each player receives 7 tiles. The remaining tiles form the face-down draw pile. One tile is turned face up, and the start player tries to match a tile from their hand to the face-up tile so that one edge matches. The next player then tries to match one or more edges etc. Black is a blocking colour and cannot be matched.

If a player manages to make a  match, the score is the sum of the printed-on values of the tile they placed plus those of the tiles that this tile now is adjacent to. Some tiles have stars, these double the value of the edge that the edge showing the star is matched with.

If a player cannot match a tile on their turn, they draw another tile and game continues with the next player.

Once one player runs out of tiles, all other players get one more turn after which the values of their remaining tiles are deducted from their scores. Highest score wins.

The players represent members of a band. After a concert, they try to spend their whole salary from the gig. The goal of the game is to spend all your earned money, accumulate 8 hours of time cards, and go to your apartment.

The game is played by moving your pawn over a mapboard, showing the city of Vienna, overlayed with a grid. On your turn, you may move your pawn one or two spaces by foot, or three or four spaces by taxi, the latter costs money. You try to visit event locations (like Rosie's Café, your band's rehearsal room, a hot dog booth and various others). On most spaces you have to draw cards, which will help you to spend money and acquire time cards.

The trick is to keep the right balance between spending money and acquiring time cards. You need at least 8 hours worth of time cards, then you can try to get to your apartment and win the game - but only if you have no money left.

This game's pawns represent members of a locally known band in Austria; the title is Viennese slang and translates to "night shift".

Its Pitzelpatz (a teddy bear) birthday! You want all 6 guests to come. For this 2 players each get a house and then try to use the colour die to pick the appropriately coloured guest. The first one to roll all 6 colours and thus get all 6 guests to his house wins.

5150: Star Army is a rewrite and update of the military and bugs sections of the original 5150.

This game has a strong focus on military skirmishes of up to about a company, with a platoon being more typical.  Each figure represents one man, alien or vehicle.  There are three basic scenarios- patrol, attack and defend, which are each altered by a system for randomly laying out the board as well as generating opposing forces for solo players.  Individual games can combine in a campaign where individual soldiers improve over time and new recruits might become available.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A 50 card deck of death predictions: ways in which people can die.

Rather than being a game in itself, the cards are a game system: they are used to create and play other games, or optionally supplement other existing games. The cards come with rules for games with players being encouraged to create their own.

The cards feature a single word or phrase detailing how a person can die: for instance, OLD AGE or TRAGIC FLAW or FED UP BARISTA. These predictions can be used in party games ala Apples to Apples: one player names a weapon and the rest submit a card from their hand that they feel could have been given to a person killed with that weapon. They can be used in storytelling games, where each player gets a card and the group must construct a series of events in which everyone dies. The rules also include an RPG of sorts, where players must buy items to kill targets with the deaths dealt from the deck.

Spao is a 3-player ball-stacking game that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set. It uses a '4x4 holes board' and balls in 3 colours.

Each player has an allocated colour (white, black and red). Players take turns placing a ball of their colour in a legal position (hole or 2x2 platform). When the pyramid is full the game ends. To get your score multiply the sizes of your groups. Highest score wins.

Variant for 2 players:

Red balls are neutral. Each player takes 10 balls of his colour (white or black) and 5 red balls. On your turn you place one of your balls in a legal position. Same scoring.

Rules for this game are included in the soft cover Shibumi Rule Book

At the starting gate the tension is very high, the  snow is perfect, the atmosphere is the one of the big occasion. In an explosion of force, power and technique you face the first run of the giant slalom during the first meeting of the white circus.
You are one of last season's best skiers and lots of fans are expecting a great result from you and you cannot disappoint them. You will count on your incredible talent, your explosive strength, your cold lucidity. As cool calculator you will fight against all the hazards of the track to the best of your abilities.
Win the race and be on the cover of the most famous sporting magazines of the world.

Slalom is a game for 2 to 4 players (the single descents serve only for training). The competition is divided in 2 runs where every skier in turn tries to descend in quickest possible time to complete the slalom. The skier will move on the 14 tiles, using the values of the card they received at the start and at the two intermediate times.
Every move on the race tiles will be followed by a positive or a negative change of the starting time.
The hourglass will force the players to organize the sequence of their plays in the available time, in order not to lose precious hundredths of second.
The cowbell represents the support of the people near the track and it will help the skiers during the critical moments of the race...

A trick-taking game centered around US politics.  Each card represents a controversial and/or humorous issue being discussed on 24-hour news stations, and tricks represent gathering the most viewers (via making the most controversial statements) at different time slots.  Instead of conventional bidding, players select their true party (hidden) and their apparent party (visible).  Each party offers different scoring incentives and different special powers, and players sometimes have the option to switch parties.

StrikerZ Card Game Description:
 StrikerZ Card Game is a fantastic football game with cards! It is not a game "about" football. It IS football! Players pick and discard through a full deck as they try to get one of the three combinations to shoot for a goal! Each team has 11 players, 3 super-subs and a manager! Enjoy light and fun strategy as the shooter and keeper try to outsmart each other on shot attempts: Big Goals! Big Saves!
 Do you think that you will manage your team to easy victory? Will the referee and assistants influence the game for you or against you? Watch out as there are whistles, offsides, yellow cards and red card ejections! Either manager can make a key substitution at the right time to change the game! A game of skill and luck!
 This is an excellent game for football fanatics, couples, or children.

For 2 players, Ages 7 to Adult, 30 minute time frame.

Agricola: De Lage Landen is an expansion for Agricola that is published by the Dutch game company 999 Games. It combines certain decks previously released separately in other languages, as well as some new decks.

The expansion is divided in 3 decks (240 cards in total):
- Netherlands-deck (NL-Deck)
- Flanders-deck (FL-Deck)
- Wallonia-deck (WA-Deck)

Next to this, it also contains:
- Agricola: Through the Seasons
- Stylized grain and vegetables resource pieces (to replace the standard resource discs)
- Sticker sheet
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Kingdom is united no more! Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland have risen up to invade their historical oppressor! 2-4 players take the role of the four countries as they carve up England for their own glory.
Primarily card-driven, players will play attack cards to cities of different strengths (including bluff cards) to wrestle control of the cities away from their opponents. However if more than two countries turn up for a fight, politics and alliances intervene as players make simple deals with those in the battle.
Every time a city is captured, the final points value of the city drops. After five rounds of play the country with the most control points is the victor.
Little England is a free print and play game requiring only five pages for the game (four double-sided) and one double-sided sheet for the rules.
Card game with trivia questions about the Bible. It also has rules for a Biblical Patience game using the cards.

First the Pigeon wanted to drive a bus. Then the Pigeon found a hot dog. Now the Pigeon wants a match in this new game from University Games. Based on the Pigeon book series by Mo Willems, The Pigeon Wants a Match Game finds players trying to collect the most matches of hilarious images from the books. Color-coded borders and backgrounds make pairing tiles easy and fun for even the youngest matchers. The game is designed to help develop visual literacy and coordination skills. This game brings the fun of the books to kids in a new way.

The difference between The Pigeon Wants a Match and Memory is that The Pigeon Wants a Match has a tile that will allow you to gain an extra turn and one that will force you to lose a turn if you turn over a certain tile.

From the Publisher's website:
Space Ludo is a space sci fi themed version of Ludo, a traditional race and capture game enjoyed around the world. Ludo is related to Pachisi, Trouble, Sorry! and others. All of them are simplified descendants of the ancient Indian cross and circle game, Pachisi.

Space Ludo offers an addicting blend of luck and strategy. Dice are involved yet crucial choices occur frequently. The rules are simple yet the game is always interesting and pleasant to replay. Such are the hallmarks of a classic and so, Ludo has stood the test of time.

Space Ludo utilizes the standard, widely known Ludo rules. These comprise the foundation of play and will be familiar to long time players. New players are encouraged to play the standard game first.

Space Ludo adds the deathmatch element, wormholes and other optional rules to expand and enhance the game.

The theme of Space Ludo comes from the spaced-out universe of the indie computer games Strange Adventures in Infinite Space (2002) and Weird Worlds: Return to Infinite Space (2005).

boardgame: The Great War: Over the Top
description: Over the Top contains a full year-by-year overview of the 1915-18 period, new Battalion-level army lists for British Empire, French and German forces, and a new army list for the American Expeditionary Force, including the Devil Dogs and US Tank Companies. It also contains detailed rules covering such actions as assaulting a trench line, or leading a hand-picked party on a trench raid, as well as background missions to set miniature wargames within the larger backdrop of some of the greatest actions of the First World War.

One player draws a card and describes the shown image to the other players who are simultaneously drawing what is described. The images are generally geometric shapes. After 1 minute the drawers judge their image against the original and receive 1 point if it matches. First player to 15 points wins.

Word Scramble bills itself as a "phonological awareness and word-building game".  Tiles are used to make words in the middle of playing board.

Based on the squares on which their pawns land, players either blindly draw a tile, move a tile already on the board or read all the words on the board.  Five board spaces direct the player to draw a tile give them the choice between the word beginning or ending piles.  Nineteen spaces specify from which pile the player should draw.  Those spaces also direct the player to define the word played, rhyme it, use it in a sentence or use it in a question.  Four spaces direct the player to read all the words already on the board, awarding four points for doing so.  Landing on one of the four corners allows the player to move a tile already on the board.

Tiles drawn or moved are placed on one of 61 spaces in the middle of the board which have either word beginnings or endings.  Harder-to-match tiles or spaces are worth more points than easy ones.  Points values range from one to six.  Play ends after one player reaches a predetermined number of points.

Players represent taxi drivers and have to drive passengers through the city, earning money.

Every player starts with a passenger card, which gives him an address where to pick up a passenger, and a destination, where that passenger wants to go. He then rolls the dice and moves his taxi on the mapboard to the given locations. On grey colored spaces, he has to discard one of his four petrol cans; if he discards the last, it's time to visit the petrol station for refuelling. On his moves, a player has to obey all traffic regulations like road signs, speed limits, pedestrian crossings, and so on. If he violates a rule, any other player may point that out and the offender has to pay him a fine of 20.

After bringing a passenger to his destination, the player gets payed and draws another passenger card.

The taxi driver who first collects the sum of 400 fares wins the game.

Casper the ghost is the star of this game where two teams must to hide their 10 ghosts. Each turn the players will choose an object of the other team hoping to find a ghost. The winner will be the team who will find all the ghosts of the opponent.
family: TV Shows: Casper the Friendly Ghost

description:
A replay game of grand prix motor cycle racing.

Players control all motorcycles in a motor cycle grand prix race. The game includes statistical data for every circuit raced in 1984, and for all classes of motorcycles, like 80, 125, 250, 500c.c. as well as sidecars.

After you have decided which race to run, you set up all the drivers (represented by cardboard figures). For every racer you turn up a card from a deck of race cards and compare the individual driver's rating with a table printed on each card. The card now tells you if the racers stay in the group of motorcycles they are currently in, if they fall back to the previous group, overtake other drivers within their group, or even catch up with the group in front of them. Mechanical troubles as well as possible accidents are also covered by the results of the card.

After a number of draws, depending on the racing course and class of motorcycles chosen, the race is over.

The game lets you replay any single race from the 1984 season or even the whole championship.

An expansion for International Road Racing that uses statistical data to recreate individual races or the whole championship of the Grand Prix motor cycle 1987 season.

The game also includes optional rules for positioning individual racers within a group.
An expansion for International Road Racing that uses statistical data to recreate individual races or the whole championship of the Grand Prix motor cycle 1988 season.

Ice Racing is an expansion set that lets you recreate the Ice Speedway world championship finals from the years 1983 to 1991.

The expansion consists of a data sheet for each year and a new set of trouble and injury charts representing the different hazards for ice speedway.

Kasse-Gasse is played with a deck of 36 cards and a die. Each card shows a word from which one letter is missing. The die shows six letters: b, d, g, k, p and t (the 'plosives').

At the start of the game, the cards are shuffled, and eight cards are drawn and placed on the table, face-up. Then, the die is rolled. All players now search for a word on one of the cards that can be completed with the letter rolled. The first player that finds such a card and pronounces the word correctly gets to keep the card.

Then, a new card is drawn and the next round starts. At the end of the game, the player who has collected the most cards wins.

Italian board game of the '70, based on the TV show "Chi?".
Each game consists of 4 rounds: memory test, ability, identikit and crime fiction.

In the first round (memory test), the player who plays the host role (or presenter) shows an image, to each player, for a very short time (10 seconds). Each player, then, must answer to the questions and remember the details.
In the second round, ability, the players, to gain the priority to answer, must to insert quick as possible a colored ball into a "tower" selector.
The "Identikit" is a character hunt.
Last round is the crime fiction. Who is playing the host role selects 3 cards, one for each deck: "chi" (who), "dove" (where) and "che cosa" (what). The players must to use clues to find the solution.

At the end, the player who scores more points is the winner.

Generic Sci-Fi wargame aimed at divisional level forces and playable in an evening.
Figures are mounted on bases that represent platoons and are activated by a command point system resulting in a simple and quick flowing game concentrating on high level tactics rather than individual weapon stats.
Rules and organization tables are included for two human and two alien forces models for which were available from Irregular Miniatures in the UK.

Cadbury placed this game into a children's Christmas annual, called Chick's Own, in 1952, as an advertisement for their hot cocoa drink.
Any number can play. Give each player a piece of paper and get them to write down Cadburys Bournville Cocoa on it.
The idea of the game is to collect all the letters that spell Cadburys Bournville Cocoa. Almost every square on the board has a letter, so if you arrive on a square you can tick off that letter from the name on your paper.
You need a 6 to start (of course!).
If you land on a square with special instructions, you must obey them.
The first player to tick off all their letters, wins. And the rules say you should get a steaming cup of lovely chocolately Bournville Cocoa.

The Great War (1914)

It is the summer of 1914. The Archduke Franz Ferdinand (heir to the throne of the Austro-Hungarian Empire) and his wife have just been assassinated in Sarajevo. In retaliation for this offense, Emperor Franz Joseph of Austria-Hungary has declared war on Serbia, an ally of Russia. This sparks a world of controversy and very quickly large military powers such as Germany, The Ottoman Empire, France, and Great Britain join in the fray. Two allegiances emerge, The Central Powers & The Allied Powers, with one goal in mind: WAR!

Do you have the economic aptitude and tactical know how to help your nation survive The Conflict and rescue a world in peril. Are you brave enough to face tanks, planes and other modern warfare in order to lead your alliance to victory? “The Great War” has begun, and ready or not, you better lace up your boots and prepare for battle.

The Conflict is a global-strategy based game designed around the economic and military conditions of Europe in 1914. Players play in one of two alliances, The Central Powers or The Allied Powers, to defeat their opponents. Players choose among seven nations (Austria-Hungary, Germany, Great Britain, France, Italy, Ottoman Empire, and Russia). After selecting a nation, players take turns completing their objectives during each round.

Note: The Conflict requires players to strategize both economically and militaristically in order to win the game.

Note: Military units will vary per nation, based on the technological restraints of the nation and its overall capability for growth.

Planned release date is 2012.

The great World Tree is rotting...

The forces of Asgard and Vanaheim have turned against one another.  The minions of Jotunheim and Svartalfheim have gone insane.  Diseased, toxic creatures spew forth into Midgard, and the gods, once protectors of that land, have forsaken humankind in their blind rage against one another.  Once their sacred protectorate, Midgard is now their battleground.

In The Last Edda, players take on the role of one of six Norse gods and do battle, taking control of the monstrosities pouring out of the tainted Yggdrasil, in order to defeat their rival.

The Last Edda blends the mechanics of player combat and deck building card games.  Each turn, a player has the option of directing monsters from a common pool at his opponent, while his opponent has the opportunity to defend the attack by playing unit cards from his hand.  The turn also provides an opportunity for players to buy cards from personal and common supplies, and construct buildings and artifacts that remain in play and help you to defend and attack more effectively.

Yggdrasil, the World Tree is rotting.  The air grows colder and drier by the day.  Murmurs of Fimbulvetr and Ragnarok can be heard across Midgard.  Which of the gods is strong enough to survive the coming storm?
A game published by Ovaltine, presumeably a free giveaway to advertise their beverage. It is a simple roll and move game through the solar system.First to finish, wins.

boardgame: Ascension: Chronicle of the Godslayer – Control Room Promo
description:
Promo given to stores for Ascension game days around September 21, 2011.

Control Room

Cost: 5 Runes

Mechana Construct

Once per turn, you may put a Mechana Construct you control on top of the center deck.  If you do, acquire a Mechana Construct in the center row and put it in your hand.

5 Honor
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: The target for this Hoop-La type game is a rabbit with large ears, eating a carrot. Players take turns to try to throw their three wooden rings over the rabbit's ears. The player who has the most successes wins the game.

In the box:
Who will be the first to fill her basket to Grandmother? But be careful with the wolf! A game full of surprises tour based on the famous story of "Little Red Riding Hood".

System:
The goal is to bring grandma flowers, honey and bread. Once inside the cabin with some of the gifts you have to turn the figure of Little Red Riding Hood. Grandma's bed has a mechanism that makes the wolf in granny "wake up" some times when you turn the figure of Little Red Riding Hood and the player must play must start from the beginning.

The way to get to Grandma's is a clue boxes free of obstacles.


De la caja:
¿Quién será el primero en llenar su cestita a la Abuelita? ¡Pero mucho cuidado con el lobo!
Un juego de recorrido lleno de sorpresas basado en el célebre cuento de "La Caperucita Roja".

Sistema de juego:
El objetivo del juego es llevar a la abuelita flores, miel y pan. Una vez dentro de la cabaña con alguno de los regalos, hay que girar la figura de Caperucita Roja. La cama de la abuelita dispone de un mecanismo que hace que el lobo disfrazado de abuelita "despierte" algunas veces en las que se gira la figura de Caperucita y el jugador debe jugador debe empezar desde el principio.

El camino para llegar a la casa de la abuelita es una pista de casillas libre de obstáculos.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description: Guild Quest tells the story of the last great quest ordered by the king of Hawksvale. Each turn, a player will have the choice to either pursue the quest, or find an inn or tavern to recruit more heros. Quest cards describe an obstacle your guild of adventurers must overcome or even a raid against another player. Battles are resolved by placing heros in a strategic formation and activating simultaneously their combat skills depending on their position in the formation.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Objective: Score points by drawing faces, which match most - compared with the one shown to the players earlier

out of the rules: "Look and remember" 
1. for a certain (short) time each player is shown a face simultaniously.
2. Using a pencil and a stencil each player draws a face during an agreed short time with at many details (up to 20) he/she can remember
3. Each player gains points: no difference 10 p., 1 diff. 5 p...
4. Game ends after a previously arranged no of rounds/time

The game was invented and designed by Ora & Theo Coster - Theora Design

Tornisto is an abstract strategy game for two to four players where each player attempts to move all five of his towers to the opposite side of the board.  The towers vary in height and can jump over any tower that is shorter than itself.

Early British game using Tiddlywinks' counters.
The board is placed in the centre of the table. On the board are placed a set of small wooden skittles. The object is to knock over skittles using your counters Tiddlywink fashion. If you do knock over a skittle, you score points depending on where it was standing.
The box image illustrates the game rather well

Spight is a ball stacking and connection game that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set. It uses a board of 4x4 holes, 8 white balls and 8 black balls.

Setup:

Place 8 black balls and 8 white balls according to the figure.

● ○ ● ○
○ ● ○ ●
● ○ ● ○
○ ● ○ ●

Rules:

Two players, White and Black, alternate turns moving a ball of their colour either:

a) jumping like a chess knight from a hole to another empty hole or...
b) jumping up from a hole to an adjacent 2x2 platform or...
c) passing if there are no available moves.

Notice that other balls may drop as a result.

Aim:

Win by connecting all your pieces into a single group.

Rules for this game are included in the soft cover Shibumi Rule Book

boardgame: 25 New Games to be Played with Lexicon Cards
description:
A book of variants for the game Lexicon, published by Waddingtons in 1935 or so. They had had great success with selling the original game, so they decided the market would like more variety. In effect, in modern terms, this book is an expansion as it adds lots of new rules that will need different strategy.

The 64-page booklet sold separately (red cover for one shilling) and advertised 25 variants. The pale blue/green version was included for free in first edition blue books sold in 1935, but has rules for only 23 variant games with the Lexicon deck: Bridge; Whist; Scramble; Dominoes; Cribbage; Poker; Stud Poker; Eights; Clock Patience; Brighton Patience; Dictionary; Riddance; Donkey; Criss Cross; Twister; One Better; Jumble; Tap and Grab; Mine; Don't Forget; Demon; Pay Up; and Teasers. (The red cover edition also includes Lexicon Newmarket and Lexicon Duplexicon.)

Memoir '44: Campaign Book Volume 2, the second hardcover supplement for the Memoir '44 game system, contains 46 new scenarios and builds upon the campaign rules introduced in Campaign Book Volume 1 to let players experience scenarios in succession, with the outcome of each battle impacting the next.

These 46 scenarios are grouped into eleven different campaigns, with two set in the Pacific Theater – "Island Hoppers", a Grand Campaign covering the progressive re-conquest of the Pacific by the Allied forces following the attack on Pearl Harbor, and "Bicycle Blitzkrieg", the Japanese invasion of Malaya in 1941-1942 – and the first Breakthrough campaign for Memoir '44: "Breakthrough Normandy", covering the Allied offensive in Normandy from D-Day through August 1944.

Two other campaigns – "Fall of Poland", depicting the desperate resistance of Polish forces against the German Blitzkrieg of September 1939, and "Air Aces", a campaign following the exploits of famous air aces throughout World War II – round up the Theaters of Operations covered in the book.

Memoir '44: Campaign Book Volume 2 comes with a punchboard containing fifty game tokens and requires the Memoir '44 base game as well as theater-specific expansions for some of the campaigns, specifically Memoir '44: Pacific Theater, Memoir '44: Air Pack, Memoir '44: Terrain Pack and Memoir '44: Breakthrough.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Objective: be the first to reach the target (field 1)
‘Roll and Move’ game
Equipment: board, 6 meeples, 3 dice
1. Start positions are to be filled (use one die by turns – 3 turns max) – ‘1’ allows you to place your meeple onto the board
2. Race: use 3 dice - standard speed   3; gaining a double  6; a triple  9. Per die-roll of a ‘6’ one additional space, so that a triple ‘6’ let you move 12 spaces forward 
3. Events (brown marked) on the board may accelerate or decelerate or block you

Rules state that number of participants is not limited

A brand new region of space has been discovered and it is brimming with planets ripe for the plundering. Blast off into uncharted territory and be the first to claim these curiously uninhabited wellsprings of wherewithal.

The goal of "Spacefaring: For Fun & Profit" is to colonize the most valuable planets before your rivals. The game ends as soon as every planet has been colonized, and your total score is based on your planet value, the number of factories you built and the economic level of your homeworld.

boardgame: Samarkand: Routes to Riches – Family Connection
description:
Family ties are established even tighter now.  Being merchants, the players equip their sons and daughters with goods and gems and send them out to travel the trading routes.  The only thing still missing is the adequate partner.  Which marriage is most promising?

Components:

20 caravan children - 10 "sons" boards and 10 "daughters" boards
    15 neutral goods markers
    67 - gems (in 5 colors)
    10 coins
    10 son/daughter markers

Sister game to Touchdown (an American Football game). Game consists of a large board depicting a baseball diamond, batter and pitcher cards, some standup figures, D6 dice and a scorepad. Game mechanics consist of Pitcher choosing a pitch card, batter choosing a type of swing, both players rolling a D6 and adding and subtracting modifiers according to  the Batters or Pitchers mastercard. The result will be one of: Strike or Ball, Hit, Infield Hit, Deep Hit, Line Drive Catch and Flycatch or Home Run. Special rule cover stealing bases and the bunt.

boardgame: Hurry Hard! The Curling Card Game
description:
Hurry Hard! The Curling Card Game is a fun, fast paced card game suitable for curling fans of all ages.  Grab your teammates and take on your fiercest rivals to decide once and for all who really rules the ice.  Or go head-to-head against a fellow curler to determine who will be King of the Rings.

Playable by two, four, six or even eight players, Hurry Hard! recreates all the action on the ice in a fun, easy to learn card game.  Players take turns throwing guards, draws, or takeouts.  Your teammates can then improve the impact of your throw, including through the play of the powerful Hurry Hard! sweeping card to help get your rock a little closer to the button. Watch out though!  Your opponents can also affect your throw.  The timely play of a Miss the Broom card can ruin an end, unless you have a Plan B card in reserve.  After the player with The Hammer throws the last rock, the score for the end is calculated the same as in real curling, based on whose rocks are closest to the button.  Then a new end begins.

For non-curlers, the rules explain not just how to play the card game, but also the basic terminology to help you understand the game of curling itself.

For curlers, the game has been well balanced to reward real curling strategy.  Deciding when to throw a guard or when to come into the rings can make the difference between scoring a big end or giving up a steal.  Just as with real curling, though, no matter how good your strategy, you still need to make your throws.

Alhambra: The Magical Buildings, a promotional item from Queen Games for Spiel 2011, consists of six new tiles – one in each of the six colors of buildings – that are placed in the bag with the other tiles at the start of the game.

When drawn, they are placed for sale as with any other building. What's different about these buildings is that no direction is up, so when added to a player's Alhambra, the tile can be turned and placed in any legal position based on all the building rules except orientation. During scoring, these magical buildings count as a building of the appropriate color when determining majorities.

Fresco: The Bishop's Favor, a promotional item from Queen Games for Spiel 2011 and included in the Big Box and MegaBox, consists of 25 small tiles and rules.  The Bishop's Favor can also be referenced as Module 11.

At the start of the game, these tiles are placed face-down, randomized and placed one per fresco tile on the game board. Whenever a player completes a fresco tile where the bishop is currently located, the player claims both the fresco tile and the favor tile – which is revealed and grants an immediate reward of paint (red, blue, yellow), money (1 or 5), one VP, or one mood improvement.

If a player completes a fresco tile where the bishop is not located, the favor tile is removed from the game face-down with no benefit for anyone.

Kingdom Builder: Capitol, a promotional item from Queen Games for Spiel 2011, consists of two capitol building tiles and rules.

If the game board sections with the harbor or oracle are in play, place a capitol building on one of the castles in that section. (If both are in play, you may use both capitols.) At the end of the game, you earn 1 gold for each of your settlements built within two hexes of a capitol.

Steam: Map Expansion #2 provides three maps in the same pack that can be used with the Steam base game. One side of the four-panel game board features a map of Great Britain suitable for 3-4 players that's subtitled "Progress from Canals to Rails". The other side of the game board has two maps, each covering two panels of the game board: California 2090 ("Gain Prestige but Prepare for Disaster" for three players) and China ("Unite the Country on the Floodplains" for 2-4 players).

Steam: Map Expansion #2 also includes an extra tile sheet for Steam.

Action game. The board consists of a length of baize, which is around 6 feet long. One player rolls a ball at the stumps (nice stumps and bails included)whilst the other player tries to hit the ball with his bat. The rules do not say how many runs are scored after the ball has been hit, you can decide for yourself.

The box suggests a date of around 1925 and it illustrates the game mechanic quite well. Note that the game is trying to appeal to adults (no children playing on the box image).

Players roll the color-die and match game pieces in rolled color on their boards.

Player who finishes his picture first is the winner.

Contents:

     4 player boards
     24 game pieces punched out from the player boards with different colors and shapes
     color die

18NK is a Japanese 18xx game set in Nagaokakyo.  While the rules are as-yet-untranslated, it appears to be not dissimilar to the rest of its family - a combination of stock manipulation, route-building, and capital management.

A few details: there are 15 companies, and there appears to be some kind of "destination" system.

More details to come as translation efforts begin.

18SS is a Japanese 18xx game, apparently without a historical location.  While the rules are as-yet-untranslated, it appears to be not dissimilar to the rest of its family - a combination of stock manipulation, route-building, and capital management.

A few details: there are 14 companies, one central "big" city, highly variable off-boards.

More details to come as translation efforts begin.

In English Horses and Ponies.
What you need to ride? Like a horse is kept? What you see in the horse show? With knowledge, some luck and a good memory players collect chips.

To win a chip player must be on any theme board, where the knob has posistioned so that the imaged symbol matches with symbol on one of the player's face-up picture card.

Player with the most chips at the end of the game is the winner.

Contents:

     5 theme boards, 
     5 knobs, 
     30 picture cards, 
     26 chips, 
     1 pawn, 
     1 stand

Rolling the special die players move around the carousel collecting chips for his tray tables. If the special symbol is rolled all the characters must enter and take a spin with the carousel.

Player who has collected all 4 type of chips first is the winner.

Contents:

     A carousel (consisting of 2 parts)
     4 tray tables
     1 die
     4-Benjamin game characters
     16 chips (4 each in orange, red, yellow and blue)

16 cards featuring images that are a cryptic clue for a profession of some sort. A hen walking away from her roost and leaving three bricks where her eggs should be, would denote a Bricklayer.
The cards are placed around the room before the party starts and when the guests arrive they are given a record sheet, on which they should write their answers when they come across a card.
The player with the most right answers at a pre-determined time, wins

Party game.
Contents
18 Pirate cards
12 Treasure Island maps
The host places all the pirate cards (except card 1) around the room where they can be easily found and seen by the guests.He reads out the first Pirate card and players now look for the Pirate card next in sequence for their chosen route on their map card. When they find it they will be either right or wrong. If wrong, they must go back to their start position on the map. And play progresses like this until one player has discovered the exact location on their map card where the treasure is hidden. He or she wins.

Party game.
There are 12 Cunning Couplet cards and these feature couplets made up of two words that are not related. The 12 cards are placed around the room where players will be able to see them. Each player will have one word that is the first correct word to a couplet. They then look for another card that has a second correct word. For example, they know the word Turn, so they look around and evenutally find Coat, which makes Turn Coat. Now they must look for a word that goes with Coat and so on until they find a word that has Finish next to it. The first person to do this wins.

A box of three party games known as the Easy Chair Series of games.
In game 1 you have to try to follow a word square of 4 by 4 letters and so arranged they form the names of rivers. This type of game is similar in mechanic to Boggle.
Game 2 is Rhyming Pairs. You are given ten examples of question. The first information given is a word os short phrase. This is followed by a blank, then another blank and then a phrase again. You have to think of a word that goes into the first blank, thus forming two words 'with a close relationship'. Then you need to complete the second blank and make another word that links to the last phrase in your question. The trick is, the two blank words must rhyme. An example; A pool is the first word so the correct word to go with that is Lagoon. The last phrase is Very Light Ball and the correct answer is Balloon. Lagoon/Balloon is correct and rhymes.
Game 3 is a quick trivia game. You are given a grid of five by six boxes. Down the left hand side is a subject for each row, Countries, Professions, Flowers, Prominent People, Motor Cars and Boy's Names. You are given a letter and you must fill in as many boxes as you can with answers that begin with that letter.

Party game, published under the Easy Chair Series of games by this publisher.
12 cards are distributed to 12 guests who want to play. The card takes the form of a story, with several blanks in them. You are given the first letter of the missing word and you must try to finish the story as it is read out by the host. All missing words are things that might be found in a flower garden. The most correct answers, wins.
An additional game is provided on the other side of the card and this is a simple Anagrams game. The first to get the 10 anagrams right, wins. All ten anagrams are names of motor cars.

Party game, another game in the publisher's Easy Chair Series.
Up to 12 guests are given a card and must write down any two things in the (rather nice graphics) picture. The two words must be related somehow. An example is given of Storm and Tempest being a close enough match. The guest with the most correct answers after time runs out, wins

Objective: 
Start from NY around the world

Simple 'Roll and Move' game: 
starting in NY, followed by South America, Africa, Europe, Asia and as traget back to NY city.

This ASL scenario pack consists of 8 tournament sized scenarios, 2 pages of historical background material, as well as British and Italian SASL generation tables.

The following scenarios are included:


    1: FACTORY FARMING; 25 January 1944; Aprilia, Italy
    2: FORMING THE THUMB; 30 January 1944; south of Campoleone, Italy
    3: TALLY HO!; 4 February 1944; south of Campoleone, Italy
    4: A PRODUCTIVE DAY AT THE OFFICE; 16 February 1944; Aprilia, Italy
    5: SPARKS FLY; 17 February 1944; Albano-Anzio Road, Italy
    6: HERE COME THE BASTARDS; 18 February 1944; Albano-Anzio Road, Italy
    7: EVERYTHING WE GOT!; 19 February 1944; Albano-Anzio Road, Italy
    8: GET READY; 29 February 1944; Carano, Italy

description:
With the help of Benjamin the Elephant children must match the pairs with different professions - one with the Benjamin in uniform and other with tools for this professions.

The smarter kid who finds out the most pairs is the winner.

Contents:

     12 pairs of cards, each card consists of a pair of Benjamin Blümchen card and a picture card

boardgame: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia Board Game
description:
Step into Paddy’s Pub with Mac, Dennis, Charlie, Dee and Frank.

All friends working in harmony for a common cause: themselves!

As a member of The Gang, you’re trying to purchase the most shares in Paddy’s Pub. You need to collect beers (tokens), make your way to a pub to convert them to empties, trade your empties for Paddy’s Pub Dollars at a beer store, and make your way to Paddy’s Pub Headquarters to purchase some shares.

Along your quest, don’t forget to ‘Bang the Waitress’ and backstab other members of The Gang with some Charlie Work!

Components:

•Game Board
•70 Episode cards
•64 Sunny cards
•42 Paddy's Pub Dollars cards
•24 Charlie Work cards
•20 Paddy's Pub Shares
•130 Beer tokens (26 of each 5 different types of beer/character)
•5 Game tokens (characters)
•5 Bang the Waitress tokens (also characters)
•1 six sided die

Prestige is a strategic placement game, in which two players compete to reach the power needed to bring their kings on the throne. The city of Nova is divided into four districts, each of which hosts a different power. It’s up to you to manage your family, by placing each member in a strategic position and choosing how and where to gain  either political, religious, economic or military supremacy.

A game of high strategy that will bring a growing challenge to the two contenders.

"Not all battles are won with weapons".


                                                        
                                                    
                                                
                                            
                                        
                                    
                                
                            
                        
                    
                
            
        
    


Prestige è un gioco strategico di piazzamento, in cui due giocatori si sfidano per conquistare il potere che condurrà i loro re al trono. La città di Nova è divisa in quattro quartieri, ognuno dei quali rappresenta una sfera d’influenza. Sta a te gestire la tua famiglia, piazzando ogni membro in un punto strategico e scegliere come muoverti per acquistare supremazia, che sia politica, religiosa, economica o militare.

Un gioco di alta strategia che porterà una sfida sempre diversa ai due contendenti.

"Non tutte le battaglie si vincono con le armi".

_________________________

PRESTIGE

The city of Nova, capital of a great kingdom, is now lost in big turmoil. The king is dead and the power vacuum is spreading. Two important families are going to take the field, ready to do anything for supremacy. A silent war is about to begin. The two queens, ruling their families, will lead their followers in the attempt to conquer Nova in strong and subtle ways through palaces, markets, strongholds and temples. Who will set up their own king on the throne?

Prestige is a strategic placement game, in which two players compete to reach the power needed to bring their kings on the throne. The city of Nova is divided into four districts, each of which hosts a different power. It’s up to you to manage your family, by placing each member in a strategic position and choosing how and where to gain  either political, religious, economic or military supremacy.

A game of high strategy that will bring a growing challenge to the two contenders.

Not all battles are won with weapons.

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME
The players must install members of their own House in Key Areas of the four ruling districts in the city of Nova in order to exercise always more and more influence and gain Supremacy over the other House to finally put their own King on the throne.

COMPONENTS
House White        
1 King
1 Queen
1 Bishop
1 Knight
1 Rook
3 Pawns

House Black
1 King
1 Queen
1 Bishop
1 Knight
1 Rook
3 Pawns

36 Influence Tokens in different colours:
9 blue Tokens (Religious Power)
9 yellow Tokens (Political Power)
9 green Tokens (Economic Power)
9 red Tokens (Military Power)

1 Game Board is divided into the four ruling districts of the city of Nova, with four different Key Areas and a Secret Key Area in each district.

2 House Boards

Setting up the game
Open and place the Game Board at the centre of the table. Randomly pick up the first player. The first player will get all white pieces and will guide the White House. The other player will get all black pieces and will guide the Black House. Each player gets the House Board of his/her own House. 
Both players place their own King on the square marked with 0 (zero) on the Game Board. 
The player using Black gets one Influence Token of his/her choice.

Prestige is played over a series of turns. Each player’s turn has four consecutive steps. 
1. Acquisition
2. Ability Use 
3. Placement
4. Promotion

END OF THE GAME
If at the end of your turn you reach or gain more than 22 Supremacy Points, the opponent will play his/her last turn. The game ends at the end of that turn. At the end of the game each player gets Bonus Supremacy Points according to the following conditions:

Bold and Strong: +2 Supremacy Points if all of your Pawns are on the Board in any position at the end of the game.

Conformation: +3 Supremacy Points if you have at least one piece in three different districts in any position at the end of the game.

The player with more Supremacy Points is the winner. In case of a tie the player with more Influence tokens wins the game. In case of a further tie, the Black Player wins.

Simulation of 3 sport games: soccer, basketball, hockey.

Players subsequently move all they players.

Soccer: Each player has 10 players and goalkeeper on the pitch. Goalkeeper cannot move out of penalty area. During each turn players can run with ball (max. 6 hexes), pass to another man (at most for 10 hexes) or shoot (max. 10 hexes). Each pass can be blocked if opponent is next to the line of the pass. If on the line of the pass - opponent always steals the ball. Rules saving a shot by goalkeeper are the same, but there's possibility to score the goal even if he's on the line of the shot. There are also tackles, fouls, outs and penalties, but no yellow/red cards.

Basketball: each player has 5 players. Players can move at most 3 hexes (no dice rolling), shoot, or pass. Defenders can steal a ball only during a pass or a shot (blocking it or rebounding), and if they're next to a player passing the ball or the one that catches it. There are steals, fouls and rebounds. Scoring is for 1, 2 and 3 points.

Hockey: each player has 5 players and goalkeeper. Goalkeeper cannot move out of the goal area. Moves, shoots and passes for maximum 6 hexes. All rules are combination of soccer and basketball rules written above.

Player wins after determined time or result. Producer recommends 2x30 min for soccer, 3x20 for hockey and 2x20 for basketball.

Mechanic: each action is decided by dice. In soccer - 1-2 dices, other sports: 1 dice.

2 sided board: on one side there's soccer pitch, on second - basketball and hockey.

Color-Combi is an abstract card placement game for 2-5 players.  The game is comprised of 70 square cards, each containing a multi-colored cross, and five sets of colored tokens, representing up to five players. The game is housed in a cardboard box with color printed top and unprinted bottom.  The box contains a plastic tray to hold the cards and the tokens.

Each player is dealt two cards, and the remaining cards form a draw pile.  The first player draws a blind card and then lays one card from their hand face up on the table.  Playing clockwise, each player takes a turn, drawing a card and playing a card on the table.  Cards must be placed, edge to edge, so the colors match.  Players must place a card if they are able, but rows of cards cannot exceed three cards in length.

The goal of the game is to create squares of cards and claim them with the colored tokens.  Additionally, your can seize an existing square if you are able to replace one of the cards on the table with a card from your hand.  The first player to claim four squares wins.  There are additional rules for a points game.

Tricala is a two- to three-player Kalah game played on a triangular layout of twenty-one pits (six to an edge).

Players alternate placing stones in whatever pits they choose to set the board.

As with conventional Mancala games, a player's turn consists of "sowing" stones: all stones are picked up from any cup containing at least one stone of the player's color, then stones are redistributed one-by-one along a line of pits leading away from the source. Unlike most Mancala games, [1] there are between two and six sowing directions to choose (depending on the cup), and [2] the direction deflects when a board edge is reached. Sowing is extended ("re-sowed") if the last stone drops into a pit containing only the active player's stones.

Stones are captured when the last piece sowed lands in a pit containing an opponent's stone.

The game is won when a player captures nine stones or claims the majority in each corner pit.

45 playing cards divided into 15 Fantastic Flyers

Each player has 5 cards and is trying to make sets of 3 cards to make a flyer, by trading with the deck or the other players.  When a player makes a complete creature it is placed on the table.  The player with the most complete creatures is the winner.

The included flyers are Bat, Bee, Butterfly, Cockatoo, Cranefly, Dragonfly, Duck, Eagle, Flamingo, Ladybird, Owl, Pelican, Robin, Wasp, Woodpecker

A game for larger group of players, in two teams, featuring words and definitions. At first glance the game offers a well-known standard mechanism, you must describe something to the members of your team, avoiding given taboo words, the team must guess the item or term.  A new feature introduced in Yes & No is that you also have a list of compulsory words that you must use. And this results in a completely different game.

Contents:

     game board
     Yes & No. 110 card
     1 timer (30 sec)
     1 dice
     2 characters

The detectives win the game when they find out the secret code of a raid array before the first gangster reached this field. The crooks win when he reached the last box with the jewels. A gangster on the train rolls and moves accordingly, he moved to a secret map and places it among the questions on the next raid box. The detectives dice and move or ask a question with a card from the next raid.

Monster Mash is a turn based combat game for two or more players set in a world where kids can buy designer monsters as pets and have them fight each other in a global sports league. The game allows players to create and control one of several different types of miniature monsters. Monster Mash gives players complete control over the creation of their monster. Players can create an existing monster from popular media or one from their own imagination. The possibilities are endless. The object of the game is to beat any monsters that oppose you in the arena and win the match.

Monster Mash is a standalone game that can also be used as an expansion for any other Toy Battle SystemTM game.

The little ghosts floating through the castle and look for their missing toys, who is first to find all of his favorite things is the winner.

Players roll the die and try to place colored and shaped game-pieces on their cards. If the game-piece doesn't fit on player's card he can keep it and use next round in exchange.

Benjamin Blümchen must represent the zoo director to check and count that there are correct number of animals at zoo.

The cards with the animals are stacked and covered with the Benjamin Blümchen card. The 12 animals at the zoo are placed easily accessible for everyone in the middle. The player whose turn it is draws the top animal card and all players look at it. 
The player who first recognizes which animal is missing or is too many, takes the animal figure from the table. Who gets the proper figure, receives the card. The beast goes back to the middle. If nobody got the right animal, the card goes out of play. When all cards are played, the child with the most cards wins.

User Summary

StockCar Jr. is an auto-racing game. The object is to be the first player to complete 15 laps.

Players qualify for racing order with a dice roll. Car movement is controlled by rolling the dice as players seek to vie for positioning in "The Groove," an area of the track which allows for the fastest possible movement. But this is more than just a roll-and-move game...

Players can choose a car setup that's either Tight or Loose. A Tight setup is more conservative and safe, while a Loose set up gives more reward at the cost of greater risk. Players can also switch between high and low gear. Special rules cover cornering, getting "out of groove" and "out of shape," and accidents/collisions.

Another interesting feature of the game is the ability to collect Power Points. Cars can gain these when they are caught in traffic or have no legal moves. They can be used later to help with tricky maneuvers.

All cars pit after their 4th and 9th laps. Each car gets a Pit Card which indicates how long the pit stop is how many spaces of movement it gains while exiting.

The game comes in rather large "pizza box" style container with a checkerboard patterned cover.

Components include:
- A large gameboard with a track three spaces wide for the cars to travel on, 
- 2 sets of instructions,
- 6 sets of control tables which represent high and low gear for the Loose and Tight car setups, 
- 6 sets of gauges to track Power Point usage, 
- A color-coordinated pair of dice and wooden playing pieces for each of the six race teams (aka players), & 
- A set of 20 Pit Cards

A "Tri-Level Game for Ages 7 to Adult" described on the side of the box as a "Fast Game of Fun and Strategy for Two".  The board has some similarities to that of games in the Morris family, though piece placement is guided by die roll.  The game's objective is to form sets of three pieces in a row, either via direct placement or via moving the pieces along lines on the board, with victory coming when a player reaches 16 points.

In the mid-nineteenth century, the players lead their families into a new world, using a hand of cards to explore, develop, moderate the climate, and conduct other activities. In The Pioneer Card Game: Chapter resources, each player receives his own hand of cards, places them all in an order that he speculates will lead to good results, then executes these cards sequentially. Compete with opponents for resources and incentives, and lead your family to a new world...

The Bisca is a Portuguese variant of Briscola.  Like that other game, each player is dealt 3 cards and another one is turned up as the trump suit.

The first to play can play any card he wants to, but the second needs to play a higher card from the same suit previously played or a card of the trump suit to win a trick. The winner of each trick replenishes his hand first and a player owning a 7 from the trump suit can "rescue the bisca", taking to his hand the card turned up in the beginning of the match.

The total points in each game is 120 and whoever has the higher scoring wins. Playing with 3-6 players just takes a few minor adjustments to the rules or cards and the versions for 4-6 are played with partnerships.

The point system goes as follow:
Aces - 11 points
Fives - 10 points
Kings - 4 points
Queens - 3 points
Jacks - 2 points

Playing with a traditional spanish deck of cards is recommended but not mandatory, and the point system follows the same structure, with the "cavaleiro" (knight) card replacing the queen.

The game is usually played with a hand of three cards (Bisca de três - literally three's Bisca), but there are two variants:

Bisca dos Nove (Nine's Bisca) for a hand size of nine cards;
Bisca dos Sete (Seven's Bisca) for a hand size of seven cards.

Essen 2011 promotional land pack.

Include 3 new lands, rice field, linen field and mill.
Replace the starting food lands with some or all of those.
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Each player starts with a shuffled stack of all cards from one suit (or two for a longer game). Players deal cards face-down into their own rows of front and back "trenches". A player may peek at his own cards anytime.

A turn consists of one of three actions:

     Reveal a card in the opponent's trenches.
     Swap positions of two neighboring allied cards.
     Attack: Flip two directly opposing cards. Discard the weaker card, and return the stronger card to the owning player. Each player slides a card from their back trench into the spot emptied by the attack and lays a replacement card in the back trench.


A player left with only three cards loses.

Cardgame made for Museum of Eduard Vilde.
Eduard Vilde was famous Estonian writer.(4th of March 1865 - 26th of December 1933).

There are 120 cards in this game: 60 green period-cards and 60 yellow creation-cards both in six time periods. There are questions on face-up and correct answer variant on face-down of the cards.
Cards are shuffled and put face-up stack in the middle of the table. Players/teams must answer the question of the top card in their turn and if the answer is correct they may keep this card. If this card is creation-card player/team must have also correct period-card to score a point.

Player/team who reach to prearranged score is the winner being "the most successful Eduard Vilde"

Benjamin Blümchen helps children learn more about our earth: the 6 continents, important monuments, people, animals, countries, and cultures. Depending on the game chosen, there are different ways to win, but always the flower forms must be placed correctly - this flower system allows control of the correct answers - the flowers only fit into one position.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The New Era: Spiel 2011 Promo Pack
description:
The New Era: Spiel 2011 Promo Pack, a set of seven cards available from Portal at the Spiel 2011 game convention, can be used in either The New Era or 51st State. Five hundred copies of this promo pack were produced.
Microbadge for owners

On the cards are motives from Lauras world, five cards are laid out face up, each player also receives 5 cards. The active player looks at the cards and tries to remember them, then he closes his eyes and one card is turned down. Now the player has 20 seconds to name the card correctly. If he is right he can place one of his cards face up on the table, if he is wrong a card from the draw pile is added. The player to discard his last card wins the game.

Objective: Be the first to arrive at field 70

Simple 'Roll and Move' game; different editions - rare collector's item: communicates strong economical grow of
early W-German economy, remake of an elder KdF ('Kraft durch Freude')game - see images

From the Kickstarter description:

The game consists of two decks: the action cards and the event cards. The action cards make up your hand, and the event cards are played at the end of each each round in an attempt to "ghostify" your friends (a.k.a. kill them). Once a player has died, the game is not over for them! Players who die continue playing as ghosts, not able to win, but fully capable of messing with others.

The game is played in rounds. A round consists of players playing action cards, and a single player playing an event card at the end of the round. Players use action cards to draw extra cards or cause other players to discard in an attempt to favorably control the number of cards in players' hands, their own included. Events target players based on the number of cards in their hand, adding to the strategy elements of the the game. Some action cards also allow players to dodge events the main component of survival in the game.

The Kickstarter campaign failed to meet its funding goal on 27 November 2011.

An interactive literature-based game that uses ANY work of fiction.

The aim of the game is to get students talking and learning about literature.

Players move around a the illustrated game board, trying to reach THE END.  Students travel ahead by correctly answering the reading comprehension questions on the game cards and by responding to each other's viewpoints.  All types of learners are engaged through singing, acting, and drawing.

boardgame: Postman Pat's Giant Picture Card Game
description: Over-sized cards for little fingers. 4 games featuring cards related to the TV favourite, Postman Pat. The games are as you would expect for a game aimed at very young children, Snap, Pairs; Memory; Happy Families.
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
For its age, some quite clever ideas. Here are the important rules;

The game is intended for two players, one taking the 'planes' and the other the 'shells', both represented by discs.
The aim of the game is to get as many planes as possible through to the Objective. Each player moves in turn commencing with a Plane.
The Moves;
The discs move according to the number obtained by spinning the arrow. The exact number of places must be moved each time and only one disc for each move. Planes must be moved forward (never backward).
The red-circle shells move horizontal (sic) left or right only. The black-circle shell may move horizontal (left or right) or vertically (backwards or forwards) but not outside the green half of the board.
Shells can move only if the number of spaces to be moved brings the shell to a circle of the same colour as a plane in play.
Rules;
The planes' player moves any plane he chooses. He may try to reach his objective with one at a time or more if he so desires, but he must not forego a move if it is possible to move a plane the required space.
Shells may pass over planes but not over another shell.
Planes may pass over another plane or shell.
Losses;
A plane is hit (and thus out of the game) if it comes to a circle occupied by a shell or if a shell hits the plane. That is, if a shell is able to move to a circle occupied by a plane. A hit is also registered if a plane comes to one of the red circles in the yellow half of the board, provided the red line is connected with a circle of the same colour as the plane and this circle has a shell on it or a shell hits it before the plane moves on.
Now place the discs on the circles as marked taking care to put the black-circle shell on its appropriate circle.
If the arrow should stop dead on a dividing line, spin it again.

These rules make a little more sense when you look at the images. Note that it is never properly explained who wins. Does the planes' player win if he gets just one plane to the objective? Or more? I guess you need to make some 'house rules' before playing.

The box top shows Smurfs riding logs which are floating down a river. The description from the inside game box: After a picnic in the woods, the Smurfs are on their way home.  In order to get back to their village, they will travel along the stone path and take shortcuts by riding a log up the river.  But watch out!  The sneaky Gargamel is traveling up and down the river in search of Smurfs!

Each Smurf gets two log tokens. Using a spinner, player move Gargamel up and down a river.  The Smurfs travel on a stone path, but can also travel up the river using a log token.  Running into Gargamel while on the river causes the Smurf to be chased out of the river, losing the log token for the rest of the game and moving back to the nearest river crossing. First Smurf to finish wins.

Contents:

     Smurf Gameboard
     5 Smurf Characters
     1 Spinner
     8 Log Tokens
     Die
     Instructions on box lid.

Card game about collecting sets but this has a twist in that sets are of unequal sizes, from 2 card sets to 5 card sets. The cards picture street characters in Victorian London, who call out their wares or services, such as "Oranges, two a penny". At the bottom of the illustration in this case, it says Oranges. The associated three other cards in this set say Two, A, and Penny.

Simple roll and move game. The board depicts a mountain with the track running to the top. On the track are short-cuts and it will get more competitive because the track thins as it gets higher.It is a race to the summit. There are several pathways at the start of the game and quite a few obstacles to prevent the player from getting ahead.

Operation Neptune is an Advanced Squad Leader Platoon Leader campaign game featuring the assault crossing of the Seine in France 1944 by the British 43rd (Wessex) Division, opposed by the German 49th Infantry Division.

The game includes the Campaign card, colour overlays for the village of Vernonnet, historical notes and comments by a playtester. In addition to the counters, you need ASL boards 2, 7 and 19 to play this game.

A simple wargame designed to hand out at Halloween time.  The game consists of two cards, the rules, two dice, and some candy.  The candy forms the units with different types of candy having different special powers.  Defeated candies are eaten by the victor.

boardgame: Vehicles! A Supplement for Operation Squad: World War Two
description: After fighting dozens of battles with your men, the time has come to deploy some armour!  Motorbikes, cars, trucks, armoured cars, half-tracks and tanks are ready to take part in your games and make them even more exciting.  But now the infantry have got some anti-tank weapons to face these steel monsters too...  The Rules are still fast and easy to learn so enrich your army with some vehicles and have twice the fun.

boardgame: World at War: Into the Breach
description:
From the LnLP webpage...

"World at War seems to have everything. There are Cubans with SU-100s, South Africans with Oliphants, French AMX B-2s, T-80s with different armor factors, and a designer who seemingly ignores logic, but there is one thing that World at War is missing. That one thing is LOTS of American tanks. Into the Breach cures that. This expansion to The Untold Stories explores the Yank’s battle on the front line in the first days of the war. Now you get to fight with lots of Abrams, Bradleys, infantry, and even Sheridans. On the flip side of the tank, you’ll fight a mass of Soviets and Polish armor.

It’s great big fun, with some great big scenarios. There are 12 of them to be exact (unless you order the upgraded version with an additional eight scenario campaign), including an East German counterattack, West Germans on defense, and Amercans defending in depth, screening main forces, attacking, and counterattacking."

In other words, this expansion for World at War: The Untold Stories adds a lot of new scenarios while adding a lot of American and Soviet units.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: World at War (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description:
British golf game that can be played on a carpet.
Metal 'rings' are provided, each with a hole in the centre and a number. Place these rings around the carpet and then you putt the ball into the hole of Number one ring. When that hole is completed, putt towards Hole 2, and so on. The player who takes the least shots after all the holes have been played, wins.

Easy to grasp high concept mind game (a math equivalent of Scrabble that is played like Domino).
 It teaches/applies the four basic math operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division) on various positive numbers. Future versions will contain also negative numbers, and fractional joiners (to form fraction numbers)

CONTENT:
Key game components are octagonal tiles with numbers in the center, equal signs, and half math operations between them (addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division), decimal joiners, and tile holders.  
Basic Edition contains: 33 Positive Tiles, 11 Joiners, and 2 Tile Holders.
