Electric quiz game with extraordinary graphics. Answer questions and point to the correct answer, and a bulb will light up. The game contains 6 answer sheets but double-sided to give you more replayability.

The pre-hysteria with stones.

After their first game on the subject called "Cro-Magnon" the team behind that game comes up with the follow-up called "Cro-Magnon Rrrévolution", which is a completely independent game from the original.

As in the original, you play as a tribe that will evolve over time. The change is that this time every time you move your pawn on the track and change area, the playing conditions change. As in the first version, the basic principle is to make your tribe guess as many words as they can.

The method of making the tribe guess the words is what changes each time. In the first age it's miming combined with an uncontrollable effect of a die; in the second age, you will make shapes with stones; later you can talk only with words that never contain the letter of your tribe taboo; and finally in the fourth age you can use overlapping cardboard shapes to evoke the mystery word...

"Cro-Magnon Rrrévolution" is a game of communication and pure atmosphere.

description:
The title appears to be an old journalism phrase, relating to special editions with lots of photographs about a special event.
Players must collect sets of two or three, by enquiring of other players "Do you have card so-and-so?". If a player gets a pair, he may play it in front of him but should someone get the third, they can claim the other two. At the end, a set in front of you scores points (each set varies in this regard). First to 50 points, wins.
The Splash Page Special card is a kind of Joker and is the only card not relating to a unique set.
Variant rules are provided for playing a type of Snap with the same cards.

Don't let Clumsy fall! Clumsy Smurfs sits on top of a wall of bricks. Take turns using Gargamel’s wand to push out the bricks one at time, but it's only a matter of time before Clumsy tumbles down. Don’t let it be YOU!

Roar like a lion, waddle like a duck, draw an elephant on the table, explain what a dolphin eats, and tell where a bear lives: In Kikerikimäh!?, for two players or 3-6 players, whoever can do that the best, leading the other players more often than not in the right direction, will win the game.

To set up the game for 3-6 players, shuffle the 56 animal cards, then deal out cards as evenly as possible among all the players. If a player receives a matching pair of animal cards, remove these from the game as they are not needed. Shuffle the challenge cards and place them in the middle of the table.

On a turn, the active player draws the topmost challenge card – with challenges being something that you draw or describe or imitate, e.g., "describe the animal's feet", "using your fingers, mimic how the animal walks" or "describe or show how the animal eats". This player draws the topmost animal card from her deck, then completes the challenge as best she can. Each other player then looks through their personal deck of animal cards, chooses one animal they think best matches the description or sounds given, then reveal these cards simultaneously. If someone has matched the active player, those two cards are removed from play; likewise, if any two players have revealed matching animals, those cards are removed from play. All other animals are returned to their owners' decks. The next player then undergoes a challenge, and so on.

As soon as one or more players have no animal cards remaining, the game ends and those players win.

In the two-player game, each player takes a set of animal cards and they take turns doing the challenges. If the non-active player reveals the right animal card, those cards are removed from play; otherwise, the challenger's card and the matching card from the other player's deck are placed in the middle of the table as a penalty point. The goal is to score as few penalty points as possible.

In Marienkäfer und so, ladybugs and other insects are crawling all over the cards. Try to find them based on their silhouetted profiles before the other players do – but that's not easy as the insect marked through on your search card must not be anywhere on the insect card!

The 63 insect cards feature three colored insects (all different) on one side of the card and three silhouetted insects on the reverse side, with one of those insects having a large X through it. Shuffle the deck with the black-and-white images face up, then deal one card to each player; this card is a player's search card.

On a turn, the player who last claimed a card (chosen randomly in the first round) flips over the top card of the deck. Each player races to slap this card – but only if the newly revealed card depicts at least one of the two non-Xed insects on that player's search card and that player's Xed insect is not on the newly revealed card. If the first player to slap the card has done so correctly, he claims the card, placing it black-and-white side up on his stack to become his new search card; if the first player has slapped the card incorrectly (as his Xed insect is on the card or neither of his other two search targets are present), that player must remove the bottom card of his search stack from the game. The slapped card is also removed from play. (A player can't lose his only search card. If no one slaps the card, remove it from play, then reveal the next card.)

Play continues until the final insect card has been slapped. Whoever has the most cards in his search deck wins.

For children as young as age three, Marienkäfer und so can be played with players taking turns consecutively instead of playing simultaneously. If a player can claim a card, he does so, making it his new search card; otherwise he removes it from play.

A second game is included in Marienkäfer und so. Shuffle the insect cards black-and-white side face up, then deal seven cards to each player. All players simultaneously flip their top card color-side up onto the table, then race to slap an insect card in front of another player that matches the search criteria on the newly revealed top card of their personal deck. Each card can be claimed by only one player, i.e., whoever slaps it first. If a player slaps a card correctly, he places it on the bottom of his deck black-and-white side face up; if a player slaps a card incorrectly, his current search card is removed from play. All unslapped cards are also removed from play. The game ends when one or more players have only a single card in their deck; the player(s) with the most cards in their deck(s) wins.

Game No 4375. For two or more players.

Directions for Playing : Place a ball upon each of the pedestals in the bottom of the box, placing them on the pedestal or corresponding color. To Wind the top: Place the wooden disk over the center pin with the small metal pin and end of wire spring against the top of the top. Hold the bottom part of the top firmly in the left hand and give the wooden disk a few turns to the right. To release the spinning part of the top, hold the wooden disk firmly in the hand and press one finger on the center of the metal pin. The spinning top must be dropped into the center small circle of the playing board. Each player has one spin at a turn and at the end of his turn the score is taken, counting the numbers in front of the pedestals from which a ball has been thrown. If two balls of one color are thrown, it doubles the score of those two balls. A game may consist of 300 points; or it may be played by rounds, the player having the greatest score at the end of an agreed upon number of rounds, being the winner."

The pedestals are numbered as follows in clockwise direction :

Red: 20 points
Blue: 15 points
Green: 10 points
Yellow: 5 points
Orange: 10 points
Red: 5 points
Blue: 20 points
Green: 15 points
Yellow: 10 points
Orange: 5 points

There is a wooden coloured ball, corresponding in number and colour to each pedestal. The game is played inside a square wooden box with a height of about 5cm (2"). The box is 34cm square (13.5").

Rules for this game come from --- Famous Belgian (famousbelgian). He also contributed a couple of pictures of the game which was in the Unidentified Game geeklist.

Бронепехота ("Armored Infantry") is a sci-fi tabletop game with miniatures. Each player is the head of the military forces of one of the opposing star powers.  The rules aren't extensive, but wide enough to cover many nuances of intense fighting in the Star Era. There are two ways of shooting: real and virtual. Some weapons actually launch plastic missiles. Virtual firing is resolved using dice rolls. The rules contain corresponding chapters if players wish to switch from one firing way to another. Warriors, cannons and fighting machines are snap together kits requiring no glue for assembly.

From the official rules of the game:

ARMORED INFANTRY is a sci-fi game that can be played by several players at the same time, each with their own army. To win, it is necessary to defeat the enemy army or complete some task - a mission. Missions can be very different, for example, to capture an object, destroy an enemy command vehicle, hold a line, etc.

Player armies consist of squads of armored infantry, manned combat vehicles, and artillery pieces. Their game parameters are indicated in special "documents" - Army Lists.

Each vehicle or squad of infantrymen is a separate unit. During the battle, players take turns operating their units – moving them, shooting at the enemy, or engaging the enemy in close combat. When one unit has finished its actions, i.e. has completed its turn, it is time for the next unit's turn, and so on. Each unit can only make one turn per round, so when all units have made one turn, a new round begins. At the beginning of each round, the players play the initiative, i.e. determine who will go first, who will go second, etc. If all soldiers - infantrymen and machine pilots - in the player's army are destroyed, then he is out of the game. The rest of the participants continue the battle without him. Thus, round after round, the battle continues until one of the players wins.

A rare mineral was found on the planet Arktal-3. Needed by all advanced civilizations in the galaxy.
Grab the field, build factories, research centers and increase the power of your army, to achieve victory and secure your civilization the most important resources.

Звездный рубеж ("Star Frontier") is a light miniature war game played on modular hextiles.
The units are composed of infantry, mechas and various tanks, produced in factories.
Players can use research cards to increase the power of their units.

Background of the game is set during the Three Kingdoms period. Each player will take the role of one of the lords. Each lord has an unique ability which if well utilised may be one of the factor impacting a player’s route to victory. In addition, players will get to come across well know generals from the Three Kingdoms era. With a unique battle system, military force will not be the only key deciding factor to winning. Clever use of tactic may change the situation of the war, making it possible for a weaker general to beat a better known general. To unify the world and win the game, having a good strategist is definitely indispensable.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Race game which tries to be original in that you have two pieces (an animal and a rider) you can consider moving after you have thrown the die. After both pieces have started separately, the players must get their two pieces together on the same square. Once achieved, you can move both as one unit because you cannot win whilst your two pieces are apart.
First to the end of the track with rider mounted on animal, wins.

Card game that owes a debt to Rummy as a similar game mechanic but with a lot of extra options.
The objective is to use all the cards in your hand first, by laying down cards that represent a journey. A journey may include such cards as destinations, Visas, passports and currencies.
When a player 'goes out', all the cards played by him onto a single journey or multiple journeys, will score him points. The black number on each card represents the card's value. Cards also have a red number, and that is the penalty value of cards not yet used by the opponents.
Six deals equals a game. The player with the most points after those six deals, wins.

Contents: 4 plastic aeroplanes, one die and a board. The instructions are on the back of the box.
A simple roll and move game. Each player starts in a different corner of the board and will advance their pawn along one of four tracks (coloured Red, Green, Yellow or Blue. No two players must use the same track. Then you roll and move, obeying instructions if you land on special squares. First to London wins.

A game whose aim is to improve your mastery of the Gaelic language. The game focuses on increasing players' exposure to 12 frequently-used, improper Gaelic verbs. An audio cassette is included so that players can hear native speakers pronouncing Gaelic words and phrases. Players roll a D6, land on a numbered square of the colorful board, and refer to one of six laminated lists to find the corresponding instructions for that space. The instructions are written in Gaelic, and the current player must respond to the instructions correctly. The winner is the first player to get his token to the end of the board. Players are to speak entirely in Gaelic while playing.

Race game with some novel twists.
The track on the board takes you on a journey to England. 18 picture cards are shuffled and placed face down on numbered circles at the top of the board. On your journey, should you land on the same numbered square as one of the circles above, you take the face-down picture card. This card is a souvenir of your trip and will score the points indicated. Only the first player to arrive at a square that is linked to a circle above, can take the picture card.
The first player to reach England gains 10 points. Now everyone counts up the points on their souvenir cards and the player with the most points, wins.
One unusual rule. If you arrive home with the Slave-Girl (souvenir?!) cannot arrive in England with such a find. It must be returned to the board and he can choose any other souvenir card that is still unowned. England would appear to be the only country in Europe that bans slaves! By the way, if you want a Slave-Girl of your own, the place to go is Istanbul (square 65).

Super-sized tabletop golf game.
Play real golf shots with your miniature metal golf clubs. The greens are represented by green card with holes in the centre. The ball must go constantly onto the floor I would think (out of bounds?).
The box says:
An exact counterpart, in miniature, of the popular outdoor pastimeComplete with Golf Clubs, Balls, Putting Greens, Bunkers and full instructions. This game of skill entrances the Golf enthusiast as well as the learner.A really scientific Game of Golf can be played indoors with the appliances without any risk of injury to furniture.

I love that last line of the box text.

Not Rummy at all, but it is a set collecting game.
The top left corner of a picture and the bottom right of the same picture are placed in such a way that you can add 9 cards between them to form a finished picture. You draw and play cards to your picture much the same as you would in most card games of this type.
The pictures are very nice (see image for a coaching scene for example).
The first player to complete a picture, wins.

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Young Legacy Promo Card
description:
Pauline Felicia Parsons, the daughter of Paul Parsons VIII, having just turned 18, developed new powers of her own.

Innate Power: "Atomic Glare" - Legacy deals 1 target 3 Energy damage.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Home is a game of skill where the players have as principal aim displace his cards and manage to reach the cabin "HOME".

Objective: To mark six points in the cabin HOME, placed in the center of the board.

Beginning: One distributes in a random way to every player 3 cards.

Placement you register:    There are placed 3 cards received in the cabin corresponding to his number in the end of the board to the right of the cabin "Beginning"

The first player: One adds the values of every card that the player has received. The player with major quantity, is the player in moving first.

Movement: It is possible to displace in every shift a maximum of "1 card" to an adjacent cabin on the one that is.

Every cabin corresponds to one of six cards of game, thus on having placed on a cabin with number "1" it corresponds to which is the turn to move to the card "1" and here to depend that player is his owner.

The value of this system resides in whom he is the owner of the card who has to play in the new shift.

Marking points:    Once there is reached the central cabin HOME, there is marked " 1 point ". Later, the card that has reached the cabin HOME, returns to his initial cabin in the zone of beginning of the board corresponding to the player.

Winner:    The first player who marks 6 points, which it is equal when reaches the cabin HOME six times.

Special rules:    Every player possesses his area of playground. It is not possible to invade the opposite field.

Once there is reached the Cabin HOME and the card is returned to his cabin of corresponding exit, there is activated the norm of the following card in moving, being this same to a new adjacent cabin.

Every player gets six differnt colored marbles and 6 same-color caps. One cap is pulled over each marble. The marbles must reach the goal in the right order. The players rolls a die to move the caps over the board. If the player rolls a 6 he can look under on of his caps. The first player who finished the sequence wins the game.

the goal sequences can be changed.

Love It! Hate It! is a game that asks how well you really know your friends and family. Do you know what type of super power they would love and hate to have? Could you name with whom they would love and hate to be trapped in an elevator?

Each round, someone reads a scenario card out loud and waits for their friends to secretly reveal what they think the card reader would love and hate in the situation. Once all the answers have been written on the dry-erase speech bubbles, one person holds the speech bubbles over the card reader's head and reads the answers out loud. The card reader will then pick the answer they liked best from the Love It options and the Hate It options. If your answer is picked, you get a chip. The object is to get three Love It chips and three Hate It chips to win.

The game comes with 18 Love It chips, 18 Hate It chips, 294 scenarios on 147 cards, three two-sided blank cards, six dry-erase speech bubbles, six dry-erase markers, a chip bag, a bubble tray, and rules.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: There are 8 cards for each of the 8 themes. In play 8 rows with the themes are laid out, each theme once in every row, the first player to place all his cards wins the game. In his move a player lays down as many cards as he wants, never two cards from one theme into the same row, but always in the same sequence of themes. If a player cannot place cards, he can switch cards with his stack. Before each placement round players fill up their hands to the maximum and then draw a card from their neigbour.

There are 60 cards in the game with a knight motive and 15 theme cards, which show four motives belonging to the theme. Players must attribute the motive cards to the theme cards. The player placing the last motive card next to a theme card, receives all 5 cards, the first player to collect 4 theme groups wins the game. In his move a player draws 2 cards and discards two cards, either placing them next to theme cards or onto the discard pile.

The game has 60 dinosaur cards showing 60 different dinosaurs. The header of the card states four facts: size, length and weight of the dinosaur and how many million years ago it lived. Players start with 6 cards, the die determines a category for each round, players then play one card. The player who has played the highest value in the current category wins all cards of the round. When all cards are played, the player with the most cards wins the game.

All players have seven cards, one card is placed in the middle of the table with condition which animal on the next card can be or cannot be. Player who has gameturn plays that animal, if possible, that satisfies the corresponding condition, and now calls itself a new condition that have to be on the next card. 
If player hasn't correct card to play he mustdraw a card. Who plays his last card, immediately wins the game.

For each of the six continents, there are 10 maps with attractions, monuments, buildings, landscapes or animals. In the game series will be placed with the continents, each series comes once in every continent. Player who has gameturn reveals his top card and places them in a row where the continent is still missing. He is already represented in all ranks to begin a new series. Who sets the last continent in a series gets the whole series. Those who completed the ninth row, finished the game, who has collected the most series wins.

Players travel in the city by car and look for a place for parking. Players who park illegally are threatened by invisible trapss and must pay a penalty. If all cars have reached the parking place, the player with the most cash cards wins.

Time travel through 2000 years of history, always with the focus on "what would I have done here and there?". The cards are shuffled and stacked according to back sides, players travel through the epochs, separated by turn of epoch squares and action cards, the winner is the team reaching the finish first. For correctly answered questions one rolls the die and moves, in the next round the question for the square reached needs to be solved. The special squares have special rules.

A board with triangle and rhombus spots for the playing pieces, diagonally arranged black starting squares and coloured squares for finish spots in the middle. On that special board a dice and movement game is played with special dice, which denote up, down, right, left, double move and lose a turn, directions rolled give a move of one spot, double move allows two moves of the player’s choice, but must be made. For some spots there are special rules, playing pieces in the finish spot are removed from the game, a player with all his 4 pieces out of the game has won.

Players have a set of small animal tiles, the big ones are slid one by one into the wood wall and must be guessed by the players. The Caretaker of the round reveals the holes, the player to guess the hidden animal may discard the corresponding animal card. A player who guesses an animal that does not live in the wood, may discard any small tile. The player with only two small tiles left wins the round and receives a chip. When all players have been caretaker once, the player with the majority of chips wins.

The children are playing Robbers & Police, in the game the players are the police trying to find the the robbers in their hiding places. 48 cards are distributed face down on the board, 12 of them are robbers. Players roll the die and move along the paths on the board. If they suspect a card to hide a robber, they pick it up and turn it over. If the suspicion was correct and it is a robber he is captured and the player takes the card. If the suspicion was wrong, the player has to free an already captured robber and put him back on the board. Who has the most robbers at the end of the game wins.

When humans are away... the robotic toys will play!

Pick your favorite toy robot equipment, ranging from dart guns to electro-magnets, and use it to push, pull, or short circuit the other robots running lose on the factory floor. Make sure to avoid the hazardous factory equipment like crushers, hot plates, and trash pits while navigating the turn tables and conveyor belts to victory!

Use your logic and foresight in this chaotic game to outmaneuver your friends and achieve your goals.

Game Features:

- Double-sided board for twice the fun!
- 10 Unique toy powers to choose from!
- 216 Different ways to play using rule mutators!

Learning game for the "big" 9 times tables in three phases:
The learning phase teaches the 9 times table and children check with the ruler.
In the Using Phase the child draws cards, if the answer is correct a chip is placed on the corresponding number.
In the Training Phase players hold 8 cards each and play one to top the card of the previous player, the cards do not show the result, only the question, so the children need to calculate.

5500 questions and answers in a new edition of the former noris quiz games, a new name and new graphics, the categories are: History & politics, nature and environment, sports & games, art & culture, heaven & earth, fairy tales and folk tales, technology & science, general knowledge. New is the possibility to advance on a money scale for correct answers.

The children are little gardeners and try to have as good a harvest as possible in their garden. Each has his own garden with 9 squares, in the middle of the table there are 12x3 vegetable cards, 4 watering cans, 4 x weeds and 4 x parasites. One card after the other is turned over, vegetables are placed in the garden. So are weeds, for a parasite a vegetable has to go out, for a watering can one can turn over another card. Who has managed to plant 3 cards each of three different kinds of vegetables in his garden has won the game. 2 game variants are given.

The little mermaids playing in the moonlight ride with beads on fishing, diving for treasure or maintain her long hair. Animals and Plants of the water are their best friends and in three different games, children can dive into the world of mermaids. In the picture game players form an underwater picture with the cards, in the collecting game, each child must find 5 cards of their color, and in the memory game, there are 40 pairs from the world of mermaids.

The Scourge are coming! Lead your rag-tag band of surviors against the an alien race that would see all humans extinct! Featuring a Mini-Campaign system and 3 different scenarios! Will you be humanities last hope?

The Day of the Scourge box set contains all the figures you need to play a Scourge invasion! Each box contains 21 Scourge, 6 Scourge Heavy Weapons, 6 Post Apoc Survivors and a PDF of the Day of the Scourge Campaign!

DoS is a supplement/scenario for the Two Hour Wargames 5150: New Beginings core game system. You must have the Chain Reaction 3.0 core rulebook to play this scenario (or 5150 Star Army). You can download Chain Reaction 3.0 for Free at Chain Reaction 3.0

The miniatures are made of lead-free pewter.

T-game can be played on a rectangular grid of any size. Even the small 6x6 grid used in these rule illustrations leads to interesting play.
Each player in turn draws a horizontal or vertical line that connects two previously drawn parallel lines. The new line must be drawn along the board's grid lines and must be perpendicular to both of the lines it connects. At the start of the game, the board edges are considered to be previously drawn lines.
A player may not draw a line that crosses a previously drawn line, nor may a line connect with the end of a previously drawn line. In other words, lines may only come together at T intersections; there may never be a four-way crossing.
The winner is the last player able to move.

In the 44-year long history of the Cold War, few conflicts symbolize it as fully as those in Vietnam. To contemporary observers, it was a war between the two ideological blocs of Communism and the West. From the forces of the Viet Cong and NVA and the American, South Vietnamese, Australian and other Free World forces, the wars that ravaged Indochina throughout the second half of the 20th century can be recreated with Vietnam, the latest companion volume for Force on Force. Orders of battle, scenarios and special theatre-specific rules give players an immersive experience of gaming in in the jungles, fields and towns of Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: A Throne Vacant: Wars of the Spanish Succession 1701-1714
description:
Background:

When Charles II of Spain died in November 1700 the both mentally and physically weak king had no heir to the crown. His kingdoms included not only Spain but both parts of the Low Countries, Italy and colonies in the Philipines and the Americas. Now two dynasties claimed the Spanish throne, the French Bourbons and the Austrian Habsburgs. Charles last will proclaimed that Philip, Duke of Anjou (grandson of Louis XIV) was to be offered the whole Spanish empire. However, the German emperor, Leopold I had launched his son the Archduke Charles as pretender to the throne. England preferred the Electoral Prince Joseph Ferdinand of Bavaria who was Leopold’s grandson. In February 1701 the 17 year old Philip was crowned in Madrid and was reluctantly recognized by England. In the same time French troops secured the Spanish Nederlands and cut of England and the United Provinces from Spanish trade. This resulted in the Treaty of Den Haag 1701 which formed the great alliance between Austria, England and the United Provinces and was later supported by Prussia, Hanover, Denmark, the Holy Roman Empire, Portugal and Savoy. The treaty did actually recognized Philip as the king of Spain but more important, it retained the Spanish trade, gave the Spanish territories in Italy as well as the Spanish Nederlands to Austria and formed a defensive alliance against France. On the other side Bavaria and Cologne supported the French in this upcoming conflict. When Portugal joined the great alliance the focus turned to put Archduke Charles on the Spanish throne.

The war now started slowly in Italy where an Austrian force commanded by Eugene of Savoy invaded the Duchy of Milan, one of the Spanish territories in Italy, which prompted a French intervention. Things are now set up for A Throne Vacant™ to begin.

The game:

A Throne Vacant™( # 107-1 ) is easy to learn and is played in a fast phased manner. The units are blocks in regimental and army size for greatest flexibility in taking steps and differentiating the quality and strength of the troops, like in its sister game Pax Baltica. The high quality map is divided in territories which you have to conquer order to control it. This is made by taking each and every town in a territory. Navies will also play an important part in ATV in the sense that you have to keep the sea lines open to the colonies, and of course transport British troops to the continent. As this intriguing conflict covers the whole of western Europe the map covers a large area from the Atlantic coast in the west to Prague in the east, from northern England in the north to Oran in the south. Playable nations are England/Great Britain, France, Spain, Austria and The United Provinces. Other countries represented in the game are Portugal, Savoy, Bavaria, Denmark and The Holy Roman Empire.

So, why don’t you enter the roles of some of the great military commanders of history, Marlborough, Prince Eugene, Villars or Vendôme. Lead them to great victories and secure the Spanish crown for your pretender to the throne. We are quite sure that this game will bring a Machiavellian smile to your face.

The game, packed in a book case includes a 50 x 70cm-size full color map, 80 high quality wooden blocks, 4 dice, various markers and rules, including different scenarios.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: War of the Spanish Succession
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Guerrilla Print (Three Crowns Game Productions)

description:
Smaller than Reinhold Wittig's Spiel, tinier than the Spiel expansion 55, Abacusspiele presents Wittig's Spiel Mini, which consists of a pyramidal base, twenty dice of the same color, and a plastic lid to keep everything in place.

Like Wittig's Spiel, Spiel Mini is a game system in which users are encouraged to create and submit their own dice games to Abacusspiele for inclusion in the produced version of the game. Spiel Mini will initially be released at Spiel 2011 in a "barebones" edition that includes all of the components and none of the rules.

Reimplements:

    Spiel
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
An educational game (as are all the other games by this designer). Again, it uses his principles of a Lotto mechanic. Players will learn to recognise vowel sounds and vowel combinations. When a player recognises a vowel sound, he then checks his card and if that sound is also in the name of an object on his card, he may match with it.
Part of the publishers Phonics series. Aimed at children from 5 to 10 years old.

Each Player gets three additional tokens with a special feature. During the game a token can be used to buy a fourth action OR to use the special feature. Then it is discarded. For example one token allows you to write a history about one of your own monuments. At the end of the game you may use any tokens you have left AFTER the usual end of the game. In case you have all tokens left, you are even allowed to write a history then!
Antipatros is a free give away in Essen 2011 and is a limited edition.

One may still be able to remember the last Grand Prix season with it's dramatic finale, but who can tell what has happened in the seasons 50 years ago? The game has 165 cards with more than 1000 questions in three categories: Cars and Technology, History and Drivers, Grand Prix Aktuell. The players roll a die and move forward accordingly . For each roll of the die he must pay with chips, also for wrong answers. For correct answers a player receives up to three chips, for overtaking another player 1 chip. If a player has finished one round (or more, if wanted), the player with the most chips is the winner.

How do I get more pocket money and what I do best with it? This eternal question of children's attempts gets answer with this game, with all sorts of activities players may spend their pocket money. Players learn to count money and also convert one currency to another - there are Deutch Marks and Euros in this game. The mechanism is simple - roll - move - follow instructions of the target field.

Rows of three fruits are the aim of the game! Strawberries, grapes and pears are depicted either in a square, a circle or a triangle in yellow, blue or red. The tiles are heaped face-down and  each player draws three tiles. In your turn you place one of your tiles on the board to complete a row of three either of the same fruit or the same color or the same background shape. Each completed row of three earns you a chip, and of course you can complete more than one row with one tile and use rows already placed as part of a new row. When all spaces on the board are filled you win with most chips.

A collection of games featuring funny dwarves and offering three different ways to play: 
Back into Pixies Land! Is a collecting game, each player has a board, frameless side up, You throw the dwarf dice stick and take a corresponding part, pants, head or hat, when this spot is still empty on your board, and place it. For the stars you take any missing part. If you have filled your board first, you win. 
Let’s go home together! is a cooperative game, the boards are placed frame up. You throw the cloud stick. For a cloud you place a corresponding part, for the stars any missing part. When all dwarves are complete, all players have won together. Let’s visit King Dopey! is a stacking game. First, you take a board and place all parts for a complete dwarf. Then you throw the dwarf stick. If it shows pants, head or hat you take the necessary part and place it on the table to achieve a correct dwarf, first pants, then head and hat. For the stars you take the next missing part, if you finish your dwarf first, you win.

With the components of this toy/puzzle/game box you can play a cooperative roll & move game. The board is put together - side without stripes up. The boat base sits in front of the board at the side with the red wind mill, the other wooden parts are distributed on their images on the board. You roll the die: For a color Sam the Sailor advances to the next space of this color, on a red-yellow ring he must stop and place the corresponding part on the boat. For Sam himself all players applaud, but he stays in place. When you roll the boat, you advance it one sea space. If Sam reaches the boat, all win together, if the boat leaves the board before that, all have lost together.

The 3 sides of the 25 wooden pieces show different characteristics (form, colour, number and filling) of symbols. Player may alternately turn or relocate a piece to bring three correct pieces into a horizontal, vertical or diagonal line. To be correct they must be a trio and a trio must have each of the characteristics either the same or completely different on all pieces. Who forms a trio may take it out of the game, the player with the most trios wins the game. Variant of the card game Set.

There are 3 symbols in this game: sword, hear and scale, all with their strengths and weaknesses.

Each player represents one of the symbols. In the game, players turn to different means to achieve this primary goal. They try to uncover and capture enemy stones. You bluff by pretending certain strategic positions with their hidden rocks or by seeking an unexpected winning condition.

Contents:

     30 triangular pieces (10 each in white, red and black). 


In every color there are 3 stones sword, 3 scale blocks, three blocks and a heart stone eyes. 

     folded game board inside the pyramid-shaped game-box)

Objective: be the first to move your three meeples (cars) back home.  Simple 'roll and move' game, combined with educational touch (learning road signs). Your meeples (Wartburg 311) can't be thrown out, but you should try to block your opponents.

Second (better known) edition 1965/68 by 'Spika'

This pack provides 18 Advanced Squad Leader (ASL) scenarios covering a variety of actions in the Budapest campaign in Hungary during WWII.  The 18 scenarios include a good variety of scenarios from large to tournament sized.

Included also are two new boards; FE 1 is a rural board with some farms, a gully and some small hills; FE 2 is a city set on a hill. The boards are on heavy card stock and each comes in two pieces that connect along the Q hex row.

boardgame: Tercio: Wargames Rules for the Period 1480 to 1700
description: Tabletop miniature rules for the period 1480-1700 including army lists.  The ruleset allows you to recreate any battle from the pike and shot era and is designed for 15mm or 25mm figures, but the rules could be adapted for any scale.  The army lists allow you to dive straight in to your chosen period with an army representing a standard formation from the period.

boardgame: Constantinople to Vienna: Wargames Rules for the Period 1450 to 1700 AD
description: Tabletop miniature wargame rules for the period 1450 to 1700AD.  The rules allow the recreation of battles from the age of pike and shot to be made using either 15mm or 25mm figures.  Rules are included to cover field battles as well as sieges and assaults.
family: History: Classical Age of the Ottoman Empire

boardgame: Fast Play Rules for the Pike & Shot Period (1550 - 1720 AD)
description: Tabletop miniature wargame rules for the period 1550-1720AD.  The rules are designed as an introductory ruleset to the pike and shot era and are basically a simplified version of the ruleset Constantinople to Vienna, also by Newbury Rules.  The rules are designed with 15mm miniatures in mind but can be adapted to any scale.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Army Lists for the Revolution in Warfare: Volume 1 1550-1660AD
description: Army Lists suitable for use with rules in the Warfare through the Ages series and adaptable for Wargames Research Group Renaissance, Tercio and Newbury Fast Play Rules.  The lists cover every major battle and nation for the period, from English Civil War through Rennaisance Europe, to Japan, North America and Africa.  Each army list is accompanied by a descriptive paragraph explaining which troop combinations are allowed and what the army's historical basis is.

A comprehensive set of WWII army lists for British, German, American, Italian, Russian, Japanese, French, Hungarian and Czechoslovakian forces.  Each nations entry is preceded by a brief overview of that nation's forces, followed by a set of diagrams showing how the various organisations employed by that nation were organized.  This is then followed by a number of suggested armies for tabletop battles.  All the information contained within the book is set out for use with three rule sets in mind: Combined Arms, Cambrai to Sinai and Wargames research Group.

Ha-Shin is a tabletop miniature wargame set in fantasy medieval Japan, where the forces of Light battle the forces of Darkness. It was published in issue #9 (February 2002) of Ravage, a French miniature wargaming magazine.

Each mini has several stats describing its ability to move, shoot, fight and use "Chi" powers. The most important is Action Points (AP) which represent the character's life energy.
A player's turn begins by choosing one of several tactics. Tactics influence how many figures can be activated, how many APs are recovered at the end of the turn and what figures can recover APs. The player then chooses which figures to activate, and expends the figure's APs to move, charge, use a ranged weapon or a Chi power. Each fight is duel; duel are resolved by secretly choosing a combat action for each combatant (which can give bonuses or penalties to die rolls), then rolling for initiative, then trying to beat the opponent's Armour score with 1d6 + the fighter attack score. Each damage point lowers the victim's APs, minis with no APs left must make a save roll each time they are wounded to avoid death.

The last player standing wins the game, also players are encouraged to create scenarios and alternate victory conditions.

The rules include two army lists with classic Japanese folklore figures (samurais, ninjas, shaolin monks, kappas, onis...), a set of markers and tactical advice for both featured armies (Light and Darkness).

Scale: skirmish (1 mini = 1 combatant, 1 cm = 1 m, a turn = a few seconds)

Founders: A More Perfect Union is a trivia-plot game for 2-3 players or teams. Each turn involves roll and play, as well as the possibility of play cards.
     Each player has authored a draft of the Constitution that contains the principles and protections necessary to unify the states. However, in order to present the draft at the Constitutional Convention, each author, must convince a certain number of states to join his or her delegation in support of the draft. Once the delegation is large enough, the author can travel to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania and present the draft before the convention in Independence Hall.  If you are successful in persuading the convention in your favor, your draft is ratified and becomes the Constitution of the United States! Players will have to study up to prove to these learned delegates will test your knowledge and  reason before choosing to give you their support.
Answer trivia questions to get your state cards necessary to go to Philadelphia, but be careful, another player might have the play cards needed to steal a member of your delegation, or just send them packing home!

A Wildlife trivia game.
Players are read out a question and then told to touch a box on the answer sheet with an electrode. If their answer is correct, a bulb will light up.
Player who gets the most questions right, wins.
Look at the superb artwork on the box.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Urban Structures
description: Urban Structures is an expansion pack for Flash Point: Fire Rescue. It was initially printed in low quantities in 2011 for supporters of the Kickstarter project for the base game.  It is now available commercially.  It comes with 2 additional maps as well as a "Structural Engineer" role card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
British roll-and-move game, If you land on the red spaces, follow the instructions.
First to complete a pre-set number of laps, wins.

Silverstone, in Northamptonshire, was opened in 1948. The site was a British ex-WWII airfield.

This is a puzzle game using the Tiptoi (not included in the game) - an electronic pen developed by Ravensburger.

Players try to find four matching pieces (one for each season of the year) by following hints given by the Tiptoi.

The game also features a "discover mode" to provide information, music, trivia about the seasons of the year in general

Players take turns to throw small rings made of rubber at the target board. If the ring catches on to one of the scoring hooks, that score is added to the player's total so far.

First to reach a pre-set total, wins. Or, it can be ended after a pre-set time limit and then the player with the most points, wins.

1-4 player co-operative card game that lets players take control of a post-apocalyptic, retro-future, caravan.

"It feels like Race for the Galaxy Vs. Magic the Gathering with a splash of euro gaming with a nuclear fallout post apocalyptic setting."

Why are you in a nuclear desert?
Players control a rag-tag caravan that contains elements of how society was before the bombs dropped. Everything from road-converted steam engines to solar harvesters staffed by inviting female engineers, and it's your job to get these guys the-hell-outta-here.
AtomPunk makes use of some inventive new puzzle-mechanics and loots the best ones from elsewhere. It steals the best bits of card interactions but dispenses with the "Spend loads of money to play with our new cards" CCG crap - it is one game. It uses resource tokens like some of our favorite euros, but keeps them in an elegant card format. Its core puzzle mechanic means that players will have to use your mind to work out the best path in this treacherous dystopian backdrop
You and your buddies are alone against the harsh wastelands. The riding is tough. Horrific mutants, shady merchants, hardened outriders, religious tech zealots and crazed robo-emus. BUT on your side you have have speed, cunning, all the guns you can handle, and transformer-like laser beam trains.

What are the mechanics?
1. You control 5 caravan elements in a horizontal line that give each card to its left and right static bonuses. These bonuses activate special abilities on the elements. You will constantly need to be thinking about getting these in the best configuration.
2. An event deck throws everything at you from crazy cleaning robots, twisted mutants, resource giving oasis, and caravan weaponry upgrades
3. Each turn pits you in a certain land. This land card tells you what resources you need to move on to the next land. After 5 of these you will come up against the 6th and final hard-as-nails boss land which will tell you the final quest you need to complete.
4. Not only do you need to conquer these 6 lands, but each player has a secret achievement goal card that they MUST complete in order to be allowed into the winning team. Will you tell your team mates yours?

How do we win?
Once you fulfill the requirements to bear the 6th and final quest land the "players" team will be successful. But only if you have succeeded your secret mission goal will you truly win, otherwise you have not won or lost, but you are cast back into the wastes, to win another day...

Qubic games evidently used same 3D board for two games, Qubic and Letter Go.

Letter Go is a 3D game to form 4 letter words on a 4x4x4 grid. The contents included the 3D board and cardboard letter chits. draw chits blindly and score points for words.

Similar to:

    Four Letter Words

Expanze 2 is the first expansion pack for the Czech talisman style boardgame Duchové, Démoni a Draci.

The expansion contains 48 new cards that are added to the games basic stack, and introduces several new statistics.

The player does now have to defend himself from three new types of attack (Hypnosis, Venom, Black magic) and a new creature ability is introduced (Flying).

The goal of this expansion is to increase the difficulty of the game, playability, replayability and entertaining value.

The designer of the game said, that the expansion is best played when two sets of the new cards are used.

An even greater peril awaits with More Brutes! Expand the Brutes Fantasy Microgame with this add-on that includes 12 new Disposable Heroes Paper Miniatures and a total of 15 new character, weapon, and armor gimmicks/traits.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Redhorn Gate
description:
Game description from the publisher:
 Beginning the second cycle of Adventure Packs for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, The Redhorn Gate allows players to experience the thrills of a mountain voyage full of surprises.

When some of Middle-earth's greatest heroes volunteer to escort Arwen to Rivendell, their pass through The Redhorn Gate begins an epic journey that will take them through bone-chilling snowstorms and hordes of enemies, into the twists and turns of the entire "Dwarrowdelf" cycle of Adventure Packs. With 60 new cards, including a new scenario and three copies of each new player card, The Redhorn Gate builds upon the encounter sets from the Khazad-dûm expansion to offer players exciting new challenges during an all-new narrative set in the Misty Mountains!

description:
1959 Toy Rocket Space Game by the Trans-o-gram Co. Yep, the same company that made cookie cutters and other baking products!

Cover states: "Force enemy rockets out of orbit.... and CONQUER OUTER SPACE!

A simple game with a spinner (1-6 values) where players move their rockets to knock others out of orbit by moving their rocket on a atomic symbol shaped track to collide with an opponents rocket to take it out of orbit.

boardgame: Mistral: The War of the Austrian Succession at Sea-Med Theatre 1739-1748
description:
Mistral is the first of a set of naval games from Red Sash Games; a series called Sea Lords.

Mistral covers the War of the Austrian Succession at sea, plus the concurrent War of Jenkin's Ear. Though relatively unknown, these conflicts, raging from 1739-1748, comprised the first "world war". The primary naval actions took place in three theatres: the English Channel and Bay of Biscay, the Caribbean, and the western Mediterranean. Forces also clashed in India, along America's eastern seaboard, and in the Pacific. Each of the other games covers one of these theatres. Mistral covers the western Mediterranean, where Spain tried to keep her lines of communication open to her army in Italy, and France's Toulon Fleet continually harassed the British.

Mistral is a two player strategy game. You command either the British or Bourbon (French and Spanish) fleets. Orders are handed down to you from your Admiralty, and you must try to fulfill them within the framework of the wider war, which is represented by a set of events. Your opponent will have orders of his own to fulfill, but with his spare forces he will be attempting to foil your plans. Success will earn you Prestige (victory points), and may even have an impact on the land campaign. You must decide when and where to commit resources, where to feint and where to strike, organising your "sail" (ships) into squadrons, and your squadrons into flotillas commanded by leaders (commodores and admirals). Maintaining sufficient forces at "battle readiness" is critical, but very difficult, since ships constantly suffer wear and tear – a battle may be won and the campaign lost. Scenarios are one year (16 turns) long, playable in 3-4 hours (the number of counters in play is quite small). The campaign game consists of a number of years pre-chosen by the players. A special campaign scenario allows the players to link Mistral with its companion Lace Wars game – Queen's Gambit.
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description:
Dark streets, vile sewers, and twisting alleys – all home to the terrible criminals that control this city. Welcome to Rook City, where evil never sleeps.

Sentinels of the Multiverse: Rook City, the first expansion for the cooperative comic-book themed card game Sentinels of the Multiverse, features two new, grim heroes, four terrible villains, and two challenging environments. Besides just expanding the number of characters and settings available to players, Rook City introduces new mechanisms and exciting new challenges while maintaining the rules that make Sentinels of the Multiverse work.

description: Sword of Neurasthenia, named from the Japanese phrase "Shinnkei-suijyaku", is a game which tests your memory. The players are studying martial arts, and they practice lessons by turning over cards and taking those actions. Players collect and use these cards to get points and compete against one another. By successfully collecting the cards, they achieve different degrees and even kill monsters with their martial arts. Fail to remember your moves, and you might suffer accidents.

As opposed to most published "drinking games", Drunk Sloppoly is a party game created by people who actually party. This is so much more than a drinking version of Monopoly...this game has four categories of cards that include group games, drunken duels, Puzzle bottle caps, alcohol trivia, sobriety tests and much more. The game also comes with four spinable wheels with different themes to flavor your party experience (sweet, sexy, fun, and evil). The game is intended for coed audiences 21+ and is "Not Recommended to be Played with Alcohol". There are alternate rules included if you're not in the company of people who like to kiss and remove articles of clothing. This game is ridiculous and fun and best with 4-8 players.

The game includes: the board and box, rules, bar deeds, 130+ game cards, 8 game pieces, 4 wheels that attach to the board, 2 dice, and 30 second timer.

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – Shadow of the Monolith Asylum Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The sixth and final Asylum Pack in the "Ancient Relics" cycle for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game, Shadow of the Monolith continues the cycle's development of the exciting new Relic trait and concludes the cycle's exploration of the Ancient Ones' global reach.

Sixty new cards (three copies each of 20 different cards) take the game's different factions to the South Pacific, where they are greeted by malevolent cultists and their dark spells. Who will prosper when the region's Relics are unearthed? Who will fall mad or die along the way? Shadow of the Monolith removes the final veils of the Ancient Relics cycle of Asylum Packs, 
but its secrets aren't for the faint of heart!

description:
There are 3x3 field game board and 2 dice for younger players and 4x4 field game board and 3 dice for older players in the game. 
The player who has three chips in a row (horizontally, vertically or diagonally) first wins.

In his turn player roll the dice and numbers on the dice may be added together, subtracted, multiplied and divided, so that the result may be used in any free field to place the chip.

for example: There are 1, 3 and 5 dice, so there are many different results.
1+3+5=9
5-3+1=3
3:1+5=8
(3-1)x5=10
etc.

The question cards in this Quiz/Learning game come from the daily life of a child. The front side shows the german word and a picture. The backside of the card has the english translation and the phonetic writing of the word to teach the correct pronounciation. Furthermore there is a simple sentence which uses the word in and easy context, also with phonetic transcription and the german translation of the whole sentence. The children move their playing pieces through the Inner City of London, at each of the 12 monuments a card is drawn, if the child answers correctly, it receives a knowledge-chip. Who has the most chips at the end of the game, wins.

Quiz about Europe, the questions range across all 48 countries that belong to Europe geographically. There are picture questions and topics such as history and politics or art and culture in this game. 
If player answer correctly to questions, he stand in the capital of a country, he gets the star.
Player who gets 12 stars first wins the game.
Each player tries to get as quickly as possible 12 stars.
Expansion 03:  additional questions (1000+) to be played with "Le Docte Rat".

In Åkeri ("hauler firm"), players assume the roles of lorry drivers transporting goods, mostly fragile or hazardous ones, across a city. Four warehouses contain goods waiting to be evenly redistributed, and deliveries can be made along the bypass that connects the warehouses or through the unpredictable streets of the city centre. Hazards are found in the form of event cards and the blocking vehicles of other players. When a car crashes into a wall or hits another car, a die is rolled to determine whether there's damage to vehicles and goods, which can be costly or even disastrous. Players have the option to purchase insurance policies to mitigate the effect of such misfortunes, or to join into cooperatives with shared assets and some added benefits (the promotion of cooperative ideology being an explicit goal of the producer). The goal is to end up with the most money having made successful deliveries of valuable goods and avoided expenses caused by the destruction of goods or vehicles.

Although the game is nominally for 4-8 players, it would no doubt be possible to play with 2 or 3, optionally with adjustments like having more than one car per player.

The box sides show titles and short captions in Swedish, Norwegian, Danish and Finnish, but the Swedish edition at least (assuming there are others) has a rules sheet in Swedish only.

Zombie Dice 2: Double Feature includes three new dice for Zombie Dice and – true to its name – is two expansions in one package.

The first "movie" on the billing is Big Summer Action Movie with two heroes: the Hunk and the Hottie. The Hunk has a double-shotgun icon, but if you eat his brain, you score double. The Hottie is a hot-pink die with two shotguns and three feet icons, making her fast AND dangerous.

The other offering is Santa Claus Meets the Zombies, with a cheery red-and-white Santa Claus die brimming with brains. The Santa die has three special gift icons: a helmet, an energy drink, and a double brain!

You can also add all three dice to see whether Santa can rescue the heroes in The Direct-to-Video Sequel.

In a future where casinos run the US government, people flock to casinos nightly, betting their lives for the big jackpot. The most played game on the floor: Lucky Lead.

Played a bit like Blackjack (but with mechanics somewhat reminiscent of Russian Roulette), Lucky Lead involves 2-3 players and a dealer*. At each hand, the dealer chooses six Chamber Cards; each one is either Loaded or Unloaded. At each player's turn, the player is dealt one Chamber Card. Before he plays it, he has to choose whether he's going to play it against himself, another player or the dealer. Each action has a different consequence. It's a game of strategy and, most importantly, luck.

Do you have enough skill to hang onto all of your Life Cards, or will Lady Luck cash you out...eternally?


     Dealer is recommended, not required; the game can be played with only two players. Review the rules for details.

Taiwan is a beautiful island also known as "Formosa". It's time for you to travel around the island to explore the spectacular scenery, experience the interesting traditional activities, and taste all kinds of delicious food! By careful planning, you can have your friends to pay the bills for you.

In Hello! Taiwan players are traveling around Taiwan by playing cards in 4 directions starting from the centered card number 8). Each card shows a destination and a serial number (from 1 to 15), which is to be placed following the ascending or descending orders. If you can't play a card with an appropriate number, then you must collect all cards in that direction and pay for the bills shown on each card. The one who spends the least travel expenses is the winner of this fruitful trip.

This game is inspired by the dazzling sights the designer has observed during his bicycle trip around Taiwan. The graphics on the cards are developed from his hand-drawn postcards which shows the beauty of Taiwan in a variety of perspectives.

----------Description in Chinese----------

"禮賀臺灣"這是一套108張紙牌，包含了40種臺灣特色主題的紙牌，每張紙牌都有臺灣特色圖片與文字解說，更可以經由遊戲方式旅遊臺灣，不是單調的紙牌而已。更是一款臺灣旅遊的紀念商品。 
臺灣是一個豐富奇趣的島嶼，因不同的氣候、地理位置與人文風土，在四季節慶的交互掩映下，更衍生出五花八門、精彩繽紛的臺灣特色，不論是民俗廟會、原住民祭典、夜市美食、檳榔文化等，在一座小島裡包羅萬象，豐富多元;讓我們藉由「禮賀台灣」遊戲紀念卡，一起從臺灣頭完到臺灣尾，體驗臺灣的旅遊樂趣。

----------Description in Germany----------

"Hallo, Taiwan!" ist ein Kartenspiel mit 108 Spielkarten, erfunden und gestaltet vom taiwanischen Zeichner AZA. Auf den Karten abgebildet sehen wir 40 verschiedene Motive, die kulturelle Eigenheiten Taiwans widerspiegeln. Jedes Motiv wird durch kurze Texte anschaulich erklärt. Der Spielmechanismus ist weit interessanter als der von herkömmlichen Kartenspielen wie z.B. Maumau und lässt die Spieler auf lustig-leichte, schnelle und doch informative Art die kulturelle Vielfalt Taiwans kennenlernen. Fuer Taiwan-Souvenir-Jäger gilt: Diese Kreation AZA's darf nicht in der Taiwan-Kollektion fehlen!

Integrates with


    Indigenous people of Taiwan

Spaiji is an evolution of TAIJI that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set, as it uses a 4x4 holes board and balls in two colours (white and black).

Rules: The 4x4 board starts empty. Two players, White and Black, take turns placing one ball of each colour adjacent to each other. The balls can be placed on board holes or on top of 2x2 platforms. Notice that you can place a ball on a hole and the other on a platform, as long as they touch each other.

Aim: The player with the largest group of connected balls of his/her colour when the pyramid is full wins. In case of a tie, the second player wins.

Variant for beginners: Consider only visible balls for scoring (not the 5 hidden ones).

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

Soft cover:

http://www.lulu.com/shop/cameron-browne-and-nestor-romeral-andres/shibumi-rule-book/paperback/product-18940123.html

Downloadable PDF:

http://www.nestorgames.com/rulebooks/SHIBUMI_EN.pdf

Objective: 
Game 1: Avoid loosing more balls than your opponent
game 2: Catch three balls, fix them into intended space - 
faster than your opponent
Board with battery powerd flippers, which move the ball into 
random directions
Each player tries to avoid 'goals' (game 1), using a slider
Each player turns the slider (190 °), tries to catch three balls, one for each hole (game 2).
seems that frantic gamers may enjoy it

Description of the game.  Totality is a unique board game that combines the features of a card game with the mechanisms of a board game; it plays in the current era and simulates the total range of the human condition. The heart of the game is the deck of 64 unique and intriguing cards.  There are 4 suits of 16 cards.  These suits represent the 4 planes of existence - physical,  biological, cognitive, and social.  Each plane (suit) has 4 unique elements and a color.  Each card in the suit is composed of a combination of those four elements.  Cards have a number of elements from zero to four.   The mix of elements is shown on each card.  Each card has a game action line.  The game area (world) consists of a 4X4  array of cards laid face up and a space in front of each player to keep sets and action cards.  Players move through the world to gather cards.  Each player is represented by a marker or token.

Object of the game: to build the character with the most experience by gathering sets of cards while moving around the board (world).

Setup.  1)  Shuffle the deck; 2) Deal one card to each player.  The player with the highest card number goes first.  Players keep this card as part of their hand; 3) Then deal the 4X4 world face down.  Before play begins, each player, starting with the first, places his token in the world on a different location.  After the tokens are played, the location cards are turned face up; 4)  Deal 2 more cards to each player for a total of 3. The rest of the deck is placed face down as the draw deck (DD); place it so that the longer side of the cards is next to the world; 5) A discard (recycle) heap (DH) will be created during play; place it so that the shorter side of the cards is next to the world.  Cards are discarded face down.

Play.  Each turn consists of just one of the following actions unless there are effects in play that allow more: 1) Activate or remove a special effect.  The game line of most cards can create effects that change the play.  2) Move.  Movement is in a vertical or horizontal direction only (like a rook in chess).   At the beginning of the game, players can move one location per turn.  3) Gather a card from the world.  Players may gather the card under their token. 4)  Build or dismantle a set.  This is the heart of the game.  A player grows by playing sets in his character area.  Sets consist of one or more cards that satisfy specific criteria from set theory.  5.  Combat.  Combat may occur when a player (attacker) tries to move to or through a site occupied by other players (defenders), or attacks players at his current location. Combat involves betting; outcomes are governed by a simple table.  6) Open a trading session. Any player may  open a trading session his turn. Anything may be traded: token locations, sets, cards, seats, drinks.  There are two ways to end the game.  A player may end the game when he has sets with all 16 elements of the deck present.  Otherwise, the game ends instantly when the draw deck is depleted for the first time.

7 Wonders: Catan is a mini-expansion for the 2011 Kennerspiel des Jahres winner 7 Wonders that pays homage to the SdJ poster child The Settlers of Catan. 7 Wonders: Catan debuted at Spiel 2011 with all funds collected from sale of the expansion being donated to Aktion Deutschland Hilft.

Contents:
1x Wonder cardboard
1x Wonder randomizer card
1x "Klaus Teuber's note (advertisment mostly)
all wrapped in shrinkwrap.

'Bomber über England' (Bombers over England) is like a pinball game that featured a map of England. The map contained holes in the location of key cities such as London, Liverpool, Birmingham and Newcastle, as well as points representing shipping targets in the North Sea.

Players shoot spring-driven metal balls representing "bombs" at these targets and are awarded points for hitting the enemy targets. Players are awarded a maximum 100 points for landing either on London or on the home base of the British fleet at Scapa Flow. Other cities and shipping targets are worth between 20 and 70 points. The game was clearly published during the "Phony War" period of late 1939/early 1940, since Calais in France is also worth 100 points, but neutral Amsterdam and Brussels score -80 points each (and hitting Germany is -100).

As with many games published during the Nazi regime, it was pure propaganda intended to focus players on supporting the war.

For the basic game players look at the movie from DVD and then play the game, they roll the die, draw and answer a question at the current field. Player gets chips for correct answers. Who has 8 chips first, wins.

Learning game with the DVD to learn the many interesting things about the airport and the aircraft are modern, from the perspective and experience of a child. 
Players look at the movie and then play the game, they roll the die, draw a card and answered a question at the current field, Player gets chips for correct answers. Who has 8 chips first, wins.

Learning game with the DVD to learn the many interesting things about the Fire Department, from the perspective and experience of a child. 
Players look at the movie and then play the game, they roll the die, draw a card and answered a question at the current field, Player gets chips for correct answers. Who has 8 chips first, wins.

Scénario is a game about producing movies. It is a trivia game with set collection and negotiation elements

The players' goal is to make and publish movies. Movies are made by collecting a set of matching Actors, Title, Director and Producer cards as well as enough money to produce the movie. Money and cards are collected by moving on the gameboard and answering movie-related questions. A player can only have one card of each type in hand, so if additional cards are collected (for example a second Director card), they can be either discarded or traded with the other players.

Movement on the board uses a roll-and-move mechanic, with a few tweaks, like no "reroll" spaces, and several spaces allow the player to move her game piece anywhere she wants on the board. At first players only have access to the board spaces that allow them to collect cards or money (provided they correctly answer a question). Once a player has at least produced at least one movie, she gains access to all of the gameboard, including spaces which allow players to collect cash from their produced movies.

Once a player has made more movies than other competitors, she can win the game by going to the "Palme d'Or" space and answering a special question. End of game questions are designed to be more difficult.

Scénario won the 1989 "As d'Or" for best simulation game at the Cannes international games festival.
The famous Monopoly now themed around Swabia in southwest Germany. Monopoly game play, only the theme is - like all themed Monopoly versions - resembles the new region.

To make the first words in english very easy, children play the well-known games of lotto or memory or bingo and use 36 picture cards showing english words belonging to one of 9 groups: Family, animals, body, toys, at home, at school, food+drinks, clothes and seasons. Speaking and hearing in this game are very important so a grown-up who speaks English should be present during play.

Kids' Trivia includes 1,500 questions on Art, Science, TV, Movies, History, Famous people, Superheroes, Comics, and Music (in large, easy to read print).

Recommended for kids Ages 7 upwards.
