From the rulebook Designer's note:

"The original version of SC3600 was developed and published by Mariner Games back in 1997. The original launch was accompanied by a small range of miniatures developed by Brigade Models. However the original designers, Patrick Doyle, Mike Tastsides and Doug Glover were serving members of the US armed forces and as such had more pressing demands on their time than playing games, so development of the rules understandably stalled.
[...] We've avoided unnecessary changes to the core rules, being content to tinker around the edges with minor changes to the design system and the inclusion of a hex-less movement system. Our main goal has been to streamline the rules a little without drastically altering the flavour. Probably the one major change has been to do away with the 12-point movement system that allowed movement along hex edges - we abandoned this in our own games very early on. We've also removed the distinction between the Basic and Advanced versions of the game in favour of a single unified set of rules."

The game uses miniatures of starfighters, it is played on a hex map but a set of rules to use hexless maps is also included. The main dice roll used is the 2d6 roll, this is modified by another kinf of dice (ranging from a d4 to a d12) based on the pilot skill. These dice rolls are used to resolve fights and control rolls when your fighter is going spinning around due to damages or wrong manouvers. The damage system is very interesting, being it not the "classic" set of boxes that have to be crossed out but a system that takes in account a series of debilitating damages that during the game, imposing each a number of malus and penalties, can render your fighter inoperative or totally destroyed. Statistics for the official fighter models are in the end of the book.

These are introductory free rules, Brigade Models is going to publish in a near future more complete rules (this time you are going to buy them) that will also include campaign games rules, pilots' skill progression and others more convoluted situations not presented here.

Japanese card game that has numbered sickness cards and numbered medicine cards. When you get sickness and medicine card with same number, you can throw them away. First player to get rid of all hand cards wins the game.

ABOUT THE GAME

Outside the Box™ was designed to be unique in many ways. First of all, the box top or cover actually becomes the game board (we don’t think anyone has ever done this before), hence the name Outside the Box. Secondly, it is a team game (there are very few team games out there) where everyone is actively involved throughout the game. This makes it a great party game, but also gives every single player a chance to be a hero by helping his or her team win. Most importantly, Outside the Box is all skill. There are no dice. Teams can only advance by getting the answers right. It’s very fast. It’s very fun and it’s very, very addictive.

GAME PLAY

Outside the Box™ consists of four types of rounds: Volley Box, Team Play, To Each His Own, and Speed Round. In most rounds, answers are given, in order, A through Z. Teams begin on the Start square with a Volley Box round. The team that wins the Volley Box moves onto the board to the first Team Play space, receives 3 PassCards, and will play the first Team Play round. As your teams progress through the game, you advance through 3 phases, and the 377 categories become more challenging. To win the game, you leave Phase 3, flip over the Start square (which reveals the Winner's Box), and attempt your Final Challenge.

FOUR ROUNDS OF PLAY

With four different rounds and hundreds of categories, it's like playing four games in one...so it's never the same, as it changes each time you play. Here are some examples:

Team Play - You have been chosen as the Captain of your team for this round. The category is: "Musical Groups and Rock Bands." The timer is set for 2 minutes, and the pressure is on! You must go, in order, A through Z, and name a group or band that begins with each letter of the alphabet. Your team may help you, but only by giving you "outside the box" clues. Did you just say, "That's easy?" Ready, set, go!

Volley Box - In this round, teams battle back and forth through the alphabet in a verbal hot potato! The category is: "Things you give, share, or offer to others." The timer is placed away from view and is set for 1 minute. Again, going in order, A through Z, the first team could shout, "Alimony!" The other team might respond with, "Benefit of the doubt!" The first team says, "Condolences!" (or "Cup of sugar!" or "Custody!" or "CPR!" or "Coat!") The team that is stuck when the timer beeps loses the round. Just how far "outside the box" will you go to help your team win?

To Each His Own - The category is picked and 4 letters are randomly chosen from the alphabet. Both teams are given 2 minutes to secretly write down a response for each letter. Try not to match each other. If you're really good, you may just get the chance to "stump the Master!"

Speed Round - You are the "Actor." You have picked a Speed Round card and the timer is set. You must go in order through the alphabet and get your team to say exactly what is on that card. Go ahead, jump around, sing, use props -- but you better not say any part of the words on the card, or "it sounds like" or "rhymes with.." The timer is going... what are you waiting for?

CONTENTS

Included are 377 category cards with thousands of possible answers (which makes the game different each time you play). The Master List is a three year compilation of well over 15,000 possible responses in which you could get the chance to stump the “Master” by coming up with your own “outside the box” answers. Also included is an audio CD featuring the inventor of the game, Rick Sugarman, explaining the rules. There are also live unscripted role playing examples, as well as helpful hints. In addition, the game comes complete with easy-to-follow, learn-as-you-play instructions and quick reference cards, an electronic digital programmable timer (battery included), team game pawns, scoring chips, letter cards, and PassCards.

Each player gets 7 cards. ZOMBIE APOCALYPSE is a Card Game of survival horror where 2-4 players each control groups of Humans and Zombies in an effort to survive the Zombie Apocalypse. Humans are greatly outnumbered, but are able to use Weapons and Refuges for protection. Cards like Zombie Horde and Reinforcements can quickly change momentum, but be especially careful of the Zombie Apocalypse card which always keeps the Humans' chances of survival slim at best. In the end, the player with the most Humans and Zombies left standing (or shamblimg), as well as in their Kill Pile, is declared the winner.
    With various "Theme Decks" available (which pushes the number of unique cards in the game to nearly 300), there is a simplified Deck Building system in place that allows some customization while keeping deck strengths equal. Deck Building is more about "atmosphere" than anything else in ZA.
    Finally, all cards use stunning photography! Each card bleeds creepiness and dark humor unlike anything ever seen in a Zombie card game.

Word on the Street: Expansion 1 includes 216 new double-sided category cards (as well as an additional timer) for use with the Word on the Street base game.

For those not familiar with the game, in Word on the Street, players - either individually or in teams – try to claim letter tiles from the game board.

To set up the game, seventeen letter tiles (all the consonants in English other than j, q, x, and z) are placed in a strip down the center of the game board – the median strip of the street, if you will, which has two "traffic lanes" on either side of it. On a turn, one team is presented with a category such as "types of fruit" or "something a player is wearing", and that team has thirty seconds to come up with an answer in that category, then move the letters in that word toward their side of the game board. Any letters in the word that are not on the game board are skipped. If the answer were "pineapple", for example, the team would move P, N, P, P and L.

If a team moves a letter off the game board, it has claimed that letter and that tile will not move for the remainder of the game. The first team to claim eight letter tiles wins!

Faux•Cabulary: Expansion 1 includes fifteen new word cubes and sixty new cards (with 120 definitions) for use with the Faux•Cabulary base game.

For those not familiar with the game, in Faux•Cabulary, players create new words for descriptions that lack appropriate nomenclature by using randomly drawn "word segment" dice included in the game.

Each round, one player is the Wordmeister while the other players draw three word cubes at random from the box. The Wordmeister reads a Faux•Cabulary card out loud, e.g., "The compulsion to pop bubblewrap", then each player creates a word using 1-3 of his cubes – perhaps "Bust-O-Ific" – and conceals the word in a plastic holder. The Wordmeister mixes the words in their plastic holders (so that she doesn't know who created which word), reveals them one-by-one, then awards the card to whoever submitted the word that (in her mind) best matches the definition. Players then return all the dice, and the Wordmeister position rotates clockwise. Whoever claims the required number of Faux•Cabulary cards first wins!

Faux•Cabulary: Expansion 2 includes twelve new word cubes and sixty new cards (with 120 definitions) for use with the Faux•Cabulary base game.

For those not familiar with the game, in Faux•Cabulary, players create new words for descriptions that lack appropriate nomenclature by using randomly drawn "word segment" dice included in the game.

Each round, one player is the Wordmeister while the other players draw three word cubes at random from the box. The Wordmeister mixes the words in their plastic holders (so that she doesn't know who created which word), reveals them one-by-one, then awards the card to whoever submitted the word that (in her mind) best matches the definition. Players then return all the dice, and the Wordmeister position rotates clockwise. Whoever claims the required number of Faux•Cabulary cards first wins!

Arcanum: The Witch is a small expansion for the Arcanum base game.

When you move a court figure on an empty city area, you gain control of the Witch. When you control the Witch during the "draw cards" phase, you can draw one additional Minor Arcana card – that is, either four Minor Arcana or one Minor Arcana and one Major Arcana – or force all other players to discard a card.

This game was designed to teach children constructive and effective ways to resolve their anger. The cards allow them the opportunity to choose alternatives that can turn anger-inciting situations into more positive encounters. Through repetitive game play, they will learn how to use the new solutions in real life to avoid negative reactions. The object of the the game is for players to resolve as many anger-inciting situations as they can within a 20-30-minute periods. Players get chips for resolving anger situations, and the player with the most chips wins the game.

Contents:
20 People cards
40 Situation cards
40 Solution cards (four sets of 10)
100 Chips
Instructions

Game description from the publisher:
Fight to win in Trial by Combat, the fifth Chapter Pack in the "A Tale of Champions" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. Individuals doomed to die may find champions to take their causes. Valiant heroes seeking to uphold justice may fall to the sword. Killers may become heroes...

The 60 cards (three copies each of 20 different cards) in Trial by Combat expand upon several of the dynamic new themes introduced in A Tale of Champions. Reinforce your ability to forge fleeting alliances with other melee players with new cards providing you and your allies mutual benefits. When the temporary alliances falter, use cards from Trial by Combat to play your opponents against each other. Unique characters and support cards bolster strategies featuring their House's most renowned personalities, and characters possessing the new Melee keyword continue to find clarity in the heat of battle.

description:
Crime & Mystery: Lost Bakerstreet Files provides new exciting stories for clever investigators and is a part of the Crime & Mystery family. They have to solve the mysteries of these stories, which the police could not solve.

The players are cadets of Scotland Yard, who create stories about one of the prime suspects in a murder case using "evidence" cards. On the back of the card, the players decode a question with a red foil. If you answer these questions correctly, it will lead you to the murderers.

Crime & Mystery: Lost Bakerstreet Files is the sequal to the uncommon und innovative criminal game Crime & Mystery: Bakerstreet Files. The players have to listen closely and remember and combine the facts in the right way to find the murder and win the game.


Neue Fälle der ganz anderen Art erwarten die Ermittler. Es gilt, Fälle zu lösen, welche die Polizei selbst nicht lösen konnte und nun zu den Akten legen will. Doch nicht, wenn es nach dem Willen des scharfsin nigsten Ermittlerteams von ganz London geht.

In Crime & Mystery: Lost Bakerstreet Files, der Fortsetzung des ungewöhnlichen Kriminal-Spiels Crime & Mystery: Bakerstreet Files muss man genau zuhören können, messerscharf kombinieren und braucht ein erstklassiges Gedächtnis, um anhand der Indizien und Hinweise, den oder die tatsächlichen Täter zu überführen. Lost Bakerstreet Files kann separat oder zusammen mit den Bakerstreet Files gespielt werden.

A new murder at East Highway. There is no time to lose before the hints are lost. Due to new methods there are new ways to discover the murder for the investigators. Find root of hairs, fingerprints and autopsy dead bodies.

The players are cadets of Scotland Yard, who create stories about one of the prime suspects in a murder case using "evidence" cards. On the back of the card, the players decode a question with a red foil. If you answer these questions correctly, it will lead you to the murderers.

Crime & Mystery: Modern Investigation is a deductive game for 3 to 6 investigators. It is a part of the Crime & Mystery family and is based on the mechanism of Crime & Mystery: Bakerstreet Files. It puts the investigation into the setting of CSI in the USA.


Schon wieder ein Mord am East Highway. Wie immer gilt es, keine Zeit zu verlieren. Die Spuren müssen gesichert werden, bevor sie verloren gehen. Dank den neuesten Methoden der Wissenschaft, hat sich das Gesicht der Ermittlungen in solchen Fällen geändert. Haarwurzeln, Fingerabdrücke, Blutproben, Insektenlarven und präzise Autopsien ermöglichen eine ganz neue Form der Verbrechensbekämpfung. Und auch eine neue Art von Ermittlern.

Crime & Mystery: Modern Investigation ist ein deduktives Spiel für 3-6 Ermittler, das auf dem Spielmechanismus von Crime & Mystery: Bakerstreet Files beruht und die Ermittlungen in das Setting der Crime Scene Investigation der USA führt.

Tinker-bell game.

Players try to get rid of all of their glass balls. First a player draws a card with a number or a simple calculation like 3-1, and then the player throws a die which shows either - or +. If the die shows +, player puts as many glass balls as was written on the card on the flower in the middle of the board. If the die shows - player takes glass balls from the flower. When the flower falls down, player has to take all the fallen glass balls.

The aim of the game is to collect all 5 of your rescue cards by remembering where they are.

There is a big spinner in the middle of the table and players spin Fireman Sam as hard as they can!
If he stops by your marker, listen carefully to what is said.

Depending what Fireman Sam said player draws one or two rescue cards and if it is his hero on that card he takes it on his hand or if it's not puts it back in the circle around spinner.
But sometimes Norman is in trouble! Now player have to give back randomly one of his rescue card.

The first player to collect all their 5 hero cards wins!

Objective: Using the detailed street map of Mainz & Wiesbaden each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

The year is 2450. The players, who represent different universities from around the world, will travel five hundred years back in time to the 20th century, where they will plan and deliver one of the great advances in that century. Each of these advances has some requirements on personnel, materials and funding. During the game players take turns to play cards on the table (while keeping some cards to achieve their secret agendas); the cards played on the table help in meeting the requirements, but as more cards are on the table there are more probabilities of problems arising in the common research (at the end of each turn a number of dice is rolled depending on the number of cards on the table, and the result of the dice roll sometimes forces to remove some cards). After 10 turns, players win if they have reached the requirements, and one of the players will be the top winner if he/she has also reached his/her secret agenda. If the players have not reached the requirements, they collectively lose.

Inventum is a game of collaboration that highlights the role universites play in transferring knowledge to the community at large and hereby contributing to our economic and social well-being.

The game is published with the support of the University of Barcelona and the Fundació Bosch i Gimpera, from an original proposal by Marga Becerra and Mariona Ferrer.

Point Blank is a brand new squad level skirmish system for 1:1 modern tactical firearm combat. Players are in charge of a squad or several fire teams and control the individual actions of their soldiers, weapon teams, vehicles or support weapons. The rules are focused on the tactical concepts of fire and maneuver, command and control, and morale as well as training and experience. Any period of modern firearms combat can be simulated, from The Great War all the way to today and beyond.

The Point Blank book contains everything you need to run games with infantry, artillery, and vehicles as well as rules for spotting and hiding, as well as night, weather, smoke, and fog. There are three major periods represented for the army lists in the book: WWII (which includes Germany, U.S., Soviet, British, French, and Polish. Vietnam (which includes U.S., ARVN, Australian, NVA, and VC. As well as modern forces for the Global War on Terror or any other modern hot spot (which includes U.S., British, Mujahideen, and Insurgent). Rounding out the book are three scenarios, each one concentrating on one of the periods covered in the book and focusing on a tactical concept. The missions include Combat Patrol, Ambush, and a VIP Snatch. There is lots more besides in this 94 page rulebook to keep players busy and gaming.

Somewhere in Europe, near a big sea, there is a small village called Leiuatajateküla (Inventors Village), where it is thought as honor to be able to build and invent your own stuff. Every year they have a competition for inventions.
Who is the best inventor in the city? Maybe someone who can build a plane from paper and a string or a tractor from a old trumpet. Join in to take part in the best ever competition that ever was. This is a fast and tricky board game for 2 to 5 little inventors and fans of Lotte (Estonian animation), in where you have a lot of fun and no one will get hurt. Game fits for smaller and larger players, for academic and for school kids. Who wouldn't want to adventure with rabbit Adalbert and cat Bruno?
Be the first to finish your invention and score most with it to win the game.

With this game, children learn about geography, particularly topography. On the game board an illustrated map of the world is depicted. The continents are each in a different color, so they can be distinguished easily. The countries are easy to find by numbers and colors. At the edge of the board flags are shown.

The game contains 110 illustrated cards with questions (and answers) such as: Where do polar bears live? Where is the Eiffel Tower located? Background information, continents, countries and people pass by. Players have to respond the correct country, continent or ocean and have to designate them on the world map. By doing this correctly chips can be earned. The first player who has collected 8 chips wins the game.

The rules also describe a second way of playing. Here 24 random cards are put face up in the middle of the game board. The players take turns throwing a die and moving their pawn in accordance on the flags around the game board. They have to collect cards by selecting cards from the middle which are related to the country their pawn landed on. If no such a card is available, the player just has bad luck. Once a player reaches the last field, the player with the most cards wins.

WELTRAUMFAHRT is a roll-and-move dice game which was published around 1960.
What makes it special is the highly attractive artwork and the beautiful playing pieces shaped as small astronauts, which glow in the dark...

The Trouble with Rose is a co-operative story telling game with role playing and hand management.  Players can collaboratively build a world, use an existing setting, or select one from a number of play sets.

Components: A standard deck of playing cards and a set of double six dominoes are required for play.  Note taking materials for player created worlds and characters is suggested if a play set is not used.

Game Play: Each player is dealt a hidden agenda and either creates a character on the fly or uses one from the selected play set.  Players then pull four to five dominoes (depending on the number playing.)  The game begins with playing one of the dominoes from your hand and creating a scene based on the pips and their matching values for that character.  After the scene, the remaining people at the table can embellish the scene and vote to determine if the player earned the points on the domino and can add it to their score.  Play continues around the table until all the dominoes in hand are used.  At that time, the hidden agendas are revealed and any bonus points are awarded.  High score wins and gets to describe the fate of the players and  any NPCs that were created.

Theme: TTwR started out as a Game Chef 2011 entry and was expanded and polished after the contest.  The theme for GC'11 was Shakespeare and TTwR is designed to create character driven stories that tend to take tragic and comedic turns.  Game play is wide open, but the rules provide the structure for story telling among strangers and play testing/con games have shown that the system delivers on the fun.

The game board depicts a rug with three connected, diamond-shaped areas of 3 x 3 fields each. The goal is to get as many of one's own playing pieces to the far end of the board.
Each player has five coins. In each turn, a player can move a coin one field forward. When a coin can reach one of the opponent's coins, it climbs on top of that. This is called a Biri, it can move two fields, but not eliminate further coins. It can be caught by a single coin of the opponent. The resulting "Biribi" can move three fields and can't be eliminated. 
When a Biri or Biribi reaches the far end of the board, opponent's coins are discarded, one's own coins are scored. Whoever managed to get most coins across is the winner.

In Zombies ate my Brains (ZAMB) you are a newly created Zombie just trying to get some fresh human brains to keep you in peek undead form. Those pesky living ones however seem to want you dead, for real this time. Can you eat their brains before they destroy your rotting body? Find out if you have the right stuff come the Zombie Apocalypse.

ZAMB is a solo card game in which you have 2 decks, one is your play deck and the other is the Humans. You also have a set of cards to represent you the Zombie- which as you take damage changes your ability to draw cards and also effects your defence and offensive capabilities. Gameplay involves managing whatever the humans throw at you and using your hand/deck you try to defeat the game as more and more threats come into play. Picking the wrong option is deadly to the undead.

The Zombies over run the world and win if there are no more human cards left to play and yet your undead minion is still in (at least) one piece.

The game is a fast playing light card game which can also  be played as a 2 player format with one player taking control of the Humans. In solo play the game rarely goes over 10 minutes play time.

boardgame: Moth Eating Catz Destroyed My Broadband
description:
In Moth Eating Catz Destroyed My Broadband you are desperately trying to gain as much buzz as possible so people notice your spamchat and gain the win for the net, if only for your 15 seconds of fame.

Using advanced router technology you generate buzz but have to use moths and catz to deploy your assets to take down your fellow chatters. In this fast and fun game you will lolz, cry and win.

Remember its not the playing but the crushing your enemies that counts

This game is a random, light and chaotic game which contains- Moths, lolz Cats and random events as well as heavy dose of bad humour. The point of the game is to protect your router whilst generating buzz which in turn grants you Victory points. However you are limited in the actions you can take and will have to fight off the other players.

The game was inspired by random comments on the BGG chat feed for Essen 2011. It therefore is meant to evoke the random, bizarre and downright silly things that occur in chat feeds to live events when to many geeks gather together.

For untold centuries a war has been waged in the shadows cast by the moon. Two great powers of the underworld clash in repetitive fury, fighting for ultimate supremacy. The hedonistic Vampires believe it to be their birthright to own the night. The untamed Werewolves believe that none other than themselves should  master the Earth. Whose claim is true? When twilight descends, which species will set the ancient records in stone and prove their powers of domination? Time to settle old scores. It’s time to Bare Your Fangs!

Bare Your Fangs is a two-player dice fighting game devoted to resolving once and for all which species will reign supreme: Vampire or Werewolf. Players choose their faction dice and roll to attack! The fully customized die icons determine successful hits, blocks, and of course, one’s ability to hunt for humans and thereby heal wounds incurred in these savage battles. But there is always risk. Some humans won’t go down without a fight! And one particular human seeks only the total annihilation of all creatures of the night.  The Werewolf’s physical power is unmatched but the Vampire’s ability to heal is legendary. Only one will rule when dawn breaks, but will the victor crush his opponent into oblivion and take his rightful place as Leader Eternal, or will the fang fray destroy them both!

The Hatter’s Table is board game about discovering the hidden and wonderfully strange rules that govern an apparently irrational situation.  Set around the Mad Hatter’s table, players must dash around the Wonderland tea party while trying to figure out what secret rules govern the other players' movement.  The Mad Hatter character serves as both an audio guide and aid to chaos.

How do you play?

Each player has a secret rule.  When play begins, the Mad Hatter shouts out instructions about where to move around the table: "Go sit at a tea cup," "Take a seat next to the March Hare," "Move one place counter-clockwise."  Players move their tokens accordingly, unless they find themselves in a situation where they must follow their secret rule instead.

Your goal is to guess what secret rules the other players are following.  If you can do so successfully, you are rewarded with buttons, and the first player to get fifteen buttons wins the game!

At first, the game might seem like madness, but once you get the hang of it you'll learn to use some strategy.  Watch the other players carefully and take note whenever they disobey the Hatter's direction - that's when you know they must be following their rule.  Each place around the table has two different states, and every state has two positions around the table.  When the Hatter tells you to sit next to the March Hare, a clever player will pick the spot next to the Hare that will best hide their secret rule.

Spears action game.

THe board has 8 sections, with a goal at the appropriate at every alternate side. If two play, opposite sections are played. If four play, then all goal-sections are used.

Each player has a 'puffer', a miniature bellows that, when pressed, ejects a stream of air. The object is to 'blow' the ball around the board, trying to get it into an opponent's goal.

The first to score 6 goals is the winner.

Objective: collect as many bonus points as possible

- roll & move game with action cards; moving by turn -
Each player tries to roll & move, picking up cards, helping enviroment and moving his/her stone along the scoring board/row
for more involved detectives (age 10..) a 'tracing set' can be added

Content:
1  board
5  wooden meeples (= 'Öko-detectives') 
5  wooden stones (scoring)
1  special 6-sided wooden die
30 wooden chips
26 event cards
12 cards for environmental sins
8  action slides
5  tracing-set-cards
1  rules

Miniature rules for tactical scale.
Science fiction infantry battles.

Space Man-2-Man was designed to reproduce large scale, tactical level Science Fiction miniature battles. The scale of the game is 1" equal 6 feet and each 'turn' for a combat group taking from 30 seconds to a few minutes. The game is designed with 25mm figures, but should be easy to covert the charts, movement rates and playing surface size to suit another scale if necessary.

"In Search of Eldorado" (In search of Eldorado) is a strategy game involving 2 teams (expeditionaries and indigenous) that can be composed of 1, 2 or 3 players each

The expeditionaries mission is a sea voyage aboard their galleons, from its port to the land of the Indians.

There are 3 pyramids spread over the territory of these.

Below one of the 3 pyramids is Eldorado. It'll be known by the brand that bears on the base. The only ones who know which of the pyramids is marked are the Indians.

The aim of the expedition will be to find and seize the pyramid that contains Eldorado and take it to the port where the expedition departed.

The Indians will be guarding the 3 pyramids, thus hindering the expeditionaries discovering which of them is Eldorado.

His other mission will be hunt down and eliminating the expeditionaries as soon as they land.

If any of the expeditionaries manages to find and grab the pyramid of Eldorado, the Indians will use all means at its disposal by land and sea to recover and return the pyramid of Eldorado to their place of origin.

The members of each team will decide among them wich piece they move each turn.

Severed Union 1861-1865 is a historical miniature wargame, which is the first release from Gordon & Hague War-Games.  The game uses 10mm pre-painted wargame miniatures to allow players to relive the greatest battles of the American Civil War.  This game is a turn based in which each player will take turns moving, firing and charging.  There are multiple ways to win according to what style of game you are playing.  There are "take and hold" battles where you must capture key objectives around the battlefield in a specified amount of turns or simply play a "total destruction" game where you must destroy the enemy and run them from the tabletop!

The miniatures are 10mm in scale and pre-painted and are ready for battle right out of the box.  The Severed Union Rulebook is free to download from our the Gordon & Hague website.  There is an advanced 40 page rulebook for veteran war-gamers and there is also a 4 page quick-start rulebook for newcomers and children.

The game mechanics are based mainly around dice rolling and measuring.  Maneuvering your units is also very important as you receive bonuses for being on high-ground and in or behind protective cover such as fences, buildings and forests.

description:
The war is over.  Humanity has regained control.  The zombies have been rounded up and contained.  Time for people to get on with their lives.  But there's no TV; no internet; society needs time to rebuild.  What will the masses do for entertainment?

Zombie Roller Derby.  In this game, you take the role of a roller derby team manager.  Your team consists of "trained" zombies; your goal is to win the race, and maybe kill a few zombies along the way.

Race your zombies along the derby track.  Play cards to urge them forward or to fight other zombies.  Avoid the ire of the crowd, or stir them up on purpose.  Play to win; to kill zombies; or to incite a riot.  Just don't let the zombies get out of their cages...

It's supposed to be the so called "Reparations - Zeppelin" LZ 126, which obviously inspired the artist to draw this box cover

This is actually a yacht racing game - not an air race despite the cover.  Players sail from New York and the first player who lands in London wins. Each port has a number which matches an event on the board.  Players must perform each event as they land on each spot.   Rules are pasted on the inside of the box and are in English, French and Spanish.

GB Racers: The Ultimate Car Racing Board Game (Second Edition)

Strap in for the ride of your life in this fast-paced Formula 1 simulation board game.

Appleton Games proudly presents GB Racers (Golden Ball Racers), a fast-paced game inspired by F1 Racing.  This exciting new F1 Racing board game infusing the ‘Appleton Way’ a sporting chance of success for everyone, every time.

boardgame: Shadows Upon Lassadar: Sorrow of Salilth
description:
Sorrow of Salilth is a microgame for one or two players lasting about 20 minutes. When playing a 2 player game, one player assumes the role of the Council of Wardens, trying to defend the city of Salilth from the servants of the Grayking and close four barrier gates within the city. If the four gates are closed or all of the Servant units are defeated, Salilth is saved and the Warden Player wins.

The other player controls the forces of the Grayking trying to overwhelm Salilth and reach the Citadel of the Wardens where they attempt to place 10 Siege Corruption tokens within the Citadel, If they are successful, or can defeat all of the Warden Units, Salilth is lost and the Grayking Player wins.

When playing the solitaire variant, the player assumes the Council of Wardens role.

Needed: one 2-page hex map, 1 rule sheet, 1 unit counter sheet and 1 sheet of nomograph tables.

Setup: The Warden Player places all of their units inside the Citadel, stacked by type.
The player should also take the 4 Barrier Gate tokens, placing them within reach. They then place the marker on the Sparkblades icon on the Spell Rondel.

The Grayking Player divides their units into 6 stacks each with 6 unit counters. These stacks may be configured as desired by the player. They then place one stack on each of the 6 numbered zones outside the city. The player should also take the 10 Siege Corruption Tokens, placing them within reach.

Play: Each round of the game has 4 phases: Movement, Combat, Damage, Siege. Then a new round begins. The Grayking Player always goes first each phase, followed by the Warden Player.

Win: If all 4 gates are closed or all of Servant units are defeated, Salilth is saved and the Warden Player wins. If 10 Siege Corruption tokens are placed in the Citadel or all Warden Units are defeated, the Grayking Player wins.

Solitaire Variant: At game start, randomly sort the Grayking units into the 6 stacks of 6 units each, and place each stack randomly on each of the 6 numbered starting zones. During the Movement Phase, the player will move the Grayking units based on the rules as outlined above. Movement will always occur towards the Citadel and units will always form a larger group if there is a path choice. When splitting 4 or more units, always move units in order of the largest Spirit Strength first. During the Siege Phase, one Siege Corruption Token is added each round as long as any Servant unit occupies the Citadel.

This game uses nomographs instead of look-up tables and was designed to use a minimum number of components.

Sorrow of Salilth is a prequel to the Print and Play game - Shadows Upon Lassadar. A further game - Revenge of the Raven Consort - will complete the game trilogy.

Winner of the 2011 BGG Microgame contest

A game published in junction with the student carnival "Lundakarnevalen 2010". The game is a word-game, where players try to descibe words without mentioning specifics (very similar to Alias).

Players take turn to try to describe the words on the card, each one with a specific theme, without mentioning the specific word or part of the word. The other players, teammates or opponents depending on the variant, try to guess as many as possible in a set period of time; and players score accordingly. First one to a set target of points is the winner.

What sets the game apart from Alias is the distinct tongue-in-cheek "lundahumor", i.e. humour from the student city of Lund. The themes and words on the different cards are often odd, screwball or intergroup.

In addition to the original rules, several variants is included in the base-game.

"They are hungry!
The only way to safety is to climb up. . . or is it?"

Dexterity based game in which your zombies are trying to reach the human of the other player. Be careful, or your zombies will tumble down and they'll have to make their way up again.

Game is simple: On the player's turn he or she gets a randomised zombie which must be placed against the opponent's wall. The goal is to get the pile high enough to touch the human. Players place one zombie at the time and try to catch the opponent's human before their own human is zombie food.

Objective: Collect as many cheese cake pieces as possible by moving around the board
 - moving/acting by turn -
each player has to collect cheese cake pieces for Diddle. You can achieve this if you have to draw a question card => give the right answer  or
if you draw an action card => perform required action properly
If all players are back in Diddls' cave the one who gained most of the pieces wins

Rules for game: First, place the animals (Elephant, Alligator, Camel, Zebra, Lion and Goat) on the board using set up rules , then 1st player rolls dice and then can do any of 3 things.

1) Place hunter on the board and then move him the total of both dice

or

2) place hunter on board move him total of 1 die then place second hunter on board and move him total of 2nd die

or

3) start hunter move him total of 1 die then place peg on board horizontally or vertically the distance away from hunter equal to the 2nd die.

Object of the game is to continue to make those die rolls and try and to surround the animals. Game ends when all animals have been caught. Scoring is as follows: Elephant, Alligator and Camel= 4 points. Zebra and Lion = 3 points and Goat = 2 points.

User Summary

Slapjack is a simple children's boardgame that appears to be related to, but distinct from, the Slap Jack card game...

At the beginning of the game all 44 cards are dealt face down to the players. (These are kept face down during the course of the game.) Players use a spinner to move cardboard pawns around a board. Each space has an instruction for the player to follow. Some spaces ask players to perform physical actions (which have no real effect on game play). Some spaces ask players to give or take cards from other players. This is significant because the object of the game is to collect all the cards. If you run out of cards you are eliminated.

There are a few strategically placed Slapjack spaces on the board. If someone lands on one of these spots, then each player takes a card at random (again all cards are kept face down at all times) and puts it in the center of the board. If someone puts down a Slapjack card, then the first player that slaps the card wins the hand.

There is a variant game listed in the rules where players use just the game cards and play a single card down one at a time. If a Slapjack card is played, then the first player to slap the card wins the hand. The last player with any cards wins. (This is essentially the Slap Jack card game, albeit with a smaller  than standard deck.)

Straddle is a skill-action party game played on a giant planisphere (5 ft x 6 ft plastic playing mat) and combines a fun mix of practical and observational astronomy. Playing like Hasbro’s classic Twister game, the object is to touch a star or constellation using only your feet or hands and be the last player not to touch any other body part to the mat.

Each player stands at a corner of the mat in socks or bare feet. The umpire spins the spinner and calls out the body part and the star or constellation, for example, left hand Orion. The first player puts his or her left hand on the constellation Orion. Using the supplied star charts, the umpire can help a player determine what stars are in the constellation. Play continues to the next player.

If a player touches the mat with any body part except a hand or a foot, that player is eliminated. Be the last player not to touch any other body part to the Straddle mat to win the game.

An abstract game on a 5x6 grid of African origin. It is similar to Dara with a few exeptions.

Each player has 12 pieces (stones, bids, etc.) of his colour. During initial set-up players take turns to place their pieces on empty squares of the board, two pieces (one piece in Dara) at a time. They must avoid 'three in a row' orthogonally during this phase.

In the second phase players move their pieces to adjacent squares trying to form 'three in a row' (diagonals don't count). After making 'three in a row' a player removes an enemy piece which is adjacent to either side of this row (that's another difference to Dara). A player loses the game when only 2 pieces of his colour remain on the board.

After the retirement of captain "White Beard", the crew of a pirate ship decides do rotate positions. Each round, a new player becomes captain and pick roles for everybody else. In order, the pirates take a treasure (pearls, diamonds, rubys, emeralds or rum barrels) from a hand of cards. Precious stones translate into victory points and rum barrels confer "special powers" to the players who hold them. But not all treasures will be banked safely - Spanish, French and English pirate-hunters will force the crew to discard some cards along the game.

description: "Juegos olimpicos" (Olympic Games) is a game for the whole family, consisting of different Olympic events (marathon, long and high jump, hammer throw, sprint race...) wich take place in different boardgames, each with it's own rules and mechanics. After each event, the winners receive individual medals (gold, silver and bronze) and at the end of the Olympics, after having competed in all events (having played in all the boardgames), the one who has amassed the largest number of medals, taking into account its value (a gold medal is worth more than two bronze), is declared the winner.

Empires and Generals is a Print and Play Collectible Card Game with free content available from EmpiresandGenerals.com.

description:
Description:
Players control the fate of several regions in the Alpine arc (Rhône-Alpes, Piedmont, the cantons of Uri and Tessin, Tyrol, South Tyrol, Carinthia and Slovenia). Each two of these regions are connected by the Alpine crossings of Mt. Cenis/Fréjus, the Gotthard, the Brenner Pass and Karawanken. At each round, traffic increases. Within ten rounds, regions must try to contain the consequences of traffic by taking actions in the fields of economy, tourism and the environment as well as by implementing measures for transport regulation.

Aim of the game:
The player with the most intact region at the end of round 10 - that is, the player who has obtained the highest scores in Environment, Economy and Tourism and therefore has managed to govern the traffic volume - is the winner.

Course of the game:
The game is played over 10 rounds, in which the following three stages are repeated:
1. Drawing of traffic and event cards and execution of tasks
2. Investment of money
3. Determination of consequences.

Regions can invest only the money they have collected. Players can decide to impact on Tourism, Economy or the Environment and move the corresponding game piece of one field per money invested.
Also, they can play the action cards with traffic regulating measures. The prices for these actions are indicated on the corresponding cards. Some cards can only be played with other regions. 
Other cards affect the traffic in neighboring regions and therefore these players must find the right countermeasures to control this new "unwanted" traffic otherwise the situation get`s out of control and the environment is ruined loosing many points.

description:
A simple game, rather like Snakes and Ladders but without snakes or ladders.

The board has 100 squares and the object is to be the first to reach square 100.

This must have been a free game sent by post because the board is paper and the teetotum needs to be cut out to act as the die. And you can cut out 'figures' for playing pieces.

Boolean is an abstract strategy game that uses the concepts of Boolean Logic to play. At first created as a learning tool by College Professor Edu Jacober, Boolean was published in 2011 by Ceilikan Jogos. It is now out of print.

Boolean is actually a game system and comes with two distinct sets of rules. The board represent a logic circuit with boolean operations that can be changed by playing cards on the game board.

Basic rules: Players are either black or white (0 or 1) and, by setting the logic gates on the game board, try to get their team to score more points. In your turn you can: a) buy a card, b)play a card. Circuit inputs are driven by a FIFO line

Advanced rules: Players are also either black or white and, by setting logic gates on the game board, try to get their team to score more points. In your turn you can: A) buy a card, b) play a card, c) feed a free input with a piece from your score track. Circuit inputs are copied from gameboard latches existent on more valuable locations in the gameboard.

Players try to repair holes in their umbrella before rain.
Each umbrella has 5 different colours, player throws a dice, if the dice shows a colour that player doesn't already have he gets to put a counter on that colour, if the dice shows grey a rain cloud is put on the table. When there are five rain clouds, the game ends. Any player who has got five different colours before that wins the game.
"Can you ride skateboard - Only when I'm sitting in the bus." This fun party question and answer game for any number of children provides 2700 possible combinations of questions and answers.

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – Written and Bound Asylum Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The quest for a deeper understanding of reality permeates Written and Bound, the first Asylum Pack from the upcoming "Revelations" cycle for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game.

While a few sensitive minds shudder at the darker powers they perceive, the bulk of humanity goes about its daily business blissfully unaware of the terrible powers that slowly dig their claws into the very heart of society. Cultists and servitors worship the dread Ancient Ones and seek to extend their influence on earth. Secret societies conduct dark rituals in hidden chambers beneath public notice, criminals buy and sell eldritch relics on the black market, and otherworldly creatures flit through the dimensions, existing beyond the realm of human perception.

Written and Bound introduces 60 new cards (three copies each of 20 cards) that reinforce the power of knowledge and the importance of knowledge seekers in the Call of Cthulhu mythos. New Tome cards, a Library, Criminals, Scientists, Investigators, and Relic Hunters all provide the different factions powerful new ways to use knowledge to manipulate the world to their own ends – or to liberate it from the Ancient One's ever-tightening grasp.

Variation of DKT with a satirical theme. Money is transferred to the Bahamas and Liechtenstein, in the end all Tickets are sold to the state and the rest of the money in the bank - that still exist - all players divided, whoever has the most money wins.

A dexterity game in which players roll a die to select a letter then they must add that letter to the accumulating tower of letters on an unstable platform - without knocking down the tower.  If you knock down the tower then you get all the letters that were in the tower.  The first person to use all of their letters wins.

There is a journey along a path of hazards and barriers and
players "cooperate" creatively to innovate solutions that let them get home together.

The first path card is laid on the middle of the table and every player plays one tool card from the hand and explains how it could be used to overcome the obstacle on the path card. Then the all players together discuss about the solutions and decide which are the best or how to obsatcle is solved with those tools. If the solution have found all tools are discarded and the next path board is laid out for next round.

There are options for length of play and alternate rules to extend interest in the game. For 2 to 5 players.

Contents:

A portable, magnetic roll-and-move maze game for two players. The object of the game is to escape the graveyard. To do this, you'll have to navigate the graveyard maze and reach the Gravestalker's house in the center of the board. Once there, you may claim the key that will let you escape. But players will also have a chance to move the Gravestalker, in an effort to block their opponent's path. Also watch out for skull spaces on the board, which can send you further away from your goal.

Once you have the key, it's a mad dash to the finish. The first player out wins.

description: Slasher Movie is a game of Survival Horror, Detection, and Betrayal for 2-6 Players. In Slasher Movie, you play as a character in your very own horror movie. In 5150: New Beginnings you're thrust into a bustling, living urban environment hundreds of years in the future. Or maybe it's just a place far, far away. In any case, your goal is to take your characters; we call them Stars, as far as they can go. Acquire wealth, fight crime, commit crime, or something in between; it's all up to you. Want to be an interplanetary smuggler or how about a bounty hunter? Yep, you can be that. Want to feel the rush of jacking up your body with the latest technology and running on the edge. The more players the more challenge, as the space to tilt your pyramid is limited...

The primary game mechanic is a "Move/Effort" system, in which 2 eight-sided dice are rolled, and one is used for movement and the other for actions. The goal of the game is to completely remove the dark forces by closing all the gates and destroying all the monsters. Over the course of the game, various things (the biggest being rolling doubles at any time) cause the doom track to fill up. The players need to remove the dark forces before the track fills, or they all lose.

Title means in English: "We play government!", game was released in 1952.
It was far ahead of its time and with an outstanding equipment.
It was a two-part-game played on both sides of the gameboard:
The first part was a roll-and-move game, but it had many twists to change the later part of the game. 
This second part was played on the backside of the board, on a chess-like pattern, divided in 3 color-coded zones.
Not only that the first part decided which chess-figures you had to play with, no, the figures could be equipped with different heads, depending on which party-side you participated.
Winner was the first party to fill 3 squares at the end of the board.
As you would believe, the color-coded zones played a prominent role in moving and capturing, the rules were really interesting, but an awful lot to learn.
Fascinating stuff from the early German post-war era...

From Wargamedownloads.com


Blasters & Bulkheads utilizes the popular Goalsystem game engine to allow players to run Space Opera skirmish battles in a far-flung future. Build a crew of adventurers and ne'er do wells, or command a kill-squad of Galactic Hegemony enforcers. Rules for combat, campaigns, character advancement and more allow for story-driven campaigns, and four pre-built scenarios allow players to jump right into the action. Written for both 15mm and 28mm miniatures, B&B requires only a handful of figures for each side. Complete character creation rules let players build exactly the type of fighters they want, and rules for the mysterious Essence allow for the existence of strange powers. Prime your blaster, draw your energy sword, and battle for the fate of the galaxy's future!

Money Mania is a stock-speculating boardgame which is completely depending on the luck of 4 different specially designed dice.
The game can be played as a basic game, where each turn a player rolls the two Company Dice and the two Share Dice to see which of the four Companies he may trade in and how many blocks of shares may be traded (each dice is marked 1, 2 or 3). When shares are bought or sold the price goes up or down one unit on the central gameboard for every block of shares traded. As the share price changes players attempt to buy low and sell high in order to make the most profit.

In the advanced game players can expand their possibilities - take out loans and put options. Various random events, such as market trends are also introduced through the use of event cards.

What adds a little fun is the fact, that you can decide what actions you will take with the different results...
Winner is the first player to amass 1.000.000 credits.

This game is only playable with a tiptoi, a 'smart' pencil with an integrated mp3 player, that reacts when you point it on enabled products like this game.

In this game the players have to solve tasks given by the tiptoi, by pointing the tiptoi at the right pictures, letters or words on the game components.

The games designed to help kids get more familiar with letters. Some sample categories of tasks given by the game:
- Find two pictures, beginning with the same letter
- Decide if a given letter is part of a given word
- Construct a word out of a few given letters
- Find a picture with the right rhyme
- ...

The game has 3 difficulty settings, and is a good way to familiarize kids with their letters, who are still struggling with reading. Once their reading skill has reached a decent level, this game will get boring.

Merrills-meets-memory game where players try to eliminate stones of the opponent by getting three of their own pieces in a row. All pieces, though, are hidden, and all of them have the same figure on their backs: initially the orientation shows which figure is which but after a couple of turns players will have moved, swapped and twisted the pieces often enough to confuse anyone without a very reliable memory.

To add insult to injury, some special pieces (Horus falcons) can be uncovered, with fatal consequences for either player (depending on the situation).

Move Out (1971, ADA, $1.50), by Tom Kaeter, simulates man-to-man combat in Western Europe in 1944. It pits all or part of an American infantry platoon (31 men, 31 counters) against a German platoon (32 men, 32 counters). Each side has one of five possible missions as they enter the board from opposite sides. Upside down placement and blank counters are used to add the fog of war. The terrain includes brush, hills, woods, a creek, a pond and two small buildings, one with several rooms. The men have their separate weapons and also a number of individual differences. Some can see better, some run faster, some are sharp-shooters, and others experts at hand to hand combat. Unfortunately, it is a very unfinished game. The rules are hard to figure out and some are almost impossible to carry out. Things are particularly difficult when fighting develops around the buildings. The game is an offshoot of Terry Griner's Patrol, published as a set of rules in Strategy & Tactics Nr. 7, and reading the rules in that source does help to figure out Move Out. Furthermore, the reproduction of the rules and map is quite poor. There are no counters, only pictures of counters and the pictures are hard to decipher. In spite of its faults however, Move Out offers something unusual and worthwhile for anyone who wants to investigate small unit tactics through a game. (Moves Magazine description)

Robofactory is a zero-luck business simulation economic game about manufacturing robots around the world.

The game is modular and based on a large number of different and unique contract cards with different types of order.

The rules come with many different scenarios for players to decide on the length of the game that ranges from introductory games (60 minutes), quick games (90 minutes), normal games (120 minutes) and long games (180 minutes). Players are also encouraged to set up their own scenarios by combining the many contract cards available (please note that playing with ALL available contract cards will turn the game into epic and last a weekend).

Hexenhaus is a simple dice-driven game for children. One player is the witch, all others are knights. The witch tries to capture the knights, while the knights try to rescue a princess imprisoned in the witch's cottage and bring her back to the castle without being caught by the witch.

The Siege of Budapest, 1945 - With the issuance of Order Number 11 on 1 December 1944 declaring Budapest to be a "fortress" city, Hitler effectively sealed the fate of 70,000 defending Axis troops and approximately 800,000 civilians.

Festung Budapest, the eighth Historical ASL module, takes players to the western side of the Hungarian capital. Seventeen scenarios and three Campaign Games cover the fighting on the Buda side of the Danube just west of the Castle Hill area during the period from 1 January through 10 February while representing the bitter struggle for the heart of the city.

Festung Budapest also comes with 4 maps and 9 counter sheets. In addition to the entire German SS MMC order of battle (from 8-3-8 to 4-4-7 and including assault engineers), counters are provided for several new types of Hungarian infantry — SMG units, assault engineers, the fascist Arrow Cross Militia, and the anti-communist Vannay Battalion — as well as the Buda Volunteer Regiment, Hungarians fighting for the Russians. Counters are also provided for both Hungarian and Russian air support.

Contents:

    4 22" x 28" maps
    9 countersheets
    1 full-color, 3-hole punched rules Chapter FB (FB1-FB46)
    2 Chapter Dividers 
    3 Campaign Games 
    17 ASL scenarios

The war for the crown has not yet ceased ... not even when the sun goes down. description: A memory-matching card game used to teach children about fire, fire hazards and fire safety.  The 30 cards are laid out face down and players turn cards over to make matches.  When a correct pair is found, it is removed from the board and the player scores a point.  If a match card or lighter card is turned over, it is removed from the game along with the other overturned card.  The player with the most points wins.

Based on the popular game Crazy Eights, this lively card game delivers fast-paced fun while helping kids become aware of their unique and special qualities. Players draw, match, and shed cards, answering questions as they go. (For example: "Would you choose yourself as a friend? Why or why not?")

Because only half of the cards include questions, the game moves along at a brisk pace. Special cards--Switch Direction, Leap, and Wild Cards--introduce an element of surprise. Players race to get rid of their cards and be first to proclaim "One of a Kind." For added excitement, the rules provide a way for quick-thinking players to jump in at the last minute and keep the game alive.

After each round of play, smiles abound--because kids are not only having fun but also feeling better about themselves.

Image search under time pressure! For each question there is to find the called number of matching images. For example, "Find 4 pictures with something that has eyes" or "Find two images with a fish." Who is in it, gets all the playing pieces, and it will read a question. Then the timer is started, and the player tries to find as quickly as possible, the popular image maps. Accompanying photos are marked with a character. If all images have found, the timer stops. The remaining time is the points standings for the block. After four rounds the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Donald Duck's Party Game for Young Folks
description:
Donald Duck's Party Game for Young Folks is a "spin" and move game for young children.

The object of the game is to collect three prize tokens. The first player to do this is the winner.

The prizes are collected by moving the player pawns (four versions of Donald Duck) around the board by spinning a movement spinner until one player has landed on three "prize" spaces. The spinner is divided in to 4 quadrants numbered 1 to 4 with each one containing one square for each colored pawn that will determine the color of pawn to be moved and the number of spaces to move. While moving the player must read aloud what is written on the spaces as they pass them and perform any action that is in the space. For example "Stand up and take a bow" Once a player has landed on three prize spaces they are the winner. The winner must then do what the three prize tokens say.

Felix travels through Germany to visit many sights. Player plays a destination card and a kilometer card, shows his destination and its picture on the board and if the distance is lower than the kilometers you played, you set the card aside for a victory point. If there are other destinations en route, you can set them aside as well. Another player who has cards for destinations on that route can set them aside, too. When Felix is back in Münster and one player has played all his cards, the player with most cards wins.

Flocki and his doggy friends play at retrieving socks. Each player chooses a dog disc, the sock tiles are briefly displayed openly and then turned over. The active player rolls the die, this is the sock the dogs should retrieve. All players now look for this sock and place their dog disc as fast as the can onto the tile they think is the correct one. More than one disc is allowed on a tile, they are stacked. When all have placed their dog the tiles are turned over: If you did find the correct sock, you score chip points: 3 if you were the fastest, 2 if you were second and all others score 1 point. If you selected a wrong sock, you lose points. Then you switch 2 sock tiles. When all chips have been given out, you win with most points from your chips.

MAPOMINOES is the game that takes you to the edge of the world!

This portable game is fun, easy to learn, educational and highly addictive and makes a great present for any family member interested in geography or travel.

It’s like dominoes but with maps, as you build a map of the Americas, by connecting countries that share a common border. Stay one step ahead of your opponents, as you race to be the first player to play all your country cards on the table and win the game. Block your opponents by holding on to Mexico or Colombia or open up South America by playing Brazil. Take a boat through the Caribbean Sea to Cuba or transit by train through the USA to Canada. Try not to get stuck with Suriname, or miss a boat to the Bahamas as you watch the country cards spread across the table. The excitement builds as you have fewer and fewer cards left to play, but less space to play them in as you reach the edge of the world. (The table’s edge). Every country card is different due to their varying border connections and hence no two games are ever the same in this clever game that you will want to play again and again. Play as a stand alone game or combine with Mapominoes games for other continents to make to expand the game to cover the world. A simple game with hidden strategic depth that gives it broad appeal to both children and adults.

Druggies is a flip toss game where each player takes the role of a drug dealer trying to get and sell drugs in an effort to be the first seller to reach the sales goal.

WHAT IS A FLIP TOSS GAME?

A flip toss game is a boardgame that uses a coin of some sort, instead of dice or cards, to determine different results during the course of the game. This is achieved by flipping a coin and trying to get it to land in a desired location on the game board.

The top card of the deck is turned over, the player guesses on his turn, whether the animal is larger or smaller on the next card. There are a total of nine delightfully drawn animals: mouse, dog, monkey, sheep, tiger, bear, horse, elephant and giraffe, and therefore is in the appearance of giraffe or the mouse does not rate very difficult, but with monkey, tiger and sheep it is not so easy to estimate. Hero Express is a superhero re-theme of Arkham Express by David Willems.

Players will select one of the six included Heroes, or make your own, each with a unique special ability.

During a player's turn they will roll 5 Hero dice with unique symbols on each side, after rolling they have the option of re-rolling 3 more times.

If the "Villain" symbol is rolled on a die it is locked and cannot be re-rolled (unless the hero has an ability that allows it).

If 3 Villain symbols appear a new crime is reported and a new villain appears in Dark City.

The other sides of the dice include movement, Hero tokens (spent to use superpowers and items), recovery, and combat symbols (used when fighting the villains).

Capturing Villains allow the gathering of Justice Points, which are spent at a super secret location to purchase new abilities and items.

If at anytime there is 9 active crimes on the board, they Mayor will replace your team.

Each player must carefully choose how to use their abilities, items and hero tokens to thwart crimes and ultimately draw the Crimelord from hiding and defeat him.

Again I would like to thank David Willems for allowing me to create this re-theme, and for creating Arkham Express.

This is a set of rules designed for use with 6mm or 2mm strips of figures, though other figure scales could be used if desired. The Isle of Man TT races are held every year. The TT stands for Tourist Trophy. Bikers get to ride very fast around the lanes and roads on the island.

ZeN is a connection game played on a torus, which can be visualized as a board whose edges "wrap" around to the opposite side.  As in Quax, players alternate between either placing a stone of their color or connecting two of their stones diagonally.  One player attempts to create either a vertical "loop" or a NW-SE "spiral" around the board, while the other aims for a horizontal "loop" or a SW-NE "spiral".  Like in many connection games, draws are impossible and exactly one player must win.

boardgame: Fish Tales: A Flip Toss Game
description:
It's time for the annual fishing derby and you and your buddies have decided to make it a little more interesting this year by making a wager. Whoever catches the most fish doesn't have to buy his own drinks for the next year and there is only one thing better than fishing and that's fishing with free drinks.

Fish Tales is a flip toss game where each player takes the role of an angler trying to be the first one to catch 10 pounds worth of fish.

WHAT IS A FLIP TOSS GAME?

A flip toss game is a boardgame that uses a coin of some sort, instead of dice or cards, to determine different results during the course of the game. This is achieved by flipping a coin and trying to get it to land in a desired location on the game board.

From the publisher:

Developed over four years by the Father and Son team of Bob and Jeff Wiltrout, "Corner Kick!" is an innovative yet elegantly simple and straightforward game that produces not only games equal to the energy and excitement of the real thing, but even the elements of tactics and strategy that give it depth.

The rules have been extensively play tested and enjoyed at Conventions such as HistoriCon and Cold Wars for years, and they are now ready for you!

Besides the basic rules, there are extensive Designer's Notes which help explain the theories applied, and show how the actual tricks--some dirty!--of the game apply to the table top as well.

Playable with miniatures in several scales now available from a growing list of providers, the rules come with the full particulars of how to build your own "Pitch," Movement Sticks, and other accessories.

Two card stock Goal Cages for self assembly are provided with the book to start you off, as well as Team Rosters and a form for a play-by-play Game Record to note Stoppages and major events--a unique permanent history of your games and good times! A set of color Deflection Templates are included as well for determining where wild shots go, ricochets off the Goal frames, etc.

There is also a "Cheat Sheet" holding some 90% of all that's needed to play the game without having constantly to reference the rulebook. The 8.5" x 11" book itself is 62 pages, with three of full color photos of games and miniatures. Figure and other resources are provided to help fans of the "The Beautiful Game" get started immediately.

To quote from Bob Wiltrout's Introduction, "In our experience, people who play this game once play it again and again. In three years of play-testing, nobody has ever played Corner Kick and walked away saying, “This just doesn’t feel like soccer.” And I’m betting you won’t be the first.

You’re going to enjoy this!"

Subtilla is a two-player, head-to-head naval battle game. Each player has a sub, five boats, and a base on his side of the board. A player wins by either destroying all five of the opponent's boats, or by occupying both spaces of the opponent's base at the end of the opponent's turn.

Battles between ships involve dice-rolling, and the subs have special stealth, movement, and torpedo rules.

Plutocracy is a game with elements of business and politics. You are a serial investor starting and building companies. You can also collect political favors in various ways, and use those to gain an advantage to your own companies, or to slow down your competitors.

Plutocracy is a card game. The opportunities available to you in a given round is represented by your hand. Your resources are collected separately, as money and political tokens ($ and poli-chips).

There are free actions you can use each round if you cannot (or will not) play any of your cards. Events add some uncertainty. One event is the "End Game," which you can use to adjust the length of a game, by placing it in the deck either near the top for a short game, or near the bottom for a long one.

This is a game which was produced when Denmark reached their first European championship (EM).

The object of the game is to win the soccer match.
The 2 players agree before the game how long they will play, and then try to move the ball in a position where they will be able to score.

Each player takes a turn, rolling a dice. 
The player with the ball rolls 0,2, 4 or 6 enabling him to either move his players in position or dribbling with the ball (at double cost). After movement he will have to pass the ball at least one time (kicking it away if unable to reach a teammate). And of course score, should the opportunity arise.
The player without the ball rolls 0,2,4,6 or freekick. He will try to block the way to his goal. If he rolls a freekick, he will get the ball and start his attack.

Scenario Designer's Handbook (ISBN 978-0-9782646-8-0) is intended as a reference for those interested in designing historical scenarios for the Advanced Squad Leader game system. The book features 216 full-colour pages with a variety of information to assist in force and terrain selection, including company, battalion and divisional break-downs of the major armies that participated in the Second World War. Additional chapters deal with scenario lay-out, publishing, researching, walk-throughs of the design process and discussion regarding the various components of ASL scenarios.

Includes four complete scenarios:
Designer's Handbook Scenario 1 EXHAUSTING BUT PRODUCTIVE
Designer's Handbook Scenario 2 SIEGE WITHOUT SHELLS
Scenario SF 1.1 SOLID GOLD REAL ESTATE
Scenario MD1 THE BRIDGE AT RAMELLE

And also includes a discussion draft scenario KRUGLOWKA RAILWAY.

All are company-level recreations of tactical combat in the Second World War consistent with the ASL series of games.

Scenario Designer's Handbook is not an official publication of Multi-Man Publishing or Hasbro and neither the author nor canadiansoldiers.com/tacticalwargamer.com are in any way affiliated with MMP.

description:
The board is in the shape of a "Star of David". A path goes around the star and through the center.  In the center of the star is a space marked "Heaven". Each point on the star is the start position for a player and is marked with a biblical person whose name starts with one of the letters in ISRAEL.  Each player has a block that spells out ISRAEL.

Players roll a die to advance around the board.  The spaces on the board determine what question based on the bible the player has to answer.  A correct answer allows the player to roll again.  If the player lands on a space marked with the name of a biblical person and answers a question correctly they get a chip to place on their block.  There are spaces that give players the ability to remove a chip from other players or get a free chip. When a player has their block completely covered they must enter the Heaven space by an exact roll and answer a question correctly.  If the player answers the question correctly they win the game.  If the answer is incorrect the player must leave Heaven on the next turn and try again.

Essentially this is a rip-off of Trivial Pursuit.

description:
Viewpoint Reflections is the first expansion to the Viewpoint card game. It can be played as a stand-alone game or combined with the original Viewpoint. Viewpoint Reflections adds seventy additional cards and introduces new characters, including the Lazy Eye Guys - Dirk and Pedro - and the Evil Eye Pet.

The release of Viewpoint Reflections also sees the introduction of new keywords - PASS and REPLACE.

PASS is essentially a weaker version of the STEAL action because the player "targeted" gets to select the card to give to the other player.

REPLACE allows you to remove a card from your Field of View and "replace" it with another card from the top of the Draw Pile.

Viewpoint Prize Cards are also included in 1-in-10 Viewpoint Reflections decks.

description:
Flames of War: Burning Empires is a hardcover supplement for the Flames of War World War II miniatures game system. It is about the Early War battles in North Africa in 1940 and 1941. It contains armylists, scenarios, painting guides and background information about the battles that took place.

Included are the army lists that did not make it into Hellfire and Back, for example the Fucilieri, Greeks, and French.

In addition, rules and scenarios are included to fight out small raiding force actions, for example the British Long Range Desert Group and the Italian Sahariana

Everyone is informed of the terrible cataclysm which endangers this little planet at the far end of our galaxy. We have to help the different races that inhabit it, as they have no choice that to seek refuge on new planets. Today, we learned that Earth will welcome 5 different extraterrestrial tribes: the Bloups, the Paroks, the Faars, the Mutas and the Napils. The issue is that no-one is able to predict how these 5 species will co-habit together...

The game is played in several turns, each made of 2 phases:
- New Flouks
- Entrance in the Village

Each player takes 4 cards, keeps 2 of them and give the 2 others to his neighbors. Everyone then choose one of the cards in his hand and play it simultaneously. The cards are resolved in the following order: Decrees, Flouks, Chief Flouks then Actions.

Decrees have an effect only on their owner: it increases the amount of a type of Flouks depending on a some conditions. The action of a decree lasts all game long and we can have only 1 decree per village.

The Flouks are only used to populate the village. The Chiefs Flouks can enter a village only if a Flouk of the same specy is already there. The Chief has a power which apply when he enters the village.

The actions all have different effects that apply only when they are played.
