description:
It’s the 1920s. There’s prohibition and... sadness. The townspeople cry out for an unsobering savior! You don’t know the first thing about brewing, but there’s rumor about of secret distilleries deep in the forest. If only you could find them, you could caravan back these barrels of liquid joy and become the pride of the town. That is, if the other would-be heroes don’t get to them first. Do you have what it takes to claim that oh-so-coveted title, Regent of Rum?

If you like dominos, and you like mancala, then you'll love The Rum Run! At its simplest, this game entails building roads from the town center, acquiring barrels, and bringing them back. At its most strategic, you'll be at constant warfare with other players; racing them to distilleries, breaking their roads, building over them, and stealing their barrels. Be prepared to think not one or two, but six or seven moves ahead!

Originally designed especially for the Italian game shop chain Città del Sole, they used to give it as a present to children for their birthday.

There are birthday parties everywhere and animals wander to wood, pasture, house and pond to celebrate. The guest cards need to be deposited in the correct sequence at the party cards, the first player to discard all cards wins the game. The party cards are laid out, the guest cards are dealt to the players. In his turn a player must place one card and may place more. The first card next to a party card must be a 1 or 6, the following cards must be in sequence, ascending or descending. If a player cannot or does not want to place a card, he must draw a card, if the stack is finished he must place a card if he can.

Premise and Gameplay From Publisher: All the animals must attend the most correct birthday party: in the wood, at the farm, at home or inside the pond. But at the entrance they find a special command, so players must be really skilfull to try to get rid of all their cards, sending all the animals to the correct birthday party. The game mechanism is quite free and players will decide how many cards to play at each turn; they can even not play any card, but instead pick up a new one.

An educational game to promote the acoustic sound insulation. Ideally suited to school in preparation for kindergarten age amd is especially recommended for dyslexic students in early education..
Game is based on initial sounds. There are 72 cards and 4 cards per initial sound in this game. 
Ways to Play: Collect all four cards with the same initial sound or looking for pairs. Player who has most sets or pairs at the end of the game is the winner.

(User summary)

A promo card for use with Chez Geek with the following attributes:

Aktivität
Slack 1W6
Kosten 2

Pegasus promo card #02

boardgame: G.I. World War II Tactical Combat
description:
Rules for skirmish combat during the Second World War.

Game Scale:
1 turn = 1 minute in terms of movement
1 figure = 1 vehicle or soldier (in general)
1 inch = 15 meters

Opposing forces are divided into two to four tactical groups (units) either by the players or based on scenario rules. Units are activated one at a time during the random activation process. Units are composed of one or more formations (squads, weapons sections or vehicles) and formations are composed of one or more figures.  Recommendation is no more than four units per side to control the size and pace of the game. Each unit will be represented by a marker (a playing card, marked poker chip, or specially prepared index card) during the activation process.

A turn is comprised of 4 phases:
1. Artillery
2. Mark figures conducting supressive fire
3. Activation phase
4. Remove markers

Pondus Pub is a quiz game set in the Pondus universe by cartoon drawer Frode Øverli. Each player takes turns in throwing a die and answer questions to be awarded with cheese pops, beer or red wine. These assets are needed to hit on the girls in the pub. Once you have gathered at least five of these assets (by answering questions correctly), you can try to hit on a girl. Each girl has different needs, so you might fail, and need to collect more alcohol and snacks. If you are caught by the bartender, you are sent out of the pub to go around the block and get some fresh air!

--

Fra esken: "Glem Robbie Fowler, Steven Gerrard og el Niño Torres! Hat-trick kan nå scores på en pub nær deg, nærmere bestemt Pondus Pub. Og vi snakker ikke om fotball, det er nemlig Jokkes bedrifter utenfor oldboysligaen som danner bakteppe for spillet. Holder du hodet kaldt, flaksen varm og hjernen koblet til ryggmargen, kan du bli med helt til siste runde. Spille skal svare på spørsmål i kategoriene: Fleip eller fakta, Hvorfor vet jeg dette? Rubb og rake."

Affenzirkus is a dexterity card game for children.
The game contains 49 cards with animals.
All the cards are placed face down on the table. By turns, players draw cards. Thank, depending on the animal drawn, players have to do dexterity tasks. For example if you draw a penguin, you have to place the card on your feet and walk 5 steps without dropping the card.
If you succeed, you get the card.
The first player to have 5 different cards is the winner.

objective: be the first to put on Toni's shoes
 - moving/acting by turn -
each player places his meeples at one side of Toni's coloured segments
each player gets 13 shoes
roll the coloured die - step to the next coloured segment an put a wooden shoe on
continue - if the segment you stepped on is already equipped - bad luck
if the die shows white - free choice
winner is who puts on Toni's last shoe
variant: the one who puts on Toni's very last shoe (out of general supply) wins

boardgame: Ran Sat1 Fussball Quiz Super Q
description:
Objective: be the first to pass the finish line finding correct answers to question cards
 - moving by turn -
each player rools with 2 dice
die 1 selects question card (difficulty level)
die 2 shows no of spaces to move if successful
special areas allow to move faster (yellow, red and penalty cards) 
answering questions for other players or combined activities are possible
remark:
'Ran Sat1' - this title was used for several games (Schmidt, Klee..) which can create some confusion

The Game of Photographic Memory...

Imprints is a trivia/picture identification game.  Players try to earn a card from each of four categories, which is done by drawing from the deck, correctly identifying the picture on the front, then answering a trivia question related to the picture.

Worzle.  The strategic word puzzle game.  It’s cute and creative, fast paced and fun, and you might even learn a thing or two along the way.  Two to eight players evenly distribute a total of 100 puzzle pieces. Each has a printed letter along with four wild pieces. Begin by placing a puzzle piece on the table.  Start the timer.  Taking turns, each player adds a piece to the puzzle. How the puzzle expands is up to you.  Once every piece has been placed or time runs out, players pick up their pencils.   Start the timer again and find as many words as you can.  As long as the puzzle pieces are next to each other, any direction goes, however a player may not skip over a void in the puzzle to complete a word.  When time runs out, using the Worzle Scoring System, the points are tallied and the player with the most wins!

Worzle is the next progression in word games.  It combines the strategic component of building hidden words with the fast paced nature of finding as many as you can.  It is not as simple as you might imagine.  As the puzzle expands, pieces are placed in multiple directions leaving them upside down and sideways.  Each player around the puzzle has a unique perspective of the game. The “building the puzzle” dynamic adds huge interest to the game.

Worzle will be packaged in a printed drawstring cotton bag complete with a timer, several custom pads and pencils all in a full color box.

boardgame: The Ancient Americas: Cities of Promise
description:
From South America to Central America to North America there are remnants of large civilizations that have disappeared leaving behind only the ruins of temples, city structures and fragmented settlements. The Ancient Americas are shrouded in mystery and intrigue. The people were religious with a fashionable culture. Their cities were the central hub to life and these civilizations enjoyed much abundance and prosperity all while in the midst of conflicts that were trying to destroy them.

General Rules

This game is for 2 to 4 players ages eight to adult. Players build a 'city of promise' by building their civilization with important structures and with valuable characters that then defend the city from corruption's power.

Building Cards - Throughout the deck are Building Cards. To build structures for the 'cities of promise' and to use important historical figures within the cities, a player  must try to collect specific cards and in the right amounts. Some cards cost more than others and the value of the cards vary in power. Players who gain the right building cards pay the cost of the card and a new part of the city is then added.

Game Timer - Corruption Cards play as timing cards and determine when the game ends. When the 18th Corruption Card is placed down the game ends for all players.

Each player add up their cities power points. If the total is greater than the total points of Corruption Power Points  then they have WON! Yes, that's right, there can be more than one winner in this game making it more rewarding for the whole family. And if the friendly competition is that HIGH you can find out who's city had the most strength and they can be the Grand Winner!

Types of Cards

Corruption Cards - New cards are drawn at the beginning of every turn. Any time a Corruption Card is drawn it must be played during the player's turn and added to the Corruption Card area (typically the middle between all players).

Building a City - Players that purchase City Cards with the Build Cards will be able to piece the city together in any combination left to right or right to left. As players strive to get the better City Cards it may be necessary to discard unwanted or undesirable City Cards in the hopes of gaining a better one on the next draw.

Specialty Characters - Character Cards can be added to your city by purchasing them with the Build Cards and then placing them under the correct City Card they belong to. These cards also have a power point value so players can seek to add the best characters to their city to give it the best advantage.

Bonus Power Points - Saints Cards, Resources Cards, and Faith & Works Cards can be stored under Character Cards for later purchases of City Cards and Character Cards. Unused Build Cards can add additional power points at the end of the game.

boardgame: Wreck Age: Post-Collapse Tabletop Skirmish game
description:
Wreck Age is a game/world, based on the imagination of two friends and a multitude of compatriots. A world in which you are able to tell your own stories and have your own adventures.

It's a tabletop game involving miniatures that you can collect, paint, and play with, as well as a role playing game; where your imagination takes you to new lands, and on adventures. Basically, make believe and craft night for adults all rolled up into one.

A tabletop wargame is a simulation; where models are used to represent forces on a field. Our brand of tabletop game is: part wargames simulation, part strategy game, and part board game. Like chess or risk, but which allows you to "color outside the lines" a little bit.

Wreck Age play occurs on a 2'x2' board (or larger), which can either take the form of a gaming mat, or just your kitchen table. The only limit to the size, and scope of the gaming surface is your imagination.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Necropoly (Game of the Dead) is a spin on Monopoly but the differences are a dead giveaway. Two to six players make their way around the board beginning at Death and navigating through cemeteries and pitfalls trying to build up their stash of personal burial plots and even adding tombstones and mausoleums so they can charge more for other players standing on their grave. The “Fate” and “Communal Coffin” cards are hysterical. But beware the devil in one corner; he will send you straight to the fire. 
Players advance with the roll of the black dice, paying bones to the bone collector or taking in bones for fees, and the game ends when one player is the last to have bones.

A lot of cultures celebrate the dead with holidays like Halloween, Day of the Dead, and All Souls Day. This game is great for all ages and takes perhaps the scariest thing in life and makes it a fun and competitive adventure. The entire family will get into the spirit and will be laughing out loud.

boardgame: Royal Purple: The Game of Kings
description:
The unusual features of this game are the moves of the Prince and his acquisition of the territory upon which he sets foot. A Prince moves up or down on a dice throw of 1 or 4. Moves right or left on a throw of 2 or 5, and diagonally on a throw of 3 or 6. A player places a Dominion Marker on all squares stopped on by his Prince, and no other player may trespass on that territory when so marked. This limits the squares a Prince may move to making and unusual situation, and calling for increasing skill as the game progresses.

The person starting is determined by high roll. Turn moves to the left. Each player starts from his Corner Castle and is given a Prince, 30 Dominion Markers, and a die. Each player in turn rolls and places a Dominion Marker where the Prince lands. The first objective is to move to and place Dominion Markers on five Noble Squares (indicated by a purple board square with a white center). Then with the support of these five Nobles the Prince moves toward the Throne Space at the other side of the board. The first Prince to enter a Throne Space earns the right to wear the Royal Purple (wins).

The game plays like a cross between popular games of the day Chess and Halma with a roll and move mechanic added. A number of "Go To" squares on the board jumping players ahead to another square are an added twist.

The Sortie of the Prince of Wales and Repulse December 1941

Operation Matador is a historical simulation game recreating the Allied attempt to intercept the Japanese transports heading for the Malay peninsula during the opening hours of the Pacific half of the Second World War.

This game as two components a strategic move using a map of the pacific covering the Gulf of Siam and South China Sea, specifically covering  Indochina with city of Saigon and Malaya with the city of Singapore. The ship to ship combats are conducted on a small hex map.

Victory points are assigned for the number of destroyed enemy air units, submarines, transports destroyers units destroyed. In addition points are allocated for shore bombardment and any damage to enemy carrier or battlecruisers. If the Repulse or Prince of Wales are able to leave the south or south west map edge.

You're the Banker is an educational game, designed to be played by large groups of students (up to 30 in 5 groups of 6).  The game aims to teach the purpose and practices of the Federal Reserve Bank, by having teams of players run banks.  Many of the consequences in the game are automated, but the teacher/moderator of the game is required to decide whether loans are sufficiently sound to accept as collateral for borrowing from the Federal Reserve.

From the TwoHourWargames site

The crowd packed the stands, all dressed in their finest clothes, a riot of color, pennants and banners flapped in the breeze, there was a clarion call of trumpets, and the crowd fell to a hush. In twos, the Knights paraded into the Lists, horses draped in color tossed their heads and stamped their hooves to the jangle of golden spurs. The sun shone in splendor on shield and harness. Passing the Berfrois the ladies and damsels threw favors of scarves and sleeves to their favorite Knights. The presiding prince proclaimed the rules, and the heralds announced their champions.

Joust-Heroes of the Lists allows players to re-create the combat between Knights during Tournaments. Each player builds his Knight using a variety of Attributes. The goal is simple, defeat your opponents in one on one combat and gain fame, glory and yes, wealth, as you fight your way to the top, the International Tournament Circuit where all Knights are champions.

Joust can be played solo against non-player opponents or head to head against your friends. In fact, you can even play on the same side, making Joust the prefect game to teach your friends and family.

Not the traditional wargame, Joust can be played in as little as twenty minutes and is perfect for campaign games as your Knight gets better with each win.

Joust includes the Jousting Field and six colorful Knight cut-out counters.

This game is available as a PDF download and/or Print and Mail from the publisher.

This fan made miniatures game uses the setting of Warhammer 40,000 but the game represents fights between squads with a leader and a handful of troops, each controlled individually. The statistics of each character are Grit, Fighting Value, Shooting Value, and Speed.  Several scenarios are given or players can make their own.  There is also a campaign system for linking battles.

The game turn is split into three phases- movement, shooting, and melee.  The players alternate turns activating all of their figures in each phase.

Objective: 
be the first to let all of your 4 flowers (=cards) reflourish  
 - moving by turn -
all of the flowers fell asleep
each child tries to visit one of the elves
if this elf is matching with one of the 4 flower cards the kid started with, the card is going to be flipped (reflourish)

boardgame: Air Show: Circus of the Skies
description:
Every summer, crowds gather in the New Mexico desert for a show like no other. Daring pilots compete in exhibitions of aerobatic skill and bravery. Dogfights, air races, and bombing runs are guaranteed to test the mettle of even the most seasoned veteran. Have you got what it takes?

Air Show is a game and a game system. Take the controls of a stunt plane and put your piloting to the test in a variety of aerial events. Manage your altitude and airspeed, perform cool stunts to turn the tables on your opponents, think fast, shoot straight, fly true, and maybe you'll take home the trophy this year.

This game uses a simultaneous action selection mechanic to program a flight plan three maneuvers in advance. Players have thirty seconds to "lock in" their maneuvers and stake out a position in turn order. When everyone is ready, maneuvers execute in turn order. Planes may dive or climb, turn right or left, accelerate or brake, or perform a stunt at the expense of altitude or airspeed. The goal differs with each event, but strategies emerge from anticipating what other players are doing and planning around that. Unlike other games of this type, such as RoboRally or Wings of War, there are no cards to play. All maneuvers are available to all players at all times, to the limits of what their current altitude and airspeed allow.
This game was ultimately published by Goldbrick Games as "Rumble Pie"

boardgame: Oil Quest: The Game of Energy and Excitement
description:
A game designed for group involvement. The object of the game is to get your drilling rig on the board, drill for oil and if oil is struck, replace the rig with a pump jack. Each time you go around the board you get revenue from each pump jack you own. When all the lease sites are allocated the game ends and the player with the most pump jacks is the winner.

Extra wealth can be gaining control of a whole section of the board. If you land on a square called "Option to Auction" you can place sites up for auction and get a commission for each site auctioned.

If a player lands on a Seismic Report he can drill for oil.

Only a thousand games have been produced with hand made wooden pieces.

A combination of chess and poker that uses standard playing cards for special effects.  The chess pieces move as they would in an ordinary game of chess, with certain exceptions.  Players draw cards from a standard poker deck on every turn.    Different cards correspond to different playing pieces.  The red cards correspond to the white pieces and the black cards correspond to the black pieces. Being able to form and play different Poker hands allows the players to do such things as teleport a piece or move multiple times in a row.  For the white player, red poker hands effect the player's own pieces and black poker hands effect the opponent.

The effects of the various poker hands are potent, thus, the game is a much more tactical chess that is quicker, more severe, and with added dimensions to consider.  Playing in the traditional sense generally does not work as the playing of the various poker hands change the traditional rules.

The game has 36 cards showing combinations of clowns in different colors. Move the cards always color on color in a row. Who has so placed as the first all his cards wins the game.

Simple observation and logical deduction game, better with more players.

With this game the german quiz show "1, 2 oder 3" is coming to the nursery. Everyone has the opportunity to slip in the role of the quizmaster or the candidates and can hop around on the fields to collect points. Who is clever and the first player to eight questions answered correctly, wins the game.

boardgame: Princess Lillifee: I Pack My Bags
description:
Princess Lillifee would like to go on a journey and is packing her bags. Since she would like to to take so many things along, she takes notes on small pieces of paper. But where did she place all her things in her room? Can you help Princess Lillifee to find her stuff?

Components:
24 function cards
24 item cards
1 Princess Lillifee figure with base
1 Manual

How to play the game:
The youngest player starts. When it is your turn you show the top card of the function cards. It shows two or three things that Princess Lillifee would like to take along on her journey and which have to be packed into her bag. You have to try to reveal these items by showing the correct item cards. The order is up to you. If you make a mistakeand show the wrong item card with an item that is not on the function card, you turn only this item card face down again and it is the next players turn to try and find the correct items on the function card.  As soon as one player has revealed all items on a function card, he can keep the card and as soon as all item cards are turned face down again, he can continue with a new function card.

End of the game:
The game is over as soon as all function cards are given out or if one player got 6 function cards. Now you count your points on the cards. Each item on a function card counts 1 point, so you have as many points as items on your function card. The one with the most points is the winner.

The goal of the game is to utilize the deck of standard playing cards in order to be the first to get your four color-coded pegs all the way around the board, and finally back to your safety zone base before any other player.

Depending on the card you play during your turn, determines the amount of movement one of your pegs may move. Landing on an opposing players peg causes their peg to be returned to their home/starting location.

Some of the cards played have special abilities that can be used in a strategic manner, such as plying a 4, which allows one of your pegs to move four spaces backwards instead of forwards. You can also play a jack, which allows your to switch the places of any two pegs on the board, which do not even have to be your own.

Game Includes:
- Wooden Gameboard
- 16 Pegs of Four Different Colors
- Deck of Standard Playing Cards
- Rules Sheet

boardgame: Ascension: Chronicle of the Godslayer – Vortex Promo
description:
This promotional card was given out to participants at the Planar Showdown event in San Diego, CA on November 13 2011.

Cost: 6 Power to defeat

Monster

Reward: Gain 4 Honor, Flip this onto the center row from a height of at least one foot. Once it lands, choose a card it touches. You may acquire or defeat that card.

Mysterious spy organizations work toward world domination. It's up to "Lovely Agents", gorgeous news reporters and undercover police officers work with clever disguises to uncover corrupt plots and steal secret information.

Secret "Target" cards show the toughness and worth of an object. Players as spies try to take or keep these targets from each other. Players (all hot anime chicks of course) have protection cards (weapons, disguises, skills and events).

3 to 6 players with optional variant for 7 players who will target each other rather than an object.

The Grain Drain is a game intended to improve understanding of world hunger.  At the beginning of the game, some players are randomly chosen to be rich countries, the rest are poor countries.  Depending on this choice, players roll and move around either the "rich" loop track or the "poor" loop track for the rest of the game.

The object of the game is to avoid starvation:  all players must be able to afford to feed their people once per lap of the gameboard, but because event squares on the "rich" track typically award greater amounts of money, "rich" players are less likely to suffer.

Part of the educational package Food For Thought (1976), produced by Christian Aid jointly with Oxfam and the Catholic Fund for Overseas Development.

boardgame: Quest: Zeit der Helden – Shakes & Fidget
description:
This mini-expansion consists of a new miscreant: Untoter Schurke.
It was presented as a free give-away in 2011 by Pegasus, the publisher.

The "Untoter Schurke" could be played on the event "ambush". The Quest-Master places the "Untoter Schurke" in the starting zone of the heroes. They have to fight him. This is an happening alternative to the regular card text.

See images for more information.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This is a game for young children based on the story of Noah's Ark.

There are four identical boards representing Noah's Ark. The interior of each Ark is divided into 20 squares and each square contains the picture of an animal. Each player is given one of these boards.

There is a bag full of animal picture tokens. During a player's turn the child will remove one token from the bag at random. If the token matches an image on the kid's Ark, the player places it in position. If it doesn't match, the child loses a turn and puts the token back in the bag.

The first child to fill an Ark wins the game.

Designed for children 3 to 6 years old with no reading required.

Promotional game made by Black Cat cigarettes. It consists of 28 cards, but only 14 cards are used in any one game. Several games should be played to find a winner.

One player is elected Banker. He starts the Pool by placing 6 counters into it. He then shuffles and deals 14 cards face down. All the other players now place a bet (counters, not real money) on any card they wish. No more than one can bet on any one card. When all bets are placed, the first card is drawn. The player who bet on it must follow the instructions.

If the Banker role changes hands, a new deal shall be started. But Note: Sometimes a player's call against the Bank cannot be met. The deficiency is made up as far as possible from the Reserve. Then the Bank is 'broken' and the Bank role is transferred to the player on the Banker's left. This player starts a new Bank by putting in the 6 counters that are required.

Cards are:
One In, Two In, etc. = Player puts counters into the Pool
One Out, Two Out, etc. = Player takes out counters from the Pool
Reserve = Every player puts 1 counter into the Bank Reserve. This Reserve is set aside in a special pool, for use on special occasions. It transfers when the Bank is transferred, and only withdraws when Bank Holiday is turned.
Official Receiver = When turned, the Bank and Reserve (if any) transfers to the player who has backed the card, but that player pays over to the retiring Banker counters equal to the number then in the Pool (but not counting in the Reserve). The Banker taking over shall not scoop the Pool, which stays untouched and the game proceeds.
Distribution = When revealed, every player, including the Banker, receives a counter from the Pool.
Contribution = Every player, including Banker, puts a counter into the Pool.
Scoop = The player backing this card scoops the entire Bank, which now transfers to him. The Reserve remains untouched. He starts a new Bank with 6 counters.
Stake = This gives an advantage to the last player to have put down a stake of 6 counters to start a Bank. He claims 6 counters from the player who backed this card. The current Banker usually will be the one who staked the 6 counters into the Pool, but if a new Banker takes over because of the Official Receiver card or the Transfer Bank card (neither of which do not require 6 counters being paid into the Pool, so the player who benefits by this card will be the previous Banker perhaps).
Transfer Bank = The Bank transfers to the player who backed this card, but its capital cannot be increased or the Reserve interfered with.
Bank Holiday = The Bank's Scoop card. When revealed, the Bank scoops both Bank and Reserve, and starts a new Bank with a stake of 6 counters.

The game is a promotional game for BMW.

Objective:
Be the first to gain "required experience chips" listed on the 'task-card' you draw in the beginning.
Chips are handed out as a reward for correct answers on coloured areas.  Questions are related to nature, BMW, environment, history of automobiles

Objective: throw out all smoking monsters which have invaded (y)our home (cave) - or - enter the cigarette slot machine before all monster puzzles (20) are gone
 - moving by turn -
decide which die to use (3 red spaces = tough one)
roll the die and - let the monster puzzle grow (if red) or -
move as fast as you can from your cave to the slot machine;
on your way (different possibilities) you have to manage different obstacles like being caught (magic ring), releasing your partner(s), speeding (combining cones), answering questions (cards) ... 
content:
1 board
1 cigarette slot machine
1 cave
8 coloured little cones
26 yellow event cards 
24 red community cards
20 smoking monster - puzzle pieces 
1 "magical ring" 
1 die with 3 red spaces
1 die with 2 red spaces
1 rules (cover inside)
remark: promotional edition manufactured by Ravensburger

A card game that utilises the board that came with it but the host must provide other equipment.

It is a two player game (or 4 or 6, in which case the games is played as a partnership game).

The object is to fill your bucket or, at least, finish the game with the most water in your bucket.

Cards are laid from your hand, domino fashion i.e. as long as you match one of the numbers on the end dominoes, you can play that card. Most cards will say something like 5 Red Black 2. In this case, if you were Red, you would move your playing piece 5 spaces around the board. The spaces on the board let you add water to your bucket or cause your bucket to start leaking.

The game ends as soon as one player fills their bucket or it ends after both players have made two circuits of the board and then the winner is the one with the most water remaining in their bucket.

Thar be Treasure out thar for Take'n! Sail the Seas, to Raid Ships for Gold and Infamy, or plunder the Ports to get Pieces of the Map to Treasure Island. Collect 3 Map cards to find the Location of Treasure Island. That Island be tricky, and it's location changes every game. Only a Pirate with Maximum INFAMY (13 INFAMY Points) may acquire the Treaure Card from Treasure Island. A Pirate must move the Treasure to a new Location to keep it safe from other thieving Pirates to Win. There are 6 Possible Locations on the Treasure Card. Do all this while keeping a safe distance from the Powerful navy ships patrolling the waters. They don't take kindly to your "kill Em all, and Take thar Gold" Attitude.

Game-play lasts between 1-2 hours. A 3-5 Player game of Piratey Goodness! GOTO: dowrickGames for more info.

Agricola Ereigniskarten (Agricola Event Cards) is the collective name for new, unique Agricola cards given to attendees of various game conventions in Germany. One event card can be played at the start of a game, with the rule on the event card affecting all players.

The German text on the cards:

    Bremer Spieletage: Jeder Spieler kann im Holzhaus 1 Tier, im Lehmhaus 2 verschiedene Tiere und im Steinhaus 3 verschiedene Tiere halten.
    Darmstadt spielt: Alle Spieler brauchen in jeder Ernte 1 NW weniger zu bezahlen.
    Spielefest Wien: Das Ausspielen von Ausbildungskarten kostet keine NW, auch wenn man schon Ausbildungen im Spiel hat.
    Spielewelt in Bielefeld: Alle Anschaffungen sind für alle Spieler 1 Holz billiger.


An English translation of those cards:

    Bremer Spieletage: Each player can keep 1 animal in a wood house, 2 different animals in a clay house, and 3 different animals in a stone house.
    Darmstadt spielt: At each Harvest, all players need to pay 1 food less.
    Spielefest Wien: The playing of Occupation cards will cost no food, even if you already have Occupations.
    Spielewelt in Bielefeld: All Improvements are 1 wood cheaper for all players.

This is a set of rules for large (i.e., not skirmish) pre-gunpowder battles.  Basing is given for 6mm, 15/20/28mm; 6mm uses 1/2 the distance of other scales.  No army lists are included.  No figure or stand removal is required when casualties are suffered; units become disordered, wavering, fleeing, and finally dispersed.  Units may improve their state by spending one turn immobile and not involved in combat.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A typical game for kids (speed, fun and animals). Two frogs must compete to arrive first at the middle of the pond in a beautiful water lily.
Each player rolls a die and moves his frog on a track to reach the water lily. There are several dangers (snakes, humans) and distractions (flies, dragonflies) and some others cases give bonus to you frog.
This game looks like le "Jeu de l'Oie", but it is easier. But more important, it's game played by all kids in the northern provinces of France (something cultural, I suppose).
Material : 1 6-sided die, 2 tokens (frogs) and a board (the rules are printed on the board).

A children's game (speed, fun and animals). It's time to return home for a night's sleep, but it will not be easy with restaurants, concerts, rendez-vous and fighting with dogs.
Life is not easy for a cat.
Who will be the first to back home for bedtime ?
This game recreates the entire lifespan of a cat.
There are 6 cats involved in a race, each on its track. Each player rolls 6 dice and moves the cats by one case by matching die. Eg : If you roll 1,1,1,3,5 and 6, the cat # 1 moves 3 cases; #3, #5 and #6 move 1 case each. But be carefull with food, music, rendez-vous, whatever the number of dice you obtain the cat can only move one case by turn. And just before the finish line, there are dogs, the cat must jump over it to cross the line, it can't stop on it (2 dice are required so).
A funny game for rainy day.
PS : You can put candies on the play zone after the finish line, kids love it.

Plastic monkeys are flipped into a lagoon (the box with an insert). But when Elefun, the elefant, surfaces, he pushes some of them back out. Each monkey which is still in the lagoon now receives a banana and Elefun is submerged again. When all bananas have been distributed, the player with most bananas wins.

This is a hybrid between Dominoes and Memory. In fact, the material is suitable for both games. The playing pieces are grey plastic units which have one nearly flat colored end with some 3D pattern, and one "diagonal" one.

- In ORY, the players place all the pieces into the box and pull out two of them at a time, hoping to find two in which the patterns fit into each other. Whoever locates most pairs this way is the winner.
- In DO, two random pieces are placed on the table. In his/her turn, a player tries to add a piece that fits the pattern and another one of his/her choice. A player who cannot add pieces has to draw a new one from the box. The goal is to get rid of one's pieces first.

Come set sail with xPirate - the world's first-ever game that combines a traditional game board with the iPhone, iPod Touch or iPad! The iPad/iPhone/iPod acts as a "Game Master", controlling elements of what happens to the pirates as they search for buried treasure.

Object of the Game

Travel across dangerous waters on the hunt for 4 hidden treasures before sailing back home for victory!

Treasures - include gold, jewels, supplies & secret treasures.
Islands - while searching for treasure, you can trade on the island markets - buying and selling what you need to survive your journey.
Beware - you'll encouter raging typhoons, dangerous krakens, deadly ship attacks, skeleton armies and even attacks from your fellow players!

Giant Battle is a 2-player print and play game about the brutality of the factions during the time where great wars occur to prove who is the better faction.

In this age of power and greed, every faction in the world are battling it out to find out who is better king among all kings. Players will take turns maneuvering their unit on the battlefield, making clever decision to eliminate the opposing faction's leader. And in this game if one of the leader of either faction dies, the game ends and the faction with the surviving leader considered victorious.

You will control either a dwarf clan or the orcs battling each other on a 5x5 board. Each turn player have 2 moves and 2 actions at their disposal. Some unit have special ability triggers by just be adjacent with their leader. Some unit have the ability to attack from afar and have the healing ability. Both player will try to eliminate opposing leader to win the game.

Visit us and play more games at http://experimentalplayground.blogspot.com/

In the year 2149 a new gold rush has begun. This time the mineral rich lands are in the asteroid belt just beyond Mars. You and your fellow prospectors have gone in together on a Martian Processing Plant and you've each bought a mining ship to begin harvesting the riches of the asteroid belt. Although you may have bought-in together, you have no intention of sharing your riches with your competition.

This is a competitive game of resource collection and market management for 2-5 players. Each of you will manage your fuel, cargo, and the market to gain the most credits and win the game!

Players send their rockets to different asteroids around Mars and mine materials to sell on the Earth Resource Exchange. Players must keep a close eye on the size of their cargo hold, the fuel in their tanks, and actions of the other miners.

As you collect these materials, the other miners will know where to go to get them and you have to make strategic choices to decide where you will place your rocket and any revealed materials. Players trade materials in for credits, which they can use to buy upgrades and fuel for their rockets.

Objective: get as many coins as possible from the treasure of the bandit Hotzenplotz; game ends if all coins are gone
 - moving by turn, hide & seek -
game is split into different stages
each stage runs like:
1 one player hides the Hotzenplotz-tiles (traps, footmarks & treasure) on the board
2 other players roll 2 dice ==> subtract: difference to move;
choose, which of your meeples may walk; if you land on a symbol
3 flip a tile: trap ==> remove meeple; footmarks ==> all players know the symbol the must search behind; treasure; take two coins & and hide tiles for a new stage

Similar to Trivial Pursuit with 2500 questions and answers on seduction.
Players roll the dice, move around the board and answer the fun questions collecting the pieces of each color.

Love Trivia Gold is a real encyclopedia of everything you need to know. Over 500,000 games sold to date.

Players compete to create the longest path possible by laying tiles in front of their own tokens. Points are awarded for each tile traversed and the winner is the player who scores the most points.

The play is fast and simple making it a family friendly game.

30 girls, rated from 1 to 10 depending on their physical appearance, the higher the value, the higher the requirement in four categories: class, masculinity, morality and success.

Players have three types of card: Arguments, Joker or Bitch Slap.

The first ones can be played only during your turn. others can be played at any time.

Combine enough of class, masculinity, morality or success to accumulate conquest, score points and become the king of the evening ...

The game can be played over 30 to 60 minutes.

Objective: 
be the first passing the finish line (field 50) together with a partner

Each player chooses a figurine and puts it on the start line.

In his turn, the active player rolls a die and moves his figurine as many spaces indicated by the dice.
According to the space he lands, he plays a card from categories "Game", "Sport" or "Game Chrono".
He chooses question from the card and opponent who must answer that. If opponent answer correctly, he moves as many spaces indicated on the card. If not the current player moves.

Player who reach around the stage first receive the World Cup and is the winner of the game!

From the back of the box...

"Deceive and Decide is a hilarious word-bluff game: the players deceive each other with made-up definitions to words you probably never heard.  Then they decide which outrageous definition is correct.

Deceive and Decide contains 1000 obscure words complete with pronunciations which are known only to the poker-faced Word Master.  Each player concocts his own whacky definition and the Word master reads them all allowed, including the real one.  The players vote on the definition that seems most plausable - and everyone wins.

The game goes 'round the table at a razzle-dazzle pace as each player yakes his turn at Word Master.  The definitions range from ludicrous to bizarre - if you can stop laughing long enough, you may even want to add up the score.

Imagine creating defintiions for words such as:

Beccabunga
Gardyloo
Dorking
Xylophonist"

This game sounds similar to Balderdash, but there is no board and the card layout and playing equipment is quite different.

Domino 4 is a game of dominoes but each tile is divided by the square diagonally and includes dominoes from 0 / 1 to 4 / 4, fourteen types of dominos in four colors. Add four jokers - the equivalent of 0 / 0 - and you have sixty tiles.

Gilles Monnet devised a simple rule, but interesting. While the classic domino game are done in one dimension, Domino 4 is a game in two dimensions. Tiles can be placed so that all numbers fits with other adjacent tiles but diagonals may be turn in 2 ways.

Objective: be the first passing finish line (= one full circle into middle of the board) with all your (4) meeples

roll the die, step out if you throw a '1'

your meeple is safe (can't be thrown out) if placed on a red field (12x)

The Game of famous situations and sentences.
Player move their pawns around the board back to their starting square answering questions and guessing the authors of famous situations/sentences. Player who finish first is the winner.

There are 110 cards and 1100 situations often known, you need to attribute to the author. Dou You know what did or said Audiard, Guitry, Santini or Van Damme?

Love Party is funny trivia games about Love.
The goal is finish first together with Your parner.

To score points, players must go through the game with partners found on the game's three phases in order: romance, love and sex and answer the questions of these categories.

With your card, you build adventures that go in the order of the dredge to love and sex. Of course, your opponents do not give up the pants! Surprises, dirty tricks, breaks and exchanges, anything goes.

PJOVERT - a partygame below the belt!

What are you going to do, when the evening needs an extra twist? Pull out your Pjovert!

Pjovert, drawn and invented by Morten Ingemann, Ruth Lorentzen, Svend Schat-Holm and Susanne Ingemann Schat-Holm, is a new funny game with questions about everything that happens below the belt.

Even though the game is naughty in a funny way, you will not be embarresed, when you play Pjovert - you will get lots of laughs and new knowledge. There are 6 categories, with 100 questions or tasks about life downunder.

Quick to start: Roll the two dice, move your pjovert around on the board, and answer a question or do a task. In that way you can win rings. Whoever gets 6 different coloured rings around his pjovert wins the game!

A game takes 30-60 minutes, and can be played by two to six players.

Objective: you are a mother animal - your very young child is hidden in the wood: go out, find your child (puppy/baby lamb/baby foal/baby calf) and be the first  returning to your home
- moving by turn - 
simple 'Roll & Move' game using a die with symbols
remark:
cute wooden figures

User summary:

Party game for grown ups. Each player get one of Morten Ingemanns 20 cartoony characters, and the goal is to stay on the board. Players have to answer questions, sing songs, tell jokes, drink drinks or do other tasks, or suffer the consequenses.

The gnomes are fighting for dominance of the gardens. Gnomes from the lawn, hedge, veggie garden, orchard, flower garden, and herb garden can be recruited to build a ceramic empire.

In Invasion of the Garden Gnomes, players form melds (sets of gnomes from the same part of the garden), each representing a columns of garden gnomes armed to the teeth. The smallest set of each color will be squished and not score, and larger sets score more points. Whenever a new column is formed, a special effect is triggered (usually good). Between games, twelve different special powers are randomly allocated between the six type of gnomes, making for very different games and necessitating different tactics.

This game's core mechanisms are based on Knizia's card game Vampire. Invasion of the Garden Gnomes introduces special powers for each color and more vicious competition within each color of meld. The game includes "junior rules" that allow you to play a simpler game, more similar to Vampire, and the "full rules" are notably different from the the game's predecessor and involve deeper strategy.

description:
Similar game with Trivial Pursuit themed on Austrian sports. The categories are general sports, sports on wheels and skis, Summer Olympic sports, team sports and Winter sports. 
Who is the first player to get the score card for each color wins the game

Plan a route to complete your own secret mission before the other players do. Your explorer needs to travel over roads and sandy plains, through woods and rivers, to arrive at the next coordinate using the most efficient or tactical route. Once arrived, you'll receive a new coordinate or clue where the treasure on that rout can be found. But watch your opponents explorers! They will try to sabotage your activity with fallen tree trunks, damaged bridges, or other obstacles. Given the right action cards and perseverance, you could be the first to accomplish your mission and reach the greatest treasure of all: Victory!

There are several ways to play the game. The following variants are possible:

Basic Game
The basic game is played with 2 to 4 players and 2 explorers per player. Each player can move one explorer on his turn. The die thrown depends on which type of land the explorer is standing on. Changing type of land results in ending the players turn. During each players turn, action cards can be collected by moving to one of his own marked coordinates, Reaching a marked coordinate, a new coordinate and action card is received. The action cards are a collection of information, obstacle and help cards. Ending a route means saving the coordinates and the treasure belonging to the route. These coordinates and the treasures are the key to accomplish your own secret mission first.

Young players
To play the game with young players, the game can be played with only one explorer and without using the hiker. All other rules are the same as in the basic game.

Teams
This variant can only be played with 4 players. The 2 players sitting diagonally opposite each other are working together as a team. Both players work on one mission together. Each player will use a set of two explorers. In the team, it's allowed to move each others explorers. It's allowed to discuss the strategy and to give hints to each other during the game.

Game material:
- 4 board segments with a coordinate frame
- 14 mission cards
- 26 treasure cards
- 60 coordinate cards
- 42 actions cards (11 obstacle cards, 11 help cards, 20 information cards)
- 4x2 explorer tokens with 3 accompanying tokens with route symbol
- 28 obstacle and help attributes (8 hikers, 10 fallen tree trunks, 6 damaged bridges, 4 shortcut tokens)
- 20 'single'-tokens and 10 'multi'-tokens
- 4 dice
- rules of the game

Player are the crows who try to get some corn from the cornfield but they must beware Jack the Scarecrow who mostly sleeps but wakes up if somebody took the wrong corn. He must give back all corns he gathers this round. Who has gathered the required number of cobs, wins. In a variant, you collect all the corn of a color. corn of a color.

In Silver Screen, players try to complete movies from the 1920s by collecting sets and claiming movie cards. Points come from three sources: making movies, winning awards for your movies, and making movies with the correct elements in them. Players have to balance auctions, drafting, and set collection.

Silver Screen is a card game rendition of Knizia's Dream Factory / Hollywood Blockbuster // Traumfabrik board game. In contrast to the board game, Silver Screen has more hidden information and more emphasis on balancing alternate paths to victory.

description:
Following the great Madstone war, wizards of this land set out to rebuild all that was destroyed by dark forces.  The pinacle of the rebuilding effort is to restore the school of magic known as Bellwether.
Players choose one of four actions each turn:  One, they meditate to gain magical energy from the land.  Two, they study to gain new spell knowledge.  Three, they hunt dark creatures who remain hiding in the land.  And four, they rebuild civilization.
Victory points are connected with learning spells, building institutions of society, and hunting dark creatures.
Cover art by Sean McGrath from Quispamsis, NB, Canada.

From Publisher Website:

Hexover is a fun and fast moving new strategy game of reversing momentum. Hexover takes a new twist on play pattern with roots dating back to England is the 19th century. The play pattern, which consists of trapping and flipping opponents disks, is familiar to people worldwide who have played Reversi. The object of Hexover is to be the first player to complete a string of five disks in their color on the white hexagons on the game board, with at least one game piece being positioned on the outside row. Because of the hexagonal shape of the game board, players have to stay alert to all of directions impacted by each move. Hexover is a game of planning, positioning and rich strategy. The obvious move, may not be the best! The rules have been drafted so that there is always a winner, no ties, stalemates, or cat's games. Hand crafted from high quality hardwoods, Hexover is pretty enough to be left out on the coffee table or bookshelf to be played again and again.

From Publisher Website:

Playing Trappex is little bit like reuniting with an old friend. While it is comforting rekindling childhood memories with the time tested "Dots and Boxes" play pattern, there is still something new and different how the game has grown up in the meantime. Even though Trappex is quick and easy to learn, there is a depth of strategy that will keep you alert from your first move till your last. To play, players take turns placing their wooden "wall" pieces on the board, attempting to complete squares while preventing their opponent from doing the same. The player with the most completed squares at the end of the game wins. Trappex is simple enough to be played by children, strategic enough to engage a true gamer. The rules have been drafted so that there is always a winner, no ties, stalemates, or cat's games. Hand crafted from high quality hardwoods, Trappex is pretty enough to be left out on the coffee table or bookshelf to be played again and again.

boardgame: All Things Zombie: Risks and Rewards
description:
What's in that building? Is it empty? Zombies? Survivors? What's their Rep? Did I find anything?

With Risks and Rewards we've done all the work for you. Draw a card and you'll know instantly all there is to know about what's lurking inside that darkened building.

Risks and Rewards is a 54 card deck that gives you a wide variety of information. It tells you if you've met zombies or survivors, how many of each, what they're armed with and all based on the type of area you're exploring.

All on one card

All that plus new weapons and loot not found in ATZ - Better Dead Than Zed. And did we mention the special Carolee (THW girl) NPC?

Whose Shoes? is a boardgame to facilitate discussion on topics related to personalization in health and social services.

Players roll and move around a perimeter track of colored "footprint" spaces, taking a card of the color of the space they land on.  The card contains discussion text and may award a colored square in red, yellow, green or blue.  The object of the game is to collect sets of four differently colored squares in the center area of the board, forming a "path to personalization".

WINGEN-SUR-MODER, FRANCE, 4 January 1945: Shortly before the crack of dawn on 4 January sleeping American soldiers were suddenly awakened by what some on the scene described as “the most intensive machine-gun and automatic weapon fire that any had ever heard.” Disoriented GIs awoke to the sudden outburst of fire but there was no panic. German forces pushed out of the Low Vosges forests to capture Wingen-sur-Moder, situated along the road net needed for Operation Nordwind. The attacking enemy forces struck Wingen at the first light of day and rapidly moved through the village. In the confusion that followed, Company A lost communications with Companies B and C, and for a time the Company was without radio contact with the Battalion CP in the Kirchberg. Within the company perimeter confusion made it difficult for the platoon leaders and platoon sergeants to get their men organized. Nearly every squad was on its own. Their reaction to the sudden attack was left to the judgment of the individual squad leaders. By 0900 hours the Germans had captured and occupied the town, the high ground to the northeast of town, and established a bridgehead on the south bank of the Moder River. They immediately went about establishing a perimeter defense around the village then prepared to continue their attack southward toward Saverne.

During the first days of January 1945 German forces pushed out of the Low Vosges forests to capture the towns that lay along the snow-covered route of attack. One of these towns was Wingen, along the Moder River. American units there were initially caught by surprise as battle-hardened troopers from the 6th SS Mountain Division stormed in and captured the town. American reinforcements were soon pouring in from the west and the south and despite bitter fighting, by January 5th the German offensive had been brought to a standstill. Lacking exits from the Vosges, planned armored reserves stood idle and Operation Nordwind ended in failure. Wingen sits in a small valley along the Moder River between Bitche to the north and Saverne to the south. Described in the following bucolic terms, “...the village and the surrounding area looked very much like a winter scene one might expect to find on a Christmas card back home. Smoke rising from a few chimneys made the houses appear warm and inviting...” Wingen would erupt on 3 January 1945 and become the focal-point of Operation Nordwind.

This module consists of eight scenarios, all playable on the Wingen-sur-Moder historical map. The map seeks to recreate this wooded region, and specifically allow for the investigation of combat problems inherent to a built-up area with river, bridges, and buildings laid out realistically in a manner unlike that which can be depicted on geomorphic boards. Welcome to the NORDIC TWILIGHT*, a map-based VARIANT module that depicts the fierce fighting in the Low Vosges by the 6th SS Mountain Division during Operation Nordwind. NORDIC TWILGIHT contains eight scenarios, all playable on the new map included. You also two copies of two new die-cut counter sheets and an expanded 28 page SR and historical notes booklet. Bring your own ASLRB, markers and Americans and have at it.

Welcome to the ON THE ROAD TO BATAAN, a historical VARIANT module that depicts the fierce fighting for the Abucay/Mabatang Line from 11-15 January, 1942. Situated on the on the Bataan Peninsula, this position ran through Mauban on the coast of Manila Bay (east side) to Mabatang on the coast of the South China sea. ON THE ROAD TO BATAAN contains eight scenarios, all playable on the historical map included. You also receive a set of Special Rules and two copies of the new die-cut counter sheet (a total of 269 color die-cut counters) with Philippine Scouts SMC/MMC, new SW, etc.

From review at DesperationMorale.com:

Add-on scenarios for Advanced Squad Leader featuring Australian troops, with a few other surprises.

In early 2011, Break Contact emerged from a period of relative inactivity to release two new items, one of which is this product, The Aussie Battler Pack (ABP), a scenario pack very much in the mold of Break Contact! and The Armoured Aussie Pack.   It has an emphasis on actions involving Australian troops, with a few trips further afield.

From the publisher's catalogue:

VROENHOVEN, BELGIUM, 11 May 1940: By mid-afternoon of 10 May, infantry elements of the 4th Panzer Division took over the bridgehead at Vroenhoven, relieving the exhausted paratroopers. During the evening, the infantry pushed west to free the main road and north to join with the Velwezelt bridgehead. Two platoons of the 2nd Company were all that remained of the Belgian troops near the Vroenhoven Bridge. On the morning of the 11th, the Germans started to attack towards the south to completely secure the bridge area. As the lead panzer elements were crossing the bridge, three Fairey Battle aircraft of the Belgian 5Ëme Escadrille appeared over the Albert Canal and began a bombing run against the bridge. Because their initial mission was to bomb wooden footbridges, the planes were loaded with puny 50kg bombs instead of the more potent 250kg bombs normally available. Lead by Captain Glorie, the three Battles made a first bombing run though withering anti-aircraft fire. Due to either the wounding of the observer or a malfunction of the bomb release system, Battle number T-70 of Captain Glorie, followed by the T-63 of Adjudant Delvigne, turned about and made a second run. Both were shot down after hitting the bridge. Une vaine mission de sacrifice!

Welcome to FACING THE BLITZ, an updated edition of the Albert Canal map and scenarios that were included in CH 7/1. A second edition map was printed after the initial print run was sold out and is included with this version. No worries, if you already own the map, a 'no map' edition is also being made available so you can get the new counters and updated scenarios and save some cash.

Welcome to the BoB NORMANDY, a map-based VARIANT module that depicts the fierce fighting in the Bocage by the 101st Airborne Division on D-Day and beyond. BoB NORMANDY contains eight scenarios, all playable on the new map included. You also two copies of the new die-cut counter sheet (a total of 269 color die-cut counters) with 101st AB SMC/MMC, new counters for M5A1 trailers (such as the one they carried John Wayne around in during the film, "The Longest Day"), some extra counters for the HB3 crowd, a new German truck type, etc. All in all, the 101'st Screaming Eagles depicted in the film "Band of Brothers" and the seminal unit history, "Rendevous With Destirny" will literally drop off the sprue on to your Normandy battlefield ... and you can be sure we'll pump out more scenarios to follow Winters, Guarnere, Toy, Talbert and company ... as they move on to new battlefields from Market Garden to the Bulge.

In addition to your 8 scenario BoB NORMANDY set, you'll also receive an additional 2 FREE BONUS scenarios, playable on the new map PLUS another FREE BONUS set of 2 Brécourt Manor scenarios PLUS the large-hex Brécourt Manor historical map. What's more, you'll not only get all this FREE gear but you'll get it at a special price for a limited time only (and see above for the Priority Mail postage-only deal you can take advantage of).

A checkers-family game played on a 4x8 board.  Each player has twelve pieces and the object is to force one's opponent to capture all one's pieces.

Callaway checkers was invented by LeMar Callaway in a dream in 1996 (reference:  "He's got game", Ellyde Roko, The Virginian-Pilot, August 3, 2003.)  It is described in U.S. patent 6588753.

El Guettar, Tunisia, 23 March 1943: As soon as the movements of the enemy 10th Panzer Division were recognized as a major counter-attack Lieutenant Colonel Maxwell A. Tinscher's 899th Tank Destroyer Battalion, equipped with the new M10s, was ordered to support the hard-pressed 601st. Leaving behind A' Company in reserve, the 899th moved out at 0845 hours in a full-throttle dash to meet the Germans. Captain Kirk Adam's B' Company was in the lead and reached El Guettar just as Stukas worked over the area. Following a meeting between Colonel Tinscher, his S-3 Captain Joseph Morrison and the CO of the 601st T.D. Battalion, B' Company was ordered into the pass immediately, without waiting for C' Company. At 0955 B' Company sped into the valley floor between hills, immediately drawing fire. One destroyer was disabled on a minefield. The rest reorganized, moved off the road, and amid heavy fire from artillery, anti-tank and small arms, focused on enemy armor. Their 3-inch guns proved deadly, destroying ten Mark IVs, two anti-tank guns, and damaging two other Panzers in exchange for three M-10s burned and two disabled. For his action, Lieutenant Coady was awarded the Silver Star...

ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: RELUCTANT OFFENSIVE is a detailed new ASLComp VARIANT that tells the story of the War in North Africa. It's about time the desert warfare goodness that was spawned by Hal Hock's seminal work TOBRUK back in 1975... was brought to the gaming tables of ASLers. And now it is, in a big way.

The ninth portion of the set-piece is ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: Reluctant Offensive and takes the action into early 1941 and the first offensive to capture a city that would become a watchword later in the war: "Tobruk". The action culminates with the Battle of Beda Fomm on a map that recreates the battlefield as well as the long out of print historical map, using the overlay set. This set of 8 scenarios also bring the FORT OVERLAY, depicting Fort Mechili into play. Can you get the Italian garrison in the fort to surrender? We'll leave that to you, your dice and your tactics.

You receive a set of 8 scenarios, all depicting actions from the exciting offensive, rife with combined-arms actions. And yes, these are aimed at playability. Slap them down, bang them out, do it again. And again. Please do a little hooting and hollering while you're at it... at least when you roll 'snakeyes' (or the other guy rolls 'boxcars').

ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS—RELUCTANT OFFENSIVE contains:

    8 action-packed scenarios depicting actions from Rommel's drive into Cyrenaica 
    Color cover sheet 
    Descriptive back sheet 
    Ziploc style storage bag 
    All the interest in the War in North Africa that you have been waiting for someone to show you 


WEST OF ALAMEIN and BEYOND VALOR REQUIRED FOR USE + ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS CORE PACKS 1-2 ALSO NEEDED FOR PLAY

Tank Commander is a collectible card wargame covering tactical combat on the Eastern Front during WW2 designed by John Desch and Ulrich Blennemann and firsts published by Moments in History in 1996.

Each deck contains 60 cards, a small 56 page rule book (don't gasp -- you can grasp play concepts in seconds). Two decks are the minimum required for play. Six decks maximize the experience.

Each card, designed by Beth Queman, represents a vehicle, (tank, half-track, armored car, truck) infantry or calvary squad, AT gun and crew, special equipment (like a Panzerfaust), terrain type and special events. There are a total of 164 different card types in 3 different levels of rarity. There's some minor card errata (nothing serious) and a list of all types on Web-Grognards. Each card has a color picture, short description, and different numbers representing game factors.

Object of the game is both to invent the definition of a word in order to bluff the other players, and also to find the true
definition.

Players place their tokens to the "Start"-space.
In his turn player draws one "Kamizako" card and sets out to define the word (be careful not to specify its spelling). Each player must then draw its own definition of his paper: it can let your imagination time to an hourglass. The card are mixed
and the Game Master must read each definition, giving them a number of way, without arousing suspicions of the other players in skinning a word. Then each player must write the number of the definition he thinks is the correct definition. When a player vote for the true definition or when other players vote for its definition, player aheads 2 spaces on the board.

Player who finish first is the winner.

Objective: gain as many points as possible finding correct answers in time
 - acting by turn -
Agree setting limit: elapsed time or no. of winning points
On each players turn, the player draws a card with a theme on it, then a letter is drawn. 
Time runs. Every player has to find words for the chosen term with the given letter.

A very rules-lite medieval/fantasy wargame system.
This game uses 6-sided dice for combat resolution and morale. Units included are warriors, levies, knights and archers. Mounted units and leader challenges are included. Simple rules to allow for common fantasy races are included.
Being a very rules-lite system (just 4 pages) it does not include units' experience advancements, campaign rules or siege machines. It allows the players to raise armies and play easily on an evening at the dinner table.

Object of the game is to be the richest player at the end of the game robbing the banks.

Every round the convoy brings to bank 500 dollars. Players take turns to play the villians with value 1-5. Your goal is to successfully collect the most points with a single bandit. To do this you have the right to exchange your cards once. After the second round, who has the strongest villian wins this phase.

Winner takes the 5 dice and try to roll the 3-digits code of the conwoy to get all the money.

The object of the game is to get rid of Your cards first.

Each card has a large number in the middle and, below, an operation. From these data, we deduce the number of the next card what next player have to play. Some cards have special moves, sometimes using dice for calculating sum.

New invention of chess game form traditional chess of South East Asian Countries. The game adapts from Makruk (Thai Chess), Ouk Chatrang (Cambodian Chess) and Sittuyin (Burmese Chess). Those have very similar chess style and have some minor difference.

The game is declared on March 14th 2011 at ASEAN-Chess council, Bangkok, Thailand.

The game applies some Chess's rules to those tradition chess variants in ASEAN (such as Ouk Chatrang, Makruk, Sittuyin) for easier to settle the tournaments and record the results.

Some different rules between Chess and ASEAN-chess such as
- Bishop is called as Elephant
- Pawns start at the 3rd row and can be promoted to be Queen (and only Queen) at the 8th row
- Queen can move only 1 diagonal space
- Bishop (Elephant) can move 1 space diagonally or 1 space forward
- No Castling
- Use counting rules when neither side has any pawns and both players agree to use counting rules

Note: ASEAN stand for "Association of South East Asia Nations"

Rekorde im Tierreich Memory is a memory and trivia game played with the tiptoi. 
The game can be played in different modes. The goal of the game is always the same: to find pairs of animals.
Tha cards are laid out face down.
By turn, players touch 2 cards with the tiptoi. Depending on the mode, you hear the call of that animal, an exact characterization of the animal, or just some vague information about the animal, for example: "it can swim."  
If a player picks two identical cards, he scores them.
After all cards are scored, the player with the most cards is the winner.

Besides the games, you can also just learn about the animals by having the tiptoi "read aloud" facts about the animals.

Children will have great fun with the lectron junior developing their powers of observation, visual discrimination and shape recognition. as they play, children will also learn to follow rules and develop their social skills. In addition to explaining how the game works, parents and educators can also make exelent playmates and enhance the game's educational value. The purpose of the game is to recognize various shapes and numbers and connect them to each other using the magnetic stick. when the shapes and numbers are matched correct, a light will flash on on the magnetic stick.

Objective:
be the first to collect all the slides (10) which match with the drawings on your own board
 - acting by turn -
roll the six-sided (5 symbols + joker)
if you see e.g. the symbol "smell"  you have to choose a slide 
1. which is printed on your board
2. which refers to 'smell'
before you place it on the board - turn it, because on the back you see if you were wrong or right

boardgame: Stratak Wars: Der Auftakt / The Beginning
description:
Stratak Wars - Der Auftakt / The Beginning is a strategic- and tactical boardgame for 2-6 players. Stratak Wars is especially interesting for fans of easy wargames and for consim beginners. It offers rules for beginners and advanced players.

Stratak Wars has 6 different double sided mapboards to create variable playing fields. The counters represent tanks, fighters, bombers and landing ships of World War II of the nations USA, Japan, Great Britain, Italy, Russia and Germany. The objective is to conquer most of the cities within a playing time as agreed at the beginning of the game. So the playing time is flexible and can be decided by the players.

Each player starts with his own state on one of the gameboards and expands his state by conquering cities on the playing field.  Depending on the units he chooses for combat, he has advantages against the units of other players which are influenced by the landscape as well concerning movement and combat.  Supply points are gained for recently conquered cities only. So you have to move forward, if you want to expand your armies.

The rules include basic rules, advanced rules, alternative rules, a sequence of play description, a display section for the landscapes, cities and a counter description.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Objective:
be the first to deliver Elia 6 x with food (bread and meat)
win the impression that God is taking care for us
acting by turn
you are a smart raven who tries to find bread/meat
after you got it choose a fast route to fly to Elia
don't loose food - otherwise you must return
each time you deliver food successfully you win a card which explains how God is taking care for us
game ends if someone has collected 6 slides

produced by Ravensburger

boardgame: Super Showdown: DISTRESS Kickstarter Exclusive Card
description:
The DISTRESS Kickstarter Exclusive Card is an optional addition to the Super Showdown game that introduces an interesting change to game play.

Rules:
- Shuffle DISTRESS with all other cards & deal as normal, leaving the 19th card unrevealed.  If you do not recieve 5 of one color - your nemesis will!
- When DISTRESS is played, the opposing card is victorious!

This card was an exclusive reward for Kickstarter backers who helped fund the initial Super Showdown project.  It will never be available again in any way.

However, 120 was the "advertised" card count during the Kickstarter project, so the missing Mooks that were eliminated were printed as additional promo cards for loyal and generous backers (and pre-order customers).

The 10 Missing Mooks are:

     Roberto "Rocky" Rizzoto
     "Giggy" Franchese
     Nicky "Nails" Nastassi
     "Big Head" Mikey Altimari
     Nicky "Glasses" Zicarelli
     Johnny "Beach Ball" Baioni
     Ralphie "Bubbles" Bendinelli
     Angelo "Sockets" Milano
     Bruno "The Rug" Pacelli
     Petey "Popeye" Panzarino


These Mooks are added to the game and used just like any other Mook.

boardgame: Dead Fellas: The Original Four Promo Cards
description:
"The Original Four Promo Cards" for Deadfellas were originally available to Kickstarter funders who helped fund the project.

This set of promo cards includes the following four (4) additional Mooks:

     "Six Gun" Myrforio
     "Whak Whak" Brioni
     Gieffe "Freakin" Comboso
     Secchiano "Machine" Torre


These Mooks can be added to the game and used just like any other Mook.

Dark Galaxies is a tactical dexterity game of spaceship combat. Players maneuver their ships and fire lasers and drone missiles by flicking discs across the play surface. Unlike many other flicking games, Dark Galaxies emphasizes the importance of ship orientation; how you position your ships will determine which weapons you fire, where you take damage, and what your movement potential is going forward. It takes only minutes to learn but offers a deep combat experience.

Dark Galaxies is currently in prototype and is slated for publication in 2012.

Space Flick is the placeholder name for a tactical space combat game. In Space Flick, up to five players represent the elite captains of small star nations. Those nations have just learned that the evil empire that dominates their area of the galaxy is engaged in a major conquest, leaving behind only a fraction of their fleet for defense. The captains decide to launch a daring raid on the heart of the enemy, hoping to destroy a key space station integral to its military efforts. If they succeed, their worlds may be free; failure would be bad.

Bad, unless you're the person playing the evil empire.

Space Flick plays through a series of encounters as the captains move closer to their goal, culminating in a final battle. Game play is fast-paced and easy to learn. Movement and combat is resolved by flicking discs across the playing surface.

boardgame: Die Lieben Sieben: Mein erstes Brettspiel
description:
Objective: be the first completing three rounds on the board 
finishing the puzzle in front of you
 - moving by turn -
a bit like Pachisi, but the game is designed for the very young ones - only 1 meeple -
roll the (six-sided-)die - move your pawn
BUT..  you only may move if you know the symbol/theme covered by vour pawn (memory) 
if you meet another pawn you only may move (and hit) if you know the symbol the other is covering, too
if you've finished a round get one of 3 pieces you need to complete your puzzle

Objective:
be the first gaining a complete set (4) of cards with the same
image
 - acting by turn -
its like Memory but with following varieties:
 - you are collecting only one set of 4 cards
 - currently you are on the beach ==> due to wind and waves
   slides becoming mixed again by time

remarks:
belongs to a series of so called 'Mitbringspielen' (small present games)

The players set up tourism companies from hotel chains, restaurants, clubs and holiday airlines and try to get 100 millions of money first. The player rolls and moves in his turn, also he takes a card. On a 4-star field he may buy or sell or trade, the prices will be determined by the rate table.

Cards are shuffled, the quizmaster reads the question from the card and the current player answers. If he answers correctly, he gets the number of points credited. If the answer is wrong, the number of points will be deducted. Player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

There are 55 cards in this game each with 5 questions and answers.

Player roll the die in his turn and asks the question according the die, if "6" is rolled he can choose the question.
Player who answer correctly first gets the card. Player who has collected five cards first wins

Originally released in 2010 at FFG's Days of Ice and Fire event, these six special promotional commander cards for Battles of Westeros introduce optional, alternate abilities for Eddard Stark, Tywin Lannister, Jaime Lannister, and others. Field these commander cards, and watch your enemies’ battle lines break!

The german edition ofthe cards are found in the "Fachhandelsausgabe" of the german magazine "Tabletop Insider Nr.7".
