boardgame: Jonas On Tour CD Board Game
description:
This is a game about the Jonas Brothers singing group.

The object of the game is to gather autographed pictures of the boys. Players representing fans move around a star-shaped pathway while tokens representing the Jonas kids jump across the interior spaces.

Includes a colorful board, the CD tokens, cards, rules, and a storage insert.

Designed for 2, 3, or 4 players ages 8 or older.

The introduction to the rule book says "'DARTABOUT' is a really good game and one which we feel sure will be universally popular. .. it has only five simple rules of play .. The object of the game is for each player to move his five men across the board from his own 'starts' and occupy the 'starts' of the player opposite to him, doing as much 'damage' to his opponents as is compatible with skilful play."

As with the same maker's Sorry!, pieces are moved via playing cards. Some ('straight') cards move a piece forwards so many squares, other ('bent') cards mean the piece must make a 90 degree turn as part of its move. Three special cards exist: '69' can be treated either as a straight six or a bent nine; '8' allow two pieces to be moved a combined total of eight spaces forward; and '1' allows a piece to move one square forwards or sideways. Opposing pieces not on safety square which are landed on are 'bumped' off onto an unfavourable start square.

The main game involves dealing out the deck between the players who can then pick which card to play each turn. If the game has not finished, the discards are shuffled and dealt again. A version for children involves picking cards at random to play each turn.

User review: This is to seven-card stud poker as Autobridge is to bridge.

This game simulates playing seven-card stud with six players. Slide one of the game sheets into the game board. Slide the first tab of all the players to see what each player was dealt and how each decided to bet. The player decides what to do without risking real money.

The game cones with fifteen sheets for a total of thirty games.

Sparks is a game that can be played with the Series: Shibumi game system. It uses the 4×4 board, 8 white, 8 black, and 14 red balls.

				
				
					RulesThe board (the fireplace) starts filled with white and black balls in an alternating pattern.

The red balls (the sparks) are kept ready to hand.

Two players, White and Black, are taking turns by carefully putting a spark from the supply and a ball of their own colour from the board (the coal ball) into the hand.

Coal can only be taken if it isn’t pinned, i.e. it is free or it supports not more than one other ball on the level above.


     If the coal ball was free, the player fills the emptied space with the spark and plays the coal on any other place.
     If the coal ball was not free, a supported ball will just drop into place:
         If a spark dropped, the player returns the spark in the hand to the supply and plays only the coal ball on any place.
         If a white or black coal ball dropped, the player plays both balls in the hand on any place and in any desired order.
    


The player who places a ball of their own colour on top of the pyramid (the fire cone) wins the game.

Publisher Blurb:

Serve up some fun with America's favorite word game.
 The Cooking Edition of Scrabble includes classic Scrabble fun with a cooking twist allowing players to earn bonus points for words related to everyone’s favorite past time. Recipe cards are the perfect mix as players double and triple their scores with directives for all new custom game play. Become the master chef as you spell your way to victory with tasty abbreviations and names of your favorite celebrity chefs.
 Can you spell D-E-L-I-S-H?

Publisher Blurb:

Plant some fun and watch it grow with America’s favorite crossword game!
 The Gardening Edition of Scrabble is classic Scrabble fun with a Gardening twist! Your score will blossom when you earn bonus points for gardening-related words such as orchid, cedar, topsoil and more! Play Seed Packet cards to double and triple your score with directives for all new custom game play. Show off your green thumb as you strategically spell your way to victory with abbreviations such as ARS (American Rose Society) and names of famous gardens such as Boboli or Exbury. Let the fun bloom!

Publisher Blurb:

Deck the halls with America’s favorite word game.
 The Holiday Edition of Scrabble includes classic Scrabble fun with a festive twist allowing players to earn bonus points for words related to everyone’s favorite time of year. Gift Tag cards add holiday cheer as players enhance their scores with directives for all new custom game play. Spell your way to victory with holiday-themed words including all of your favorite things. Can you spell F-A-L-A-L-A?

A game of dogsled racing in the artic. Players compete to reach the goal first. In the race you will find perils and other sort of problems in the artic you will have to overcome. Players will try to stop you with action cards during the race.

It is a ruleset for 15mm or 28mm miniatures to race dogsled across snowed paths.

The ruleset comes with everything needed to play from the rules to the action cards, rulers and some merchandising banners.

Objective: be the first to pass finish line (140)
 - moving by turn/simple 'Roll and Move' game -
each player throws the die, moves according rolled number, follows instructions (cover inside) landing on a marked space

This is the kids version of the classic game Rummikub. There are three levels of play to gradually increase the difficulty.
The object of the game is to be the first player to use all the tiles that are on your rack. Tiles come in 4 different color sets: red, blue, yellow and green. Each set contains the numbers 1 through 10.

The player have to collect a set of 3 (or 2) tiles to place it on the table. One can also add his tiles to the sets on the table. The first player to have no tiles left on his/her rack wins.

Rummikub for Kids develops number and color recognition skills, teaches players how to build sets of consecutive numbers and logical reasoning. This first Rummikub set is cleverly designed with shapes that help children self-correct their moves.

What a wonderful alternative to simple picture matching memo games.  In this clever version, children must collect the most pairs of the animal and its shadow. A bit of extra concentration is required though, because there are 2 cards of the same animal, but in slightly different poses!!! A great twist on an old favourite.

A family trade game. Jack goes to the market with three cows. Trade them as best as you can, and when the market is closed, see how much you've made of them.
The goal of the game is to trade your three cows before the market closed. You can trade them for chickens, pigs or beans...
Each player takes his turn by trading one of his good with one good on any merchant stands.
When a merchant has the three of the same item in his stand, he closes it. When all the stands are closed, the game ends. 
You gain three points for every cow, two points for every pig, one point for every chicken, no points for the bean, unless you have three of them, then you score 10 points. They are the magical beans of the Jack and the Beanstalk story.

Your goal is to lead animal kids to their mummy who is waiting on the island on the other side of backwater. Animals can move on the islands as well as on the wooden logs that swim in the water. The logs are changing their possitions all the time and the crocodile can appear.

This game is part of the Series: Shibumi family.

Starting with a given position, each player takes turns as follows:

1. The player rearranges the free marbles corresponding to his colour in a new position on the board. In such way that every marble of his colour is connected and at least one of his balls maintains its previous position.

2. If a 2x2 square is formed with three marbles of the player's colour and
- one red ball, then the red ball is replaced by one of the player's colour.
- one of the other player's balls, then this ball is retired from the board.

3. A new red marble appears randomly on the board

Now the next player's turn begins.

The game ends when the pyramid is completed. The player with most marbles of his colour on the board is declared the winner.

Game made of 48 tiles. Each tile tile is made of 4 squares with all the manners to combine three colors.
Many rules are possible.

The first rules are :

     Quadracarta MiniSurf
     Quadracarta MaxiSurf

Each Player is given a card of a Famous Character which has a number of clues about them,
Players travel the Board landing on spaces which allows them to get clues about their opponents Character,
Some clues have to be read out aloud so everyone benefits but some are only given to you.
The First Player to guess there opponent's identity wins a series of points ,
The Winner is the one who has the most points after a certain time or one who has reached a Target number of points.

This is a great game for all ages but especially for children who are fans of the BBC programme Blue Peter, which has been on for so many years, it is now an institution. In true Blue Peter style, it is fun and educational,
When you answer a question correctly you are given a piece of your jigsaw and the aim is to complete your jigsaw of the world first. The questions are on the people and places that Blue Peter has explored during the programme's history.
The questions range from quite easy to challenging but children are guarranteed to learn some facts while they play.

From the publisher:
This is a local Estonian game made by a Palamuse museum. This is a simple roll and move game where players race to finish the nature walk and learn most about the nature around Palamuse.
Track is filled with various helping and blocking tasks.

Be first to finish and own at least 2 pawns to win the game.

Game is promotional game for Palamuse museum with a print run of 25.

1st & Goal: Mideast Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice that make up four custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Mideast division includes the following teams:

• Birmingham Black Widows – The Black Widows enter the league of 24 inaugural teams with a top 10-ranked running and passing games. Despite a strong offense, the Black Widows are in danger of getting stomped on defense. Coach Harris Gray states, "We spent all our money on offense. Our defensive squad is pretty much all volunteers." Nose Guard Mildred Dinkins had this to say: "I just go out there every Sunday and do my best. Can I say 'hi' to my grandson?"

• Columbus Cobras – The Cobras enter the league with the 10th-ranked running game and the 16th-ranked passing game. Not only do the Cobras enter the league with a top-ten running game, their defense stands at 9th in the rankings and is one of the most consistent. Coach Bobby "Bam" Boozell states, "I'm so proud of our defense. That's a tough group of young men. They may not be the best out there, but we rarely have any surprises." As mentioned, the Cobras have a top tier running game, led by running back, Cole Kutz. Mr. Kutz had not released a statement by press time. Rumors report that he was out looking for a sandwich endorsement.

• Scranton Scorchers – The Scorchers enter the league with the 7th-ranked running game and the 19th-ranked passing game. Despite an inconsistent offense, the Scorchers have a top eleven defense. Coach Gus Tufwin states, "Right now I'm afraid our passing game blows. But our defense comes in like a hurricane, so hold on to your hats!" Wide Receiver Buddy Fingers commented: "Coach makes a lot of noise about our passing problems, but I'm quick as lightning, man, fast as the wind. I just have a problem holding on to the ball sometimes."

• Toronto Thunder – The Thunder enter the league with the 17th-ranked running game and a bottom ranked passing game. Despite a weak offense, the Thunder have a top ten defense. Coach Hammond Ecks states, "We're going to hatch some surprise plays that should have the other teams scrambling to catch us." Quarterback Cuba Sugar commented: "I got the sweet moves, baby, but I'm not afraid to hang out there and take a lump or two myself."

1st & Goal: Northeast Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice which make up 4 custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Northeast Division includes the following teams:

• Boston Bombers - The Bombers enter the league with the 9th ranked running game and the 20th ranked passing game. Despite a lopsided offense, the Bombers have a top twenty defense. Coach Pete Sapalla states, "We’re pretty good on the ground, I expect us to roll through most defenses and slice ‘em up." Leading rusher Pat Riot commented: "Coach has some revolutionary new plays that give me a lot of liberty on the field."

• Manchester Marauders - The Marauders enter the league with the #1 ranked running game and the 10th ranked passing game. Despite a strong offense, the Marauders have a less than stellar defense. Coach Frank Merrill states, "We’re going to put up a heckuva fight this year. We don’t have much support in the air department so you’ll see a lot of emphasis on the ground game." Star running back Hugh Mungus commented: "Just give me the ball.  I’ll find a way through the lines. "

• Newark Nighthawks - The Nighthawks enter the league with the 21st ranked running game and the 18th ranked passing game. Despite a weak offense, the Nighthawks have the #2 defense. Coach Mack Aroni states, "Sometimes I get a little cheesed off at our half baked offense, but our defense? Boy are deze guys good. No meatballs on that squad." Defensive end Bubba Ganooj commented: "We can take whatever the opposition dishes out. I’ll mash up anybody who gets in my way."

• Rochester Rockets - The Rockets enter the league with the 20th ranked running game and the 3rd ranked passing game. Despite an uneven offense, the Rockets do have a top ten defense. Coach Red Glare states, "We have a really explosive passing game. Our new quarterback can really launch it." Quarterback Rocco Gibralter commented: "Thanks to me and my arm, we have a pretty solid offense this year. I pretty much stay in the pocket and fire."

1st & Goal: Midwest Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice, which make up 4 custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Midwest Division includes the following teams:

• Knoxville Knights – The Knights enter the league with the 2nd ranked running game and the 9th ranked passing game.  While they are able to mount a solid offense, the Knights are shielded by a less than chivalrous defense. Coach Arthur Roundtable states, "The football is our queen and we will protect it." Quarterback Lance Lott states: "Yeah, well I'm the king! Protect me! Me!"

• Louisville Locusts – The Locusts enter the league with the 8th ranked running game and the #1 ranked passing game. Despite an incredible offense, the Locusts have one of the lowest ranked defenses in the league. Coach Paul Ishtoff states, "Our team puts points on the board. Period. You can't stop us on the ground or in the air. Unfortunately, our defense bugs me." Quarterback Rick O’Shea, the mastermind behind their brilliant passing game, had this to say: "We have a very simple formula for success. Our offense only has to put more points on the board than our defense gives away. Then it's all gravy!"

• Milwaukee Monsters – The Monsters enter the league with the 15th ranked running game and the 6th ranked passing game. Despite less-than-stellar talent in running, the Monsters have one of the best passing games in the league. Coach Frankie Stein states, "We definitely need to work on our ground game, but when the Monsters put the ball in the air, we are scary." Quarterback Wolfgang Mann could not be reached for comment. When asked about this, wide receiver Billy Phantom replied, "Yea, sometimes he disappears for days at a time. By the way, did you see that moon last night? Beautiful!"

• Springfield Spartans – The Spartans enter the league with the 23rd ranked running game and the 15th ranked passing game. Despite troubles on offense, the Spartans have the #3 ranked defense in the league. Coach Colin Toksho states, "We rely on our defense to get us out of trouble. Unfortunately, we're usually in trouble." When asked about the lukewarm running attack, running back Jackie Mollasses commented, "I may not be the fastest runner in the league… what was the question?"

1st & Goal: Southeast Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice which make up 4 custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Southeast Division includes the following teams:

• Columbia Crocodiles - The Crocodiles enter the league with the 13th ranked running game and the 13th ranked passing game. Coach Al Egatar states, "Our team has a lot of heart. Our boys give it everything they've got. I just wish they had more." Quarterback, Duane Pipe, remarks, "Coach says we have to leave it all on the field. I don't know what that means."

• Raleigh Reapers - The Reapers enter the league with the 15th ranked running game and 23rd ranked passing game. Despite a lackluster offense, the Reapers do have a deadly defense. Coach Harry Grimm states, "We don't put tons of points on the board, but our defense will bury you." Kicker Doug Graves had this to say: "Settle for three, bring on the D, baby!"

• Savannah Savages - The Savages enter the league with the 24th ranked running game and the 12th ranked passing game. Despite a weak offense, the Savages have a top ten defense. Coach Brock Lee states, "I get a little steamed at times, but really, we’re not as green as we look". Defensive Lineman Terry Heddoff commented: "Grrr! Arrgh! Grrr! ".

• Virginia Beach Valiants - The Valiants enter the league with the 19th ranked running game and the 2nd ranked passing game. In addition to their one-dimensional offense, the Valiants have lower than average defense. Coach Bjorn Toulouse states, "Our offense likes to put the ball in the air. Less fumbles that way." Quarterback Orin Julius commented: "Passing is like punting. Except we get to keep the ball. Mostly."

1st & Goal: Northwest Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice which make up 4 custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Northwest division includes the following teams:

• Boulder Barrage - The Barrage enters the league with the 22nd ranked running game and the 17th ranked passing game. Despite a rocky offense, the Barrage have a top 5 defense. Coach M. T. Noggin states, "We emphasize defense on our squad. Sometimes we emphasize the first syllable. Sometimes we emphasize the last syllable. We like to mix things up." Left Tackle, Uno Ochoocho had this to say: "Man, they should have called us the Boulder Boulders. That would have been SO cool!"

• Portland Pounders - The Pounders enter the league with the 6th ranked running game and the 4th ranked passing game. Despite a great offense, the Pounders have the 21st ranked defense. Coach Joe King states, "We're very proud of our solid offense. Those boys put in a lot of hours and they're ready to take the field and put points on the board. We love our defense too, but we're not proud of them." Quarterback Jack Ammer had this to say: "We pound you, see? That's why we're the Pounders. It's not like the unit of weight thing. Pound like da verb. Not da noun."

•  Salt Lake City Shakers - The Shakers enter the league with the 4th ranked running game and the 7th ranked passing game. Despite a great offense, the Shakers have the 22nd ranked defense. Coach Harris Gray states, "It's all about clock control for us. As long as we have the ball, they can't score." Quarterback Bruce Sezesley had this to say: "We're looking forward to winning a lot of games in the RFL this year. Losing -- not so much."

•  St. Paul Paladins - The Paladins enter the league with the 18th ranked running game and the 22nd ranked passing game. Despite troubles on offense, the Paladins have the #1 ranked defense in the league. Coach Gus Undheit states, "Our defense is key to our success. We have trouble running the ball. We have trouble passing the ball. Sometimes we can't even find the ball. But bless us, our defense keeps us in the game." When asked about the lukewarm passing attack, Quarterback Stan Downwind had this to say: "I strive not to be the worst that I can be. That's how I live my life and how I play my game."

1st & Goal: Southwest Division, an expansion for 1st & Goal, contains 28 dice which make up 4 custom teams. The Dice Football League (DFL) Southwest Division includes the following teams

• Las Vegas Vampires - The Vampires enter the league with the 11th ranked running game and the 14th ranked passing game, out of our 24 inaugural teams. Despite an average running game and an average passing game, the Vampires have a defense that is, well, pretty average. Coach Pete Zaria states, "The best thing you can say about the Vampires is that we don't suck at anything..." Quarterback Drew Blood had this to say: "You think it's easy being average? It's not like being great where you can get better. Or terrible, where you can get worse. There's a definite ceiling when it comes to mediocrity and we have reached it."

• Los Angeles Leopards - The Leopards enter the league with the 17th ranked running game and the 21st ranked passing game. Despite a questionable offense, the Leopards have the 4th ranked defense. Coach Anthony Detiger states, "Our defense wins games.  They’re… pretty good! That's our motto. Points are way overrated." Defensive Lineman Oswald Matung had this to say: "We're gonna shake 'em and bake 'em and earthquake 'em! We're gonna rip 'em and crack 'em and heart attack 'em! That's the plan, anyway."

• Oklahoma City Ogres - The Ogres enter the league with the 3rd ranked running game and the 8th ranked passing game. Sadly, despite a top shelf offense, their defense stands at 24th in the rankings. Coach Jim Locher states, "I won't say we have the worst defense in the league. I'll leave that up to you guys." As mentioned, the Ogres do have a phenomenal running game, led by running back Izzy Foreel.  Foreel stated, "When you got a runner like me you don't need no defense no how."

• San Antonio Scorpions - The Scorpions enter the league with the 13th ranked running game and the 11th ranked passing game. The Scorpions have the 14th ranked defense. Coach Sal Minella states, "We don't believe in putting our eggs in one basket. We prefer to spread the eggs around. Anywhere you look on our team you're gonna find an egg. I like eggs. I like 'em scrambled." Quarterback Juan Fordemonay had this to say: "You didn't talk to Coach did ya? I don't know what Coach is talking about."

An n-in-a-row game with a twist for the Shibumi Game System.

The board starts empty. Two players take turns handing a ball to their opponent to place, either on any empty board space or on any 2x2 platform. A player may not hand their opponent a ball of the color just placed.

Example: Player 1 hands a Red ball to Player 2, who places it on the board. Player 2 may then hand a Black or White ball to Player 1, who places it on the board.

A player wins by making a full Black or White line, orthogonally or diagonally, on any level, on their turn.   A Red piece counts as both Black and White.  The winning line must contain at least one not-Red ball.

A full line on the 4x4 (board) level must be of length 4, on the 3x3 level must be of length 3, and on the 2x2 level must be of length 2.

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

 Iosara makes three consecutive Attacks. If the first causes damage, the target suffers Seal Lv. 1, the second causes Blind Lv. 1, and the third attack causes Doom Lv. 1. Each of these states cause a separate resistance check vs. Effect 15.
 
IOSARA Unborn
 Specter: If Iosara is reduced to 0 or less LP, it becomes a specter instead of being destroyed. While a
 specter, Iosara becomes Intangible, cannot recover LP through any means, and is removed from play if it takes any damage.
 Wandering Shadow: Iosara is not deployed normally at the start of the game. At the beginning of each Recovery Phase roll a die, on a 9+ you put Iosara with all its Actions Points anywhere on the board that is not within 2 inches of an enemy Unit. When rolling for Wandering Shadow, apply the following modifiers:

 Power Absorption: When Iosara destroys an enemy Unit, choose a Magic or Ki ability from the Unit. Iosara gains a copy of that ability. Iosara uses the ability as though it was on its card. All costs, benefits, and drawbacks of the copied ability remain the same. Iosara may only copy one ability at a time, so if later in the game it destroys another enemy Unit it may copy a new ability replacing the old one or retain the old one.

boardgame: Anima Tactics: Samiel, The Black Lion
description:
The Black Lion of Abel is the darkest agent of Elisabetta Barbados. Feeding from death itself and commanding a legion of ghostly beasts, he is a tortured soul torn between his dark destiny that forces him to kill to survive and his own guilt.

Halloween Dice comes with a set of big orange-and-black six-siders, with custom Jack o' Lantern designs on each face. Your goal: roll those dice and try to reach the lucky total of 13. (Normally 13 is considered an unlucky number, but for Halloween, when darkness and otherworldly things are celebrated, luck stands on its head.)

On a turn, you choose one, two, or three dice and receive respectively $2, $5, or $9, with money being represented by tokens. You then roll those dice. If the sum on the dice equals 13, you end your turn and keep the money you collected. If the total is less than 13, you can stop and pay $1 times the difference between your sum and 13, or you can again choose 1-3 dice (receiving more money) and roll again, adding the newly rolled sum to your previous sum. You again face the same options of stopping or choosing more dice and rolling again. If your sum goes over 13, however, your turn ends and you pay $2 times the difference between your sum and 13!

The player with the most money after three rounds wins.

boardgame: Invasion of Germany: A Panzer Grenadier Scenario Book
description:
From the Avalanche Press website:

After chasing the beaten remnants of the German armies across France and Belgium, American soldiers forced their way onto German soil. With thick lines of fortifications, fresh reinforcements and fanatic determination, the Germans suddenly showed they still had some fight in them.

Invasion of Germany is a book supplement for the Panzer Grenadier series. It contains 50 (yes, fifty) scenarios set along the German border. This book is a compendium of the scenarios presented in the out-of-print supplements Aachen 1944, Siegfried Line, West Wall, Roer River Battles and North of Elsenborn.

Invasion of Germany includes no mapboards or playing pieces. Players will need playing pieces and maps from Elsenborn Ridge and Battle of the Bulge; some scenarios also use boards or pieces from our Cassino’44, Afrika Korps, Beyond Normandy, Eastern Front, 1940: The Fall of France and Road to Berlin games and Iron Curtain - Patton's Nightmare supplement.

Cosmic Alliance, the third expansion set for the Fantasy Flight Games version of Cosmic Encounter, brings 20 alien races, both original and classic, exploding onto your tabletop. Players will now stand petrified by the hideous Gorgon, be baffled by the puzzle of the Schizoid, and feel obsolete before the bionics of the Cyborg.

Cosmic Alliance also makes the Cosmos even bigger, adding another player (white) as well as rules for large eight-player games (if you own all three expansions). Finally, Cosmic Alliance introduces a new variant – team rules, which allow steadfast allies to dominate the Cosmos together!

This optional Team Cosmic variant places players in randomly determined teams of two, in which they'll attempt to conquer the Cosmos cooperatively. Without discussing their race selection in advance, partners sit opposite each other at the table, where they can occasionally offer a helping hand; whenever a player gains a new foreign colony, he can choose to instead give that colony to his partner.

description:
In Straw, Sticks and Bricks players take on the roles of Pigentrepreneurs looking to be the first to finish their new housing development and start attracting buyers.  But, in their rush, will they cut corners and use the more readily available straw?  Or hold out and build quality brick homes?  Worse yet, you will never guess who the housing inspector is..  You guessed it, it’s B.B. Wolfe, and with his inspection badge, he can even knock down brick houses.  What is a pig to do….


Straw, Sticks and Bricks (SSB) is a resource management game geared toward children, but still providing a challenge for adults.  It uses familiar characters and simple mechanics to engage your children.  Honing skills of matching, set collecting, turn taking, negotiating and counting – it is a perfect introductory challenge for children, and great for family game night.

The game is broken into three phases, in which all players take actions, so as to minimize the down time for players, keeping everyone engaged throughout.

In the first phase players take turns spinning the resource wheel and collecting building supplies (Straw, Sticks or Bricks) – or encountering the housing inspector (Mr Wolf), who may condemn one of the recently built homes.

The next phase allows the players to trade the recently acquired building supplies with each other, hopefully to their mutual advantage.

For the final phase, players use their hard won resources to build homes in their housing developments – and once a development is full and ready to open, points are totaled and a winner (or winners) is determined.


The game was created as an entry for TGC Resource Contest.

This game is endorsed by the former world champion John Parrot.
The Game can be played by all ages and you need luck and knowledge.
First of all you set a winning points target or a time limit and then the first player to reach the target or scores the most points is the winner.
To score points you must land on a red block on the Board of which you score as in snooker 1 point, you then spin the dial to see what colour question you must answer (As in snooker you get 2 points for yellow,3 for Green,4 for Brown,5 for Blue,6 for pink and 7 for black) but, depending on the colour, 
the questions you must answer get harder).
If you are correct you get another turn if not then it is up to the next player.

John Parrott (1964 - ), pictured on the box. He won the World Snooker Championship in 1991.

Magic Wands will help you but Maleficent the evil Fairy will do all she can to stop you and keep Sleeping Beauty forever from awakening.

description:
Zero Point One is a two player abstract strategy game combining elements of Chess and Shogi. The game is inspired by the medieval moves of the Bishop (Alfil) and Queen (Fers) and adds some common medieval unorthodox Chess pieces such as Dabbaba and Wazir along with the orthodox Knight in Western Chess.

Zero Point One is played on a board with sixty four squares arranged in an 8x8 grid.
Each square contains a number from 1 to 8. These numbers have no effect on play but serve as a visual aid so players may easily envision the squares to which certain pieces may have access.

The pieces are named after the co-ordinates by which they move.
Pieces are moved to either a vacant square or to a square occupied by an opposing piece. The opposing piece is thus captured and removed from the board.

With the exception of 0·1 pieces, captured pieces may re-enter the game on the side of the capturing player as in Shogi. In lieu of a regular move, a player may place a captured piece on any vacant square. 
The object of the game is to capture the opposing 0·1 piece.

"Dusty Bin is in a Spin,He's Really Flipped His Lid.
If You Can Fire Your Counters in,You'll Be the Dusty Kid"
Dusty Bin was a character in the 1980's game show called 3-2-1.
The board has the Dusty Bin character sitting in the middle. Extending from the centre are 4 'hands', one for each player. The game operates with a pull-string mechanism - needs no batteries.

Each player has 10 chips. These are placed one at a time on one of the hands. You then press down on the lever and let go. The chip then flips toward's Dusty. Dusty spins around and the lid opens and closes, so it is not easy to get a chip into the bin.
Try to be the first player to flip all ten chips into the bin!

40 Chips in Four Colour's.
1 Set of Rules.

Players take a vehicle of their choice.

Players move around the board by throwing the dice. They should try to land on the coloured squares and answer questions correctly to gain points. Question subjects include Traffic Problem Pictures, general questions and traffic signs.
The objective is to score 20 Points first and thus win the game.
You must also pay close attention to the signs on the board - If you make a mistake,  you lose points.

Welcome to the future! At the dawn of the 25th Century, races from across the void have worked together to develop incredible technologies. Humans, Borganoids, Ursans and Gryzz have lived in harmony for decades... until now. Following the invention of a brand new device called PUGS, tensions have grown.

PUGS, the Pocket Universe Generation System, is an amazing invention. When fired up, a brand new self-contained universe is created, filled with precious elements ready to be plundered  – as long as the players can find them. They'll need to race around the Pocket Universe and grab what they can as quickly as possible, avoiding attacks from the opposition

Players fly about the Pocket Universe made up of hexes, controlling their starship and attempting to explore planets and mine four different types of resources. Once collected they can either choose to return them to their starbase in order to score points or keep some aboard in order to use them to perform special abilities - some aggressive, some defensive.

Once a set amount of planets have been discovered, players must then make a final race to return their remaining resources to their base and score that last points boost. Whoever has the most points at the end will be declared the winner! Will you come out on top?

The objective of the game is to score  points by correctly guessing the Password from your team mate's clues.

The players split into two teams and a word is given to one team member who must give clues to enable their team mates to correctly identify the word, This is all done against an electronic varying timer so you must think and talk quickly to find the word before the timer runs out. Some words are easy but some are very difficult to describe in a short space of time.

Pre-establish a time before play starts and the team with the most points when that time is reached, wins

Fungibility is a strategic, Euro-style, economic engine, resource management card game for 2-4 players, which uses 108 cards, and no other components.

Fungibility is also a financial term, referring to commodities which are functionally indistinguishable from one another, such as currency, graded crude oil, and pure gold. In keeping with this concept, the cards in Fungibility (the card game) are all identical copies of the same card.

To create some degree of randomness, the deck is shuffled, first with half the cards rotated, then a second time with half the cards flipped face-up. The facing, position, and even rotational orientation of cards relative to each player's seating position has a logically distinct meaning within the game, meaning that the same card in the supply may have completely different meanings to each player.

The basic game is free to print and play. It is the author's intent that, should the game be published and sold at retail, the retail cards would include art elements unreferenced by the core rules. As of writing, the author has three expansions designed, each of which would merely describe rules for the unreferenced art elements, and which would be made available free of charge, post release.

Rats (treasure) - Play on a cargo hold. This cargo hold enters play with 0 crew and may not be replaced. Any fights taking place here score one additional automatic hit per side.

Marvel Slingers combines collectibles with toys in this dexterity game. The Marvel Universe provides the setting as heroes and villains (such as Hades, Loki and Athena) battle each other for supremacy at your hands.

The basic game consists of several elements. You have a battle map, a set of battle medallions, power boost medallions, and the central element - your Slinger. The Slinger is a ball-shaped yo-yo-like creation with a magnet on the under-belly; each Slinger has its own personality in terms of power and stamina, making different ones suitable for different levels of expertise.

You use the Slinger to attack your opponents' battle medallions on the battle map, wielding the Slinger like a yo-yo. Your aim is to make contact with your chosen target and defeat it. The built-in power meter measures power, and a winner is determined. Each round is played as a 5-on-5 battle.

The collectible aspect comes in the form of different Slingers and different medallions (both battle and power boost) – all in various levels of rarity.

description:
Playing Pieces:  1 Board, 6 Dinosaurs, and 1 Die

Begin The Game

Who Goes First: The youngest player goes first.  After the 1st game, the winner of the previous game goes first.  Play proceeds clockwise.

Begin the Game: Place all dinosaurs in the Dino-Bath.  Roll 1 die and go forward the spaces indicated on the die, then follow instructions on each of  the rocks.  There is only 1 die used in this game.

Only one dinosaur can occupy any space on board at one time.  The only place that more than one dinosaur is allowed to occupy, is the Dino-Bath.

Dino-Bump: When you land on a space that is already occupied by another dinosaur, that dinosaur goes back to the Dino-Bath and your dinosaur stays on that space.  This move is called a Dino-Bump.

On a Safe rock, however, you are safe from a Dino-Bump.  See additional rules for Safe rock.

How to Win

The first player to enter the Dino Cave is the winner!   You can only enter the Dino Cave with an exact roll of the die.  If you roll a number that is greater than the number needed to enter the Dino Cave, do not move forward.  Wait until your next turn and try again to enter.

The rule is different, however, for the last Dino Jump rock.  From here, if you roll a 1, then you enter the Dino Cave.  If you don’t roll a 1, then go back number on die.  See additional rules for Dino Jump rock and Last Dino Jump rock.

boardgame: Shadows Upon Lassadar: Revenge of the Raven Consort
description:
Revenge of the Raven Consort is the 3rd game in the Lassadar Trilogy (Shadows Upon Lassadar and Sorrow of Salilth are the other 2).

This is a solitaire 'Choose Your Own Adventure' style card game lasting about 20 minutes

Needed to play:
• the 81 cards split into five separate game decks (Quest, Event, Combat, Spell and Win/Loss) each with their own icon and color
• 2d10 - used for combat and d100 percentage rolls (it is suggested that each d10 is a different color)
• 36 wooden cubes (used to mark spells (17), books (8), spirit/mana/fate (3) and combat strength (4) and special bonuses (4) some Event cards give you)

Play: 
You are trying to find 9 lost books before the Raven Consort and the servants of the Grayking do. Cards are drawn from one of 5 decks (Quest, Combat, Spell, Event and Win/Loss), with each deck forming the basis of the choices you make in the game.

Each of the Quest, Spell and Event cards has a title at the top, text in the middle which must be resolved first, and a series of icons at the bottom.

Once you resolve the text in the middle, you can choose one of the bottom listed actions and draw your next card from that corresponding color deck, discarding the current ‘in play’ card.

Game end: 
The game ends in one of 2 ways:
1. Once 9 books have been recovered by you and/or by the Raven
Consort, the game ends. If you have found the majority of the books you win the game, thwart the Raven Consort and save Lassadar. If the Raven Consort has the majority of the books, she wins and Lassadar is plunged back into misery and darkness.
2. If your spirit falls below 1 on the spirit track you have lost the game and become one of the Raven Consort’s Thralls and you are forever damned.

Utara is a dice game that makes a game board from any surface on earth. The game's theme calls to mind constellations and stellar navigation.

Utara makes use of several dice that in each face says either North, East, South, or West. The fifth face has a Sun and the sixth face is Moon. You can use any number of dice to play, with a minimum of ten dice per player. More dice will allow higher scores, possibly longer games and require more space to play.

Give the playing surface directions, meaning north, south, east and west. Players roll their dice on the playing surface at the same time. Pick one player to start randomly. The player chooses a die with a cardinal direction in it, and moves it in the direction of its face up. When meeting another dice, collect it and change directions to match the new dice face. Continue in this manner until you go out of bounds. Then the next player does the same thing and so on.

The game ends when no more North, East, South or West dice remain. Each player scores one point for every die in their collection.

It is best to play a few rounds of Utara, once for each participating player. The player who had the fewest points in the previous round should take the first turn in the next round, so they have an opportunity to catch up. Points are tallied over a series of rounds to determine the winner.

Dice Bowl Football is a two-player game where opponents coach teams in a battle of wits.

The game comes with twelve special dice representing offensive plays, defensive formations, and special teams. Each coach calls a play, and dice are rolled depending on the calls. The offense chooses a play based on risk vs. reward. If the defense anticipates the play correctly, they roll more dice and have a better chance of stopping the offense.

There are no complicated charts to search through after each play is called. The outcome of the play is read directly from the dice. Quickly the coaches move on to the next play, and the action is never bogged down.

Despite this simplicity of play, the game contains all the moves and strategy of a real football game. Everything that can happen in football can happen on your game table, from Onside Kicks to Long Bombs. Aside from offense and defense, there are also dice for special plays, penalties, and kicking.

Includes everything you need to play a game: 12 dice with customizing stickers, card decks representing the playing field, scoreboard, and game clock, 4 scoring rings, 2 field markers, a large cloth bag, and instructions for playing.

The field is built by laying out the provided standard-size cards, which means that all components fit into a large cloth bag. This makes it easy to take Dice Bowl along to tailgates, picnics, and parties. Dice Bowl is a great family game with a nice mix of strategy, gamesmanship, and a little luck!

The tabletop baseball game for Japanese professional baseball. This game customized to fit Japanese style baseball based on APBA Pro Baseball.

The game mainly affected by batting ability, and sometimes affected by pitching and fielding ability.

Players manage one of twelve baseball clubs. If skilled players, one game can be completed within 10 minutes.　Therefore, not only single game or short series of few games, you can play a full regular season of a year.

This game first published in 1984 with 1983 season's data. And, has been published continuously until 1990.

Try your hand as a Prince of the Slavic tribes. Prudent management of network resources to build economic power. Show your strength and courage and obedience among the warriors gain and advantage on the battlefield. Prove that you deserve to be called the first Polish ruler!

It is a breakthrough ninth and tenth centuries. The three ambitious princes at the head of the three great tribes are preparing to fight for the lands of Wielkopolska and Kujawy. Polanie, led by Siemowit extend their rule, the submission of its subsequent settlement and erected at strategic locations mighty castles. Goplanie tribe inhabiting Kujawy, led by cruel Popiel, are preparing to invade the lands lying to the left Noteć river. Lords of the Glapian tribe in Kaliska region  push to the north, trying to grab the fertile lands of central Wielkopolska. The eyes of all the princes look at Gniezno, where is Lech Hill most important center of Slav worship. The prince, who will conquer Gniezno, will gain great authority among the Slav population, which may force the rulers of other tribes to submit to his authority. However, the princes plans can be thwarted. There are rumors that at the mouth of Warta strengthen the invaders from the far north, the Vikings ...

Board game "The Birth Piast Eagle" is designed for 2-4 people. Playing time is approximately 2-4 hours, depending on the number of players. Their task is to get as much the prince's authority as possible. They can expand it by winning the general, a reasonable policy, Stewardship of goods and skillful manipulation of the priestly caste. Players may also lose the authority as a result of shameful defeats or show weakness in intertribal political arena.

"The Birth Piast Eagle" is a history-strategic game. Players moving tokens, indicating different types of early medieval armies. Players giving orders have to decide what action it will take in the next stage of their subjects. Depending on the order of the generated power player, these activities take on momentum or slow down. Events included in the pages of random events can absolutely interfere with the planned course of play, forcing players to complete the reform of the established strategy.

Game Design is the winner of a competition organized by the historiographer Polish History Museum and funded by the Ministry of Culture and National Heritage in the financing of educational and cultural activities by the "education +".

Characteristics of the tribes in the board game "Piast Eagle Rise"

Glapianie:
This is the Slavic tribe has its headquarters on the southern edge of the board of the central stronghold - Kalisz. Their unmistakable tramp over the years built a network of trade and diplomatic relations and strong economic fundamentals. Due to the high economic potential of their treasury fills up quickly. Some minor deficiencies in the disposal of the military are offset by the money, according to the rule: "War is not so much a question of weapons, what the money" - Thucydides.

Glade:
This starts the game Slavic people in the central part of the board - in Giecz. This site brings with it a blessing and a curse. Is extremely advantageous location close to major towns in the game, including Gniezno. While the downside is undisputed that the Glade are surrounded by "benevolent" neighbors from four sides. Polan's strengths is military power, whose core is the cavalry, as well as the ability to quickly build great castles.

Goplana:
This is the Slavic tribes settled in the vicinity of Lake Gopło headquartered in Kruszwica. Begin the game in the eastern part of the board. Their military potential - economic development is balanced, thus, are very formidable opponent. Have the ability to quickly mobilize resources, and a lot of defensive capabilities.

Vikings:
The tribe is led by visitors from far northern lands. They begin the game board in the north west - the Santoku. Have the greatest military potential, the most important role is played by an elite infantry Scandinavian. Neither the army nor the stockade could not stop them in the march of Gniezno. The problem may be difficulties in the supply and finance - "Money is the nerve of war," Cicero.

Spice is a short game for two players. Spice is a very simple adaptation of Ketchup for play with the Series: Shibumi game system.

In Spice, the goal is to have the largest group of stones when the game is complete. A group is defined as stones that are "touching", either orthogonally or one on top of the other. The game is over when all the spaces on the gameboard have been filled.

Players play with either RED (ketchup) or BLACK (pepper) stones, and the game begins with a small WHITE 5-stone 2x2 pyramid in the center of the gameboard. The white stones cannot be removed and are neutral, they do not count toward either player's groups of stones.

On the first turn of the game, Ketchup must place 1 RED stone in any empty space. On subsequent turns, players put 1 or 2 stones of their color in any empty space on the board.

If the largest group of stones on the board after your turn is larger than the largest group of stones (of either color) before your turn, then your opponent may remove one of your "free" stones on their next turn (and only on their next turn).

The game ends immediately when the board is full, but the top stone is removed before scoring.

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

You didn't have time to get ready, didn't have time to make sure everyone could get out of the house. How were you supposed to know the evil walking dead were going to be knocking at the door? You couldn't have known, you couldn't be ready, and now it's time to rush out the door, and into any car that works.

Zombie Rush is a Print and Play real-time speed game, where you're trying to get all of your family members (cards) out of the house before anyone else does. You score two points for each family member that escaped (each card in the central, shared, scoring area). Each family member left in the house, or dead is worth -1 point.

Players can use their escape routes to get people out of the house before they finally make their escape into the central scoring area. Players place vehicle cards in the center to start piles of escaping family members (the number on the card must go up one each time you place a new family member on the pile).

When zombies appear, they eat whoever is next to them in the deck, but if you've equipped one of the members of your family with a shotgun, you can put the zombie in the dead pile (with any already-dead family members) and save a life.

Plays from 2-6 players, plays best with 3-6. Each round takes about 5-10 minutes, with a full game (to 60 points) playing in about 30 minutes.

Expands, Re-themes, Re-implements:

     Ligretto


Zombie Rush is nearly language independent. If you know the word 'shotgun', you're in good shape. Rules in English only, as of now.

The animals bring gifts to the party and the players have to remember exactly who brought what gift, and who owns what hat. There is little time to look at the 6 animal cards before they are turned face down.

A player on his turn rolls the dice and tries to guess the card pictured on the number rolled. If he's right, he takes the card and a new card is placed to look at and then turned face-down.

The player with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Explore is designed to provide a fast-paced and fun gaming experience with materials that can be found around the house; all that's needed is a deck of cards and some six-sided dice. No need for anthologies of rules either. Explore is simple enough that many of the test-players have the mechanics memorized by the second game.

The Explore Engine uses the card deck to generate random game areas for characters to explore in a variety of different settings.

Open Beta Testing

Currently, Explore is free to download and comes with the setting, "Nightmaze." In "Nightmaze," children gather candy on a creepy Halloween night. The object of the game is have the most candy at the end of game by visiting more houses, impressing the adults with your cool costume, and avoiding bullies and the scarier parts of the neighborhood.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Battle of the Bulge I
description:
From the Publisher:
About 15 years ago Euro Pak II brought ASLers a bevy of Battle of the Bulge scenarios and proved to be one of our best-sellers ever. It's long been out of print ... and deserving of an update. And an update, and then some, is exactly what it has received! Two new geomorphic maps, both depicting rivers, scenarios updated, all known errata input, plus the new layout and art. PLUS some additonal bonus BULGE scenarios AND a FREE copy of the Wingen-sur-Moder counter sheet providing vehicles and AFVs shown on the image above. All in all it comes at you as BULGE PAK I (a hint that, yes, there will be a BULGE PAK II).

description:
Based on a very popular gameshow which was hosted by Bob Monkhouse. It ran from 1984 to 1990.

Box includes board, wipe off markers, 'Bingo' cards, golden card planner, Monkhouse mastercards, prize cards, general subject and special subjects question books, plus instruction leaflet.

As players answer quiz questions correctly, they gradually complete their Bingo card. The objective is to be the first to do just that. Your Bingo numbers are marked off with a chinagraph pencil and they can easily be erased so that the cards are ready for the next game.

Bob Monkhouse (1928 - 2003) is pictured on the cover.

Balut is a dice game similar to Yahtzee.

It was invented by an American stationed in the Philippines at the end of World War 2, and is a popular social game with the Dutch, with organized play and competitions.

The name of the game is derived from the partially-fertilized eggs popular in the Philippines and other parts of SE Asia.

The game involves rolling five dice up to 3 times to make various combinations, which are noted on a scorecard. A Balut is a five-of-a-kind of any denomination, Players who obtain a Balut announce it to the other players by calling out "Balut!"—similar to calling out "bingo!", and note it on a special scorecard. Players try to guess before gameplay how many Baluts will be made during the game; correctly guessing the total number can earn a prize.

Target shooting game for 2 players, linked to the popular TV series The Man From U.N.C.L.E., starring Robert Vaughan and David McCallum (both illustrated on the cover).

The object of the game is to be the first to shoot out all five targets spelling U-N-C-L-E.

Contents include platform with spring action trigger, targets, score pegs and marbles.

Lily Lotto and her friends are happily playing together. But oh dear! It’s verging on pandemonium. Toys, fruits and clothes are piling up in a heap of different colors. Everybody now has
to find all their belongings again. Can you help the four friends?

Whoever collects the four items shown on their picture cards collects a tidbit as a reward. The first player to collect two tidbits wins the game.

boardgame: Justice League of America Aquaman Game
description:
Players must get from the Start space, lower left of the board, to the Finish space, lower right of the board. To move, a player spins the spinner and moves to the next space with the same colour that he has just spun. Then he draws a card and obeys the instruction.

If you land on the space next to the cross-bar (The track is a large letter A, so has to have a cross-bar). This is a short cut but if taken, the player does not use the spinner. In this case, he moves one space at a time until he is out of the short-cut. Of course, after each move of one space, he must still draw a card.

The winner is the first to reach the Finish space.

Rules Are Printed on the Inner Box Base.

Play as Wellington, Bungo, Orinoco or Tomsk. Each player has a jigsaw to complete.

Move around the board collecting the pieces.
Player's try to complete their puzzle by using the pieces that they find on the path to Wimbledon Common. These pieces represent litter. And in true Wombles style, they just love to pick up litter.

The winner is the player who completes their puzzle first and in so doing, helps to tidy the Common.

Winner! Most Creative Game! + 2nd Place Overall in the 2011 TGC Vehicle Game Design Contest

Nag! is a backseat driving board game where all the players cram into the back seat of one car and try to control it by yelling at the driver. It's a unique trick-taking hand-management game, where you don't necessarily need to win any hands to win the game.

Poor Harold. He stupidly volunteered to drive everyone to Aunty Josephine's birthday party, but he doesn't know where to go. Each of you has a vague idea of what her house looks like, so you all cram into the backseat and start yelling and screaming at him.

You must get Harold to drive by the house you're absolutely sure must be Aunty Josephine's. Why doesn't that blockhead ever listen! Use your Nag! cards to shout the loudest. Tap Harold on the shoulder to get him to pay attention. Use the GPS to plot your route. If all that fails, call the Cops on him.

So get Nag! today, and drive your whole family crazy!

Dexterity game by Imaginarium with a japanese restaurant theme, for 2/4 players.

Players are required to assemble the randomly selected menus by getting the japanese food which moves on the tapis-roulant.

Of course, the food has to be taken using chopsticks.

Number 6 in the series of monthly releases of expansion maps for the China/Web of Power family of games. Set in Japan, it has the usual color pattern (1 purple region, 2 of each other color) but with a different geography.

Released officially December 2011 at michaelschacht.net.

This is a set of supplemental technology cards that were only available to those who pledged to support the "Exile Sun" project on Kickstarter.

These cards will add a little variety to the base game. The standard technology cards that come with Exile Sun are one time use abilities that players can buy and then use on their turn to gain different advantages in many categories. The details of this standard set of tech cards are outlined on the back of the Exile Sun rulebook.

The Kickstarter exclusive reward Tech Interrupt Cards are different in that they are termed 'interrupt' other tech cards. They allow players to effect the game outside their own turns. Using these cards will be an optional variant to the base game. Shuffled in among the regular Tech cards, these Kickstarter only cards will be rare, but when used at the right time, they provide some additional interaction and strategic planning.

boardgame: Scorched Earth: Not Just a Bunch of Hot Air
description: Scorched Earth: Not Just a Bunch of Hot Air is a "board-less board game."  The game board has been replaced by a color-changing dome, patterned to look like the Northern Hemisphere with a digital display floating in the Pacific Ocean. Players work to change the color of the globe from red to green by correctly answering questions about various environmental topics, learning which technologies are more “green” in order to invest in them and reacting to natural events. Beyond just teaching facts about the environment and global climate change, the game is set up to encourage thinking about how your actions affect the whole world and how what we do now will affect our future. Game-play also requires players to work together to improve the globe’s color, demonstrating how we must all work together to help slow the effects of global climate change.

From Publisher Webpage:

The game of words with many meanings. Did you mean "right"? Did you mean correct? 90 Degrees? The opposite of left? Like the Bill of Rights? Or did you mean write? Or rite? Or the Wright Brothers?

A fun party game, groups can team up and work on one of six different challenges for over 300 words.

Depending on the challenge and the roll of the die, players may be trying to find the most common meaning of "run", or the opposite of the word "ate". You might have to find out if you're searching for a unique definition, a list of synonyms, or if everyone has to guess what you're going to say.

This is a simple print-and-play, roll-and-move used in a MDA Workshop that is part of the game design class at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

The game is played by two players. Each player has a single track consisting of 12 spaces. Players start on their respective starting spaces, and the first to reach their 12th space wins.

On his or her turn, a player has two choices: roll a d3* and move that many spaces, or get a bonus token. Bonuses tokens can be cashed-in on a player's turn, before rolling, to automatically move two spaces; a maximum of three tokens is allowed.

Thus players can either take a chance and move 1 - 3 spaces, or play it safe and guarantee themselves 2 spaces later.


    d3 is a d6, which translates as follows:



d6 roll    d3 result
1, 2       1
3, 4       2
5, 6       3

Carcassonne: The Castle - Falcon Expansion is available as a free print & play download from knizia.de/more. Presented by Reiner Knizia, Klaus-Jürgen Wrede and Hans-im-Glück Verlag.

Original game description:

In Carcassonne: The Castle - Falcon, an expansion for the two-player game Carcassonne: The Castle, the Prince is growing and has taken up falconry, resulting in a large number of falcon nests in the castle. Also, religion has come to the castle, and there is now the possibility of building chapels in the castle.

All of this means more opportunities and challenges for the players to enjoy this great Carcassonne game.

According to Reiner Knizia, Rio Grande originally planned to release the game in 2009.

Hexed: Bewitched
Hex: Hexagon

A tile laying, race game for 2 to 6 players. Players must move from Start to Finish while avoiding their own Hex symbol. Players move their tokens across the tiles laid out by players during the game. Special ability tiles allow the course to change during the game. Players attempt to give themselves the advantage while giving a disadvantage to their opponents when placing tiles.

Game includes 96 Hex Tiles (with optional backs), Game Board, 6 Tokens, 6 Cards (with optional backs) and a PocketMod Rule Booklet.

All material needed to play is included in PDF format designed to be printed on either Letter or A4 paper or card stock.

There are four safes in different colors on the table, consisting of two card stacks; one open one and one draw pile. The players take turns rolling a die to determine a color, then look at the open card of the safe in the corresponding color. Now they must guess whether the next card they will draw from the safe's draw pile will be higher or lower than the current open card of the safe. If the guess is right, the player takes the open card(s) and replaces it by the drawn one. If a guess is wrong, however, the drawn card is added to the open card stack of the safe. Also, a player who makes a wrong guess loses all unprotected cards in front of him/her. Instead of rolling the die, s/he can also choose to protect one's loot and put it in a bag.
When all cards have been distributed, the player with the highest loot is the winner.

Silenzio is a recation game in which a play mode is determined, then the players flip open round animal cards. Depending on the mode, either everyone has to either make the sound of the animal in the picture, or a movement, or it depends on the color of the animal card in question. The last player to do the correct action or the first player to do something wrong has to take the card. The player who gets rid of his/her cards first wins the game.

Dal retro della scatola:

LA SFIDA È COMINCIATA!
Entra nei panni di uno dei 7 temibili guerrieri NINJA e sfrutta armi, protezioni, oggetti e abilità speciali allo scopo di far prevalere il tuo CLAN. Affronta la MISSIONE assieme ai tuoi compagni e allo stesso tempo usali per scoprire dietro quale maschera si cela il tuo RIVALE, poi ELIMINALO in pieno stile NINJA.

NINJA shadow forged è un gioco di carte NON collezionabile in cui ciascun giocatore ottiene un ruolo segreto con un proprio obiettivo nascosto e un proprio rivale nascosto da eliminare. Per vincere la partita basterà portare a termine il proprio obiettivo riguardante la missione in cui tutti i NINJA sono coinvolti, oppure scoprire ed eliminare il proprio clan rivale oppure restare il solo clan in gioco.
 Ogni clan possiede quattro valori: Attacco, Difesa, Costituzione, Intelletto. I giocatori dovranno affrontare diverse sfide (contro gli eventi o contro i giocatori stessi) tirando un dado e sommando il risultato ai propri valori richiesti. Il risultato potrà essere ulteriormente modificato da carte vantaggio giocate dalla propria mano (armi, protezioni, oggetti o abilità speciali) e da carte svantaggio subite in caso di precedenti fallimenti (ferite, debilitazioni, ecc.). Se la somma tra valore base, risultato del dado e modificatori vari raggiunge oppure supera la soglia richiesta, il giocatore supera la sfida.

From the back of the box:

"THE CHALLENGE HAS BEGUN!
Become one of 7 fearsome NINJA warriors and use weapons advantage, protection, objects and special skills in order to prevail with your clan.
Take on the mission along with your comrades and at the same time use them to discover your rival, then eliminate him in perfect ninja-style."

NINJA shadow forged - is a NON collectible card game in which every player takes the secret role of one ninja with one hidden goal and one hidden enemy, he can win by eliminating all the other ninjas, or by killing his enemy, or by reaching his goal.

Every NINJA has four characteristics: Attack, Defense, Constitution and Intellect; the players must face challenges by rolling one die and adding one of these characteristics (modified by events, equipments, advantages, disadvantages and special powers), if the result is equal or more the challenge's difficult the player has succeded.

description:
Bottle, Barrel, Brick is a fast paced game of resource collection. Each player is a dwarf trying to make the most ale, or dig up the most gold. Players take turns deciding if they will try to gather new resources by rolling the die, or combine the resources they have into finished goods for more points. If they choose to gather resources a die is rolled to determine how much they gain. It is easier to make ale than it is to dig for gold, but at the end of the game gold is worth more.

During his turn a player can pass a bottle of ale to another player to decrease his chances of gathering resources on his turn, or pay another player gold nuggets to buy bottles of ale before they can be combined.

Once all of the barrels of ale, or gold bricks are claimed by successfully combining bottles and nuggets the game is over and the player with the most valuable stash wins. This game includes a 6-sided die and all the resource tokens needed to play.

From the official website:
"Champions of Indines delivers the complete Strategy Tabletop experience in a single play session--from character creation, to backstory, to climactic battle--in just one to two hours!

Champions of Indines is a Cooperative-Competitive Constructed Tactical Tabletop Game to be played by 2 to 6 players. In the game, players take on the roles of a mercenary organization, the Champions of Indines, that travel around their world searching for powerful enemies to fight and people in need to protect. Each game is called an encounter, and takes around two hours to play from start to finish (though some encounters might be longer or shorter). The larger team of players form the Heroes--individual characters who work together to fight as a group. The other team is comprised of a Boss--a single player who controls one exceptionally powerful character or a number of minions (or some combination in between)."

The game is available now as a print-and-play from the official website. Players are encouraged to print the game, play it, and give feedback.

Many of the characters from Champions of Indines also appear in BattleCON. Though the two games are not related in terms of gameplay, they do take place in the same world and follow similar storylines.

Poepie Knor is a game in which 2-4 player try to rescue their piglets from his dirt bath. But if you hear a fart your piglets sinks deeper in the mud.

Goal of the game:
Be the 1st player to gather 4 cards (1 to 4) so your piglet gets out of his mud bath.

Each player gets a piglet and a bath. On his move he turns the big pig (spinner), depending on the sound the pig makes the player takes an action:
- the pig grunts. The player indicated by the pigs nose takes a card
- the pig honks. The 1st player to press the button on the pigs back takes a card
- the pig whistles. The player indicated by the pigs nose steals a card from another player
- the pig farts. The player indicated by the pigs nose places a card from his bath next to the board.

The 1st player to first collect a set (1-4) in the correct order is the winner.

Warfare is no longer just guns and bombs. It incorporates all types of Political Power. The army calls this PMESII (Political, Military, Economic, Social, Infrastructure, and Information), or FULL SPECTRUM WARFARE.
In this simulation, you will see some of the factors that commanders now must contend with to beat win a FSW (Full Spectrum War).

The game is played with 3 decks; a terrain deck, an OPFOR political power deck and a BLUFOR political power deck.  Nine territories are drawn to be contended for.  Players play cards out of their hand one at a time, to vie for the terrain and it's victory points.  In this unique system, the different political power cards (Military, Commerce, World Opinion, etc) have different values depending on the territory being contested.

The first player to win over half of the territorial points is the victor.
