 A friendly Unit within Alastor's Influence Zone makes a free hand-to-hand Attack with +3 Attack / +3 Damage. The character who makes this attack is removed from play after it is resolved.
 oo - Opugnis (Special Attack)
 A friendly Unit within Alastor's Influence Zone makes a free hand-to-hand Attack.

Objective: bet on the car which is going to be the first to pass finish line
 - moving by turn -
very simple 'Roll & Move' game
choose a die to roll with - depending on age - colours/numbers
bet on one of three cars 
move - by choice - one of the 3 bobby cars according your roll

boardgame: Britain & Ireland (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Europe)
description: This uses the same Rules, playing pieces and colour cards as Ticket To Ride Europe (TTRE), but is played on a new map of Britain and Ireland. It has two new features - Junctions, and Branch Lines - which enable players to 'change trains' en route. The goal is to gain the most points by a combination of completed journeys, completed Routes (as determined by chosen or assigned Route Cards), bonus points and penalties, as in the original TTRE.

Who will best overcome the traffic and get to the end of the road?
Vroom! The winner is the first player to get two motorbikes to the end of a high-density roadway. The result is unpredictable since other players can block your way or bump you onto another lane at any throw of dice! This game is not for the faint-hearted...Do you have the makings of a champion?

Children version of game Think: Memo Crime.
The players have to remember small details as a detectives and collect as many winning chips as possible if they story is correct.

Players roll the dice, move accordingly and draw two word cards from one of the stacks showed on the square they landed and tell a story must be both chronologically and substantively correct and if the words happen every time the word cards correctly.

Four stacks of incredible stories lie in the mysterious attic. Who remembers them best and be the most creative to retold these stories, wins the game.

Contents: 

     1 game board, 
     112 word cards, 
     1 dice, 
     40 chips profit, 
     one character

The newest 2 Player PnP Dexterity, Strategy and Press your Luck Game

Goal of the Game:  To score 10 cows points by tipping them over and avoiding tipping over bulls.

Players will draw tiles from the tile pool and strategically place them in their pasture or their opponents pasture.  If you draw the tipper tile you get to try and knock over your opponents tiles to see how many cow points you get.  Be careful, if you knock over a bull you will lose cow points!

This expansion does exactly what it's title would imply and no more: it supplies the components necessary to make a Hamsterdam base set (which can only accommodate a max of 4 players) playable with 5 or 6 players.  It does not include any new types of cards or components, new rules, or anything else that changes the game-play.

Hamsterdam is a primarily card-based game, where each player gets a set of 11 numbered "dam" cards, a set of 20 identical regular "hamster" cards and 2 "special" hamster cards drawn from a deck of 16 (which includes 2 each of 8 special types of hamsters).

The Hamsterdam 5-6 Player Expansion includes 2 additional sets of dam cards, 2 additional 20-card regular-hamster decks, and an 8-card specialty hamster deck including 1 of each type.

The expansion does not include other components needed to play the game (flood tokens, dice), thus it cannot be used as a stand-alone game.


Game-play of Hamsterdam when incorporating this expansion proceeds exactly as the base game does: Players create a "dam" made up of a line of cards numbered 2 through 12 and distribute their hamster cards (20 regular + 2 special) on top of the numbered cards. Players take turns rolling 2 d6 to see which spot in the dam is weakened. If a hamster is on top of that number, that hamster is used to plug to the hole and is placed to the side in a reserve pile to come back into play in the next round. If a hamster is not on that number, a flood token is placed there until the end of the round, at which time it can be removed by taking a hamster card permanently out of play. If a flood token is already located on a number that is rolled, another flood token is placed in a special pile of tokens belonging to that player which cannot be removed. As flood tokens pile up, your stock of hamster saviors dwindles, and flood tokens pile up all the faster. The game ends when any player accumulates 10 flood tokens (counting both those in the permanent pile and those currently on the dam). The winner is the player with the fewest flood tokens. In the case of a tie, the player with the most remaining hamster cards wins.

The box advises "More Than a Game - An Excuse For a Party!
Gamble! Race! What a Pace! - Live Life to the Full With Father's Millions!"

This game is ideally played by equal numbers of opposite sexes. The game is played on the assumption that each player is the son or daughter of a very wealthy father. He sends them into the game of life with a gift of £10,000 and an allowance of £250 each time a circuit of the board is completed.
The winner is the player or marriage partnership that survives when other players have lost all their assets.

An abbreviated version of the game may be played where the first player or marriage partnership to reach £50,000 in cash and possessions is the winner, or the wealthiest player after an agreed time limit. The rules are the same for all versions.
 How many games feature Boyfriends, Girlfriends, Marriage, Ascot, Casinoes, Auctions, Robberies and more?

Contents
1 Game Board.
Various Amounts of Money in Five Denominations.
33 Hi Life Cards(17 Blue/16 Pink).
12 Each of Boyfriend and Girlfriend Cards.
8 Married Cards.
16 Stock Cards.
24 Gift Cards(3 of 8 Different Types).
6 of Each - Car,Yacht and Cabin Cruiser Cards.
16 Investment Property Cards(4 of 4 Different Types).
8 Playing Pieces.
2 Dice.
1 Horse Marker.
1 Button Marker.
1 Set of Rules.

Jackpot is a family game in which players select a horse, or horses, and race them at selected odds, 2 circuits of the track, plus any handicap entailed by the odds chosen.
Each player bets on horses using the chips provided. The player with the most chips after the Jackpot race has been run is deemed to be the winner.
 Game contents
1 x playing board
6 x plastic horses and riders in six different colours
120 x small plastic chips
6 x large plastic chips
1 x dice
1 x rules leaflet

Game based on a popular television show where Jimmy Saville (an eccentric disc jockey) fulfils viewer's ambitions. If you were lucky and picked for the show, you not only fulfilled your ambition, you were also awarded a Jim Fixed It For Me badge. This became a much sought after item.

The players are children, wanting to have an ambition accepted. A 'Jim' playing piece starts life on the board and is moved by a Jim spinner. The player moves by a normal die. The objective is to land exactly on the same square as Jim. If you do, you draw four cards. The cards say things like "I would like", "I wish" etc., and also the subject of a dream wish, something like water-skiing or flying. If you can make a legitimate statement out of the four cards, you win. But this is unlikely on the first attempt and you will need to meet Jim again. If you do, you add two cards to your original four and again try to form a legitimate statement. If you can't, you discard two cards and you will have to meet Jim again.

The first player to fulfil a request with their cards, wins.

Rubovia was a children's television series and this game relates to the characters in the show.

RULES:
1. Each player chooses a coloured pawn.
2. To decide who goes first each player turns the spinner. The highest scorer goes first, next one on the left and so on in a clockwise direction.
3. Each taking his turn, the players move along the track from the START by the number of spaces shown by the spinner.
4. When a player stops on a YELLOW or ORANGE square, he must obey the instructions in the panel beside the square.
5. If a player spins a number which takes him to a square already occupied by another player, that player must leave the square and go back three spaces.
6. The player to reach the FINISH first is the winner. He must spin the exact number needed to arrive and must keep spinning in his turn, until he does.

Information supplied by Telegoons

Objective: be the first to pass finish line
 - simple 'Roll & Move' game/moving by turn -
each player rolls the die an moves forward according number
landing on a marked area follow instructions cover inside

IMPEDE is a abstract strategy game in the spirit of Go or Checkers.

Goal: Position 4 of your pieces in a straight, connected row before your opponent.

Gameplay: Players take turns moving one of their pieces to an adjacent, unoccupied space in an attempt to get 4 pieces in a row before their opponent. Play is a balance of offense and defense, positioning pieces for later traps, blocks, and offensive pushes.

Players draw two cards and discard one with the goal of forming one word of at least 7 letters and all other cards in hand (if any) forming a word or words of at least 3 letters each.  Using the colorful alphabetical card deck published to play Quiddler ("The Short Word Game"), Squiddler is for players who enjoy a challenge with longer words.  The name of the game is a portmanteau word combining "Seven" and "Quiddler."

Spangle is a deduction game for 4-10 players that can be played with a Series: Shibumi set. It uses a 4×4 set of stacking spheres in 3 colors.

The players are members of a family who are decorating their new house. The winner is the player or players who have succeeded in decorating most of the house with the color of their choice.

At the end of the game, when the thirtieth ball is placed on top of the pyramid, count the balls left in the sack. The color that has the fewest balls left in the sack is the winning color and any player that chose this color at the beginning of the game wins. In case of a tie between colors, count the number of players that have chosen each color. The winning group is the one that has the fewest members. In case of another tie, both group win.

boardgame: Improved Farming & Disasters (fan expansion for Agricola)
description:
This fan expansion contains 21 Occupations, 23 Minor Improvements, 23 Disaster cards, 18 start cards, 10 new types of tiles, and 1 Fog Marker for Agricola.

This expansion introduces several new concepts.  This expansion is kinda like two different expansions rolled into one.  This expansion is designed to be played with Farmers of the Moor.  If you are not playing with Farmers of the Moor, then do not include anything involving gravel tiles.

Gravel:  Spaces with a gravel tile are considered used and you may not build other things in those spaces.  However, you lose 1 point at the end of the game for every space with a gravel tile in your farm.

Water:  You gain 1 additional food when you use the Fishing Action Space for each water tile on your farmyard.

Other New Tiles:  Some of the new minor improvements have you add new tiles to your farmyard.

Disasters:  Players may play a Disaster card whenever they could normally play a Minor Improvement instead of playing said Minor Improvement. Disasters cause all sorts of mayhem when they are played and a played Disaster is worth 1 point at the end of the game.  Unplayed Disasters are worth -1 points at the end of the game.

New Start Cards:  This expansion introduces 16 new start cards.  You might now start with a clay hut, or a field, or a water tile, or some gravel.

An abstract game for two to four players. The game board has an inner area of 6x6 spaces with numbers 0,1,2,4,8,16 printed on them, and an outer ring with numbers from 1-31. Players take turns to place, move, or remove one of their 6 tokens on the board; only one token of a colour can be placed on the outer ring. None of the tokens placed may be on the same horizontal or vertical line as another of the same colour. The winner is the first player to place their six tokens on the board so that the sum of the five tokens on the inner board equals the number that the sixth token is placed on on the outer board.

From Publisher's website: HyperSwipe will put your reflexes to the test as you race against your opponents to collect the most image matches. Image cards pop out of the HyperSwiper as fast as you can grab them, so you’ll have to think or match fast and move at hyper-speed!

boardgame: See New York 'Round the Town Game
description: Published by Transogram in 1964. This game  is a roll (actually a Spin the Spinner) and move game where players take tours of Times Square, Central Park, The Lincoln Center, Chinatown, Rockerfeller Center, Greenwich Village and the 1964 Worlds Fair trying to make the most money.

The objective is to get your Davy Crockett playing piece to the Alamo.

Movement is by rolling a number on the spinner.
If you land on a white spot - Obey the printed instructions
If you land on a Green spot - Take another turn
If you land on a Red spot - If the spinner is pointing at that spot, lose your next turn
If you land on a Blue spot - Breathe a sigh of relief, you are safe

A spin-off board game from the company's table football game of Striker and Super Striker.

On the face of it, a roll and move game. Each player starts with a million pounds and two random player cards. The players are rated from 1 to 4 and the total of your ratings will decide how many shots you can take when you play a match.

When you have to play a match, you must use the plastic base in the middle of the board. Depending on their level of players, both sides will get a set number of attempts to score goals. A Striker footballer is used to take the kick whilst your opponent operates the goalkeeper. In effect, it is a penalty shoot-out. Then the opposition gets a kick whilst you operate the goalie. The winning side score points in the league. When a pre-set number of matches have been played, the player at the top of the league, wins.

Party game where guests can use their wits (if they have any!!).

There are 12 different cartoons and the object of the game is to compose a humourous or witty caption for each picture.

The host can decide the funniest for each of the 12 cartoons and the player (or team) who get the most 'accepts', wins.

Contents
A wooden board with 12 indentations and coloured red, white or blue (four of each colour)
1 spinning top
12 balls

Each indentation has a point value ranging from 1 to 12. To start a turn, place the balls into the tray. In a player's turn, he selects one of the three colours and then spins the top. The top will make the balls scatter and some will find the indentations. The player scores the point value for each ball that has settled in the chosen colour's indentations.

The first player to reach 100 points or more, wins.

This game uses the power of static electricity to ‘activate’ and move a small dart around a playing area under a plastic dome. The dart's position on the board at any one time allows players to claim cards to add to their scoring trays. The winner is the first player to get all 12 of their cards not only on the scoring tray, but in their correct positions on the tray.

Game contents
1 x ‘Holly Dolly’, a game board unit comprising a transparent plastic dome mounted over the playing area and containing a dart.
4 x sets of 12 numbered cards in four different colours
4 x ‘Dixi’ cards in four different colours
4 x plastic scoring trays
1 x game storage tray
1 x rules leaflet

This is a most unusual game. The rules suggest that players experiment with rubbing the top of the dome to see which method of activating the dart is best for them.
Apparently, the movement of the dart can be different for different people due to the varying static charge everyone carries with them.
This charge varies, mainly because of skin moisture or the type of clothing worn; wool, cotton, nylon etc, which can produce static electricity of different intensities.

Sandwich City is a game where players compete to make the best and biggest (which isn't always the best) sandwiches in town, using resource tokens such as Chicken, Fish, Carrots, Pigs, Sheep and the extremely rare token... Bricks. (to create the sandwich that is aptly titled, "Man Up".)

There is also fierce market made up of high price city shops and low end deals from street vendors. As food items are bought, others become cheaper, allowing players to have full control over the fluctuations of the ever changing market.

Players can make up to 52 different sandwiches, from "The Handout" (which is just a piece of chicken between two slices of bread) all the way to "The Kitchen Sink" (which is basically everything you can imagine).

Players will also manage their kitchen with a refrigerator, freezer and countertop. Items in the refrigerator get moldy over time, but the freezer lasts forever. Watch out though! Your opponent could sneak a "Freezer Fail" bonus up on you. It's very rare, but it's also very powerful.

[From back of the box]
The 1st Marine and 81st Infantry Divisions were assigned to take the islands of Pelelieu and Angaur. One would be fairly easy and the other extremely hard. Pelelieu became a vortex that took way more Marines lives than it was worth. The Japanese had dug themselves into the Umurbrogal Mountain and forced the battle to be drawn out for 2 months, instead of 2 weeks.

[From the publishers website]
This is the fourth in the PIC series of games that simulate island battles in the Pacific Ocean Area during WWII. The game is a blend of operational/tactical level combat and maneuver during the taking of the islands of Pelelieu and Angaur from the Japanese during the Second World War. The game is played in turns, each one divided into phases for amphibious landing, maneuver, and combat. The game system uses many of the seven Battlefield Operating Systems (BOS) to simulate actual combat and maneuver that both the US and Japanese players must use to fight the battle. Of the seven BOS's, this simulation concentrates on Maneuver, Fire Support, Mobility and Survivability, Air Defense, and Command and Control. The Japanese defenses are sophisticated and well manned with fanatical soldiers willing to die for their emperor. They fight so hard in fact that the Marines are stymied for much longer than planned and need to be releived by US Army troops from the 81st Division after they took Angaur.  The fight is bloody and sharp. Victory in the game is measured against the time and casualties it took the US forces to actually accomplish the same mission.

The game features US Marine Corps, US Army, US Navy, and Imperial Japanese forces at the Battalion, Company, and Platoon level. Units are initially setup as Battalions and Companies, and can be broken down into their respective elements. The combat system utilizes a set of combat multipliers that players must use correctly to have the best effect. The Japanese player has the ability to conduct Banzai attacks at night and these cause considerable damage as the US player cannot fall back in front of these attacks and must take casualties.

The are a number of scenarios that simulate the battle on a smaller scale and allow for play for either island or both.  There will be numerous options that can be used to even play between players and make the fight more interesting.  These include additional Japanese forces that could have been there if they were not sunk by US submarines.

Game Design and Production by:    Chris Fasulo

Map Artwork:    Kyle Gebhardt

description:
The game consists of 60 numbered color disks (five colors, numbers 1-6, two of each number in each color). 25 of these are shuffled and placed face-up on the table in a 5x5 grid. A pawn is placed on the chip in the center. 
The players now take turns rolling a 1/2/3 die and moving the pawn the respective distance over the grid (orthogonally only).At the end of their move, they can pick up the chip they are standing on and replace it by a new one.
The game continues until there are no replacement chips left. Then the players count their score: Each set of three chips in three different colors with the same number and each set of three with a sequence of consecutive numbers (i.e. 3,4,5) counts as many points as the sum of the numbers. A set of only one color counts double. The player with the highest score wins.

They Man the Ship - the correct answer is Crewe

The first person or team to hand in a correct list of 24 stations, wins. Or the guest with the most answers once the host calls time, wins.

One interesting point. The illustration on the player cards shows a train with a striking similarity to a Thomas the Tank Engine illustration. Thomas debuted in 1946, 11 years after this game went on sale. Was this game an inspiration? It might have been.

Answering to a distress call, teams of space marines descend into a mine on another planet. There, they must rescue captured miners, avoid the mysterious Shanalgar and fight a terrible alien species of unknown origin: the Zagorgs.

Each player controls a team of two marines, a Shanalgar "knight"  and, to hinder the other players, a constantly renewed supply of Zagorgs. The players score points by rescuing miners, healing other players' wounded marines and, of course, killing Zagorgs. The game ends when the last miner is safe.

The object of the game is to be the first player to travel around the world!  To do this you must journey through eight colour-coded areas of the world and collect at least one Bon Voyage card from each area.  The first player to accumulate at least 40,000 kilometres on Bon Voyage cards, from all 8 areas, returns home to win the game.

description:
A variant board for the classical game Go, based on a street map of Milton Keynes.  Milton Keynes has approximately grid-style plan, but the boundaries of the map are irregular, and there are four places where two "parallel" streets intersect.

Other rules are as in normal Go.  However, the tactical properties of the irregular boundary majorly affect gameplay:  for example, there are places where it is possible to create an eye by playing only one stone.  Also, because the Milton Keynes board (77 points) is much smaller than even the reduced-size 13×13 Go board (169 points), the game is much shorter.

This game was created as a side event at the 2001 Milton Keynes Go Tournament, and subsequently made available on the Web as print-and-play.

boardgame: En Pointe Toujours!: Afrique du Nord 1955-1961
description:
Afrique du Nord 1955-1961 (North Africa 1955-1961) is an expansion, only available in French, for En Pointe Toujours V: Afrique contemporaine, fifth module of the En Pointe Toujours! series, published with Vae Victis 80. It covers the Algeria War (1955-1960), the 1956 Suez crisis and the Bizerte disengagement in 1961.

This DTP expansion includes one sheet with counters and terrains, historical notes, sixteen scenarios and version 5.2 of the rules.

Game description from the publisher:

The rising sun turns red a hundred thousand spearpoints. Its rays burnish the armour of commanders hungry for conflict. And yet Man does not stand alone in his war against the darkness. The proud warhosts of the elder races stride out from their ancient realms, resplendent and majestic. Each warrior is the equal of a dozen lesser mortals, and yet none expect to see the dawn.
    –Warhammer, "War Unending"

The first Battle Pack in "The Bloodquest Cycle" for Warhammer: Invasion The Card Game, Rising Dawn gives players exciting new reasons to play and attempt quests. Its 60 new cards (three copies each of 20 different cards) shake the balance of power between the races of the Old World and open new strategies.

The High Elves gain inspiration from a powerful legend, Dwarfs gain strength from the fires of their ancient grudges, Orc hordes rise in unending waves, knights of the Empire ride to defend its borders, the Dark Elves profit from their opponents' suffering, and the warriors of Chaos strew devastation in their wake.

Across the field of battle, armies mass, ready to make war in the Rising Dawn, a game-changing Battle Pack for Warhammer: Invasion The Card Game.

Sparro is an adaptation of the game Captain's Mistress (think Connect Four) for the Series: Shibumi game platform. It differs from the original in that the goal is to be the first to get three in a row rather than four and the board wraps around the Shibumi pyramid. It is about as simple as it gets but has more depth than tic tac toe. Sparro is quick to play, has a surprise or two now and then, and is fun.

Sparro is played on the four faces of the three lower levels of the Shibumi pyramid. (There is no crow's nest.) To set up the board, create a 2x2 pyramid using five white marbles in the center of the 4x4 base. This pyramid will support the placing of marbles on the outer faces.

One player uses twelve red mistress marbles and the other uses twelve black pirate marbles.

Red starts and places a marble on any empty space. Players then take turns playing a marble on any empty space or 2x2 platform.

The first to get three in a row wins the sparring match. See the figure below for all the legal 3-in-a-rows on one face of the pyramid. If no one wins outright, Black wins. This compensates for Red having the initiative at the beginning of the game.

		
		
		
		
		
		
		
		
		

Extended Play Variant
The standard rules for Sparro apply but with the following three changes:

1)  Play continues until all 24 marbles have been played. The player with the most three-in-a-rows on the board at the end of the game wins. Note, when counting, count all unique three-in-a-rows. For example, four-in-a-row counts as two three-in-a-rows. Rows can intersect.

2) To resolve ties the following mechanism is used.  A tie-breaker token, for example a gold doubloon or a white marble, is given to Black at the start of the game. From then on, the token belongs to whichever player last created a 3-in-row on the game board. If there is a tie between players for number of three-in-a-rows, the possessor of the token wins.

3) On Black first turn, if Red played to a corner space, Black must play to one of the eight edge center spaces, or, if Red played to an edge center space, Black must play to one of the four corner spaces. Thereafter, there are no further restrictions on either player. (This rule is to prevent symmetric play and an automatic win for Black.)

Included in the Shibumi Rule Book

Jigger is a Children’s Game for the Decktet in the vein of “Go Fish”. One Decktet for every 3 players is recommended, so in theory the game can accommodate an unlimited number of players.

Players begin the game with a hand of 5 cards. The lead player calls out “I demand …” naming one of the Decktet’s six suits. Players who are holding cards of this suit must surrender all of them to fulfill the demand. Players who can’t answer the demand holler “Jigger!” and then go fishing from the draw pile until they can give up a card which matches suit. The next player in turn order then makes her demand and play continues in this way until the deck runs out. Cards in players’ takes are then tallied and the player with the most cards wins.

A card game based on the works of Jane Austen.  Players take the role of young women from the stories, trying to get married to a suitor of good social standing.

Gameplay consists of players playing cards to raise and lower the social standing of different characters.

description:
FROM THE BOX: Correctly guess how other players rate a variety of items on a scale of 1 to 10 and be the first player to reach 10 points.

One2Ten is a fun and often times hilarious social card game that exposes how players feel about certain items (including things about themselves and other players) on a rating scale from 1 to 10. It is a fun way to get to know new people and an even more fun  way to get to know those you THOUGHT you already knew... your closest friends and family!

EXAMPLE ITEMS  (On a scale of 1-10 how do I rate...)
The taste of green olives
My breath right now
My enjoyment of this game
My interest in going camping
My sense of humor
My ability to dance
The outfit of the person sitting to my left

There are OVER 400 items included in the game and a unique randomizer makes it so that no two games are ever the same! To view the complete instructions visit the game's website (see link below)

ONE2TEN: How well do you know your friends?

description:
Backcountry takes the thrill of a big mountain event and transforms it into a fun and exciting board game for the kids to share with friends and family. Take on the role of one of four unique characters, and compete in the uncharted backcountry of the mountain for ultimate bragging rights. With five cards in hand, can you devise the best strategy to cross the finish line with the most points and claim victory over your friends, and the mountain? A simple yet fun game, with high levels of social interaction, make for a great experience for anyone ages 7 & up.

Basic Game Play:

     Roll dice
     Move piece
     Play cards, if available


Types of cards:

     Trick - Single action; double combos are possible for 5 extra points; triple combos can be done for 10 extra points.
         Grab
         Spin
         Flip
    
     Knockdown - Opposing player looses a turn and you get the space; can be used to defend against Knockdown card and both players loose a turn.
     Boost - Advance down the track the indicated number of spaces.


How to win:

     Total Trick Points (consider combos) + Points per place finished = Total Points
     Highest total wins.

In this children's game, the players attempt to steal their opponents treasure by rolling a cannon ball (a marble) off a slope. At the bottom of the slope, their are several treasure chest space. If the marble lands in a treasure chest space containing an opponent's treasure, they get to steal it.

However, the players get to protect their treasures by placing obstacles (sharks) on the slope, which may deflect the cannon ball.

boardgame: On an Adventure with Geronimo Stilton: The Game
description:
Have you never dreamed of travelling in the Kingdom of Fantasy? Together with Geronimo Stilton and Trinket Scribbler on the back of the Rainbow Dragon and fly back to Fantasia, the world full of imagination. Geronimo Stilton finds a lovely music box in the attic with a very important message in Fantastic Script. “Help Florina the fairy queen, she is in great danger!” That is where Geronimo Stilton's adventure starts. Travel together with him through the 6 fantastic realms of Fantasia, where you will have to carry out an instruction in each one of them. Trinket Scribbler will help you in this. In this way, you will collect the 6 coloured Emeralds, which ultimately give you access to Florissant, the fairy kingdom. And above all, don't forget the music box…

In this game, Geronimo Stilton travels though the realms of Fantasia. On their turn, players move the Geronimo pawn and will answer questions and carry out assignments. The first player to complete an assignment in each realm wins the game.

Objective:
be the first to pass finish line: field 80 (Rhine Falls at Schaffhausen)
 - moving by turn/'Roll & Move' game -
each player rolls the die moving foreward according thrown number;
landing on a marked area follow instructions printed cover inside

game was published during the 1920's

La Febbre dell'Oro (Gold Rush) is a fun game and is set during the Wild West where the search for gold had become a "fever".

The game board has 72 boxes, game of goose style, and the players move their horse through a wheel numbered from 1 to 12. Some boxes (for example, the railroad, the saloon, the emporium, the river, etc.) can be bought to guarantee an income to the owner when another player will occupy the box.
When a player reaches the box  #72 has the chance to enter the gold mine (and win) if he gets 13 points using twice the wheel numbered.

Objective: be the first passing finish line (field 100 ==> Baltic sea spa Zoppot)
 - moving by turn/'Roll and Move' game - 
each player starts at 'Bischofsberg' and throws the die moving foreward according rolled number; 
landing on a marked area follow instructions printed on cover inside
similar to 'Game of Goose', but linking all places in and around Gdansk before WW II.
The game can be classified as a 'children game' due to mechanics, but very often these types of games were played by adults combined with more or less high wagers for each participant;
winner usually got 50% of total amount, 2nd and 3rd the rest

The game was produced by the Rucks & Co. GmbH about 1949 after the artist Erich Scholtis moved to Kiel. It was produced till well after 1959 when the artist moved to Aachen. The last editions were published in the 1970s.

Assault on Jac Bank is a game set in the Far West.
The game board is divided into two paths (game of goose style), each of 30 squares. A path is runned from the players who personify the good guys (the sheriff and the cowboy) and the other path is runned from players who personify the bad guys, the gunslingers.
The aim of the sheriff and the cowboy will be to prevent the robbery that the bad guys will try to accomplish in the Jac Bank (located in the center of the game board). At the end the players will have to compete in a duel with the use of the cards to determine the winner.

War Games is a strategy board game based on the Doctor Who series of the same name, which originally aired in 1969. Players take the roles of War Lords, a race of aliens who have abducted human soldiers from across time in order to build an army to conquer the universe. One player controls the Doctor, who is striving to foil their plans. Players fight each other, capture territory, and form alliances through an engaging, card-based combat system that emphasizes skill and strategy over luck. Eventually though, the Time Lords have to be called in, and the game changes into a fast-paced capture the flag style race to get to the TARDIS and escape before they arrive.

Each turn, players take three actions by playing a card for each action. Playing cards in this way allows players to move, attack other generals and their bases, fortify their bases, or use strong utility actions or delayed tactical responses. The Doctor can influence zone bases to join the Resistance and otherwise interfere with the general's plans.

During play there are several War Chief cards that provide environmental effects when they are face-up. These cards become face-up or face-down due to actions in the game such as forcing another player to be your lieutenant or capturing zones. The game proceeds to the second phase when one player controls the majority of zones or all the War Chief cards are face-up or face-down.

In the second phase of the game, action is faster, as each player plays one action at a time and moves, and doesn't draw any cards to replace their hand. Players fight over the key to the TARDIS and race to escape the world they're on before the Time Lords arrive.

A horse racing themed card game.

One player is the Bookie and will offer odds on each race. Those odds are determined by how many cards of each colour are randomly drawn before the race starts. If only one red card is drawn, the odds will be better than a colour that has no cards drawn. If 2 are drawn for one colour, the odds increase. And so on. The odds reflect the fact that there are less cards in the deck for that colour to win.

Having established the odds and all bets are placed, the race begins. The Bookie turns over a card and that colour advances one card length. A horse needs to be moved 7 card lengths first to win.

There are some wild cards that have certain effects. They tend to affect the race of the next card to be revealed. For example, if you drew a Blue Horse Stumbles card, the Blue horse is moved forward as usual. But the next colour card drawn has to suffer the effects of having stumbled.

The winner at the end of the evening is the player with the most cash. I would suggest you might end it after every player has had one turn of being the Bookie.

"300 All'Ora: Il Vero Gioco delle Corse Automobilistiche" is a simple board game focused on the Formula 1 races. The movement of the cars is determined by the throw of two dice and the winner will be the player who first complete all the laps of the circuit.

The game is played in two phases.
The first step is to take a ride in the track "Collaudo", collecting many coins every time with the throw of double dices you stoped over a box with a wheel icon.
This coins permit every player to move an extra space over the dices in the second phase of the game.

The second phase is played in the race track 8-shaped.
You throw two dices and you must move your car as the result of the dice.
The first one that turns 3 rounds of the track wins.

description:
Described as "A Game for the Black-Out", so that dates it to the early 1940's.

It is for two players. Each player gets a board with a 5 x 5 grid on it. The first player selects a letter from the 250 provided and announces it. Both players must now write it into one of the 25 squares on their board. Then the next player draws a tile and both players must now fit that into their grid. Once a letter is placed, it cannot be changed.

The object is to score the most points. Points are rewarded with a specific score, which increase as the length of the word increases. A perfect game would be 5 five-letter words across and 5 five-letter words downwards. I suspect, that would have an absolutely zero chance of happening.

Back to the box - it also says "More Entertaining than Crosswords" and "An Ideal Fireside Game".

I Shoot Randy is a card game for 2 to 6 players ages 13 and up with average length of playtime between 10-15 minutes.

One player takes on the role of Randy and must survive the onslaught of attacks from the other players by countering the effects of their played cards.

If Randy's health is reduced to 0, the player in the role of Randy looses the game. However, if Randy survives all valid attacks from the other players, then he wins the game.

Tactical level wargame based on Japanese anime animation "Fang of the Sun Dougram".
This game issue with Dual Magazine #2 to #5. Each edition can play separately and later game added another terrain, new units and scenarios.

Players control small troop of either Earth Federation Army or People's Liberation Army.
The game play is course of alternately player turns, but there are opponent defensive combat phase between the movement and combat phase. Combat is resolved in the two steps of accuracy and armor penetration.

The game contains several scenarios based on the story. Victory conditions are different for each scenario.

Tic Tac Bingo Game: Preschoolers can either play Tic Tac Toe or Bingo. For Bingo, one player drops both the color and character dice through the dice tower. All players simultaneously try to match the correct color and character on their player card by placing a chip on the correct space. The first player to get Bingo, wins. For Tic Tac Toe, one player drops the character die through the dice tower and only that player tries to match the character on their player card by placing a chip on the correct space. The next player takes a turn. The first player to get Tic Tac Toe, wins.

Contents: 

     4 Double-Sided Game Boards 
     56 Game Chips (4 Different Colors) 
     2 Dice 
     1 Dice Tower

description: The buttons are having a party, they always pair up in twos, either with the same colour or the same shape. One white button is strung onto the bracelet, all other buttons go into the bag. All players in turn draw buttons from the bag until they have five, one button is placed in the middle of the table. In your turn you may discard a button when it has the same shape or the same color as the button in the middle. The old button is taken out of the game. If you have no button to discard, you draw a button from the bag and may try to feel for the shape beforehand. White Lucky Buttons can be placed anytime. If you are the first to discard all your buttons you win and get the bracelet to wear, if you win the next round, you win the game.

A game of rampage, you take control of a Kaiju Monster and score points by rampaging the city.
This game is a print and play game for 2 to 4 players from 4 to 104 years old.
All you need are some D6. The first player of the turn throw some D6, and picks one to move his kaiju monster, if the monster ends in a square with a building, it's a rampage ! The building is destroyed. The next player picks one of the remaining D6 and so on... The next turn, the player to the left becomes the first player.
When all the building are destroyed, each player scores one point for one building destroyed by his monster. Players with the higher scores are the winners.

Rapunzel is coming home to her tower and wants to tell about her adventures - if she has forgotten the word for something she uses her magical hair to draw the item. Each player receives a tower. In your turn you look at a hair tile and use the hair string to lay out the contours of the item. The others guess, who guesses correctly receives the tile and adds it to his tower. Special cards win or lose you cards.  If you have added four tiles to your tower you win.

Five games for the price of one (and that one is free!).

NanoGames contains five business card sized games.

- Battle Lines: Destroy the enemy and invade his base.
- Business Card Derby: Destroy your opponent's car before he destroys yours.
- Elbow Room: Place and slide your pieces to create a path from your base to your opponents.
- Jump-n-Spin: Get across the board by jumping over your own counters, or swapping places with your opponent's.
- The Lineup Shuffle: Create a line of four counters be sliding in from any edge.

Download the file and print on cardstock.  Cut the two     sides apart and glue together with the Blue and Red ends lined up.  Cut the pieces from the ends and you are ready to play.  Each NANOgame is fully self contained, with all the counters and rules you need.  I recommend #2 coin envelopes for storage, to keep all the pieces together.

This 2 players wargame will be included in VaeVictis magazine.

It covers the event of summer 1618 during which the Bohemian parliament faced the power of the Habsbourg emperor. Armies will face each others to battle for the control of Bohemia and  the competition of influence will also take place in the different regional Diets to join a side.

Each player controls armies (composed of cavalry, infantry and artillery) under different generals. These armies will receive orders to conduct operations aiming at controlling or pillaging territories, destroying enemies or besieging cities.
Each player tries to control the largest amount of towns in Bohemia and particularly the capital Prague.
Combats and sieges are resolved with a roll to hit system using a d6. At the end of fights some of the routes units can be rallied, some are lost and some might potentially switch side.
Both sides also have resources they they can spend to supply armies or influence votes in regional diets to obtain their supports.
Random events will also affect the campaign such as snow, pillages, uprising or political shifts.

This is an operational level game with 108 counters and requires just a few coins (pennies will do) and a d6.

The participant who gets the most correct answers during the evening, wins.

A game to make a long car journey a little easier.

Each player (hopefully not including the driver!) is given a 5 x 5 bingo card. The cards feature various things you might see out of the windows during the trip. If you see something, announce it to the other players (who can check that you aren't cheating), and then mark the item on your card.

A long game could be all the 25 items on your card. But a shorter game should be that the winner is the first player to complete a line of items.

Made by Gulf, the oil and petrol supplier, and mailed out in an envelope so no box needed. As the bingo cards need to be cut up into single cards, I would guess this is a pretty rare item to find in its complete, original shape.

Das Kindergarten-Wörterspiel: Fahrzeuge

is a cooparative game for the languange training with childs from 3 to 6 years (with the help of one adult).

Ole, Marie and Zeki want to drive within Dad car to the zoo. And you can come along! While you drive around the board you have to achieve some challenges. The field on which the car is show which kind of challenge you get. If you were succesfull you gain one card. It is the goal to get 9 cards before the ample turn to red.

The Challenges are around story-telling, Memory and roleplay.

(user summary)

is a cooparative game for the languange training with childs from 3 to 6 years (with the help of one adult).

Ole, Marie and Zeki comes to visit you. While you going to your house you have to achieve some challenges. The field on which on the gameboard shows you which kind of challenge you get. If you were succesfull you gain one card. It is the goal to get 9 cards before the evening rise and you have to go to bed..

The Challenges are around story-telling, Memory and roleplay.

is a cooparative game for the languange training with childs from 3 to 6 years (with the help of one adult).

Together with zookeeper Tim you having a nice day at the zoo.
While you going to through the zoo you have to achieve some challenges. The field on which Tim stands shows you which kind of challenge you get. If you were succesfull you gain one card. It is the goal to get 9 cards before the zoo closes.

(user summary)

A football board game. No publisher on the box but that hasn't stopped him from a liitle hyperbole; the most "Exciting and Thrilling Game Ever Known".

The board is a checkered surface. Dice move the football piece or the footballer pieces. That's it!

The one who scores the most goals, wins.

A plastic board and 4 plastic foorballers are attached to handles. They work exactly the same as a pinball machine. Both players try to swing their footballers so that they can 'kick' the ball towards the opponent's goal.

The winner is the one who scores the most goals.

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – Words of Power Asylum Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Knowledge is both powerful and deadly in Words of Power, the second Asylum Pack from the upcoming "Revelations" cycle for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game.

Filled with eldritch Tomes, hidden Libraries, and the Relic Hunters determined to uncover their secrets, Words of Power heightens the different factions' quests for knowledge. While Independent agents offer their services to different groups, the Order of the Silver Twilight works in harmony to plunder the wealth of ancient journals. Research assistants at Miskatonic University delve unwittingly into danger as their study of powerful artifacts leads them across shadowy strangers, Cultists, Servitors, and monstrous black dogs.

The sixty cards in Words of Power (three copies each of twenty new cards) will promote exciting, new synergies with the Relics from the "Ancient Relics" cycle as well as with the musty, old cards lying dormant in your collection. Dark secrets in dusty tomes will determine the fate of the world. Who will find these Words of Power?
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Call of Cthulhu – The Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Glenn Drover's Empires: Age of Discovery – Plague Promo
description:
Glenn Drover's Empires: The Age of Discovery - Plague Promo is a set of 3 new Capital Buildings that can be added to the tile mix, giving greater variety from game to game.  There is one plague Capital Building for each of the three ages.

Age 1 Plague Building - Player succeeds on all discoveries in Age 1.

Age 2 Plague Building - Any colony with 3 or more population of any single color is reduced by one colonist.

Age 3 Plague Building - Player chooses one opponent colony and reduces it by 3 colonists.

The buildings are only compatible with the older "Age of Empires III" version of the game.

The deluxe edition already contains these tiles. The deluxe tiles are larger than this version, colors and material are also different.

Covel Dhanyata is not only one of the greatest summoners in all of Gaia, he is also a link with the spirit of the Fallen Angel Jigoku no Kami. Sometimes mortal, sometimes the avatar of a god, this man travels the world imposing his divine will over every supernatural creature that crosses his path.

Neutral Samael Optional Leader Mystic - Lv. 35
 At 4 | Dm 2 | Df 8 | Ar 0 | LP 12 | Re 10 | Mo 8/12 | AP 3/5
 Summoner Level: 2
 Affinity: Light, Dark, Void
 Master Summoner: When Covel pays a Character’s Cost to Summon, he pays 1 Action Point less. This ability cannot reduce a Character’s Cost to Summon below 1 Action Point.
 Superior Presence: For the purpose of placing a Character Covel has Summoned on the table, Covel’s Control Zone is 12 inches.
 ooo - Dark Star (Magic, Ranged Attack)
 Distance 16 inches. +1 Attack / +3 Damage.
 oo - Empowerment (Magic, Effect, Reactive)
 A Covel's Summon in his Zone of Control gain +2 Attack or +2 Defense until the end of the turn.
 
COVEL Jigoku no Kami’s Avatar
 Optional Leader: You may choose to use this skill card when Covel is in your party. If you do, Covel gains +25 levels, has the abilities on this card, and is a Leader.
 High Summoner: As the Avatar Covel’s Summoner Level increases to 3.
 Undaunted: Covel doesn’t lose Life Points due to Shock Checks.
 Divine Presence: During the Recovery Phase put a Bind Point on one of Covel's Summon within his Control Zone.
 Immortal Avatar: When Covel’s Life Points are reduced to 0, you may destroy a Character summoned by Covel within his Control Zone instead of him. If you do Covel recovers all of his Life Points.
 Enhanced Dark Star: Dark Star’s Action Point cost is reduced by one point.
 ooo - Dark Sun (Magic, Attack, Reactive)
 +6 Attack / +8 Damage. This Attack can only be used at the end of the Recovery Phase. Place a Devastation marker in a location that Covel has Line of Sight to that is within 20 inches of him. Once every character has completed their Activation during the Action Phase, Covel makes a single Attack against all units that are still within 4 inches of the Devastation marker. Remove the marker after the Attack.

The Fallen Angel Dinah, one of the Supreme leaders of Samael, is a legend among legends. Bestowed with godly powers and carrying within her soul the combined power of light and darkness, she is a positive power who seeks a better future for Gaia.

Light Samael Mystic Leader - Lv. 70
 At 5 | Dm 5 | Df 10 | Ar 0 | LP: 15 | Re 12 | Mo 8/12 | AP 3/5
 oo - Blessing (Magic, Effect)
 All friendly units within Dinah's Control Zone get +1 Attack and Defense as long as this ability remains in effect. This ability may be channeled through Lucera.
 Maintenance: o
 oo - Darkness Veil (Magic, Effect)
 All enemy units within Dinah's Control Zone suffer Blind Lv1. This ability may be channeled through Umbra. Effect 14.
 oo - Energy Projection (Magic, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (16 inches). This ability may be channeled through either Lucera or Umbra. If it is, then its Distance is halved.
 ooo - Devastation (Magic, Attack)
 +4 Attack. Dinah makes a single Attack on all units within 3 inches of either Lucera or Umbra. After resolving it, the spirit that was used to channel Devastation is removed from play.
 oooo - Reality Fold (Magic, Effect)
 Dinah trades positions on the board with either Lucera or Umbra. Dinah may take up to three friendly units within her Control Zone with her; these Characters are placed in base to base contact with Dinah after the switch in positions. This ability may be used to remove Characters from Hand-to-hand combat.
 Limit: Once per game.
 
DINAH Guardian Angels
 Divine Being
 While Dinah is in play her controller gets one additional Gnosis point per Turn.
 Umbra and Lucera
 Dinah has two spirit guardians that will be deployed when she is. They are Umbra and Lucera. These are no more than extensions of Dinah's will with no attributes other than Speed. They cannot be damaged in any way (or removed from the game by other character's abilities) nor can they take any Actions other than to move. Umbra and Lucera are removed from play if Dinah leaves play. If an enemy Character wishes to occupy the same space as either Umbra or Lucera, the spirits are moved up to 1 inch (into an open area) by their controlling player to vacate the space. Umbra and Lucera move freely and independently during Dinah's activation as Intangible units with a Speed of 12 and a Control Zone of 4 inches.
 Spiritual Link
 Dinah can channel some of her magic abilities through Umbra and Lucera. When she does this Dinah is considered as being in the same position on the board as her spirit guardian. If she tries to channel an ability through a spirit guardian that is not within 16 inches of her (measures are taken after declaring the ability activation), the ability has no effect and that spirit guardian is removed from play.
 Angel Guardian
 If Dinah would be removed from play for any reason, roll a die, if you roll a 6+ Dinah remain in play with one Life Point. If Dinah is not removed due to this ability then remove one of her spirit guardians from the board. Dinah may only use this ability if there is at least one spirit still in play.

Genmar Pheriu Dhanyata is one of the few Devah left in the world, a true rarity even within Samael. Though she has little combat skill, her mastery as a summoner knows no equal. Even the Fallen Angel Jigoku no Kami has acknowledged that, with regard to the invocation of supernatural entities, Genma is his only rival.

Neutral Samael Mystic - Lv. 35+
 At 4 | Dm 1 | Df 8 | Ar 0 | LP: 10 | Re 11 | Mo 6/10 | AP 3/4
 Summon Level: 3
 Affinity: Light, Dark, and Void
 Life Link: Genma may redirect damage she suffers for any reason to one of her Summons in play.
 ooo - Lasso of Unity (Magic, Effect)
 Place a Bind Point on one of Genma's Summons in her Control Zone.
 ooo - Vanish (Magic, Effect)
 Remove a Bind Point from an enemy Summon in Genma's Control Zone.
 ooo - Spiritual Beam (Magic, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (12 inches). +3 Damage.

A football related target game. The target has 3 holes in it and a goal. The idea is to get the football into one of these scoring areas. You get 5 points if you get it past the goalkeeper, and 10, 15 and 20 respectively for the circular holes.

The mode of getting the ball to the target is to use the footballer provided. His leg is attached to a spring so you can lever it backwards and then let go. If you are lucky, you might score some points. I expect a lot of practice is required.

The target says '50 UP' so I assume the first player to score 50 points, wins.

Translation from the back of the box - from Norwegian:

Do you dare to double? Who will be the leader?

Play the success from NRK (Norwegian Public TV) in your livingroom! Just as when Dan Børge Akerø askes questions on NRK, there is more than just knowledge that counts - you also have to control the game. Exploit the team's combined expertise and pick the right leader. Choose whether you want to change question or double the bet. Are you on the best quiz team in the room?

For the friends or the whole family: Two or three teams are asked fascinating questions in general knowledge. They get cryptic hints on what each question is about, and have to choose how hard they want to bet on how well the team knows the theme. Occasionally the questions will - to laughter and surprise - be about something completly different than what the players expect.

Quiz for everybody: The board game is ingeniously created, which makes the expert quizzer and the more normally knowledgeable may compete at the same level; as team mates or as opponents.

There are 32 cards with 2 questions and 3 answers for both questions in this game.
Player gets a card if he has answered both questions correctly.  Whoever has the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Players have 3-6 cards in hand depending on the number of the players. The first player reads the question and answer choices and his/her left neighbor answers - 
if it was correct, then the second question asked, is this also answered correctly, the neighbor gets the card. 
If it was incorrect the next player may risk with card answering this question.
If somebody runs out of cards in his/her hand, game ends and the player with the most collected cards wins.

Figure 5 cards face up and one for each category is determined. Then a letter uncovered and away you go. Each category must be an appropriate word to be found. Once the first player has to find an appropriate word to all categories he calls wise guy and all others must immediately stop writing.
If you're thinking: Urban-rural River, which is quite correct.5 cards are turned so they are face up and each get a category applied to.
Now one letter is to be uncovered and away you go. For each category the players find an appropriate word that begins with this letter, the words are to be written down. Once the first player finds an appropriate word for all categories he shouts : SCHLAUMEIER and the other Players must immediately stop writing.

Wer findet als Erster die richtigen Wörter?

Text from the Cover

Three promotional cards given out to those who pledged Forceball's Kickstarter campaign.

- Time Out: Take cards from the discard pile and put them back in the draw pile.
- Cool Head:  Enables you to draw a Stress card without ending your turn.
- Super Reflexes: Take the speed pile into your hand.

The cards are the mixed up with the regular game cards and add totally new effects to the game.

P.S.: There was a misprint on the cards that make them bigger than the regular game cards, so, they need to be trimmed.

From the game's website:

Travel Scavenger Hunt is a great new kids road trip game. To play, each player takes 5 cards and tries to locate various roadside objects. Players may have to find a stop sign, a pickup truck, a license plate with the letter Z, and many others. In addition, several funny "Hear It", "Smell It", and "Feel It" cards encouarge kids to use all their travel senses by listening for sounds, feeling for bumps, and sniffing for scents.

Travel Scavenger Hunt is the ultimate boredom beater and the perfect passenger for your next family roadtrip adventure.

From the back:
FICTION: Two exciting stories with four scenarios. Eascape from the holdfast portrays an early Klingon attempt to steal web technology. The Battle of Olsen's Reach describes the cease fire in which the Federation almost surrendered.

DATABASE: Questions, answers, examples, Why?, Battleforce 2000.

SCENARIOS: Five more exciting new battles for Star Fleet Battles.

TACTICS: Term papers, Ask Uncle Ardak, Victory at Origins.

FEDERATION & EMPIRE: A new scenario, commentaries, notes on the new edition, and optional rules.

SPECIAL FEATURE: playtest counters for space control ships.

NEW SHIPS: Federation War Destroyer and Battle Frigate.

From the back:
GHOSTLIGHT: DAY ONE is the story of a Federation F-8 fighter squadron on the first day of the Klingon invasion. Also included: a Ghostlight scenario, a description of the battle in game terms, and the class history of Federation Strike Carriers.

STAR FLEET DATABASE: Communications, announcements, product releases and schedules, Why?, Decisions of the Board of Proposals, Ask Kommodore Ketrick, Conventions and Tournaments, Battle Force 900 (Carrier Groups), and Just Say No!

DOOMSDAY: Even more information on the new Doomsday Edition of Star Fleet Battles. Explosion Strengths. Ten more questions from players. A list of rules changes from the new rulebook and notes on why they were made.

SCENARIOS: The Federation learns fleet tactics in the Battle of Adanerg. The first Kzinti carrier. Fighter pilots attack a convoy to cries of Tang Chi! Hydrans learn there is only one way to skin a cat. Pirates raid a planet defended by fighters. Romulans and Gorns learn the meaning of Cloaked Friendship. Andromedans assault an ISC base.

TACTICS: The largest selection of term papers in the history of Star Fleet Battles, including special sections on fighters and wild weasels. The Tactics Board. Victory at Origins. Ask Uncle Ardak about fighter tactics. Squadron Leader Karsh explains how fleets should engage enemy fighter squadrons.

FEDERATION & EMPIRE: Questions and Answers. A detailed example of carrier operations. The largest selection of Tactical Notes ever! Commentaries. Preview material from Total War. Kzinti CVs in DF&E. Romulan and Kzinti Options. Designer's notes on the new carrier rules. And the Four Powers War.

FOUR NEW CARRIER SSDs: The Romulan SeaHawk-B Light Carrier, Kzinti DDV First Carrier, Lyran Siberian Lion CVA, Gorn Tyrannosaurus-V CVA.

CAPTAIN'S MODULE P3 is a collection of 25 scenarios that will be used in future Star Fleet Battles products, in effect a sneak preview of several products. You have a chance to not only play these scenarios NOW, but to send in your playtest reports and propose additional rules and variants.

24 Star Fleet Battles Scenarios

This is the first of a series of informal modules containing various material from future Star Fleet Battles products. The "P" stands for: Preview, Prototype, and Playtest.

Contents include: Chaff, Chain Reactions, Death-Riders, The Frax, Hydran DD Variants, Scenarios, Tournaments, Total War Rules, and F&E Setup Charts.

From the shadows of the eastern lands, the young Kitsune Momiji
has come to serve Samael and the Fallen Angels as one of the top
spies of the organization. Trained as a perfect ninja from the moment she was just a cub, she combines her natural fox magic with the deadliest assassin’s arts.

In more than seven centuries of history, Samael has had among its
members the strangest of entities. With the possible exception of the Fallen Angels themselves, none of these beings can be compared to the creature that is Morrigan, Lady of Space and Time. Her origins are a mystery. She is a legend among legends, a being out of dark tales that roams the earth having taken physical form. The most widely believed tale is that she is one of the nine great Demon Queens, born in ancient times from a distortion in existence that gave her consciousness. However, this may simply be another of the many stories inspired by her thousands of years of existence.

Dark Samael Warrior/Mystic - Lv. 70

At 5 | Dm 6 | Df 9 | Ar 2 | LP: 40 | Re 11 | Mo 6/10 | AP 3/4

Innate Abilities: Damage Resistance, Critical Mastery

oo - Infinium (Magic, Ranged Attack)
Distance (12 inches).

o - Thanatos (Ki, Charge)
Thanatos may only be used against an Enemy Unit that has already been hit by Infinium this Turn. During the movement for this ability Morrigan is Intangible.

Morrigan - Lady of Space and Time

Phase Step: Morrigan ignores all movement penalties due to difficult terrain, as well as climbing or descending from elevations.

Space Control: An enemy Unit that crosses or is inside of Morrigan's Control Zone reduces the distance that they can Walk, Run, or Charge by 2 inches. Characters of a level equal to or greater than Morrigan's are not affected by this ability.

Nahimana is the daughter of the Fallen Angel Mo'ôhtavo'nehe, the preeminent Jayán in recent memory. Having inherited many of her father’s qualities, she is an honorable and proud warrior who fights with such ferocity that she has earned the nickname “Princess Blood” among her fellows in Samael.

Ophiel Akerontes Zeros, favourite son of the darkness, is the youngest and most ambitious of all the lords of Samael. Despite being the last to join the Circle of Fallen Angels, in only two decades he has become one of the most powerful and active members of the Circle. He is a gloomy figure who enjoys using his shadowy network of agents to advance his agenda of absolute chaos.

+ 3 Attack / +3 Damage.

Yuki's most striking feature is her icy beauty. One of the last Ice Maidens of Varje, she has been assigned to observe humanity in the name of the fallen angels of Samael. Her powerful elemental powers should help keep her safe during her sojourn.

Neutral Samael Mystic - Lv. 35

The Circle of Fallen Angels regularly employs resourceful and dangerous individuals to further its goals. Making up a shadowy network of operatives, Samael Agents are empowered to sow chaos and confusion across all of Gaia in their name.

boardgame: USSR (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
The USSR represents a whole epoch of World History. During the ten decades that this country was in existence its enormous territory was for the most part closed to foreign visitors, which excited many myths about the Soviet reality. Locked behind the Iron curtain that blocked off the flow of information, and described by Sir Winston Churchill as "the mystery wrapped inside an enigma" the USSR inspired hatred among many but curiosity among all. The memorabilia and souvenirs from the USSR are some of the most recognized and popular in the world. And with the Russian current government pursuing an ever-uncertain political course of its larger predecessors the Map Ticket-to-Ride - USSR will be of interest for virtual travelers from all over the world.


СССР олицетворяет целую эпоху всемирной истории. На протяжении семи десятилетий существования этого государства его громадная территория была практически закрыта для иностранных посетителей, что провоцировало  появление огромного количества мифов об СССР, которые до сих пор не дают угаснуть интересу к его истории. Символика уже давно не существующего государства продолжает занимать туристов со всего мира и активно тиражируется в качестве сувенирной продукции. Карта Ticket to ride СССР будет интересна не только многочисленным жителям бывших советских республик, но и путешественникам со всего света".

This is a variant of Xiangqi based on the Three Kingdoms era, but is distinct from San Guo Qi.

The board is four sided. Three sides represents the three states of the Three Kingdoms era, i.e. Shu, Wu and Wei, and have the same complement of seeds as in Xiangqi. The fourth side contains only 5 seeds, i.e. Han Emperor, 3 Chariots and 1 Cannon.

The objective of the game is to defeat the other two states.

The Han Emperor cannot move. Players may enter into an alliance with any other player by moving one of his/her Horse onto specific spaces next to the Han Emperor seed. When that happens, the third player takes control of the Han Emperor's 3 Chariots and 1 Cannon seeds. Once allianced, the two players cannot capture each other's seeds.

The Han Emperor may also be disposed, by capturing with a Horse. When that happens, the other two players automatically enter into an alliance, and the third player takes control of the Han Emperor's 3 Chariots and 1 Cannon seeds.

The movements of the pieces remain largely the same as Xiangqi except for the following differences:
1) It is no longer possible to block the Horses or Elephants/Ministers.
2) On the first move of the game for each respective player, players cannot move the Cannon out of their own territory.
3) Witin their own territory, Soldiers can only move forward one step at a time. Upon reaching the border, Soldiers may also move 1 step sideways. Once beyond the border, Soldiers may move 1 step forward/backward/sideways, but cannot retreat behind the border.

When a player is checkmated and General seed is captured, his/her remaining seeds are taken over by the player who captured the General seed.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Spook is a game that can be played with the Series: Shibumi game system. It uses the 4×4 board, 15 red, 14 black, and 1 white ball.

				
				
					RulesThe board (the haunted castle) starts empty.

A Visit to the Haunted Castle

Two players, Red and Black, take turns placing one ball of their colour either on an empty board hole or on a 2×2 platform. Starting with Red the pyramid is built up until in the last turn Black places the white ball (Spooky the ghost) on top.

Then Red makes the first move of the second phase of the game.

The Getaway
(To be played in candlelight.)

On their turn a player must either


     remove a ball (of any colour) to drop Spooky or
     remove a free ball (of any colour) which is orthogonally adjacent to Spooky and move Spooky there. If possible this move may be repeated arbitrarily removing additional balls of the same colour.


Only if no move is available then


     if Spooky is isolated he must be placed orthogonally adjacent to any ball on the board or on a platform,
     otherwise one unpinned ball of the opponent must be removed (a ball drop may occur),
     only if there are no unpinned opponent balls, the active player must pass his turn.


As usual, pinned balls (supporting more than one ball) may not be removed.

Aim

The player who has no more balls of their colour on the board wins the game.

OutRank your opponents and be the first person to exhaust their playing hand. OutRank features the five branches of the Military. Army, Navy, Marines, Air Force and Coast Guard.
In OutRank each player is dealt 7 cards, the remaining cards become the draw pile. The first player challenges the player to their left with 1-5 cards of the same rank from their hand. The player answering the challenge can either surrender or try and OutRank each card with a card from their hand of the same suit and higher rank. The answering player may choose to surrender at any time by picking up all cards in play and adding them to their hand. All other players are in a support phase and have the option of supporting a challenge with cards from their hand which have the same rank as cards already in play. If all the challenging cards are answered and there are no other cards being played then the cards in play are removed from the game. The player who was answering becomes the new attacker and play continues clockwise. A game is over when a player has used all of the cards in their hand.

Become a hockey team manager. Your goal is to create a strong team roster. To accomplish this, You have been given a specific amount of money. Think carefully about every bid You make. How much really is every player worth? Bid too much and You risk losing everything, bid too little and someone else will get this player. Because there are other managers biding for the best players.

"Lissy horse behavior" in English
Players have 3-6 cards in hand depending on the number of the players. The first player reads the question and answer choices and his/her left neighbor answers -
if it was correct, then the second question asked, is this also answered correctly, the neighbor gets the card.
If it was incorrect the next player may risk with card answering this question.
If somebody runs out of cards in his/her hand, game ends and the player with the most collected cards wins.

"Lissy Around the horse" in English
Players have 3-6 cards in hand depending on the number of the players. The first player reads the question and answer choices and his/her left neighbor answers -
if it was correct, then the second question asked, is this also answered correctly, the neighbor gets the card.
If it was incorrect the next player may risk with card answering this question.
If somebody runs out of cards in his/her hand, game ends and the player with the most collected cards wins.

Game description from the publisher:

Qua-Boom! After an epic battle between Quaxos and the Three-Eyed Dragon of the Quarmageddon, the Quarmageddon is released upon us! Quarriors! Quarmageddon, the second expansion set to the Quarriors! dice-building game, adds forty new custom dice powering six new Creatures and two new Spells for you to bring a serious smack-down to the battlefield of hexahedron monster combat. Some battle-hardened Creatures come equipped with a new "Immunity" ability so they can laugh off whatever your opponents throw at you! No guts, no Glory!

This expansion set features an updated Quarriors! rulebook and a box that stores the expansion and base set.

description:
Lilly the Witch conjures up animals into the nursery and the player must find the correct counter-spell to let the animals disappear before the parents come home. The animal cards are laid out in a 5 x 5 square and there are witch figure in front of four rows. You take one of the characters, places them in front of the free row/column, revealing the cards and if he/she can disapear any animal he takes card. 
If there is a empty row/column in this game, player with the most animals wins

World of baby animals
Facts from the animal kingdom: How can an emperor penguin its young among thousands? As newly hatched turtles find their way to the sea? Hedgehogs have spines at birth?

There are 2 questions in each card with 3 answers for each questions. The winner is whoever can answer most questions correctly and thus can collect the most cards.
If the current player answers incorrectly the next player may risk with one of his collected card to answer it.

Contents:

     32 large format cards with color photos

World of dogs
Which is the fastest dog in the world? Where the Cocker Spaniel gets its name? Here, players learn amazing facts about the most popular dog breeds.

There are 2 questions in each card with 3 answers for each questions. The winner is whoever can answer most questions correctly and thus can collect the most cards.
If the current player answers incorrectly the next player may risk with one of his collected card to answer it.

Contents:

     32 large format cards with color photos

"Wonders of nature" in English
The cards are divided into eight habitats: forests, lakes, rivers, mountain rain forests, savannas, steppes, deserts. 
The habitats have different frame colors for clarity. For each habitat, there is one card each of the following five groups, ie a total of 5 cards: mammals, birds, amphibians and reptiles, spiders and insects, and plants. The aim of the game is to win by correctly answering the questions, all five cards of a habitat. For each question there are three possible answers,

boardgame: Survival Camp: Kickstarter Exclusive Threat Cards
description:
This is a set of additional Threat cards for the "Survival Camp" game.  These cards were available exclusively to those who pledged to support the "Survival Camp" game project on Kickstarter.

The 5 Kickstarter Exclusive Threat Cards are:

     GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL DESTROYS ALL OF YOUR DEFENSE UNLESS GUARDED.
     ILLNESS STRIKES YOUR CAMP. DISCARD YOUR TENT IN FEAR OF CONTAMINATION.
     RATS INFESTED YOUR CROPS. LOSE 1 COMPONENT OF YOUR GARDEN.
     PACK OF HUNGRY DOGS THREATEN CROPS. FEED THEM YOUR GARDEN OR ELSE.
     WATER WAS NOT PURIFIED PROPERLY. DISCARD ALL WATER COMPONENTS.


Add these extra threat cards into the deck with the other Threat cards to make the game a little more challenging.

World of cats
In which country cats were worshiped as gods? Why do cats eyes glow in the dark when light falls on them? How do cats orient in complete darkness? Interesting facts about cats.

An exciting card game of horse track racing, odds and wagering. Horses (cards) advance on the track (cards) each assigned odds of winning (based on card value) and wagers (punts) are made by the players before the race based on those odds. There are six races in a game and the player who wins the most "money" is the winner.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Fire Academy Challenge
description:
This PDF was a promotional item released only to the game's backers on Kickstarter in conjunction with a contest held by the game's creator.

The Fire Academy Challenge is a series of scripted "puzzles". The objective is the same: to rescue at least seven trapped victims before the fire kills four or the building collapses.

Included are sheets to log your progress through each of the puzzles along with a scoring system designed to allow groups to match their scores to other teams.
