Educational trivia game for 5's and under.

Simple trivia questions (for us!) but challenging to a 4 year old. It works by battery. The child answer the questions on the game cards by pushing the wand into the appropriate holes.  It "smiles" at the right answer, "frowns" at the wrong one.

There are 141 Questions and 16 game cards that fit over the electronics. The rules are inside the lid.

The message in this educational game is "Stay safe on the roads".

See who can be first to visit the library, school, friend's house and post office by crossing the roads safely. This will help build awareness of road safety, in particular, how and where to cross roads.

Objective:
gain the largest amount/value of coins stored in treasure chests
 - acting by turn - 
coins (gold, silver, copper) are going to be distributed randomly into treasure chests - 6 per chest 
chests are going to be stapled (start position)
throw the die - now you may distribute/split rolled number on one or more chests to move with: if you succeed placing the very last moved chest on a space showing the same colour:
open the chest picking out the topmost coin 
forming gaps in between chests is not allowed, they must touch at least another one;  
game ends if chests are empty:
counting coins: gold 10, silver 5, copper 1 point(s)

The Natural Disaster Cards are a set of promotional cards for the Rolling Freight game.  Natural Disasters are shuffled into the contract deck during setup - one into the ‘B’ phase and two into the ‘C’ deck. During the game, when a natural disaster card is drawn from the contract deck, draw a completed contract card at random and remove that link from the game board, along with all saturation markers at both ends of the link. Finish filling the contract track. Finally, place the destroyed contract face-down on top of.its deck. If multiple disasters are drawn while filling the track, destroy additional completed contracts and place them face down on top of the deck, in the order they were drawn.

The Texas Map for the Rolling Freight game was made available as an exclusive item to people that pledged to support the Rolling Freight project on Kickstarter.  This map was designed to be an apegames.com download, but was instead printed copies for each copy of the game that we ship for Kickstarter backers.  It was printed on thick paper and quarter-fold it so that it fits nicely into the Rolling Freight box.  This map is designed as a quick 2-3 player map which can be played in about an hour.

"It's all fun and games until someone lands in the slammer!"

Another monopoly clone but this time for the Scranton, PA area.  The board features notable business such as the Radisson Lackawanna Station Hotel, The University of Scranton and Savvi Formalwear by Sarno & Son.

boardgame: Grunt: The Modern Death-dealing Card Game
description:
Grunt is a warfare card game using a board to depict 6 combat areas where Grunts (infantry) engage in combat.

The 6 combat areas depicted on the board (Town, Woods, Ruins, Hills, Swamp, and Fields) affect combat potential dependng on equipment and attack types used. Players take turn drawing and playing cards. Types of cards are: Black (Action) cards, Red (Explosives) cards, Green (Strike) cards, Orange (Equipment) cards, and Blue (Special Defense) cards.

Black Action or Green Strike cards are played hidden and take effect only on the next turn. This is done to simulate the time it takes to perform combat manoeuvres. Orange Equipment and Red Explosives cards must be played immediately when drawn. Blue Special Defense cards can be played at any time.

Play proceeds until resources (the main deck of cards) have been exhausted. The player with the most victory points is the winner.

Objective: gain 8 (knowledge) chips, acquired by successful answered questions

each player moves according rolled die - drawing a question card due to space landed on - correct answer (multiple chice - 1 out of 4) get a chip
landing on an image get 2 chips;
if the answer was wrong you are going to loose chips

boardgame: Eagles of the Empire: Medina de Rioseco
description:
(from Compass games website :)

The Battle of Medina de Rioseco, 14 July 1808, was fought during Napoleon’s War in Spain between two Spanish Armies under the commands of General Joaquín Blake and General Gregorio García de la Cuesta, and Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bessières in which the Spanish force was defeated after a closely fought, but determined assault by the French.

Medina de Rioseco was the sole French triumph in the initial invasion of Spain, which failed to seize the country, ultimately requiring a fresh campaign by the Grand Armée under the command of Napoleon himself to (temporarily) secure Spain.

Medina de Rioseco is the fifth game in the Eagles of the Empire series, and is a stand-alone add-in battle to Spanish Eagles (Talavera, 27th and 28th of July 1809, and Albuera, 16th of May 1811.) and is completely playable by itself. Medina de Rioseco features both a historical and alternative battle that allows players to recreate the engagement as it was fought or to bring in additional Spanish forces available but not present at the battle.
family: Series: Eagles of the Empire (Avalanche Press)

description:
Lassos & Longhorns is a cooperative dice game where players work together to drive a herd of cattle north to the rail station. By balancing the speed at which they travel, the weight of the herd, and the health of the cowboys, players race competitors to be the first to Wayward Falls to sell their cattle for the highest price. But Butch Montgomery's gang of cattle rustlers, as well as the terrain, weather, and wild animals, aren't going to make it easy for them.

Lassos & Longhorns uses simple, push-your-luck mechanics, to allow for one to four players to work together on the cattle drive. The game is free to download over on the Corvid Games website.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"Humanity, since its beginning, admires the restless beauty of fire. It's hard to say if this fascination comes from its light, its heat or from the fear of its destructive power."

"In Piro, players are stimulated to reflect about the dificulty of controlling the fire and will discover why it is called the most rebellious of the four elements."

"The conflagration has just begun... and you with your own hands have it under control!"

"The goal: Every player must spread the fire through the most number of circles on the board. A circle is said "aflame" when it contains exactly one piece of each color (1 red, 1 yellow and 1 orange) or 3 of the same kind (e.g. 3 yellow pieces). Players receive points for completing this task and the winner is the player who first reaches 25 points!"

In the 80’s, a game company called Tendino created a revolutionary game console called Tendino Entertainment System (TES). Your company, 8-bit Inc, is one of the many companies trying to make the best games for the system and compete with them in the Game Prix Award (GPA).

This is a solo game where the player becomes the manager of 8-bit Inc. The player's job is to manage workers to create game points in graphics, sound, and programming by distributing 3 rolled dice (3D6) on workers. The more dice the player puts into a worker the more game points he will create and the better the game will become. Some workers are better than others.

The player also manages money and hires more workers by using the rolled dice. At the end of summer, the player must pay the workers wages (or lose them) and must compete in the Game Prix Award against other companies.

Tune Up, "where you are the mechanic," published by Total Source Parts and Accessories,

For ages 6 and up, 1 or more "mechanics." It plays like the classic game OPERATION, but instead of a human patient, you're working on a cartoonish forklift, dealing with items like an empty tank, sparkless plug, squeaky wheel, sore bottom, kinked hose, etc., that need to be replaced with a TotalSource replacement part.

A word game. The pieces have both letters and black squares on them. The objective is to use all your tiles by making a correct Crossword. The player wins if he has used his last tile.

It can be played as a puzzle or it can be played competitively.

The box claims:
A Boon to the Sick
Days of Amusement
Entirely New and Novel
A 'Service' Favourite
186 tiles

The 'Service' reference probably means the game was published within the years 1939 to 1945 (World War II).

An expansion for Heroes of the Blitzkrieg.

In Defeat, Defiance brings the British Expeditionary Force to the battlefield of France 1940. An expansion to the hugely popular Heroes of the Blitzkrieg, In Defeat, Defiance is the biggest British expansion ever. Included are over 170 counters, and fourteen intriguing scenarios.

The 170 counters include Vickers, Cruiser, and Matilda tanks, Bren carriers, 2pdr and 25mm Anti-tank guns, 51mm mortars, Leaders, heroes, snipers, Squads, Bren guns, Boys Anti-tank rifles, and much more. The fourteen scenarios, all designed by Dale Holmstrom (The Swift and the Bold) and Mark Walker, cover everything from British and SS fighting near Arras to Rommel crossing the Brasse Canal."

This game is no longer available and you can find the new, improved and expanded edition in Lock 'n Load Tactical: Heroes in Defiance

Objective:
collect 5 chips before you are allowed answering the final questions
 - acting by turn -
determine difficulty level first 
level one: give 1 correct answer for 1 question (5 times out of 5 different terms); level two: 2 correct answers for 2 questions ...; level three: 3 correct answers...  
roll the die, move your meeple, draw appropiate (colour) question or event card, follow instructions if you land on a space with a symbol.. if you had bad luck even back to starting point.
As soon as you've acquired 5 chips reaching target spaces you are protected - now you only have to find the correct answer(s) for the final question(s)

"Hoops of Fire" is a print-and-play* two-player card game in which you coach a basketball team. The goal is for the game to be enjoyable both for basketball junkies who are new to card/board games, and for gamers who have little idea about basketball.

Draft your players and defensive strategy, then control the line-up  throughout the game as exhaustion and fouls pile up. Stick to the shots that your star players are most comfortable with, or set up a pick and roll to see how the defense reacts. Finally, steal an unlikely away victory with that buzzer beater!

Example of play video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nmLNsrNt4ts

* - that being said, Quique (oladola) developed a beautiful Tabletop Simulator module (http://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=493360050&searchtext=hoops+on+fire - let us know in case you'd like to try it out), while Pedro (torontoraptors) is currently working hard on an Android app!

description:
The game where is mixed football, dice rolling and trivia.
There are 10 footballers and 1 goalkeeper on the big game board for both players. Player rolls 4 dice on his turn. He may move his footballers as many squares indicated on 2 usual dice.
There are yellow or red card on one side of the 2 other dice.
If yellow card is rolled player's opponent draws yellow card and asks a question about Estonian football (soccer). If current player answers correctly he may continue his turn.
If he answers uncorrectly he misses the turn.
If red card is rolled player must remove one of his footballer from the game.

Player who scores most at the end of the game hitting the goals is the winner.

From the publisher:
This is a dice game, that combines trivia, football (not American "football") and dice luck.
Game goes by the rules of football and also gives advantage to the player who has more knowledge about Estonian national football team.
Each player tries to score goals, by going by the game board instructions, while each correct answer will give you another chance to hit a goal.

The unicorns from all over the world organize a competition flight.
Player with his unicorn has 2 options: move forward 1 space on the game board or risk and roll 6 dice with combination of courage, foresight and luck. If he lucky he may move more than 1 space or sometimes needs to reset his character this turn. Who plays and sent at the end arrives first, wins.

The objective is to locate six items that will enable to transorm Star pony into a unicorn.
49 cards are laid out face down on the game board, and the stall is being prepared. Plyer roll the dice and move either horizontally or vertically and in the target field he may uncover an adjacent tile. Player may continue her turn if she opens an item she doesn't have yet, but if she opens Buddy the dog she loses everything opened this turn.

Who has 6 different items and find also a stall cards may try to open the barn and win the game.

Game played with two identical sets of 34 tiles. Each tile is marked with one of the card suits: spades, hearts, diamonds or clubs, plus one of the values from 7 up to ace.

You try to get rid of your tiles in a domino-like manner. In the same suit with a value one higher or lower than the tile next to it. Or in a different suit with the same value. The black and red joker have special rules.

First one to get rid of his tiles wins.

Sheltie and the other ponies escape and the players try to catch them. In order to retrieve as many ponies on the paddock, you need luck, courage and the right spin with the spinner.

The top card is revealed, the player spins the spinner and press down the edge of the roundabout before the roundabout even touched the table. Is the subject form the card is touched the next card is revealed and player may continue her turn but if the sucject is not touched she loses all cards revealed this turn. Sheltie acts as a wildcard.

Player with the most cards revealed = ponies catched at the end of the game is the winner.

From Publisher Website:

Abstractables is the fast-paced, creative thinking, word guessing game where you try to get your team to correctly guess a word or phrase by arranging and interacting with simple shapes before the time runs out.

Do you know what a cow looks like? Of course you do, but can you design one with basic shapes? And get your team to guess it correctly? And do it all in 60 seconds while your teammates are screaming hilariously wrong answers at you? Welcome to ABSTRACTABLES!

With 2000 words and phrases from 8 categories, including animals, people, locations, food, sayings, motions, objects and entertainment, the possibilities for fun for the whole family are endless and the replay value incredibly high. You simply roll the die, draw a card, and start moving the pieces. It’s so fun and easy, anyone can play. Bring Abstractables to your next family gathering or game night and watch the hilarity ensue!

It levels the playing field. Not artistic, don't worry, you don't need to draw anything. Just move the pieces to form the shape to communicate the word or phrase to your team.

Abstractables contains 3-layer European Birch hardwood pieces, category cards with over 2000 words or phrases, a one-minute timer, and an 8-sided category die.

Twelve Days of Christmas is a press-your-luck game based on the classic English Christmas carol.

A player's turn consists of three phases: a card-drawing phase, a set-playing phase, and a gift-giving phase. First you draw one or more cards, and possibly pass them out to other players. Then you must lay down one set of cards. Finally, you give one card as a gift to the player on your right, ending your turn. When 12 “Days of Christmas” cards are showing, the round is over and you all total your scores.

This is an expanded version of the game Stocking Stuffers. http://www.boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/15075
Simple spin and move game from the mid-1950's from the Whitman Company. Object is to feed animals of the game in order to score extra points.

As in the fairy tale the animals must jump on each others’ backs.

Some donkey cards are on the table. Each player receives six cards, which he keeps hidden in his hand. The remaining cards form the draw pile.

In his turn player play a card on the table. 
He may play dog sitting on donkey, cat sitting on dog and rooster sitting on cat. Additionally player may play card upon another card with same color
If the player has no matching card, he draws one from the draw pile and his turn is over.

But beware! The rooster will mess up the entire game. If the player has placed a rooster, then he draws a card from the deck and looks out one of the two animals shown. Then the card is placed to the left player and asked if he has the animal in the selected color in his the hand. If no, he takes it to his hand. If Yes the both cards move to next player unitl someone has taken all this cards.

The player who discards the last card must make the sound of one of the animals depicted on the last card. 
The winner is the first who plays his last card.

boardgame: The Stately Homes of Great Britain
description:
A rare party game from the Goodall company.

Each guest or team receive a card with anagrams of the names of well-known Stately Homes. During the evening the guests can look at the anagrams and work out their answers. When the host calls "Time", the guest or team with the most correct answers, wins.

Party game of trivia questions.

Each guest or team is given a card with 36 questions on it. During the evening they are encouraged to fill in all the correct answers, or just guess the ones they don't know. When the host calls "Time", the guest or team with the most correct answers, wins.

This is a Roulette related game from around the 1930's by Chad Valley. The game is set inside the box with a metal spinner. All the players except the bank can bet any amount of counters they wish, on any colour they wish. Then you place the ball into the device, and give it a spin, and see where the ball falls out. This lights one of the circles at the side and who ever has bet on that colour wins.

The colours offer the odds as follows:
Green 2 - 1
Yellow 3 - 1
Blue 5 - 1
Pink 7 - 1
Red 15 - 1

You can bet with counters, or real money if everyone agrees. The Bank should be a different person each 'race'.

A dice game for very young children. Player move animals along a path by rolling a colour dice (moving to the next square of the colour the dice is showing). Special dice envents are the butterfly (move to the same colour you are standing on) and the bug (moving backwards). The winning player of a run gets a chip, then the race starts againg. First player with 3 chips is winner.

"Back of the box" desciption: Little animals are walking along a path. The die shows red! The little dog can move onto the next red square... A game that encourages communication between toddlers and their parents.

Educational game recommended for Sunday School teachers to help children with their numbers' recognition.

In a turn, the child spins a number and then reads out the word on their board relating to that number. That word can then be crossed off, i.e. a Blackout counter is put on to it. Play proceeds until one child has crossed off all their words.

The lessons can be graded. Younger children can start with Blue cards, the Yellow can be attempted, then Green, and finally Red. It is suggested that Red is not used until the teacher is sure that the children are ready

boardgame: Tout est bien qui finit bien
description:
40 illustrated cards. The cards are divided into four series designated by different colored borders and by an illustration in the upper left corner of either la Maison, le Chäteau, le Jardin, or le Moulin. Each series is further divided into cards illustrated with the same objects, i.e. a dog, a cat, a key, a door, a banner, a man, a woman, a young boy, and a mousetrap.

The object of the game appears to be to collect all the keys, all the doors, etc by asking other players for the cards you need, in a Quartet or Authors fashion

Children's game, aimed at improving their spelling.

In a turn, the player spins the spinner and this will indicate two letters. He writes them down and, if it is the very first turn, he spins again and notes two more letters. If he can now make a word, using the letters, he tells the other players and he scores some points. If he cannot make a word, he passes. When it is his turn again, he spins the spinner but only once now, and gets two more letters. The longer the word, the more points. The first to score 30 points is the winner.

This is a mini-expansion for Shootin' Ladders. It adds the board hazard 'Glass of Milk'.

From the rules:
It should come as no surprise that milk & cookies are a lethal combination - for cookies, that is. And so, much like Oil Barrels in other shooter games, Glasses of Milk can be used as explosive hazards in SHOOTIN' LADDERS.

This expansion comes on a single 8.5" x 11" glosssy thick sheet of paper. The lower section of the paper is designed to be cut apart giving six different glasses of milk.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – A Poisoned Spear
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Throughout the "A Tale of Champions" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game, unique knights, lords, and ladies have assembled to observe the spectacles of battle and to use such assemblies to further their hidden agendas. Now the inevitable confrontations come to a head in the cycle's final Chapter Pack, A Poisoned Spear!

Its new cards continue to support the melee format's layered alliances and enmities, as well as the new Melee and Joust keywords. New characters, attachments, and a fantastic new location are certain to promote players' appreciation of the most notable and unique characters from George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire.

description:
Card game issued one card at a time in cigarettes made by W A & A C Churchman. Two games are described using these cards.

Frisky Families is a Happy Families game. 52 cards were issued, in 13 sets of four. Each set of four is attributed to a different animal. Frisky himself, is card no 1 and he is a rabbit. The game play is similar to other set collecting games. In his turn, a player may ask another player for a certain card. If that player has such a card, he must hand it over. The turn can now be extended by asking for another card, and so on. When you have a set of four, you may lay it in front of you. After all 13 sets have been collected, the player with the most sets, wins.

Game 2 is just called Frisky.

This is a trick-taking game. In effect, you play with 52 cards, all in one suit, and the higher card is most likely to take the trick. The cards are ranked 2 to 52. Card 1, Frisky' is the highest card though and will always win the trick in which he is played. After all the cards have been played, the player with the most cards in tricks, wins.

The original game was published in 1935. Since then, there has been at least one reproduction set.

Other than her prodigious elemental powers and obsession with the procurement of supernatural artifacts not much is known about this mysterious young woman. One of the foremost mercenaries of Gaia she works for the highest bidder, only to disappear suddenly once the contract is completed.

Race Condition is a card game that allows competing players to modify a shared computer program represented by playing cards.

Game cards and counters are arranged on a table top to represent the order of and relationship between instructions, the current instruction, and the values of variables. Competing players take turns modifying the computer program until one of them achieves his or her objective of setting a variable to a particular value. Players accomplish their goals by appending instructions to the program, and by performing special actions such as inserting, deleting, or moving instructions. As the players modify the program, it advances, modifying variables and potentially moving players closer to or further from their objectives.

description:
The game was published in 2005 as a part of a local educational program called "Mina Ise" (I myself). The game consists of 45 different cards, where there are 22 card pairs, where each pair has a regular (local Estonian, non-foregin) letters and J letter as being a part vowel and part consonant alone, making it to be 45 cards.
Each card pair has something drawn on it, either a living thing or some item. Under the picture there are a word and under a word a sentence to use the word in. Trick is the correct "kääne" (word ending).
Scoring has multiple possibilities.

Variant 1: Naming the thing on the picture and the letter it begins with scores you the card. 10 cards wins the game.

Variant 2: Each word has parts of it different colored, while yellow part is 1 point, red part 3 points and blue part 5 points. 35 points and you win the game.

From the publisher:
Game made by a local travel agency to find about more about traveling in Europe.
Game is basic roll and move, with treasure collection and trivia. Each players goal is to travel to a location shown on the card they have. Game comes with a 104 pages rule book which contains info for each country destination.
Be the first to reach destination to win the game.

Game comes in the nice box shaped like a briefcase.

First game in a series of 4, in which each game is one season in the year.
The series in meant to be education for children.

Each game in the series is meant for 2-12 young players. The first game called "Midrimäng" is about winter and the wonder of Christmas. All games later can be combines into one large game.

Game is played very easily.
Each player is a small elf that must hide behind pine trees and deliver candies into the shoes of kids. Game has a eyes of children part (for adult player) that once each turn moves around and tries to find little elves.
Do you hide or deliver for points - you decide!

Winner is the first to deliver all candies.

From the publisher:
This is a game about finding connections between different things. Game comes with so many great cards that will blow your mind.
Each player moves around the game board (Monopoly like board), where each space is a category.
Under that category someone will draw a card and asks you something. Example: "How is connected chicken egg and A4 paper format?".
Answer correct, collect the card and keep moving. Idea is to reach the center field and be crowned as smart as Da Vinci himself.

Educational game made for schools to introduce 4 parts of the nature. Game is simple, just roll and move through the game board and collect knowledge.
On the way to the end, you must stop on card spaces and answer what it on the picture.

Also, the rule book is 76 pages and can be used as small RPG with d20 dice and a journey to the nature.

What is Chemistry: An Atom Building Game?
Students compete to create the largest atom using their knowledge of chemistry in this exciting deck building game.  Each player starts out with a hydrogen atom and must answer a question based on chemistry to gain quarks.  The quarks are converted into one of three subatomic particles.  Players can use various discoveries in chemistry (i.e. Chemistry cards) to enhance their turn to gain more quarks.

The game is designed for chemistry students and it is a fun and nontraditional way to review for tests.  So, if you want to be ready for your next chemistry test, play Chemistry: An Atom Building Game.

Cup Final is a soccer board game, played on a 30x50 grid.  Each player has 11 footballers on the board, which exert a zone of control which is used to intercept the ball.  The ball is moved by means of dice throw which tells you how many squares the ball moves, and the direction type (e.g. diagonally).  The board is also divided into 6 zones, and when the ball is moved into a new zone, an Action Card is drawn which may make you lose possession, or allow you to move the ball through ZoCs.  Every five minutes the players are allowed to redistribute their footballers around the board.

boardgame: Battlefield Evolution: Warbirds of World War II – A Supplement for Battlefield Evolution: World at War
description:
From the back of the Book
It was during World War II that the aircraft truly evolved into a state of the art military weapon, far out-shadowing its not inconsiderable achievements of the previous war. The citizens of every nation were already living in dread of the bomber, and its promise to rain death down upon the cities in which they lived with impunity – that nightmare was now a reality.

From the outset of the war, Germany demonstrated just how lethal aircraft could be when used in conjunction with infantry and armour. Indeed, this was the very cornerstone of Blitzkrieg. However, aircraft evolved into weapon delivery platforms in their own right. For the first time, ship-launched attack aircraft could destroy vessels far beyond the visual range of their parent carrier, as demonstrated first by the Royal Navy at Taranto, then at Pearl Harbour by Japan, and finally by US forces as they hopped across the islands of the Pacific.

Heavy bombers pounded cities and factories throughout the war, but the dreams commanders had of aircraft being able to win wars proved to be mislead. A population bombarded did not automatically result in a breakdown of national morale – quite the opposite. Also, technology evolved to counter bomber attacks, from the dogged defence of the British Isles during the Battle of Britain, to the sophisticated layered defences of Germany against the thousand bomber raids.

On the battlefield, the importance of air superiority became apparent, with the dominant force claiming the skies. Once enemy aircraft had been chased from the battlefield, fighter bombers used cannon, bomb and rocket to annihilate ground forces. Even mighty tanks were fodder for the Stuka, Thunderbolt and Typhoon.

Infantry and armour were still needed to wage war and take ground. The addition of aircraft made their task either a great deal easier or far more difficult, depending on how their pilots fared in the air.

This book contains aircraft from every period of the war, for every military force featured in Battlefield Evolution: World at War. Each aircraft has been painstakingly researched, and includes upgrades throughout the war, and multiple payload options, guaranteeing you will find a use for all the weapons included in your average aircraft kit!

This is a shooting game with a moving target.

Instructions for setting the game up:
Fit the round uprights into the wood bases, highest to the right, shortest to the left, and ensure the slots run inline down the board. Fit the printed housings or "pens" to the uprights. Target to the highest. "Home" to the shortest. The slope or ramp is made in two halves; join these together and fit into the slots in the uprights, but before securing to the highest upright, thread the Duck on the ramp. Move the Duck to its starting pen and the game is then ready for playing.

After that, if you still have energy to play;
Load the repeater gun with an agreed number of rubber bands and take aim at the target to start the duck in motion. When this has been accomplished fire the remaining bands on the gun to knock over the Duck before it reaches the "Home" pen.

Scoring is calculated according to the number of points indicated by the duck's head on the base board when it is pointing downwards,

If a player has some remaining bands on his gun after scoring, the Duck is returned to the starting pen for further play.

Summary:
Sphlag is a capture-the-flag game for two players.

This is a novel Capture-the-phlag game design for the Shibumi game family.
In this game, 2 players face each other, and try to surround their opponent's flag (or, in this case, "phlag") with their own spherical pawns.
The game uses the 4x4 Shibumi board, with a pre-defined inset of immobile pieces, creating a battleground for rounds of joy with capture-the-phlag.

Rules: 
Starting position:
The board starts with four Red spheres, two in opposite corners, and two diagonally to an imagined line between these corners in the center of the board. The two players sit opposite each other. The sphere in front of each player represents their respective flag (or, in this case, "phlag"). The spheres in the middle represent a wall. These four spheres cannot be moved.

Movement rules:
Two players, White and Black, take turns either placing a sphere of their color within their half of the game board (the half facing the player, e.g. anywhere within the triangle B7-G2-G7 for the lower player, and A6-F1-A1 for the upper player), in any level possible, or by moving their sphere up to a total of 4 spaces. No spheres can be initially placed along the diagonal line indicated by the wall (A7-G1), or the opponent’s half, although spheres can be moved into these positions.
A "space" is defined as a space on the grid (not the holes). This means that moving from one hole to the next (for example, E7-C7) involves moving 2 spaces. Player movements may involve movements up or down levels. If a sphere that supports only one other sphere is moved, the top sphere drops down into the vacated hole. If a sphere supports more than one other sphere, it is locked into place.

Other rules:
The last turn's board position cannot be repeated (ko rule). Passing is allowed. If a player cannot make a legal move, the player passes.

Aim:
Players aim to touch their opponent’s flag with as many of their own spheres as possible. There are three legal positions at which an opponent’s flag can be "touched"; E7, F6, G5 for the lower flag, and A3, B2, C1 for the upper flag.

Winning conditions:
Players win instantly upon moving at least two of their spheres into touching contact with the opponent’s flag. Otherwise, the game ends when both players pass sequentially or if both players agree to end the game. The player that has more spheres touching the opponent’s flag after ending the game wins. In case of a draw with respect to the number of spheres touching the opponent’s flag, the player with fewer spheres on the board wins. If both players have identical numbers of spheres touching their opponent’s flag, and equal numbers of spheres in the game, the game ends in a draw.

Examples of legal moves are shown in the files section.

Designed by Lars Rose, 2011.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Tyranid Enemy Pack, a print-on-demand expansion for Space Hulk: Death Angel - The Card Game, includes two additions for Death Angel: a new deck of enemy cards to replace the standard Genestealer deck, and a new Location card type. The new Tyranid deck contains a variety of enemies to challenge even the most seasoned Space Marines, while the Hive Lord Location cards introduce the Tyranid’s epic and deadly overlords!

Not only is the new enemy deck chocked full of Tyranid baddies, but many of them are Elite Tyranids, equipped with deadly special abilities that trigger at different points within the turn sequence. The unpredictable nature of these enemies makes it even harder to coordinate your strategies, creating tenser moments as you make your way to the final location.

Speaking of final locations, the new Hive Lord cards in the Tyranid Enemy Pack offer an even greater challenge for the bravest combat teams. Rather than facing a deadly location, players will go up against a mighty Tyranid overlord, creating a thrilling boss fight for anyone able to survive long enough to experience it.

Soccer Game published by Perma Toys.

The game has plastic players that are set into the game board in depressions. Those depressions attract the soccer ball. The players are spring loaded and when the soccer ball falls into their area you can attempt to pass the ball or score a goal by flicking the player pieces. There is a goalie who can block shots on goal.

Sail forth from your home point seeking buried treasure. Players roll dice to see how far they sail. An bad roll results in damage. Once each player reaches buried treasure, they must dig it up. The first player to reach their home port with all the buried treasure wins the game.

CloverLeaf is a free print-to-play card game. It is a simple-to-learn yet thoughtful light strategy game for 2-4 players. Players place cards from their hands on the table in accordance with simple rules. Points are scored for covering more than one already-played card while matching the symbols on both cards.

As a two-player game, players can set up their opponents for big scores. With three or four players, the game becomes more about finding creative plays on the table.

There are four pdf files in the downloadable archive. Two are the fronts of one set of 16 cards and the rules. The other pdfs are for card backs and are optional. You will need one set of cards per player (32 cards for a two-player game.)

Party game. Players are given a story and they must look around during the party, to spot the correct words that are needed for the blanks in that story. The player who adds the most correct answers, wins.

Action game. The board is a red circle and the idea is to drop 5 white smaller circles on to the board from around a yard above, so that the red circle is completely covered. First to achieve this, wins.

"A Fast,Thrilling,Action Packed Game."

Robin the Boy Wonder has been captured by Batman's enemies. It is impossible for him to escape without assistance. Only Batman can set Robin free and to do so, he must overpower three of his villains,who are on guard in adjoining positions.

Each player has a flipper,a Batman and 4 counters. Batman is placed on the flipper and is flipped with a finger towards the board centre - Thus swooping down on the target. If he lands on one of the 8 enemy spaces, the player's coloured counter is placed there. Land in the centre and you gain an extra turn.

The player who first gets 3 counters in adjoining spaces has freed Robin and wins the game.

Contents
1 Game Surface.
4 Flipper's.
4 Playing Pieces.
24 Counter's.
Rules are printed on inner box lid.

The object of the game is to be the first player to save one of each of the five animals (dog, cat, pig, cow and chicken). Players can do so by correctly answering interesting animal fact questions, visiting the Cafe’and ordering from a kid friendly menu (which offers healthy alternative items like soup & salad, pasta, etc.), visiting the Animal Shelter and adopting a pet or by the luck of the spinner at the Farm.

Fur & Feathers game features various spaces that allow players to move ahead, forward, visit one of the three animal areas and other positive experiences. It was designed with children in mind and has no negative consequences, the players always have a fun experience during game play.

There are plenty of fun ways to win the game but strategy and a little luck can help too!

description:
From the game Introduction:
"A game about Bull Riding? Really? Yep.
8 Seconds from Glory is a fun little game that's made for having a good time with friends, family and everyone in between. It's a game with a little luck (drawing the "right" Bull) and a little skill (knowing when to use your Ride Dice). But it's mostly about fun!

Will you play it week in and week out? Probably not but you'll be able to pull it out occasionally and play without learning how to  play all over again. It's simple. It's fun. You remember fun right? That's why you play games"

8 Seconds from Glory is a very little and easy game that uses the simple throw of 2 dices to adjudicate the many situations the bull rider will face during his brief cavalcade. The result of the dice is confronted with compiled tables that will tell players the outcome of the phases, the dice is no way a blind roll. Every rider (and even the bulls!) have their characteristic and their traits, so a sparkle of role-playing game is gladly implemented, as are rules to make your rider or bull progress during rodeo seasons.

Very fun to play!

Similar to Monopoly, this game has you buying properties and collecting rent on them.  The financial scale is a bit different and one is buying franchises as opposed to houses and hotels.

All of the properties/franchises are related to Johnstown, Pennsylvania.  It was published to sponsor and promote the Conemaugh Valley Hospital, Junior Auxiliary, Group 9.

Place, a Swedish geographical trivia game, tests players' visual recognition of famous places worldwide, their ability to place them on the world map, and their ability to answer questions about each place within a simple roll & move and memory game framework. If you answer questions incorrectly, you have to memorize the answers because the same question can become relevant again. The first player to travel around the world and fill up his game board wins.

Present Drop was the winning entry in the BoardGameCamp 2010 game jam, for a game that fitted entirely on a sheet of A4. The prize was it being printed on the back of every Cadbury selection box the following year.

It's a Christmas tile-placing game for 2-4 players in which presents fall down chimneys into hearths. However, the path of falling presents does not always run smoothly and players may divert presents into their own hearths by altering the course of the chimneys.

Chimney tiles are positioned each turn by players hoping to funnel presents into their hearths or away from other players'. The chimneys may split, curve or even multiply the number of presents that fall through them. Some chimney tiles have presents on them that signal a drop. Before those tiles are placed, presents fall down each column and into the hearths. Each present is worth a point. At the end of the game there is a final present drop and the players total up their points.

Based on the Television show of the same name. There are many international versions of the show, including The Shark Tank, in the United States.

From the CBC website:

You are a Dragon. You are going to hear about some amazing ideas; some are great successes and some are embarrassing flops! With over 300 inventions from Victorian times to the present, can you sort out the brilliant from the bonkers? Are you in or out?

The goal of the Dragons' Den Board Game is simple - make as much money as you can! You start with $500,000 and three Dragon cards. In each round of the game, you and your fellow Dragons hear about six inventions. You must decide if each invention is a Goldmine, a Success or a Flop, and make an investment on the appropriate square on the board. You double your money if you're right; you lose your money if you're wrong, and you can outsmart and out-deal the other Dragons along the way.

The game includes: a table-top size full colour game board, 340 invention cards, 18 Dragon cards, 2 markers, and a big bundle of Dragon dollars.

This is an additional map card available as an exclusive reward for those who pledged to support the "DiceAFARI" Kickstarter project.

Game description from the publisher:

boardgame: Bare Your Fangs: The Hunt is On
description:
"Bare Your Fangs: The Hunt is On" is an expansion to the "Bare Your Fangs" game and requires the core game to play. It adds a third active player by replacing the non-player-character (NPC) Hunter die with 5 new customized Hunter character dice. The NPC dice can be kept in case there aren't 3 active players. Also included is a bloodstone life tracker and rules, which will fit and be shipped in the zippered game pouch.

Now up to 3 people can battle royale! As with the core game, multiple copies will allow for team play.

It's a fencing tournament, where they can participate 2 to 8 players simultaneously. It is a game dominated intuition, but also requires a  little strategy to determine which will be the  action  most suitable sensing and analyzing the possibilities of the rival.

Actions on Pedana are carried out contemporary, following  a timeline, with a dynamic system that  determines a number of  actions variable simultaneously on each shift.

"Korrutuskalake" is a simple math game for very little kids, to teach them multiplying in the range of 100.
In the game you have small river with a table for each number from 1 to 10, making it the multiplications of 1 to 100.
Each player on their turn roll 2 d10 dice and then have to quickly say the multiplication of the two and add their fish to the board, on the right number.

The winner is the player who manages get all their fish into the lake first.

The game about reducing the waste produced for The Environmental Board of Estonia.

Usual roll and move game where winner is the player who reaches first to finish. Player who landed space with logo must say before next rolling something about reducing or avoiding the waste.

There are 4 cards with different garbage can and 46 different packages for sorting the correct garbage cans.

Players place the cards from their hands for the garbage cans and write down their card numbers. If the deck is exhausted they check cards have they placed to correct garbage can. 
Player who has played the most cards correctly is the winner.

From the back cover:
Who's got the most extensive telecoms coverage? Who has the best quality? Whose coverage includes every village, town and city - right through to the most far flung corner of every region across the whole country?
COVERAGE! is a challenging and exciting board game where you have to develop your won telecoms network. Your playground is whole Estonia - so competition is fierce!

The telecommunications sector is developing rapidly: you have to be in it to win it, with victory going to a truly talented network operative - one who's able to plan several steps ahead, able to think big and act local, one who's not afraid of taking risks!

Elisa'a new board game COVERAGE! is a tricky, fast paced showdown between players who need to rely on both cunning and luck to win. The gauntlet has been thrown down: let the game begin!

Includes: game board, 60 game cards, 186 tokens, 15 coverage markers

Tactyx is played on a 12 x 12 board. There are 4 coloured sections on each edge of the board. Diagonal moves are not allowed. To make this important rule visible all the time, the board is painted with 12 x 12 circles which are connected with vertical and horizontal lines (and no diagonal ones).

There are 2 red and 2 black banks on two opposing sides of the board. These bands are used to determine the coordinates of each place in a section on the board.

Each player has 6 tokens. At the beginning of the game the board is empty. By throwing the 2 dice, one red, the other black, the players determine the location where their tokens enter the game (in the section corresponding their color).

The goal of the game is to move all 6 tokens from the starting section to the diagonally opposing section. The first player who reached this, is the winner.

From the back cover:
"Eesti Laulude Mäng" (The game of Estonian songs) is a board game for ages 12+, although we are sure, that even a younger players can handle the game and find answers the adults cannot. So, one might say, that the game is meant for all ages just like the differential Estonian music. The can be played alone and as a team.

In this game you need to answer six different questions. Players move around the board according to the track drawn there and answering the questions according to the space they are standing on. Important thing is to keep a fun and happy attitude and even sing along with the songs. When a player suddenly finds themselves on  Rock board on a Pirate space, the player must give up one card he had already won. The reason this game has a Pirate space is because in real life a lot of music is stolen as well, so hopefully this space has a small motivational purpose as not to steal music. We are sorry! The Pirate space will not affect a player who already has 6 cards. When players finds himself on a corner, then he can choose the category. You need to collect 6 different colored cards, this is how you win the golden record. 
To win the game, you need to stand on one of the finish squares and the player to the left ask one question and this time you need to sing the answer. Get it right, and win the game.

From the back cover:
Board game "Teadlik Tarbija" (Smart Consumer) got started in the Consumer Protection Department competition for school children named "We can do it". The game was designed by a Tartu Hiie school children and they presented it in 2005 for the named competition. All the art were made by a local school girl Cathlyn Olev (from Viimsi High 7th grade).

In the Tartu Hiie school all children there wither have a hearing or seeing disability. They say about the game they made like this:

"It was really hard to find the idea for the game at first. We looked at many different types of games and finally we thought to make something like Monopoly, but simpler. We thought, that a usual dice game with questions would be simple enough for us to do. We thought up the questions and made the cards. So that we wouldn't have to know all the answers, we made few cards with answers already on them, so we could move forward and backwards as well. The goal of the game is to learn about consuming and how to do it with reason!"

Game is played with dice rolls and answering questions. First person to reach the end, will win the game.

Following in the sandy footprints of Isla Tetra comes its card-driven 2-player cousin, Isla Tetra 2.

It’s been three days since that terrible event: The violent turbulence; the acrid smell of smoke; the sudden descent and then the panicked screams of other passengers. You’re in shock, hungry and disoriented. The shore is littered with debris from the crash, but what to do first? Food? Fire? Explore the island? Help!

Expert survivors will accrue more points during their time on Isla Tetra through cunning inventory expansion, clever health management, and successful completion of a set number of survival tasks. The game is played solo, or as a survival competition against the clock between 2 players - yes, it didn’t take long for an argument to break out over scarce supplies and resources, and now it’s a race to simply stay alive.

You might think you can get by, simply walking the sun-kissed sands hunting for red snapper, allowing the hypnotic effect of the crashing waves to ease your injuries, but as you soak up the sun events back home are taking a turn for the worse: As the months tick by, the search for your downed plane is eventually called off, your job is readvertised, and your car has been sold to keep up with the bills. After two years of waiting, your family holds a memorial service and your partner is now returning to a social life as a single person.

Players must strike a balance between plundering the island’s resources for points while ensuring they’re not last off the island to face the worst news once home (and the most penalty points). Each day you’ll beachcomb the golden sands to see what has been gifted to you from the azure, salty brine. Use this flotsam and jetsam, such as rusty cans and broken umbrellas, represented by chunky, 3D DriftCube shapes, to build a shelter, craft essential tools and a fishing spear; then explore deep into the island's heart. Link the same DriftCubes together on your playmat to ‘build’ action grids, such as exploring, fishing, and foraging for food. In these instances DriftCube shapes represent time as opposed to raw materials.

It’s a race for survival. It’s a race to get home...

On a hexagonal gameboard, two or three players must move their pawns and drive out competitors. SPIN. A solitaire game for the Series: Shibumi set

Starting the game
The player starts with a given hand of marbles of diferent colours. The other balls remain in the bag and are used as a resource pool.

The turn
1. The player places one ball from his hand on the board.
2. If the marble touches another ball of a different colour, both balls are replaced with two balls (taken from the pool) of the other third colour. 
    
Game end
The player wins if every ball from his hand is on the board and all of them are of the same colour.

Campaigns in WW2 #2 Expansion Kit

As part of Army Group South’s effort in Operation Barbarossa, the Romanian 3rd and 4th Armies were tasked with covering the right flank of the offensive, clearing the area between the Dniester and Bug Rivers, and occupying the port city of Odessa. Axis planners hoped the badly outnumbered Soviet garrison in this area would collapse quickly, but the hastily assembled Independent Coastal Army managed to withstand 73 days of siege before being evacuated to the Crimea by the Black Sea Fleet, effectively gutting the cream of the pre-war Romanian Army in the process.

The Objective: Odessa expansion kit includes a map extension and additional units to cover the added area and forces that participated in this region during the campaign.

Note, Objective: Odessa is not a ‘stand alone’ game. You must own a copy of Objective: Kiev to use this expansion kit.

Game Data:

Complexity: 3 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 7 on a 9 scale
Scale: Each unit is one German corps or Russian army, and each turn is approximately one to two weeks.

Game Components:

•    Eight 5/8" square pieces
•    One 8" x 11" map extension
•    One Rules booklet

description:
Word Wave is the fast-paced game of word making and taking.  This game challenges you to find words fast.  Create words and shout them out as wave after wave of letter tiles launch from the device.  Steal words from other players by adding letters to make new words.  The player with the most points when the last letters launch, wins!

A switch on the side of the Word Wave machine lets you choose how often new letter tiles shoot out of the device.  Each round lasts ten minutes or less.

Yahtzee variant with five colour dice instead of number dice. Unlike most such variants for children (for instance Yahtzee Jr. which in Swedish has the same name as this game), in this one you do not keep a record of categories chosen in previous rounds. Instead each round involves rolling and aiming afresh for one of five categories: two pairs (2p), three of a kind (3p), full house (5p), four of a kind (7p) or five of a kind (10p). After ten rounds, points are added up to determine the winner.

The one who first arranges a fragrant, colorful bouquet - wins!

To win the game you need to collect 6 (2 players), 4 (3 players) or 3 (4 players) tokens with flowers on them.
Players move around the game board and trying to collect flower tokens, but not all tokens are good.
Broken branch - move 3 steps to which ever direction
Crown - miss a turn
Rubin heart - miss two turns
Shoe - stay until roll a 3

Snow White made a little party for her friends. She is still waiting for the seven dwarves, who are on their why home from the mines. Help the all get home as fast as you can. Road is long and Snow White is waiting them badly.
Snow White's Party - fun game on 3D game board.

Each player plays one dwarf and game is simple roll and move game. On the way to the house, there are many paths and on each path there are many spaces for a dwarf to land on.
Butterfly stone - roll more than 4, you get +3 moves, you roll less then 4, you miss 2 turn, you roll 1, you go back to the beginning.
White cloud - change places with who ever on the board.
Rabbit - +2 moves
Mushroom - -2 moves
Squirrel - miss a turn
Little bird - miss a turn
Evil witch - miss 2 turn
Sleepy - whoever reaches him first, must carry him with himself.  Trick is that with Sleepy, you only move when you roll odd number (1,3,5), so be wise.

First player to reach Snow White, is the winner.

Promotional game published by Cadbury's. It must have been quite expensive to make because it utilised three companies. Cadbury paid for the project. Britains, the world famous maker of toy soldiers, made the playing pieces. And De La Rue printed everything.

The rules;

1. Each player may choose their favourite Cococub playing piece. During the game you should address yourself by his name.
2. Each player starts from Mr. Pie Porker's garden (in the centre of the board). A player throws the die and moves along the track, the number thrown.
3. The blue and black squares are penalties. The yellow squares are rewards.
4. You cannot land on the same square as another Cococub. If that should happen, then you don't move at all for that turn.
5. The first player to get back to Pie Porker's garden, wins. It is necessary to roll the exact number to get back into the garden.
6. The winner should ask mother for a bar of Cadbury's Milk Chocolate.

"UNITED" is the goal, a pure strategy game for 2 or 3 people.

It is a game that opens the mind, as they play the games is achieved more quickly display the wide variety of possible moves for each piece.

In a hexagonal board, further divided into "rings", each chip has the potential to move in 14 different ways.

Try to connect the pieces themselves go while seeking to block and separate the adversaries.

It comes in three exciting game modes.

"UNIDOS" es el objetivo, un juego de pura estrategia para 2 o 3 personas.

Es un juego que abre la mente, a medida que se juegan las partidas se logra visualizar más rápidamente la amplia variedad de posibilidades de movimientos para cada pieza.

En un tablero exagonal, dividido además en "anillos", cada una de las fichas tiene el potencial de moverse en 14 sentidos diversos.

Hay que tratar de ir conectando las piezas propias mientras se busca bloquear y separar las adversarias.

Se presenta con tres interesantes modalidades de juego.

Each turn a player takes the role of entrepreneur, the other players become insurers.
The entrepreneur must negotiate insurance contracts with other players to protect themselves from harm.
5 categories of events, fire, theft, illness, accident, water damage.

Players have cards in their hands that will be part of contract negotiations.

Once all players have toured as a businessman, the richest wins.

A game for two buccaneers.
After placing the 12 treasure cards (six vertically - including two face-down cards and six horizontally including 2 face-down cards), each player takes his pack of 18 cards buccaneers.
The players draw cards from their packs and play a in turn on the table.
When all cards are played, they look at who is in the majority for each treasure in each row and column.

Players add up the values ​​of their treasures to know how many points they totaled for that round.

Each player takes a set of cards and place before him the map engine. At the same time, players will return the first card in their deck and place it next to the locomotive. The following cards are drawn and then:

1) be placed at the end of the train if the number of animals is greater than the last card of the convoy
2) be placed on a card already in the convoy when the number of animals is less than or equal to the last card of the convoy

When all cards have been drawn, the player with the longest convoy wins. A tie, the one with the largest number of animals visible wins

An abstract roll and move game for two players.  Each player has eight pawns, divided between two start areas. The goal is to be the first to get one pawn to the center of the board, following the pathways.  This is complicated by the twelve polished rocks that start on designated spaces.  pawns cannot pass rocks, but by landing a pawn on a rock exactly, a player can relocate the rock to hinder the other players pawns.

As a present to all of their fans, WizKids offered these three Print and Play Creature cards to add some of the holiday "spirit" to your Quarriors!

The three Quarriors! X-Mas Promo Cards cards are:

     GHOST OF X-MAS PAST » When Ghost of X-Mas is destroyed, name a die and draw one die from your bag. If you draw the die you named, add it to your Active Pool. If not, either give the die to the player on your left (add it to their Used Pile or add the die to your own Used Pile (your choice). Merry X-mas!
     GHOST OF X-MAS PRESENT » When Ghost of X-Mas scores, name a die and draw one die from your bag. If you draw the die you named, add it to your Active Pool. If not, either give the die to the player on your left (add it to their Used Pile or add the die to your own Used Pile (your choice). Merry X-mas!
     GHOST OF X-MAS FUTURE » When Ghost of X-Mas is summoned, name a die and draw one die from your bag. If you draw the die you named, add it to your Active Pool. If not, either give the die to the player on your left (add it to their Used Pile or add the die to your own Used Pile (your choice). Merry X-mas!

British edition. The board has little indentations, into which the ball might roll. When it settles into the indentation in front of one of your players, you can bend him a little and let go. The ball will now be 'kicked', hopefully to one of your other players.

Most goals wins.

"How many birds can you discover".

Party game. Each guest or team is given list of 14 different cards (which are placed around the room). Each card has a little rhyme that is a cryptic clue to the name of a bird.

First player or team to get all 14 right, wins.

A game purely about gambling.

There are 8 previous English Derby winners and players are invited to place a bet or bets. The wheel is something like a Roulette wheel but features only the names of the horses. Once bets are placed, spin the wheel, and collect your profit if you are lucky. The role of Bookie should be passed on until everyone has had a turn of being the Bookie.

The Eight Derby winners featured in the game;
Blue Peter (1939)
Dante (1945)
Airborne (1946)
Pearl Diver (1947)
My Love (1948)
Nimbus (1949)
Galcador (1950)
Arctic Prince (1951)

Hoopla version, where you try to land your rings on parts od Mr. Turnip's body. On the board that the Mr. Turnip figure sits, there are also scoring areas, four with a positive score, two with a negative score.

Mr. Turnip was created by puppeteer Joy Laurey (credited in the top left corner of the box lid), for the children's programme, Whirligig. The show was a Saturday, fortnightly, BBC TV children's programme, broadcast at 5pm. It ran from November 1950 to 1956. It was the first TV variety show specifically for children and was broadcast live from the studios in Lime Grove. Not many people owned TV sets in those early years, but those that did usually opened their homes to others, especially when children's programmes featured. Virtually the whole country watched this one and only TV channel.

Promotional board game, published by the British roadside assistance company, Green Flag.

Box says it "Contains the Official Theory Test Questions."
It should prepare the learner driver - Assist the young cyclist - and refreshes the experienced driver.

Approved by the Department of Transport, the questions are mainly from the government's Learner Driver's Official Theory Test.

The object is to be the first player to reach the National Car Park barrier. Movement is with a dice and the game has car playing pieces. Answer various questions as you travel round the board. Watch out for penalty cards and direction arrows.

Contents
1 Game Board.
6 Car Playing Pieces.
3 Packs of Cards.
1 Die and Shaker.
1 Set of Rules.

A game of pure chance.

The game consists of a wheel with six horses on it. The wheel can be made to spin, causing the horses to go past the Winning Post each circuit of the spin.

Players bet on which horse will be last to go past the Winning Post. That horse will be numbered and that number light comes on at the side of the board.

The onjective is to have the most tokens that you used to bet with (you might even try to bet with real money).

Objective: be the first to pass finish line (field 88)
learn more about Franconia
- moving by turns/simple 'Roll and Move' game -

each player buys in getting an equal amount of (numbered sightseeing) city cards (18 in total);
(pewter) meeples are moved according the die roll; 
ending on a space possessing a card of it should be flipped, read and can be used as a joker, if later player ends on a marked space (see rules) with bad news...
remark: game belongs to the series: "Scholz' künstlerische Spiele" with very nice graphics/images
game was published early 1930's

You and your friends must complete the mission. The king needs your help. The goal is complete the mission figured on the mission´card or destroy your enemies (the opposite team)
Put 4 Crystals anywhere on the gameboard and begin your adventure.
To move your heroes, you must roll the roulette in the center on the board.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Objective: be first to pass finish line (field 100)
 - moving by turn/simple 'Roll and Move' game -
rolling the die each player moves und follows instructions (rule sheet) ending on a marked space

- one of Spika's very typical games -

In Co-opoly: The Game of Cooperatives , players must collaborate to found and run a democratic business. In order to survive as individuals and to strive for the success of their co-op, players make tough choices regarding big and small challenges while putting their teamwork abilities to the test.

Challenges consist of mini games of charades, guess the drawing or "unspoken", in which the active player has to get the other players to guess the word on the card without saying the word or related words.  Completing challenges earns points for your co-op.  As the co-op marker moves around the board world cards may have to be drawn that can take points from the individual players, from the co-op pool of points or can be beneficial by giving bonus challenges or points.  The players win when they achieve the goal of starting up a second co-op.

This is an exciting game of skill and solidarity, where everyone wins – or everybody loses. Will the Point Bank continue to dominate the players’ lives, or will they break free and take control by jump-starting the movement for a truly democratic and cooperative economy in their community?

By playing Co-opoly, players discover the unique benefits, challenges, and operations of the cooperative world – as well as the skills needed to participate in a co-op!
Co-opoly is for teens and adults.

Abstract: 
A strategy game for two players in which each attempts to control the most space on the hexagonal table by placing and flipping coins.

Overview:

Board: A hexagonal board divided up into a number of hexagons, resembing a honeycomb. Standard board size has 61 hexagon spaces measuring 1.25 inch across arranged in a hexagon shape, with each side of the board the length of five hexagon spaces and the middle of the board being nine hexagon spaces wide if measured between any two opposite corners. Non standard boards may be used, as long as the shape is a true hexagon.

Pieces: 100 coins with distinctive heads and tails markings. Standard coins are the same size and weight as US pennies, with each face painted a distinctive color. 
Coins may also be used in place of standard game coins.

Objective: to control the most spaces on the hexagonal board.

Rules:
1. For two players. Players alternate turns. Each player chooses a color or coin face On a player's turn, he may do one of the following:

A: place a coin on any space on the hexagon spaces on the board, with his own color or his own face up.

B: touch a coin that is already on the board. see "touching a coin"

C: Flip a coin of his opponents over to his own color(or face). see "flipping a coin"

D: make a castle. see "making a castle".

2. The last coin that has been put on the board, flipped, or touched cannot be flipped. It is "safe" until another coin has been put on the board, touched or flipped.

3. The game ends when all spaces on the board are filled. Count up the number of spaces each player controls. The player who controls the most spaces wins.

TOUCHING A COIN
On your turn, if you choose, you may touch a coin with your finger. That coin cannot be flipped until another coin has been touched, flipped, or put on the board. Use this to defend a coin for one turn.

FLIPPING A COIN
A coin of an opponents color may be flipped to your color if These criteria are met:
A: You have two coins of your color/face on hexagonal spaces adjoining the coin you want to flip
B: The coin is not "safe"(has been put on the board, flipped, or touched on the opponent's most recent turn.
C: The coin is not part of a castle.

MAKING A CASTLE:
If there are three coins of your color on adjoining spaces arranged in a triangle, you can place a coin on top of those three to make a castle*. Now these coins cannot be flipped for the entire game. A coin can be part of more than one castle, as long as the coin making the castle is placed on top of three coins on the board.

    Note that only one coin may be flipped or placed per turn. A castle will take a minimum of 4 turns to build.

Chip-A-Tak is an abstract, checkers-like game that allows both capture and recapture of an opponent's pieces.  Unlike checkers, Chip-A-Tak employs a dice roll to determine the number of spaces that a player may move his chips as he attempts to reach the other side of the board.

Originally intended as a gambling game, Chip-A-Tak includes rules for wagering and for repurchasing captured chips using either actual currency or the imitation money included with the game.

Chip-A-Tak is played on an 8x10 checkers-style board, with each player having a rear row of 8 spaces filled with chips and an adjacent row with the center four spaces filled.

boardgame: Safari dans les Pays de la Loire
description:
Cultural and historical quizz about a french district.
Players must reach the finish (Nantes) by rolling dice and answering questions.
For each error a player set back one space (city) and must answer an other question (we go back 3 spaces maximum).
For a good answer, the player stay in the city and the next player take its turn.

boardgame: Quiz: Jede Antwort bringt eine neue Frage
description:
"Try and guess" game : 52 cards having a question and a letter.

Each player try to answer a question with a word beginning with one of its letter. This will give the next question.

The first player who plays their last card wins the game.

(NOTE: This is an ASL expansion.  A game of similar title is published for the ATS system by the same publisher).

From the publisher:
"Darkest December is a detailed ASL comp variant module that tells the story of the Battle of the Bulge from the perspective of the 3rd US armored division, facing down the vaunted 116 Panzer Division.  Hitler's Wacht Am Rhein offensive was under way, and the yanks of the 3rd Armored, along with engineers from the 23rd and 51st engineer battalions faced the 'Greyhound' Panzer Division and the 560th Volksgrenadier Division - and were soon cordoned off form friendly lines...."

The module includes three historical maps, two counter sheets and 20 scenarios.
family: History: World War II (Battle of the Bulge)

description:
Play Hide-and-Seek on your living room table!

This card game is based on an old-fashioned game called "Free the Box" that was played outside by friends many years ago. It's a table-top version of Hide-and-Seek with a twist, where you play cards to try and capture your opponents or those childhood friends you just could never find! They have to match the same card or play a "Capture" card or a "Wild" card to avoid being found or captured. Each game has several rounds or hands and when one player finishes theirs all other players add points for any left-over cards. The game ends when one player reaches 60 points but the lowest score wins the game.
This card game is quick to setup, learn and play and has a few hidden surprises waiting for you around the corner!

Shooting target game. The target is unusual, as it stands on a tripod and spins freely. When the Red Indian target is hit, the targets spins around.

Most hits, wins

That's Life! was a magazine-style television series on BBC1 which aired from 1973 to 1994, presented by Esther Rantzen (1940 - ) throughout the entire run. Esther features on the box for this game. The show concerned itself with consumer problems and complaints and this theme is addressed in this game.

The object of the game is to be the first player to acquire a Bath, a TV Set, a Cooker, a Car and a Footballing Dog for their home. (That is right, I did say a Footballing Dog - the show often featured viewer's pets that were rather unusual).

Players move around the board in order to try to obtain the items. There are the usual squares, such as trade squares (players can trade amongst themselves if they wish).

Watch out! If you sell a leaky bath or a broken-down car,you could be put in the hot seat by Esther. Esther can Be called in to expose the rogue traders.

Contents
1 Game Board.
4 Playing Pieces.
4 Sets of Pieces(Bath,TV Set,Cooker,Dog and Car).
1 Pack of Service Cards.
1 TV Camera Piece
1 Die
Rules Are Printed on Inner Box Base.

"Race Around the House With Sooty and Friends"

The object of the game is to be the first player,to race around each of the rooms in Sooty's home. You must avoid being caught for the 'Mischief Doings', and arrive at the finish with an exact throw. Instructions on squares must be obeyed (miss a turn, etc.).

When a player lands on a Mischief Square it is read out. The player who holds that Mischief Card is deemed to have caused the mischief and must miss the set number of turns.

There are two routes to take in the bedroom - If you cannot get over the bed in one throw, you will miss extra turns (so, you cannot 'bounce' on the bed!!)

Contents
1 3D Game Board.
3 Card Sections.
15 Mischief Cards.
4 Playing Pieces With Stands.
1 Die.
Rules Are Printed on Inner Box

Essen 2011 promotional mini expansion for the game Fief.
Include 5 holy relics (reliques) and each holy relic has a special power that its possessor may use if they wish.
Available in the Plato Magazine issue #41

Objective: be the first to travel to arrival point
learn more about folks and country
- simple 'Roll and Move' game, moving by turn -

rolling the die each player moves forward:
you can't kick opponents - ending on a marked space
follow instructions ==> see rule sheet

Objective: be the first ending on field 24
 - acting by turn -
all stars -coloured on 1 side - are flipped, tools (e.g. crib) are assembled
starting on field 1
each player draws a 'star' (instead of using a die);
if the star shows a colour of one of the next three spaces ==>
move forward to that space - otherwise don't move
if that space is marked follow instructions printed on cover inside
if all stars are discarded ==> shuffle and draw again
